Service
User can perform a Start, Stop or Restart operations on the MoSMB service.
For any change/s in the MoSMB server configuration files /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json and/or /etc/mosmb/s3.json in any section/s it is mandatory to restart the MoSMB server.
Notes: : 1. systemctl support is available after release builds 073. 2. One can use Either service commands or systemctl commands throughout the cycle.
MoSMB Start
Command : EITHER service mosmb start OR systemctl start mosmb Output  : Starting MoSMB service...

When MoSMB is being used with ZooKeeper server, the sequence of starting these services is important as MoSMB is ZooKeeper's client and ZooKeeper takes the locks on certain files which is released after 200 secs timeout if the MoSMB / ZooKeeper server is exited abruptly or MoSMB / ZooKeeper server machine is rebooted.

1. Start ZooKeeper - Command : sh  /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh start Output  : JMX enabled by default Using config: ../apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/conf/zoo.cfg' Mode: Standalone Mode: Leader / Follower / Standalone this output will be different on individual servers depending on the MoSMB-ZooKeeper configurations 2. ZooKeeper Cleanup -
       ZooKeeper Cleanup is must when MoSMB or Zookeeper goes down (services or machines) while any smb operations are in progress.
       To Cleanup ZooKeeper nodes refer - MoSMB-ZooKeeper server cleanup 3. Start MoSMB - Command : EITHER service mosmb start OR systemctl start mosmb Output  : Starting MoSMB service...

When MoSMB is being used with ZooKeeper server, the sequence of starting these services is important as MoSMB is ZooKeeper client and ZooKeeper takes the locks on certain files which is released after 200 secs timeout if the MoSMB / ZooKeeper server is exited abruptly or MoSMB / ZooKeeper server machine is rebooted.

1. Start ZooKeeper on all nodes - Command : sh  /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh start Output  : JMX enabled by default Using config: ../apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/conf/zoo.cfg' Mode: Leader Mode: Leader / Follower this output will be different on individual servers depending on the MoSMB-ZooKeeper configurations 2. ZooKeeper Cleanup on all nodes -
       ZooKeeper Cleanup is must when MoSMB or Zookeeper goes down (services or machines) while any smb operations are in progress.
       To Cleanup ZooKeeper nodes refer - MoSMB-ZooKeeper server cleanup 3. Start MoSMB on all nodes - Command : EITHER service mosmb start OR systemctl start mosmb Output  : Starting MoSMB service...
Note :
* If MoSMB server and ZooKeeper server are configured on 2 separate machines and only MoSMB service/server goes down then ZooKeeper cleanup is not a requirement.
MoSMB Stop
Command : EITHER service mosmb stop OR systemctl stop mosmb Output  : Stopping MoSMB service...

When MoSMB is being used with ZooKeeper server, the sequence of stopping these services / shutting down the machines is important as MoSMB is ZooKeeper client and ZooKeeper takes the locks on certain files which is released after 200 secs timeout if the MoSMB / ZooKeeper server is exited abruptly or MoSMB / ZooKeeper server machine is rebooted.

1. Stop MoSMB service Command : EITHER service mosmb stop OR systemctl stop mosmb Output  : Stopping MoSMB service... 2. Stop ZooKeeper service/s Command : apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh stop Output  : .... Stopping zookeeper ... STOPPED

When MoSMB is being used with ZooKeeper server, the sequence of stopping these services / shutting down the machines is important as MoSMB is ZooKeeper client and ZooKeeper takes the locks on certain files which is released after 200 secs timeout if the MoSMB / ZooKeeper server is exited abruptly or MoSMB / ZooKeeper server machine is rebooted.

1. Stop MoSMB service on all nodes - Command : EITHER service mosmb stop OR systemctl stop mosmb Output  : Stopping MoSMB service... 2. Stop ZooKeeper service on all nodes - Command : apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh stop Output  : .... Stopping zookeeper ... STOPPED
MoSMB Restart
MoSMB service restart is mandatory in case any changes are made to the configuration file.
Command : EITHER service mosmb restart OR systemctl restart mosmb Output  : Stopping MoSMB service... Starting MoSMB service...

In case MoSMB goes in an unstable state due to node/process/network failures causing MoSMB and/or zookeeper node/s to go down or unreachable within recovery time periods, then follow the steps as mentioned below for clean restart the MoSMB along with MoSMB-ZooKeeper.

1. Stop MoSMB - Command : EITHER service mosmb stop OR systemctl stop mosmb 2. Run /etc/mosmb/scripts/zk-cleanup.sh script to clear the zookeeper database and logs on zookeeper node. ( If zookeeper is not on the MoSMB node, in that case manually delete all the files in <zookeeper-datadir>/version-2 and <zookeeper-install-dir>/logs ) /etc/mosmb/scripts/zk-cleanup.sh <zookeper-install-dir> 3. Stop zookeeper <zookeeper-install-dir>/bin/zkServer.sh stop 4. Start zookeeper <zookeeper-install-dir>/bin/zkServer.sh start 5. Start MoSMB service mosmb start

In case MoSMB cluster goes in an unstable state due to node/process/network failures causing multiple MoSMB and/or zookeeper nodes to go down or unreachable within recovery time periods, then follow the steps as mentioned below for clean restart the whole MoSMB cluster along with MoSMB-ZooKeeper cluster.

1. Stop MoSMB cluster (mosmb service on all nodes) Command : EITHER service mosmb stop OR systemctl stop mosmb 2. Delete all mo_openfiletable files from pstore path
   3. Stop zookeeper cluster (zookeeper on all nodes) <zookeeper-install-dir>/bin/zkServer.sh stop 4. Run the zookeeper cleanup script by MoSMB to clear the zookeeper database and logs on all zookeeper nodes in the cluster. <zk-cleanup script> <zookeper-install-dir path> /etc/mosmb/scripts/mosmb_zk-cleanup.sh <zookeper-install-dir path> (For Release versions MoSMB : 3.1.1.005 onwards) /etc/mosmb/scripts/zk-cleanup.sh <zookeper-install-dir path> (For Release versions MoSMB : 3.1.1.003 and before) ( If zookeeper is not on the MoSMB node, in that case manually delete all the files in <zookeeper-datadir>/version-2 and <zookeeper-install-dir>/logs OR in a workaround copy the clean up script to mosmb-zookeeper node and run it from there.)   
   
   5. Start zookeeper cluster (zookeeper on all nodes) <zookeeper-install-dir>/bin/zkServer.sh start 6. Start MoSMB cluster (mosmb service on all nodes) service mosmb start
MoSMB Status
Command : EITHER service mosmb status OR systemctl status mosmb Output  : MoSMB service is running OR MoSMB service is NOT running
MoSMB Process ID

Command : ps aux | grep mosmb OR pidof mosmb Output  : root      7521  4.7  4.5 1646804 272104 pts/1  Sl   14:31   0:00 mosmb root      7534  0.0  0.0 112708   976 pts/1    S+   14:31   0:00 grep --color=auto mosmb
MoSMB Logs

Once you start the MoSMB service, check the mosmb.log located at default path /var/log/ to see if MoSMB has started successfully without any errors. For any errors, check the Troubleshooting Guide or FAQs for assistance. Logs directory can be configured as per the requirement by editing parameter logsPath in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json . In logs directory MoSMB will create files mosmb.log, mosmb_audit.log, mosmb_vinode.log Please send your queries to support@ryussi.com
For mosmb logs, the log level values for all modules can be set in the conf file /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf
MoSMB Logs in release versions 3.1.1.039 and above
MO_CRITICAL = 0
MO_ERROR = 1
MO_WARN = 2
MO_INFO = 3
MO_DEBUG = 4
Default mosmb_logging.conf looks like

FSA_L           = 2
SMBCORE_L       = 2
CONNECTION_L    = 2
MULTICHANNEL_L  = 2
IOS_L           = 2
RDMA_L          = 2
WITNESS_L       = 2
OPLOCKS_L       = 2
MORPC_L         = 2
CONFIG_L        = 3
STATISTICS_L    = 2
LEASE_L         = 2
NDR_L           = 2
RPC_L           = 2
HASH_L          = 2
PST_L           = 2
LICENSE_L       = 2
AUDIT_L         = 2
AUTH_L          = 2
ENCRYPTION_L    = 2
VINODE_L        = 2
DEFAULT_L       = 3
COMMON_L        = 2
CLUSTER_L       = 2
PROFILER_L      = 2
The MO_CRITICAL log messages are printed with loglevel set to 0
(They will be printed for all other log level > 0 as well)
When the log level value set is to 1, error level logs are enabled (MO_ERROR Level) (They will be printed for all other log level > 1 as well)
The MO_WARN log messages are printed with log level set to 2 in the conf file.
(They will be printed for all other log level > 2 as well)

For modules CONFIG_L and DEFAULT_L, default value is set to 3, which is MO_INFO. The INFO logs are printed to give more information. You can choose to disable it and print only error level logs by setting the value to < 3
For module PROFILER_L, default is set to 0. When the value is set to 4, the detailed information is printed.
Any changes to log levels can be reloaded at runtime, (no need to restart mosmb) reload Log Level Changes
In case debug level is required, following guidelines can be used to set respective log modules to debug level

Login/Access issues- enable AUDIT_L , AUTH_L, SMBCORE_L
File I/O issues - enable IOS_L, FSA_L, SMBCORE_L
Connection related issues - enable CONNECTION_L , SMBCORE_L
Overall general profiling for timings - enable PROFILER_L
RPC failures- enable - RPC_L, SMBCORE_L
mosmb-zookeeper related issues- VINODE_L, SMBCORE_L

Use these logs for Security Monitoring.
One can set the audit module’s log level in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf file to 3 to get detailed audit logs.

AUDIT_L = 3
This level logs File operations like CREATE RENAME DELETE Or PERMISSION CHANGE With FileName and the User who is attempting the operation
Note: Logs for MO_CRIT, MO_ERROR, MO_WARN will also be included
If MO_WARN level logs are required, one can set

AUDIT_L = 2
This level logs LOGIN and LOGOFF attempts, success or failure, With Time and IP of machine the attempt was made from
The mosmb_audit.log audit log file will then be generated at the log location (default  /var/log). Further details are same as older releases.

These are the logs generated by the api used to communicate to the (zookeeper/inode) database.
In case of any vinode related errors (either inode Mechanism or zookeeper), user can refer to these logs to get more details. The vinode loglevel starts from 1. For value 0 you may get error and mosmb will not start.

VINODE_L = 1
  Or 2/3/4 etc
The mosmb_vinode.log log file is populated at the log location, with MO_ERROR,MO_WARN,MO_INFO (Rest could be the Zookeeper logs.)
MoSMB Logs in older releases

MO_ERROR = 0
MO_WARN = 1

MoSMB Logs rotation
When MoSMB is installed, the scripts for the log rotation of the mosmb logs are added in the /etc/cron.hourly directory (mologrotate) and the other files are in /etc/logrotate.d directory.
/etc/logrotate.d/mosmbauditlr
/etc/logrotate.d/mosmblr
/etc/logrotate.d/zookeeperlr
Logs are rotated when the log file size reaches 500 MB(default). The log size threshold reached is checked on an hourly basis.
mosmb.log and other log files are rotated nd refer to files mosmbauditlr (mosmb_audit.log), mosmblr (mosmb.log) and zookeeperlr (mosmb_vinode.log) present under /etc/logrotate.d/ respectively
Adding a share dynamically

Script: cmdpipe Function:
To add a new share to MoSMB server and reload the configuration without stopping the MoSMB server. Only single share can be applied at a time on reload command. To add multiple shares, add all the share names to mosmb.json under shareSection. Run the script to add each share one at a time. Reload MoSMB config will take some time. The newly added share's shareName should be unique. sharePath must be valid and must exists on the server. Before running the command, please make sure the mosmb.json is correct.
Prerequisite to run cmdpipe:
python2 should be installed.
tabulate module should be installed.
To install tabulate :
pip2 install tabulate

Example: To add a share without restarting the mosmb server 1. Add required VFS share details in mosmb.json under shareSection as a new share e.g. ‘share3' as follows. ’share1’ is an existing share. 2. Add required S3 share details in mosmb.json under shareSection as a new share e.g. ‘s3_share' as follows. ’share1’ is an existing share. 3. The command mentioned below will add share3 and reload MoSMB server configuration [root@sivm31 ~]# /etc/mosmb/cmdpipe config_reload_add -h Error occurred in GetOpt Use the following commands for mosmb config reload -n : Specifies share name -t : Specifies share type Share type can be one of these two : VFS or S3
Following is an Example: ./cmdpipe config_reload_add -n <share_name> -t vfs OR ./cmdpipe config_reload_add -n <share_name> -t S3

[root@sivm31 ~]# /etc/mosmb/cmdpipe config_reload_add -n share3 -t vfs 
Success
Command executed successfully

[root@sivm31 ~]# /etc/mosmb/cmdpipe config_reload_add -n s3_share -t S3 
Success
Command executed successfully
MoSMB statistics
MoSMB statistics can be collected anytime with the command pipe utility
/etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe statson
Optional Arguments to statson

-p, --path Specifies csv path. Default is /var/log/
-i, --interval Specifies time period in seconds. Default is 300 s
-c, --console Display statistics on 'console'
-n, --number Number of counts for statistics collection operation
-h, --help Shows this help message and Exit
Examples:

/etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe statson -p /var/log/ -i 60 & => Statistic collection will keep running in background untill mosmb is restarted or cmdpipe instance is stopped /etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe statson -p /var/log/ -i 120 -c => Display the stats on console after every '-i' interval /etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe statson -p /var/log/ -i 600 -c -n 5 => Display the stats on console after every '-i' interval for '-n' number of time
Following Abbreviations used while showing output on console using '-c' option

RIO(Read IOPS), WIO(Write IOPS), TIOPs(Total IOPS), RT(Read Throughput), WT(Write Throughput),
TT(MB/s)(Total Throughput MB/s), RL(Read Latency), WL(Write Latency), SRL(Storage Read Latency),
SWL(Storage Write Latency), FO(Total File/Pipe Opened), ES(Total Established Sessions),
DC(Total Disconnects), PV(Password Violations), PE(Total Permission Error)
When statistics are collected in csv files, following files get populated in the default path or the path mentioned in command with –p

1. preprocessor_stats.csv
2. server_stats.csv
3. worker_thread_stats.csv
4. smbdirect_stats.csv
5. processed_stats.csv
User can use the processed_stats.csv file to check the collected and processed statistics
processed_stats.csv can be used to integrate with monitoring tools to visualizing and monitoring the metrics
Performance Tuning
Set the mosmb log level to 0 (zero) in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf file.

Set the Required Message Signing to Off to improve the performance. This will not disable the signing completely: When the client sends a signed request the MoSMB server will send the signed response. For more information refer to Microsoft Network Server: Digitally Sign Communications

You can switch on multichannel by setting Enable MultiChannel to yes in the MoSMB configuration. Multiple network cards can be added at the server end. In case there are multiple network paths between the client and the server, the SMB3 protocol uses the multi-channel feature.
MoSMB is pre-tuned to perform well for most of the workloads. The MoSMB performance scales linearly with smb connections.
MoSMB service restart is mandatory in case any changes are made to the configuration file.

One can use the performance tuning script provided by MoSMB at location /etc/mosmb/scripts to apply suggested parameters w.r.t server machines CPU and RAM. To run for smbd parameters tuning ---- sh /etc/mosmb/scripts/perf_tuning.sh smbdirect To run for tcp parameters tuning ---- sh /etc/mosmb/scripts/perf_tuning.sh tcp Detailed usage is provided in script ---- sh /etc/mosmb/scripts/perf_tuning.sh -h
TCP Performance
In the MoSMB configuration, set all the transaction sizes to 8M. (Max Transaction Size, Max Read Size, Max Write Size)
Memory allocation is shared in all the TCP connections and Memory utilization is dependent on the following formula: preProcessorThreads * ringBufferCount * Transaction size

Ways to scale-up and scale-out MoSMB performance: i. In case the client connections are more in number, increase the server threads or pre-processing threads. ii. The number of threads in the worker thread-pool can be increased so that more number of SMB packets can be served at a time. The number of threads can be increased to 64/128/256 for a high-end system, depending on the CPU cores of the system. iii. The number of pre-allocated buffers can be increased as per the RAM memory. iv. Add an additional network card to make the multi-channel feature functional and enable the multi-channel settings in the MoSMB configuration. v. To scale up the MoSMB on one node, add more NICs, CPU or memory as desired. If the backend storage is a bottleneck, add high performance storage like NVMe disks. vi. To scale out, add another MoSMB node to the cluster and expose the same share.

Note : MoSMB provides a performance tuning script as per the server configuration so as MoSMB will use only upto  60% of the available memory on the server machine. This script is located at /etc/mosmb/scripts/perf_tuning.sh Just run the script to get tuning parameters like preProcessorThreads, threadPoolSize and ringBufferCount set as per the server configuration.
RDMA (SMBDirect) Performance
Note : Refer to the Troubleshooting Guide for the RDMA (SMBDirect) Communication on a MoSMB Server to understand how to make the RDMA (SMBDirect) basic setup work.

The SMBDirect protocol uses the RDMA Read/Write requests to transfer file data within the client and the server. Every RDMA Read/Write operation consists of a SMB2 Read request with 1 or more buffer descriptors . Each buffer descriptor contains information for an I/O buffer with the desired transfer size. Each buffer descriptor thus requires to be allocated and pinned in memory till the transfer is completed through the RDMA channel. So for every buffer descriptor a buffer of desired size is allocated and pinned, this is a control operation and consumes a significant amount of time. Therefore we have introduced the following parameters to ensure that we reduce control operations during data operations:

SMB Direct ring size: i. Default value: 512 ii. Per connection based parameter. iii. Accepts value in the multiples of 64. iv. The server should be restarted to reflect the effect when a  parameter is changed. The SMB Direct ring size is a parameter that allocates and reserves the specified number of buffers, each of the SMBDirect buffer size size. On every RDMA Read/Write request the buffer is consumed from this ring and replenished after use. This allows faster operations as we can avoid memory pinning operations (control) in the data path resulting in faster speeds. This can to be tuned w.r.t the I/O pattern for typical application. Setting it to very higher number can guarantee faster transfers at the cost of higher memory footprint. Whereas setting it to very lower number can lead to slower speeds due to the lack of available buffers for transfers. We recommend it to be set 1024 for media applications, and 256 for non-media operations (basic file server, disk io apps)

SMB Direct buffer size: i. Default value: 1 ii. Per connection-based parameter. iii. Accepts value from 1 to 8 inclusive. This value is in Mega Bytes (for example 1 represents 1MB). iv. The server should be restarted to reflect the effect of a changed parameter. This parameter represents the size of transfer for every buffer descriptor from SMB Direct ring . As per the SMBD Specification this parameter should be less than or equal to the maximum fragmented receive size (1MB). However, this parameter could be less than or equal to the maximum transaction size (8MB) as well. The SMB client has 1MB value  that is the maximum fragmented receive size and hence it the recommended value.

TCP parameters i. For RDMA connections only, allocate minimal resources to TCP parameters since the TCP buffers will consume some amount of RAM ii. For example, preProcessorThreads = 4, ringBufferCount = 256 (or 128 or 64)
Mac Client Performance
With the help of following settings on the MAC client one can improve performance. Mac needs reboot after applying any of the changes.
Turn off packet signing by setting following in the /etc/nsmb.conf file. signing_required=no https://support.apple.com/en-gb/HT205926
Speed up browsing on network shares defaults write com.apple.desktopservices DSDontWriteNetworkStores -bool TRUE https://support.apple.com/en-in/HT208209
for more troubleshooting with MAC, refer to the troubleshooting section for MAC

Ensure that the client machine is added to the same domain as the MoSMB server.

MoSMB Configured in the NTLM Local Authentication
Prerequisites
Ensure that the Linux local users are added as MoSMB users by executing the /usr/bin/mosmb_passwd binary. Refer to How to the configure MoSMB server to work in the ntlmLocal mode .
The Windows machine may or may not be domain joined.
Ensure that the MoSMB server and client machines are pingable.
Access the MoSMB Server
Mount the share using the IP address of the MoSMB server.
Example:

Test Data : 10.10.0.10, motest To access : 1. Open explorer and enter IP - \\10.10.0.10 2. File explorer will prompt for username and password. Enter the MosMB username (motest) and password. 3. If MoSMB is configured to use all 3 authentications, use map network drive to connect to the share using the username password of the local user Output    : Share list should be populated

Note : If the sign in credentials of the Windows client are the same as those of the MoSMB users added using the /usr/bin/mosmb_passwd binary, the authentication will be done using the signed in user by default. The File Explorer will not prompt for credentials.

MoSMB is now available as a Google Cloud Platform (GCP) solution. We are currently providing a single VM instance of Ubuntu 24.04. The package contains a ready-to-use MoSMB server.
Requirements
Google Cloud Platform (GCP) account
Google Deployment Manager API enabled
GCP account limits large enough for the instances and volumes desired
An SSH key-pair for accessing MoSMB SE instances via SSH
Google Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) with at least one subnet configured in the target region
How to deploy MoSMB on GCP
To use MoSMB in the GCP environment perform the simple procedure mentioned below:
1. Go to MoSMB on GCP market place and simply click LAUNCH .
2. On launching a form is displayed wherein you must fill in data. Fill the form according to your requirements.
The form contains the following fields:

3. Once the deployment is complete, you can find that instance available in the VM instances section of the GCP. You will find the MoSMB service running with the "ntlmLocal" authentication on this created instance.
4. Now all that remains to be done is access the MoSMB share and begin using it for an awesome performance. To access the share from a Windows machine perform the following procedure:
Example:

Test Data : 10.128.0.24(This is the Internal IP of a GCP instance), testuser123, 35.222.138.223(This is the External IP of a GCP instance) To access : 1. Open the Explorer from the GCP based Windows instance and enter the server's Internal IP - \\10.128.0.24 2. The File Explorer will prompt you for a username and password. Enter the MosMB username (testuser123) and password. OR 1. Open the Explorer from a Windows machine and enter the server's External IP - \\35.222.138.223 2. The File Explorer will prompt you for a username and password. Enter the MosMB username (testuser123) and password. Output    : The share list is populated .
To have more control over a MoSMB server refer to the following points:

The MoSMB share has a read , write , execute [777 i.e. drwxrwxrwx] rights on an MoSMB share configured by default. To have more control over an MoSMB share's access per user go through the MoSMB share level configuration details .
Refer to the MoSMB configuration parameters for complete configuration details. Note : Some configuration parameters are restricted for the GCP release.

Configure the server in the "ntlmLocal" authentication mode by adding new users other than the ones mentioned during deployment. Configure the MoSMB share in the "POSIX" authorization mode for new users in the "ntlmLocal" authentication.
Configure the server in the "krb5" authentication mode . Configure the MoSMB share in the "POSIX" authorization mode for a "krb5" authentication.
Note : A MoSMB server restart is mandatory in case any changes are made to the configuration file.

Cache Cleanup

a. Truncates the file in local cache which is older than the “threshold” time mentioned in the s3.json. b. Scans the entire local cache in interval of “periodicity” time mentioned in the s3.json and marks the file
enableCacheCleanup

Specify if cache cleanup needs to be enabled or not. 
If set to "yes", cache cleanup will be executed.
If set to "no" , cache cleanup will not be executed.
threshold

If last accessed/modified time of the file is beyond the threshold value, then the files will be cleaned up from the cache. User can specify duration in minutes<m>/hours<H>/days<D>/months<M>. Default it set to 30 days. Configuring this value in days is recommended.
periodicity

Specify the duration of how frequently clean-up thread should run. User can specify duration in minutes<m>/hours<H>/days<D>/months<M>. Default it set to 1 day. Configuring this value in days is recommended.
enableFifoCleanup

Specify if FIFO cache cleanup needs to be enabled or not. FIFO cache cleanup will start cleanup of the LRU least recently used files in the cache.
The check will happen periodically at an interval provided in 'periodicity'
If set to "yes", cache cleanup with FIFO method will be executed.
If set to "no" , cache cleanup with FIFO method will not be executed.
lowerLimit
Specify The lower limit of the disk space in percentage. The cleanup of files activity will continue until the lower limit of 'cache storage' is reached.
upperLimit
Specify The upper limit of the disk space in percentage.  The cleanup of files activity will begin when the upper limit of 'cache storage' is used and when the threshold value is satisfied.

Specify single part size in case of multipart upload (In bytes). Part size should be minimum 5 MB (5242880 Bytes). Part size can be maximum 5 GB (5368709120 Bytes). Maximum number of parts per upload is 10,000. If kept blank, default is 125 MB.
httpScheme
Mention HTTP scheme either https/http. By default it is set to https.
verifySsl
Specify if “yes” if ssl certificate needs to be checked. By default it is set to “yes”.
asyncThreads

Specify the number of async threads to be used for object store operations.
By default it is set to 16.
throttleBandwidthStrategy
Specify “yes” if bandwidth throttling is needed. By default it is set to “no”.

Specify “Write bandwidth” to be allocated for s3. Mention in KBps, MBps or in GBps. 
By default all the available bandwidth is used for s3 operations.

Specify “Read bandwidth” to be allocated for s3. Mention in KBps, MBps or in GBps.

This page briefly describes the procedure of creating MS-Distributed File System Namespace (further referred as DFS-N). The DFS-N allows MoSMB shares on different nodes to be aggregated into a common share list that is visible to SMBClient. The different shares can be hosted from different nodes of MoSMB servers in cluster.

Microsoft Distributed File System(DFS) provides way to aggregate the share list that is hosted on separate MoSMB servers. There can more than one entry in the list for supporting a share thereby providing list of servers that can be connected to for that share. This is the way to failover access to share in case of failure of a node.
Important : DFS-N provides High Availability but not instant failover.

In DFS-N failover, clients attempt to access another target in a referral after one of the targets fails to respond or is no longer part of the namespace. DFS-N fail over is only performed when a client opens a file or folder. If a client has files or folders open and attempts to read or write to them when the target server is unavailable, the application will receive a failure on that operation. Any I/O that is happening on a MoSMB node with DFS-N setup will fail if the node goes down and when the application tries to reconnect to the share, the standby node will get connected. This connection serving time depends on the DFS server and may take a couple of minutes.

Domain list configurations are needed by MoSMB for completing sssd configuration settings.
Single Domain Standalone Server Mode (Releases before March 2022)
Create a file named domain_list.txt at the /etc/mosmb/ location and mention the domain to which the MoSMB server is joined.
Example:
Command : cat /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt Output  : mosmbad.dom
Single Domain Standalone Server Mode (Releases after March 2022)

In new releases, /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt will now use SPN entries instead of domain names. Entries should be in the format of spn@DOMAIN , similar to the SPNs and will be case sensitive. Ensure that you make each entry on a new line. Entries in /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt, must match against the one present in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
Example:
Command : cat /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt Output  : mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM

shareName

You - the user can name a share as per your requirement. The default share names are share1 and share2 . The maximum length of a share name can be 80 characters. The share name can contain spaces, underscores( _ ) and alphanumeric strings.
comment

This is a comment added for the share. User can add a message if required or it can be kept blank. This parameter is for the user's reference only. Message added here is displayed as a tool tip on hovering the share when accessed from client side.
sharePath
Share path is the path of the share that the user wishes to export as an MoSMB share. State the complete path of the share to be exported.
continuousAvailability
The isContinouslyAvailable field is set to yes by default. The user requires a continuous availability of the exported share at the client end.
isCluster

The user should set the isCluster to no and enableHighAvailability globalSection parameter to no when  server is in Standalone Mode The user should set the isCluster to yes and nodeId starts from 1 . For more configuration settings in the cluster mode refer to the Single Domain Cluster Mode / Multi Domain Cluster Mode . For the clustering to work the user must set the enableHighAvailability globalSection parameter to yes in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.conf or /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file.

shareEncrypt

Encryption does not support on-disk data encryption it is encryption over the wire. Encryption will encrypt the network layer packet exchanges. Set the shareEncrypt value in the share level settings to yes if desired. For Share encryption to work the user must set the Global level parameter shareLevelEncryption to yes in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.conf or "/etc/mosmb/mosmb.json" file. When the rejectUnencryptedAccess is set to yes in the Global level parameter, un-encrypted requests from the client side are not served. The user can set this parameter to yes or no as per the requirement.

Note : When globalEncryptData from the global level parameters is set to yes , all the packets are encrypted for the server client communication. This is irrespective of the share level encryption settings.

Access Control List Strategy (aclStrategy)

An Access Control List (ACL) helps the user with share access authorization. MoSMB provides three types of ACL strategies currently, none, posix, advancePosix and ntacl MoSMB -S3 edition does not support ACL's currently, so it is recommended for MoSMB-S3 edition to set ACL to none.
None
Setting ACL to none means there will be no authorization required for creating, modifying or deleting data from the share with the ACL strategy set to none .

POSIX
When "acl": "posix", is used MoSMB permits the underlying filesystem to take a decision for the shared file/directory's read and write permissions.
However, in case
"aclStrategy":{ "acl": "posix", "checkPosixPermissionBit": "yes" },
is used MoSMB takes the decision for the shared file/directory's read and write permissions.

mosmb.conf representation - ACL Strategy "posix"

In case of posix ACL, MoSMB maps the Active Directory user who has established a SMB session to an equivalent Linux user. The effective UserID (UID) of this user is used by the file system to check access. To facilitate this, MoSMB needs to populate nsswitch.conf to use a proper ID mapper. Example: SSSD In case of the posix ACL and Kerberos authentication the user is required to set the permissions of the share path to a domain user and domain group by running the chown command.
sudo chown -R ''<domain-user-name> @ <domain-name> '':''<domain-group-name> @ <domain-name>'' <share path>
Example:
Test Data : mosmbad.dom, mouser (domain user), /opt/share Command   : sudo chown -R ''mouser@mosmbad.dom'':''domain users@mosmbad.dom'' /opt/share

In case of posix ACL, MoSMB maps the Active Directory user who has established a SMB session to an equivalent Linux user. The effective UserID (UID) of this user is used by the file system to check access. To facilitate this, MoSMB needs to populate the nsswitch.conf file to use a proper ID mapper. Example : SSSD In case of the posix ACL and Kerberos authentication the user is required to set the permissions of the share path to a domain user and domain group by running the chown command.
sudo chown -R ''<domain-name>\<domain-user-name>'' : ''<domain-name>\ <domain-group-name> '' <share path>
Example:
Test Data : mosmbad.dom, mouser (domain user), /opt/share Command   : sudo chown -R ''MOSMBAD\mouser'':''MOSMBAD\domain users'' /opt/share
In case of the posix ACL and ntlm_local authentication the user is required:
To set the permissions of the share path to a Linux local user.
To add the user group as an MoSMB user using the mosmb_passwd binary.
Refer to the ntlmLocal authentication mode settings for details.
sudo chown -R <linux-user-name> : <linux-group-name> <share path>
Example:
Test Data : motest, mogroup, /opt/share Command   : sudo chown -R motest:mogroup /opt/share
advancePosix
In the case of advancePosix , authorization is done based on the advance permissions set from the server or client side. To set advance acl permissions use setfacl command.
Example:

Test Data  : motest, mogroup, /opt/share Command1   : sudo setfacl -d -m -u:"motest@mosmbad.dom":rwx /opt/share (default permissions or inherited permissions for user) Command2   : sudo setfacl -m -u:"motest@mosmbad.dom":rwx /opt/share (user permissions set on the share path) Command3   : sudo setfacl -d -m -g:"mogroup@mosmbad.dom":r-x /opt/share (default permissions or inherited permissions for group) Command4   : sudo setfacl -m -u:"mogroup@mosmbad.dom":r-x /opt/share (group permissions set on the share path)
ntacl

In the case of ntacl , authorization is done according to the Active Directory Users (similar to ntfs). ntacl rules are used to check access. In this case, MoSMB uses the extended attributes provided by the file system for storing the ACLs. ntacl is not supported on "authMode": "ntlmLocal" Warning: The MoSMB Server will not start successfully when the "authMode": "ntlmLocal" and "acl": "ntacl", . Note : The ntacl authorization mode is not supported on the ntlmLocal authentication mode.

User Based Enumeration Strategy

User Based Enumeration (UBE) provides the configuration capability to control share visibility. It makes the share visible only to those users and groups that are listed in the configuration file.
To enable and apply setting related to UBE per share, make necessary changes in ubeStrategy section of sharesSection Supported in mosmb.json version 1.2.0 onwards.
kerberos authentication
The users that have one of the mentioned groups as their primary group or  secondary group can see the share. Nomenclature :

ubeUsers  : <domain_username>@<FULL_DOMAINNAME> 
   ubeGroups : <domain_groupname>  or <group_name>@<FULL_DOMAINNAME>
Example

“ubeUsers”  : [“mo_user1@MOSMB_AD.DOM”],
   “ubeGroups” : [“domain users@MOSMB_AD.DOM”]

ntlmAd / NTLMSSP authentication
The users that have one of the mentioned groups as their primary group or secondary group can see the share. Nomenclature :

ubeUsers  : <domain_username>@<DOMAINNAME> 
   ubeGroups : <domain_groupname>  or <group_name>@<DOMAINNAME>
Example

“ubeUsers”  : [“mo_user1@MOSMB_AD”],
   “ubeGroups” : [“domain users@MOSMB_AD”]
ntlmLocal authentication
Only the users that have one of the mentioned groups as their primary group can see the share. Nomenclature for domain joined clients :

ubeUsers  : <mosmb_username> or <mosmb_username>@<DOMAINNAME> 
   ubeGroups : <group_name> or <group_name>@<DOMAINNAME>
Example

“ubeUsers”  : [“user1”,”user2@MOSMB_AD”],
   “ubeGroups” : [“group1”,”group2@MOSMB_AD”]
Nomenclature for non-domain clients:

ubeUsers  : <mosmb_username> or <mosmb_username>@<client_hostname>
   ubeGroups : <group_name> or <group_name>@<client_hostname>
Example

“ubeUsers”  : [“user1”,”user2@my_client1”],
   “ubeGroups” : [“group1”,”group2@my_client1]
File and Directory Level Permissions (fileCreationMask & directoryCreationMask)
Specific permissions can be applied to newly created files or directories from the client end. This is an "octal value"  that represents permissions.
The default value for file permissions is set to 0644 [-rw-r--r--].
The default value for the directories permissions is set to 0755 [drwxr-xr-x].

metadataStore
Specify the storage strategy for the metadata storage. The values can be xattr or none .
When the metadataStore is set to "none" it means that the MoSMB server is not going to store any metadata associated with the file in the extended attribute space.
When the metadataStore is set to "xattr" it means that the MoSMB server will store the metadata associated with files or directories in the extended attribute space.
In this case, file metadata includes the security descriptor and smb file-attributes.
Note
When the authorization mode is "ntacl" the metadata storage must be "xattr".
For MoSMB -S3 edition the metadata storage must be "xattr"

Note :
The following features are supported on Windows Client only.
RECALL_ON_DATA_ACCESS Attribute

a. When we upload any file or directory directly through the Object Store Dashboard a local representation of the object, that is thumbnail, is created on the local share on server init. b. On accessing share from the Windows client, the uploaded file is visible as offline. The 'M'(RECALL_ON_DATA_ACCESS) attribute is set for the offline file. As the metadataStore is xattr, MoSMB fetches the modified time and file size of the file and directory. Only metadata is represented on the local cache(server side) which also replicates on the Windows client side on accessing share. c. Performing any operation on this file downloads it on the local share. This file is visible as offline until it is completely downloaded on the share. Note : If there are any files/directories present on the local cache and not on the object store, then users need to perform read/write operations on file or directories to upload them to the object store.

Pin Unpin Download
Installation

Installation package also includes pin-unpin-download.zip bundle from release 088 onwards. Once XPKG script is ran bundle will be extracted at location /opt/mosmb/package/client_side_scripts/ This will add respective changes to the registry editor on windows clients.
To enable pin unpin download option and to add them in the context menu of required windows clients follow below steps:
Transfer pin-unpin-download.zip file on all required windows client. (installation to be carried out on all required windows clients explicitly)
Extract the pin-unpin-download.zip file
Right click on install.inf and click install.
Users will be able to see these options (pin, unpin, download) in the context menu on right clicking the targeted file.

Pin

The files which user wants or uses frequently and don’t want that files to get truncated, users can pin such files If file is pinned mosmb will make sure that such file will not get truncated during scheduled cache cleanup. To pin the file write permissions are required.
Pinning Limit
Only files which are downloaded can be pinned.
While pinning the files if Total Pinned File Size (sum of file sizes of all pinned files) exceeds the 40% of disk space, user will get popup of warning.
While pinning the files if Total Pinned File Size (sum of file sizes of all pinned files) reaches to 50% of disk space, user will get error message and will not be able to pin anymore files.

When file is unpinned it is considered as a normal file and mosmb will allow such files to truncate during cache cleanup. To unpin the file write permissions are required.

Download

All files uploaded directly from s3 storage are considered offline. mosmb provides exclusive download option which allows users to download files. Files downloaded by users are truncated by cache cleanup after a particular duration of time ( threshold parameter in s3.json) or after exceeding cache cleanup trigger limit ( periodicity parameter in s3.json) To download any file read permissions are required.

Limitations
At any given time only a single file can be pinned, unpinned or downloaded.
This functionality is only supported by Windows 10 and above versions. As FILE_ATTRIBUTE_PINNED was introduced in WIN 10.
Partial Download

This feature enables on-demand data downloading. When a user opens a file (e.g., by double-clicking), only the requested portion of the data is retrieved and delivered. This allows seamless video streaming without requiring the entire file to be downloaded in advance. The remaining data continues to download in the background during playback.
Queue Manager
a. Queue manager queues all the tasks. b. Download operation gets the higher priority than the upload operation.
Cache Clean-up

a. Truncates the file in local cache which are older than the “threshold” time mentioned in the s3.json. b. Scans the entire local cache in interval of “periodicity” time mentioned in the s3.json and marks the
file offline which are truncated from the local cache. c. If "enableFifoCleanup" is enabled, it truncates the files in local cache which are older than "threshold" time mentioned in s3.json; when "upperLimit" of cache space is utilized, untill it reaches to "lowerLimit".
Bandwidth Throttling

a. Throttles the bandwidth for the s3 operation provided to Read/Write bandwidth parameters in
s3.json.
Upload on MoSMB restart
a. On mosmb restarts, upload will be resumed.
Bucket notification

Bucket notification is notifying any changes on the bucket to the mosmb server. From mosmb version 099, Bucket Notification is implemented only for the minio object storage . We are using NATS server as a notifying server from object storage to mosmb

Limitation : From build 099 onwards this feature will be in incremental implementation state. And as of 099 the support is added for single NATS server for all  buckets. And notifications implemented for only s3:ObjectCreated:Put and s3:ObjectCreated:Post
NOTE: Following steps are given to just guide you to setup the NATS server and integrate with minio so that it can be used in MoSMB

NATS server installation steps : 1) curl -L https://github.com/nats-io/nats-server/releases/download/v2.0.0/nats-server-v2.0.0-linux-amd64.zip -o nats-server.zip
2) unzip nats-server.zip
3) cd nats-server-v2.0.0-linux-amd64/
4) ./nats-server -DV -l /var/log/nats-server.log &

Steps we need to carry out on minio server for bucket notification to be enabled : 1) download minio client(mc) if not already downloaded
 # wget https://dl.min.io/client/mc/release/linux-amd64/mc # chmod +x mc
 # ./mc --help
2) use mc config set for setting correct subject for subscription in minio configuration along with NATS server ip
   Command: ./mc admin config set myminioserver notify_nats:1 subject="bucketevents" address="10.10.11.171:4222" enable=on 3) Add events using minio client (using command mc )
   • Add bucket: Command: ./mc mb testbucket
       Output: Bucket created successfully `testbucket`.
   • Add event: Command: ./mc --insecure event add myminioserver/testbucket arn:minioserver:sqs::1:nats Output:   Successfully added arn:minioserver:sqs::1:nats
   • List events Command: ./mc --insecure event list myminioserver/testbucket arn:myminioserver:sqs::1:nats Output: arn:myminioserver:sqs::1:nats   s3:ObjectCreated:*,s3:ObjectRemoved:*,s3:ObjectAccessed:*   Filter: NOTE: myminioserver is the minio server name and testbucket is the bucket name
   • Get the current setting on MinIO server Command: ./mc admin config get myminioserver/ notify_nats • Set required parameters on MinIO server This is another way to set config for minio (optional way to do step 1, using mc)
       Command: ./mc admin config set myminioserver/ notify_nats:1 address='10.1.0.111:4222' subject=bucketevents enable=on
   • Restart minio Command: ./mc admin service restart myminioserver/ NOTE: whenever any changes are carried out in  minio configuration file you will need to restart minio server
Before starting mosmb, you can update your NATS server ip in the s3.json , in bucketNotificationSection

In mosmb.log if you have received a log as:
[MO_ERROR]:[SMBCORE]:[TID 5134]:[EC02010013]: Configuration Server init failed OR Configuration file empty

then check the Error code string here they are EC02010013 and EC02000103 simply search the page for 02010013 and 02000103 to get the possible solution or action to be taken at users end.
Once the action is taken restart mosmb service and check if issue is resolved.
In some cases action can not be taken directly by the end user, in such cases if the issue is blocking the work please contact us at - support@ryussi.com

Two authentication modes of the MoSMB server provide the POSIX ACL view:
Kerberos Authentication
ntlmLocal Authentication
POSIX ACL is Portable Operating System Interface - Access Control Lists.
Note : POSIX ACL for MoSMB means only the Server user's ID mapping to the MoSMB share and its included files and directories.
Contents
1 MoSMB Configuration 1.1 Kerberos 1.1.1 CentOS Chown Command 1.1.2 Ubuntu Chown Command 1.2 NTLM
1.1 Kerberos 1.1.1 CentOS Chown Command 1.1.2 Ubuntu Chown Command
1.1.1 CentOS Chown Command
1.1.2 Ubuntu Chown Command
1.2 NTLM
MoSMB Configuration
For the POSIX ACL view  MoSMB share configurations are required as well. Refer to Share Configuration .
Kerberos
In case of krb5 Authentication you must:
Execute a chown command.
In this chown comand you must set permissions for the share path to a domain user and domain group .
chown -R “<domain-user>@<domain-name>”: “<group-name>@<domain-name>” <share path>
Example:
chown  -R "mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM":"domain users@MOSMBAD.DOM" /opt/share/
chown -R “<domain-name>\<domain-user>”: “<domain-name>\<group-name>” <share path>
Example:
chown  -R "MOSMBAD\mouser":"MOSMBAD\domain users" /opt/share/

The ACL view will not display proper names in the Security tabs if: 1. The ldap client configuration is not properly carried out. 2. The File or Directory owner/group is not a domain user. In such cases the names will be displayed as:
"LOCAL ACCOUNT" or "S-1-5-113" which is mapped to the owner permissions.
"SYSTEM" which is mapped to group permissions.
"Everyone" which is mapped to other permissions.
If properly configured, the domain user name will be displayed as shown underneath:
NTLM
In case of posix ACL and ntlm_local authentication you are required to:
Set the permissions of the share path to the Linux local user.
Add the User group as a MoSMB user using the mosmb_passwd binary.
Refer to ntlm_local Authentication for details. The ownership of the share path should be set to the Linux user and the user group must be set as the MoSMB user.
sudo chown -R <linux-user-name> : <linux-group-name> <share path>
Example :
Test Data : motest, motest, /opt/share Command   : sudo chown -R motest:motest /opt/share
The user name and group will be displayed on the client side as follows:
In order for MoSMB share access to work with krb5 authentication Kerberos ticket should be generated and the ticket should be valid.
Generating a Kerberos Ticket (Releases before March 2022)

To generate a ticket on the server without having to use the password, use command kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab <SPN>@<DOMAIN NAME> . -k Specifies to obtain the key for the ticket principal from a key table. So you are not prompted to enter the password for the ticket principal. -t will refer to the keytab file specified. For MoSMB it should be /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
Example:
Test Data : mosmbad.dom, cifs/my-mosmb-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM (keytab entry of cifs) Command   : sudo kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab cifs/my-mosmb-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM
Generating Kerberos Ticket (Releases after March 2022)

In new releases, user will NOT be needed to acquire a ticket manually. To generate a ticket on the server, MoSMB will use principal name from /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt , match against the one present in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab and use it to generate the kerberos ticket on MoSMB service start.
Verifying a Kerberos Ticket
To verify if the ticket is acquired for the domain_user, run the klist command.
Example:

Command : sudo klist -A Output  : Ticket cache: KCM:0:96571 Default principal: cifs/my-mosmb-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM Valid starting       Expires              Service principal 05/16/2022 11:33:50  05/16/2022 21:33:50  krbtgt/MOSMBAD.DOM@MOSMBAD.DOM renew until 05/17/2022 11:33:45
Notes :

1. By default, the Kerberos ticket is valid for 10 hours . 
   2. The Kerberos ticket defaults may be modified by the Group Policy Management → Kerberos Policy → Security Settings in the AD.
   3. MoSMB releases after March 22 - Tickets will be auto renewed on every MoSMB start/restart.
   4. On releases after March 22 - ldap ticket will also be taken at mosmb start/restart if ldap configurations are correct.

Prerequisites

MoSMB has certain dependencies that you need to install on the server machine/s. Dependencies will be promoted to the user while installing the MoSMB package on the server machine/s on which MoSMB is not installed previously. Note : Install only the pre-dependencies that are prompted. The OS dependent package versions change according to the server OS distribution.
libicu
libaio
jemalloc or libjemalloc

Note: for centOS jemalloc is available in EPEL repository, EPEL is "Extra Packages for Enterprise Linux", so
yum install epel-release  -y
yum install jemalloc -y
openldap-clients or ldap-utils
cyrus-sasl-gssapi or libsasl2-modules-gssapi-mit
json-c or libjson-c-dev
heimdal-libs or heimdal packages for support libraries of Heimdal Kerberos
Note : Depending on the MoSMB Edition provided following packages will also be needed. The OS dependent package versions change according to the server OS distribution.
zookeeper
curl
libibverbs
librdmacm
librdmacm-utils
Note : For kerberos and ntlmad (ntlmssp) authentication on MoSMB following packages will also be needed. The OS dependent package versions may change according to the server OS distribution
krb5-workstation or krb5-user or krb5-client
realm or adcli or PowerBrokerIdentityServices (PBIS) any domain join utility.
adcli package is required for ntlmad (ntlmssp) authentication specifically.
Note : Also cron and logrotate related packages are needed according to the server OS distribution.
Installing MoSMB

User will be provided XPKG-MoSMB script<XPKG-MoSMB-....sh> according to the OS distribution. In this script, the MoSMB package, Ansible tar and “MoSMB License Agreement” is embedded. One can run this script to extract MoSMB package and Ansible tar after accepting “MoSMB License Agreement”. For the successful execution of this script, install the ncurses library package When user runs the script, MoSMB License Agreement page pops up. User can either Decline or Accept and Extract the EULA, and once accepted, the EULA will be saved at /opt/mosmb directory along with the package.

Provided XPKG-MoSMB script : XPKG-MoSMB-CL-3.1.1-20220303_0.centos-8.x86_64.sh Command : ./XPKG-MoSMB-CL-3.1.1-20220303_0.centos-8.x86_64.sh Output : MoSMB EULA is present at : /opt/mosmb/MoSMB_EULA.pdf MoSMB ansible is present at : /opt/mosmb/ MoSMB package is present at : /opt/mosmb/package/mosmb_install MoSMB zookeeper rpm is present at : /opt/mosmb/package/mosmb_install
Depending on OS distribution and MoSMB version, user will get MoSMB package at location “/opt/mosmb/package/mosmb_install” on accepting MoSMB License Agreement.
CentOS distribution users will receive the .rpm package of MoSMB. And for Cluster builds,  a zookeeper rpm will also be provided along with MoSMB rpm, which can be used for zookeeper installation.
Ubuntu and Debian distribution users will receive the .deb package of MoSMB.
Naming convention used for MoSMB packages - Standalone Builds : MoSMB-SA Cluster Builds       : MoSMB-CL S3 Builds              : MoSMB-SA-S3
Standalone Builds : MoSMB-SA
Cluster Builds       : MoSMB-CL
S3 Builds              : MoSMB-SA-S3
Note :
The /etc/mosmb and /opt/mosmb directories will be created on successful installation (if not present already).
Configuration files for MoSMB mosmb.json and mosmb_logging.conf will be copied to the /etc/mosmb/ location.
s3.json will be copied at the /etc/mosmb/ location depending on the MoSMB version.
Plugin files will be located at /usr/local/lib/mosmb/ depending on the build type libios_vfs.so  libmosmb_pst_plugin.so and libios_s3.so will be part of installation.
For hourly basis log rotation mologrotate will be kept under /etc/cron.hourly/
/etc/mosmb/scripts has multiple scripts including Performance tuning, zookeeper cleanup etc
On CentOS / RHEL / ROCKY / SLES / SUSE
Install the .rpm package extracted at location /opt/mosmb/package/mosmb_install with the following command:
rpm -ivh /opt/mosmb/package/mosmb_install/<name of MoSMB rpm>
Example:

Provided rpm package name : MoSMB-SA-3.1.1-20190620_0.centos-7.x86_64.rpm Command : cd  /opt/mosmb/package/mosmb_install Command : rpm -ivh  MoSMB-SA-3.1.1-20190620_0.centos-7.x86_64.rpm Output  : Preparing...                          ################################# [100%] Updating / installing... 1:MoSMB-SA-3.1.1-20190620_0 ################################# [100%] Running performance tuning script..Done. MoSMB is now installed. Please configure licensing and shares before first run. Run MoSMB as service with "service mosmb start"
On Ubuntu / Debian
Install the .deb package extracted at location /opt/mosmb/package/mosmb_install with the following command:
dpkg --install /opt/mosmb/package/mosmb_install/<name of MoSMB deb>
Example:

Provided deb package name : MoSMB-CL-3.1.1-20210625_0.ubuntu-18.04.x86_64.deb Command : cd  /opt/mosmb/package/mosmb_install Command : dpkg --install MoSMB-CL-3.1.1-20210625_0.ubuntu-18.04.x86_64.deb Output  : .... Selecting previously unselected package mosmb-cl. (Reading database ... 119334 files and directories currently installed.) Preparing to unpack .../MoSMB-CL-3.1.1-20210625_0.ubuntu-18.04.x86_64.deb ... Unpacking mosmb-cl (3.1.1.000) ... Setting up mosmb-cl (3.1.1.000) ... Running performance tuning script..Done. MoSMB is now installed. Please configure licensing and shares before first run. Run MoSMB as service with "service mosmb start"
Uninstalling MoSMB

Un-installing MoSMB will remove all the files created by MoSMB during installation from the server machine except the MoSMB log files namely /var/log/mosmb.log and /var/log/mosmb_audit.log and other user created MoSMB files and directories. Also mosmb.keytab, mosmb_u.dat files are left behind in /etc/mosmb/. So next time u reinstall MoSMB package the mosmb users related data and any keytab if generated is available for the mosmb service to use.
On CentOS / RHEL / ROCKY / SLES / SUSE
To un-install the MoSMB package from the server machine run the following command:
rpm -qa | grep MoSMB (This provides the installed MoSMB package name) rpm -e <MoSMB package name>
Example:

Command : rpm -qa | grep MoSMB Output  : MoSMB-SA-3.1.1-20190620_0.centos-7.x86_64 Command : rpm -e MoSMB-SA-3.1.1-20190620_0.centos-7.x86_64 OR rpm -e MoSMB-SA Output  : MoSMB service is not running (The output may differ according to the MoSMB status.)
On Ubuntu / Debian
To un-install the MoSMB package from the server machine run the following command:
dpkg-query --list | grep mosmb (This provides the installed MoSMB package name) dpkg --purge <MoSMB package name>
Example:

Command : dpkg-query --list | grep mosmb Output  : ii  mosmb-cl    3.1.1.000    amd64 MoSMB is SMB with MoJo. It is implementation of MS-SMB V2 and V3 protocol. GIT VERSION: 354139801 Command : dpkg --purge mosmb-cl Output  : (Reading database ... 119398 files and directories currently installed.) Removing mosmb-cl (3.1.1.000) ... .... (The output may differ according to the MoSMB status.) Purging configuration files for mosmb-cl (3.1.1.000) ... dpkg: warning: while removing mosmb-cl, directory '/usr/local/lib' not empty so not removed dpkg: warning: while removing mosmb-cl, directory '/opt/mosmb' not empty so not removed dpkg: warning: while removing mosmb-cl, directory '/etc/mosmb' not empty so not removed Processing triggers for systemd (237-3ubuntu10.38) ... Processing triggers for ureadahead (0.100.0-21) ...
Upgrading MoSMB
You can upgrade MoSMB directly instead of uninstalling MoSMB and then installing the new MoSMB package. This activity has the following dependencies:
1. The MoSMB version of the new package should be higher than the installed version. (A MoSMB-3.1.1 package cannot upgrade to MoSMB-3.1.0)
2. The mosmb.json config version in the new package should be higher or same (we support upgrade from config version 1.4.0 to config versions above those. e.g. to 1.5.0 or 1.6.0 etc)
3. The MoSMB version could be same but the package names must be different. (A MoSMB-3.1.1_0 user can upgrade to MoSMB-3.1.1_1)
4. Upgrade should be from same type of MoSMB package (i.e. One can upgrade a MoSMB-SA version to a MoSMB-SA version only and not to a MoSMB-CL version)

Note :  An MoSMB upgrade will keep the previous MoSMB configuration file with name mosmb.json.orig at the /etc/mosmb/ location and install the latest mosmb.json version while preserving your old config values. The etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf file from previous version will be preserved.
On CentOS / RHEL / ROCKY / SLES / SUSE
To upgrade MoSMB to a new version run the following command:
rpm -Uvh <name of new MoSMB rpm>
Example:

Installed package name : MoSMB-SA-3.1.1-20190620_0.centos-7.x86_64 New rpm package name : MoSMB-SA-3.1.1-20190620_1.centos-7.x86_64.rpm Command : rpm -Uvh MoSMB-SA-3.1.1-20190620_1.centos-7.x86_64.rpm Output  : Preparing... Updating / installing... 1:MoSMB-SA-3.1.1-20190620_1.centos-7################################# [ 50%] ... Cleaning up / removing... 2:MoSMB-SA-3.1.1-20190620_0.centos-7################################# [100%]
On Ubuntu / Debian
To upgrade MoSMB to a new version run the following command:
dpkg --install <name of new MoSMB deb>
Example:

Installed package name : MoSMB-CL-3.1.1-20210625_0.ubuntu-18.04.x86_64.deb New deb package name : MoSMB-CL-3.1.1-20210625_1.ubuntu-18.04.x86_64.deb Command : dpkg --install MoSMB-CL-3.1.1-20210625_1.ubuntu-18.04.x86_64.deb Output  : Preparing to unpack MoSMB-CL-3.1.1-20210625_1.ubuntu-18.04.x86_64.deb ... .... Unpacking mosmb (3.1.0.000) over (3.1.0.000) ... Setting up mosmb (3.1.0.000) ...
In order for MoSMB share access to work with krb5 authentication Kerberos ticket should be generated and the ticket should be valid.
Contents
1 Generating Kerberos Ticket 1.1 Generating Kerberos Ticket (Releases after March 2022) 1.2 Generating a Kerberos Ticket (Releases before March 2022)
1.1 Generating Kerberos Ticket (Releases after March 2022)
1.2 Generating a Kerberos Ticket (Releases before March 2022)
2 Verifying a Kerberos Ticket
Generating Kerberos Ticket
Generating Kerberos Ticket (Releases after March 2022)

In new releases, user will NOT be needed to acquire a ticket manually. To generate a ticket on the server, MoSMB will use principal name from /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt , match against the one present in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab and use it to generate the kerberos ticket on MoSMB service start.
Generating a Kerberos Ticket (Releases before March 2022)
To generate a ticket on the server run command kinit -V <AD_Domain_username>@<DOMAIN NAME> . The command asks password for the specified user.
Example:
Test Data : mosmbad.dom, mouser (domain user) Command   : sudo kinit -V mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM Output    : Password for mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM:

OR To generate a ticket on the server without having to use the password, use command kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab <AD_Domain_username>@<DOMAIN NAME> . -k Specifies to obtain the key for the ticket principal from a key table. So you are not prompted to enter the password for the ticket principal. -t will refer to the keytab file specified. For MoSMB it should be /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
Example:
Test Data : mosmbad.dom, mouser (domain user entry present mosmb.keytab) Command   : sudo kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM
Notes :

1. The domain name in above commands must be in UPPERCASE to avoid KDC mismatch issues while getting credentials.
   2. Check the ticket time with klist -a, (time of MoSMB server and AD should be always synced or Kerberos authentication may fail)
Verifying a Kerberos Ticket
To verify if the ticket is acquired for the domain_user, run the klist command.
Example:

Command : sudo klist -A Output  : Ticket cache: FILE:/opt/mosmb/kerberos_tkt Default principal: mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM Valid starting       Expires              Service principal 01/16/2020 11:33:50  01/16/2020 21:33:50  krbtgt/MOSMBAD.DOM@MOSMBAD.DOM renew until 01/17/2020 11:33:45
Notes :

1. By default, the Kerberos ticket is valid for 10 hours . 
   2. The Kerberos ticket defaults may be modified by the Group Policy Management → Kerberos Policy → Security Settings in the AD.
   3. MoSMB releases after March 22 - Tickets will be auto renewed on every MoSMB start/restart.
   4. On releases after March 22 - ldap ticket will also be taken at MoSMB start/restart if ldap configurations are correct.

All MoSMB-SA builds are set to use the "Standalone Mode" of operation by default. To use MoSMB server in Standalone Mode with ntlmLocal authentication simply follow the configurations mentioned in steps below one by one.
1. ntlmLocal and related services configurations.
Ntlm (ntlmLocal) Authentication Mode Configurations
2. Carry out required authorization (ACL) settings
Using MoSMB with Posix ACL OR
Using MoSMB without any authorization
3. Setup the MoSMB configuration file (/etc/mosmb/mosmb.json) for standalone use.
In globalSection set enableHighAvailability to no
In section authSection , set authMode as ntlmLocal
In section nodeInfoSection , give any integer between 1 to 1023 to nodeId .
In section vinodeSection set mechanism as inode
In shareSection set parameters isCluster to no
Example:
4. If the server machines have openssl version 3 and above configuration changes are required.
Open /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf
Existing/Default Configuration :

[provider_sect]
   default = default_sect
   [default_sect]
   # activate = 1
Edits/Required Configuration :

[provider_sect]
   default = default_sect legacy = legacy_sect [openssl_init]
   providers = provider_sect
 
   [default_sect] activate = 1 [legacy_sect] activate = 1
5. Start MoSMB service on the node check service mosmb status and check the mosmb.log for details. You can check details of MoSMB server Maintenance here
Notes :

1. Node id can be any integer in the 1 to 1023 range.
   2. Parameters other than mechanism is skipped from vinodeSection in case of MoSMB-SA build. 
   3. To know other mosmb.json parameters in details refer this .
sssd configuration
sssd helps during the User ID mapping for domain users.
If in case sssd is not installed before, install sssd-common and configure sssd on all the MoSMB server machine in cluster.
Create a sssd configuration file for MoSMB /etc/sssd/conf.d/mosmb_sssd.conf and it should contain parameters as shown below.

[sssd] domains = mosmbad.dom config_file_version = 2
   services = nss, pam [domain/mosmbad.dom] ad_domain = mosmbad.dom
   krb5_realm = MOSMBAD.DOM krb5_keytab = /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab realmd_tags = manages-system joined-with-adcli
   cache_credentials = True
   id_provider = ad
   krb5_store_password_if_offline = True
   default_shell = /bin/bash ldap_id_mapping = True use_fully_qualified_names = True
   fallback_homedir = /home/%u@%d
   access_provider = ad
sssd configuration files must be at /etc/sssd/ with permissions of file set to 600 (-rw-------) and for both /etc/sssd/sssd.conf and /etc/sssd/conf.d/mosmb_sssd.conf
Start sssd service with service sssd start .
nsswitch.conf Configuration
Verify that nsswitch.conf file is configured to use SSSD as a provider
Open /etc/nsswitch.conf file and verify that it points to sss for the name service switch

passwd:     files sss
   shadow:     files sss
   group:      files sss netgroup:   files sss
Note :
Restart the sssd service after making any changes in the sssd configurations.
This document describes S3 acl support and it's limitations.
Contents
1 Prerequisite
2 Working
3 Limitation
4 Permissions Mapping
Prerequisite
In the mosmb.json file set following for S3 share :

* "acl" to "advancePosix"
   * "fileCreation Mask" to 0777 
   * "directoryCreationMask" to 0777
Working

For each object in the bucket on the object store, a local representation of the object thumbnail is created in cache on the local share by the MoSMB S3 client. 
For these  thumbnails, owner and group permissions are set to "root" by the MoSMB S3 client.
Here particular user or group access is supported through inherited permissions of the parent directory.
Example: Let’s consider following example,

s3-share is share name with share path /mnt/data/s3-share The share path permissions set are as follows (permissions are set using setfacl command):
   
   $ getfacl /mnt/data/s3-share
       getfacl: Removing leading '/' from absolute path names
       # file: mnt/data/s3-share
       # owner: foo@abc.dom
       # group: bar@abc.dom
       user::rwx
       group::rwx
       mask::rwx
       other::r-x
       default:user::rwx
       default:user:acluser@abc.dom:rwx
       default:group::rwx
       default:group:aclgroup@abc.dom:r-x
       default:mask::rwx
       default:other::r-x

Here, the first four lines describe access permissions given to user, group and other which is rwx, rwx and r-x respectively. The mask:rwx acts as permissions filter (or it indicates maximum) for other user or group present in permissions list. If permissions for mask are set as mask:rwx this means there is no filtering of permissions for any other user or group present in permissions If permissions for mask are set as mask:r-x means that any user or group even if it has say rwx permissions it will effectively get only r-x permissions. The next lines in the permissions list start with the default keyword are the permissions which will transfer to the child file or directory created under this directory (inherited permissions).

If thumbnail name say “pictures” and present under s3-share directory i.e. /mnt/data/s3-share/pictures then it will have permissions as: 
   
   $ getfacl /mnt/data/s3-share/pictures/
       getfacl: Removing leading '/' from absolute path names
       # file: mnt/data/s3-share/Pictures/
       # owner: root
       # group: root
       user::rwx
       user:acluser@abc.dom:rwx
       group::rwx
       group:aclgroup@abc.dom:r-x
       mask::rwx
       other::r-x
       default:user::rwx
       default:user:acluser@abc.dom:rwx
       default:group::rwx
       default:group:aclgroup@abc.dom:r-x
       default:mask::rwx
       default:other::r-x
Here as mentioned earlier the client created thumbnail directory pictures has user and group set to root user. It will have acluser and aclgroup with access permissions rwx and r-x respectively.
Limitation

As mentioned in earlier section (working) the object thumbnails owner and group permissions are set to “root”. So only root user can add/modify/delete the permissions setting for that file or directory. Considering earlier example if one wants to have rwx permissions for aclgroup then those changes can be done only by root user.
Permissions Mapping
To know more about how windows side permissions are mapped on linux server/clients please go through " Advance_Posix_ACL#Permission_mapping_Windows_to_Linux "
In order for MoSMB share access to work with krb5 authentication Kerberos ticket should be generated and the ticket should be valid.
Generating a Kerberos Ticket (Releases before March 2022)

To generate a ticket on the server run command kinit -V <AD_Domain_username>@<DOMAIN NAME> . The command asks password for the specified user. Make sure ticket is taken for all the domains to which MoSMB server is getting configured for.
Example:

Test Data : mosmbad.dom, mouser1 (domain user of mosmbad.dom), mosmbad2.dom, mouser2 (domain user of mosmbad2.dom) Command   : sudo kinit -V mouser1@MOSMBAD.DOM Output    : Password for mouser1@MOSMBAD.DOM: Command   : sudo kinit -V mouser2@MOSMBAD2.DOM Output    : Password for mouser2@MOSMBAD2.DOM:

OR To generate a ticket on the server without having to use the password, use command kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab <AD_Domain_username>@<DOMAIN NAME> . -k Specifies to obtain the key for the ticket principal from a key table. So you are not prompted to enter the password for the ticket principal. -t will refer to the keytab file specified. For MoSMB it should be /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
Example:

Test Data : mosmbad.dom, mouser1 (domain user of mosmbad.dom), mosmbad2.dom, mouser2 (domain user of mosmbad2.dom) [all domain users entries present mosmb.keytab] Command   : sudo kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab mouser1@MOSMBAD.DOM Command   : sudo kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab mouser2@MOSMBAD2.DOM
Notes :

1. The domain name in above commands must be in UPPERCASE to avoid KDC mismatch issues while getting credentials.
   2. Check the ticket time with klist -A, (time of MoSMB server and AD should be always synced or Kerberos authentication may fail)
Generating Kerberos Ticket (Releases after March 2022)

In new releases, user will NOT be needed to acquire a ticket manually. To generate a ticket on the server, MoSMB will use principal name from /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt , match against the one present in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab and use it to generate the kerberos ticket on MoSMB service start.
Verifying a Kerberos Ticket
To verify if the ticket is acquired for the domain_user, run the klist command.
Example:

Command : sudo klist -A Output  : Ticket cache: DIR:/opt/mosmb/kerberos_tkt/tktAD124 Default principal: mouser1@MOSMBAD.DOM Valid starting       Expires              Service principal 01/16/2020 11:33:50  01/16/2020 21:33:50  krbtgt/MOSMBAD.DOM@MOSMBAD.DOM renew until 01/17/2020 11:33:45 01/16/2020 11:33:50  01/16/2020 21:33:50   ldap/<AD1 name>.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM renew until 01/17/2020 11:33:45 Ticket cache: DIR:/opt/mosmb/kerberos_tkt/tkt12qun Default principal: mouser2@MOSMBAD2.DOM Valid starting       Expires              Service principal 01/16/2020 11:33:50  01/16/2020 21:33:50  krbtgt/MOSMBAD2.DOM@MOSMBAD2.DOM renew until 01/17/2020 11:33:45 01/16/2020 11:33:50  01/16/2020 21:33:50   ldap/<AD2 name>.mosmbad2.dom@MOSMBAD2.DOM renew until 01/17/2020 11:33:45
Notes :

1. By default, the Kerberos ticket is valid for 10 hours . 
   2. The Kerberos ticket defaults may be modified by the Group Policy Management → Kerberos Policy → Security Settings in the AD.
   3. MoSMB releases after March 22 - Tickets will be auto renewed on every MoSMB start/restart.
   4. On releases after March 22 - ldap ticket will also be taken at MoSMB start/restart if ldap configurations are correct.

MoSMB Integration with Checkmk for logs and statistics monitoring

Checkmk is an open-source IT monitoring platform that helps monitor IT infrastructure, including Servers, Networks. It can be configured to communicate with MoSMB nodes to monitor MoSMB logs and smb statistics
* Ref: Checkmk Introduction
Checkmk server installation
Download the required version from the link below. Steps for installation of the server are available online on Checkmk site and also given below for quick reference.
https://checkmk.com/download
Update your OS packages with apt update OR yum update commands depending on your OS version
Once the package is downloaded, install the package, if prompted for resolving dependencies press “Y” to proceed
sudo apt install ./check-mk-cloud-2.3.0p20_0.focal_amd64.deb
Once installation is complete the version of the package can be checked with this command
omd version
Creating a monitoring site

For creating a monitoring site on Checkmk server, below command can be executed. In this example local site named server_monitor will be created & login details will be shared once the process completes.
sudo omd create server_monitor
Commands that can be executed to display the status of the site created and to start. By default it will be in a stopped state

a) sudo omd status server_monitor
 b) sudo omd start  server_monitor
Once the site has started, it can be accessed by using the IP or the hostname of the Checkmk Server node, using a web-browser followed by the username & password provided earlier during site creation
Once you log in, the default password for the site can be changed from the main menu left panel → User → Change password
Monitoring momsb logs using Checkmk agent
The Checkmk agent consists of the agent script and the Agent Controller, which communicates with the Agent Receiver on the Checkmk server
Before the agent can be installed on all nodes to be monitored, some rules are required to be added on Checkmk server.

# Go to "Setup" -> "Agents"-> "Windows, Linux, Solaris, AIX"
# Click on the "Agent rules"
# Select the "Text logfiles (Linux, Solaris, Windows)"
# Add below logfile paths under the "Configure a logfile section"
   /var/log/mosmb.log
   /var/log/mosmb_audit.log
   /var/log/mosmb_vinode.log
Add below pattern under the "Regular expressions for message classification"

State -> Critical, Pattern(Regex) -> \[MO_CRITICAL\].*
   State -> Critical, Pattern(Regex) -> \[MO_ERROR\].*
   State -> WARNING, Pattern(Regex) ->  \[MO_WARN\].*
Save it by clicking on the "Save" button on the top
Click on the fire icon, "Bake agents”; this will create a Checkmk Agent package with all of the settings added
Once the Checkmk Agent is Baked, the required Checkmk agent package can be downloaded in any of the formats RPM,DEB etc. Choose depending on your OS on MoSMB nodes
Once the Checkmk agent is downloaded, the file can be copied to every node using scp or any other method
Here in this example, command used to install the package on redhat based MoSMB nodes
rpm -ivh check-mk-agent-2.3.0p20-8e905b123879084a.noarch.rpm
Once installation of the Checkmk agent is done on all required MoSMB nodes, they need to be added to the Checkmk server for monitoring

For adding MoSMB nodes to Checkmk, navigate through the main menu on left panel Setups -> Hosts ->Add host and fill out the required host details like Host Name, IP etc. Then click on Save & run service discovery

Once Save & run service discovery is clicked, it will take a few seconds to complete the scan and show all the services it scanned on all MoSMB nodes & display a screen in which we can select Accept all or we can choose which all services need to be monitored by Checkmk server (click the green + symbol to add the services one by one  to Checkmk

After selecting required services, the exclamation mark on the right side top corner will display the number of changes that need to be done.  Clicking the exclamation mark will redirect the user to an “ Activation status ” tab shown in the image below.  The user needs to click on the red activation button that starts activating the services
Then register the Checkmk agents on all the nodes of the MoSMB cluster.

The Checkmk agent registration can be done using the Checkmk agent controller “cmk-agent-ctl” supported with the systemd version 219 or newer. Below commands should be executed on command line of the MoSMB node to register the Checkmk agent

# To get the systemd version systemctl --version # To get the Checkmk agent controller version cmk-agent-ctl --version # To register the Checkmk agent cmk-agent-ctl register –hostname <HOSTNAME>  –server <SERVER NAME/IP>::8000 –site <SITE NAME> –user <USER NAME> –password <PASSWORD> # To verify the trust relationship after successful registration cmk-agent-ctl status

Once the MoSMB nodes are registered as per the above step then the user needs to go to the Checkmk server & navigate through  Monitor -> Overview -> All Hosts , which will show all the nodes that are being monitored. Clicking one of the nodes will display all the services which are being monitored on that particular node. The page refreshes every 30 seconds. It might take a few minutes to show all the changes. Every service we can click it and brows through for additional information
De-register MoSMB node from Checkmk server
De-registration will be needed by the user to move the Checkmk agent from one node to another in case the node is removed from the MoSMB cluster or any other reasons.
Check the status of Checkmk agent & get the UUID of the node with below
cmk-agent-ctl status
The user needs to revoke the trust relationship of the Checkmk agent with the Checkmk server using the below command with the UUID as displayed in the above command.
cmk-agent-ctl delete <UUID>

Then the user needs to navigate through Setups → Hosts—> <NODE NAME> from the Checkmk server dashboard by selecting the node which needs to be removed. For that, the user has to select the “ remove TLS registration ” option by clicking the burger icon shown in image below.
Configuring the the Checkmk server to display MoSMB statistic numbers

Once mosmb is installed, you would be able to find /etc/mosmb/scripts/checkmk_mosmb_stats.py on all mosmb nodes. This script runs the statistics collection on the node which is saved in a CSV file, referred to by Checkmk agent.
Add cron job to execute the python script every 7 mins as shown below, on all mosmb nodes

cat /etc/cron.d/mosmb_stat_crontab
  */7 * * * * root /etc/mosmb/scripts/checkmk_mosmb_stats.py

Once we have added the cron job, we need to go to Checkmk dashboard and navigate to Setups -> Hosts.  We can  select all the nodes of mosmb and then click on hosts —> Run bulk service discovery and then click on start . Once the service discovery completes we activate and save the changes , same as done above.

Once the above setup is complete we should be able to see an additional service entry named MoSMB CSV Stats in the monitoring dashboard of the node selected. We can select the required service here in example MoSMB CSV Stats & browse through the graphs plotted for the MoSMB statistics.
