Verify if the firewall policies allow port 443 to connect to the MoSMB license servers.
Verify if https://wyday.com/limelm/ is reachable from the MoSMB server.
Verify if the time and date settings for the MoSMB server machine are correct or up-to-date.
Offline Product Activation
Verify if the MoSMB server's network adapters are up.
Verify if the product key mentioned in the MoSMB configuration and the product key used while activating the license is the same.
Make sure you have followed the steps for activation as per the offline product activation using product key
Offline Trial Activation
Verify if the trialresponse.xml file that is provided to you is placed at the /etc/mosmb/license/ location.
Verify if the trialrequest.xml is generated on the same MoSMB server machine on which you need to activate the MoSMB license.
Verify if the MoSMB server's network adapters are up.
Failed to check activation within grace period

When the Error - Failed to check product activation within grace period is seen in mosmb.log It would indicate that the product key was activated online on the server machine BUT now the server machine is not able to reach to | limelm site To activate the product key again, deactivate the current product key activation by running /etc/mosmb/license/.mosmb_license_deactivate Then follow offline activation steps
MoSMB Share Not Accessible
Check the mosmb.log for any obvious error messages that would indicate what is the problem.
Check if mosmb service is running, check pid or process information using ps

# service mosmb status
# pidof mosmb
# ps -aux| grep mosmb
Verify if port 445 is open for the TCP communication.
Verify if the TCP port mentioned in the MoSMB configuration file is also configured on the client machine and is open for communication over TCP.
If error is related to krb5, Refer to the Troubleshooting for the AD Kerberos MoSMB share access when authentication mode used for share access is krb5
If error is related to gss-api refer to the troubleshooting section for gss-api errors
If the authentication mode is ntlmLocal make sure that the (local linux) MoSMB user is created on the MoSMB server machine and you are accessing the share via the same user.

check the mosmb_audit.log for login from the user for whom the access is failing, to check if Login attempt is failing or user has successfully logged in.  Refer here for more details on the mosmb_audit.log
If Login is successful, Verify if the user has the permission to access the share for the authentication mode used (krb5, ntlm-AD or ntlm_local) as mentioned in Permission Issues section below.

If a previously working node is removed and Re Joined to AD , for ntlmAD to work after such a change, you will need to re run mosmb_passwd -m and restart mosmb service too. Also old kerberos ticket on client side may prevent you from accessing the share. You need to purge the cache to remove old cluster/node kerberos ticket with klist purge OR user can sign out and signing to the client. OR The kerberos cache will expire in scheduled 10 hours and next access will renew the ticket automatically.
MoSMB Share Not Accessible on Ubuntu 22.04 OR error seen with mosmb_passwd binary
possible error seen with mosmb_passwd :
ValueError: unsupported hash type md4
Verify if openssl configuration required for Ubuntu 22.04 are done
If the server machines have openssl version 3 and above configuration changes are required. Check openssl version with
openssl version
Open /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf
Existing/Default Configuration :

[provider_sect]
   default = default_sect
   [default_sect]
   # activate = 1
Edits/Required Configuration :

[provider_sect]
   default = default_sect legacy = legacy_sect [openssl_init]
   providers = provider_sect
 
   [default_sect] activate = 1 [legacy_sect] activate = 1
MoSMB Share Not Accessible for only a particular user

If access fails for a user, make sure audit logs are enabled and verify login attempts from the user for mosmb nodes. Check the mosmb_audit.log to see if Login attempt is failing or user has successfully logged in.

If login is successful check permission issues. 
If login is failed check the authentication password/domain information
If no login attempt message is seen check mosmb.log for MO_ERROR or MO_WARN logs
Permission Issues
Verify that the correct user permissions are set on the MoSMB server side and the same user is used to access the share and create or edit the shared files or directories.
POSIX Authorization Settings
NTACL Authorization Settings
Viewing File / Directory Permissions
Verify if port 88 is open on both the MoSMB server and the AD machine. This is required by the LDAP server.
When the authentication mode is krb5 verify if the Kerberos ticket is present on the MoSMB server.
In case the authentication mode is krb5 verify if all the LDAP client configuration settings are carried out.
In case the authentication mode is krb5 verify if all the SSSD related settings are carried out.
Verify if the package necessary for generating UUID is installed on the MoSMB server. If not please install the package.
ID Mapper Not Working Correctly
Verify if the sssd service is running on the MoSMB server.
check with id command that the AD user's details are retrieved and it does print the user ID and group IDs. In case it is failing to do so, try restarting the sssd service.
Verify the nsswitch.conf file and make sure that .sss is mentioned in the configuration.

Changes to the AD group membership are slow to take effect. The issue could be because of the sssd cache. When you change the primary group of the user then the old entry in the sssd cache becomes invalid. For new changes to take effect restart the sssd service and run the following command: sss_cache -E OR sss_cache -u <user-name>

If you are considering EVERYONE present in NTFS (Everyone includes the owner, group and other members) as Others (in Linux this means all the members other than the owner and group members) mapping will fail.
Note : In the case of user and group mapping, MoSMB currently supports only the primary group mapping.
Issue with cluster keytab. Cluster fqdn access not working. (gss-api errors in mosmb.log)
Check time is in sync with the AD
Make sure the keytab creation is correctly done using ktpass on AD by the Admin (w.o. any errors or warning during the creation) using correct CRYPTO values used to generate keys in the keytab.

Use following PS cmdlet to check the spn is correctly mapped the special user setspn -Q cifs/<cifs-server-name> And use the follwoing PS cmdlet to check the user's SPN Get-ADUser <username> -property ServicePrincipalName
The DNS forward lookup works for the cluster fqdn and reverse lookup work correctly for cluster node ips
Check firewall for correct ports opened for kerberos
Check that the special user created for keytab creation in the ktpass 'mapuser', has following properties set:

Go to Active Directory users and computers 
User properties -> user account -> password never expires
User properties -> user account -> Correct encryption support (in accordance with the CRYPTO values used in ktpass) is enabled, e.g. This account supports Kerberos AES 256 bit encryption This account supports Kerberos AES 128 bit encryption You can also check the encryptions used in the keytab with -e option of klist klist -kte /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
OR use following PS cmdlet to check the user's supported encryption types Get-ADUser <username> -property KerberosEncryptionType
Check KVno from keytab against the special user.

Note the msDS-KeyVersionNumber from the o/p of following PS cmdlet and compare with kvno in the keytab from the output of klist ran on the mosmb.keytab Get-ADUser <username> -property msDS-KeyVersionNumber klist -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
In mosmb.json, check that globalSection is set to have enableHighAvailability to yes
Check the o/p of klist -A to check a valid krb5 tkt is fetched during mosmb startup ( mosmb will use the mosmb.keytab and domain_list.txt to do a kinit )
Check sssd.conf, krb5.conf, ldap.conf are set in accordance with the mosmb documentation
Check permitted encryption types from the krb5 crypto-policies
cat /etc/krb5.conf.d/crypto-policies

If setup was working fine previously, and now we are getting "Preauthentication failed" error in mosmb.log, verify that the password for special user used for cluster keytab creation is not expired or changed. If so, need to recreate the mosmb.keytab using ktpass
Troubleshooting Guide on the Active Directory Kerberos MoSMB share access
Refer to the AD Kerberos Troubleshooting Guide .
Troubleshooting Guide on RDMA (SMBDirect) communication on the MoSMB server
Refer → Basic RDMA(SMBDirect) Setup Guide .
ZooKeeper Troubleshooting
1. Error 1:
Unable to start AdminServer, exiting abnormally
Solution : Check for admin.serverPort configuration in zoo.cfg :
#admin server port configuration admin.serverPort=8082
Make sure port 8082 is free for use, if not free, use some other port
netstat -ntlp | grep 8082
2. Error 2:
java.net.BindException: Address already in use (Bind failed)
Solution : Check if ports configured are available, if not free then use some other port combination
netstat -ntlp | grep 4888 & netstat -ntlp | grep 5888 on all ZooKeeper Server machines.

Example :	
server.1 = <ip_server1>:4888:5888
server.2 = <ip_server2>:4888:5888
server.3 = <ip_server3>:4888:5888
Here 4888 used for follower nodes to connect to the leader node and 5888 used for leader election.
3. MoSMB service not starting due to older zookeeper versions refer :- Uninstalling previous zookeeper version
4. Make sure after successful installation all required zookeeper libraries are present on the zookeeper server.
Microsoft Management Console (MMC)
Manage File Shares with Shared Folders MMC Snap-in. Close sessions and close open file handles can be managed via MMC
Open mmc on a windows → Navigate to the Start Menu and select Run, type 'mmc'.
On the MMC console from File menu select Add or Remove snap-ins
Select and add Shared Folders snap-in
In Shared Folder properties → Enter the node fqdn or ip and connect.
Check Shares to list the smb-shares on the nodes.
Check Sessions to list the smb-sessions connected to the node.
Check Open Files to list all the open files on the node.
MAC slow browsing performance
NOTE: sonoma 14.6.1 onwards nsmb.conf does not help, refer to below points for older MAC OS versions
To improve MAC smb client's browsing performance, following settings can be tried 1. edit /etc/nsmb.conf

[default]
dir_cache_off=yes
aapl_off=true
signing_required=no
file_ids_off=yes
dir_ids_off=yes
defaults write com.apple.desktopservices DSDontWriteNetworkStores -bool TRUE
On terminal run defaults write com.apple.desktopservices DSDontWriteNetworkStores -bool TRUE
check the setting with defaults read com.apple.desktopservices DSDontWriteNetworkStores
Restart your MAC once these changes are done
2. Disable icon previews and size calculation

connect to share
Browse to share
Press CMD + j (or rt click-> show view options )
  Disable “Calculate all sizes”
  Disable “Show icon preview”
Select “Use as Defaults”
3. some more view options
Going further u can disable showing 'date modified' and 'size' in the view options
4. Turn off indexing spotlight indexing can be turned off with
sudo mdutil -a -i off /Volumes/<share-name>
To see whether Spotlight indexing is enabled for a volume:

mdutil -s -v /Volumes/share-name
 example: mdutil -s /Volumes/share1 /System/Volumes/Data/Volumes/share1:
    Indexing and searching disabled.
5. 311 smb protocol in mosmb.json on releases 072 and later Try changing the max smb protocol to 311 on the mosmb server in mosmb.json file and restart mosmb service
maxSmbProtocol = 311
6. If access is still slow; try some more network settings
try following n/w settings on MAC , run from terminal

sysctl -w kern.ipc.maxsockbuf=8388608
sysctl -w net.inet.tcp.sendspace=2097152
sysctl -w net.inet.tcp.recvspace=2097152
sysctl -w net.inet.tcp.delayed_ack=2
sysctl -w net.inet.tcp.win_scale_factor=8
sysctl -w net.inet.tcp.autorcvbufmax=33554432
sysctl -w net.inet.tcp.autosndbufmax=33554432
No restart required, if restarted the settings will be lost. Some more settings

sysctl -w net.smb.fs.kern_deadtimer=0
sysctl -w net.smb.fs.kern_hard_deadtimer=0
sysctl -w net.smb.fs.kern_soft_deadtimer=0

In addition, if your Mac needs above network settings to be retained on reboot.
Create a file named /etc/sysctl.conf in your local /etc folder
This needs again to be done as root!

Go to the command line, become root and fire up your preferred editor (vi) by typing vi /etc/sysctl.conf
Put the following into the newly created file,

vi /etc/sysctl.conf 
kern.ipc.maxsockbuf=8388608
net.inet.tcp.sendspace=2097152
net.inet.tcp.recvspace=2097152
net.inet.tcp.delayed_ack=2
net.inet.tcp.win_scale_factor=8
net.inet.tcp.autorcvbufmax=33554432
net.inet.tcp.autosndbufmax=33554433
net.smb.fs.kern_deadtimer=0
net.smb.fs.kern_hard_deadtimer=0
net.smb.fs.kern_soft_deadtimer=0

and save the file. The file rights must be set to:
owner root:wheel and -rw-r--r--
please make sure by typing ls -al /etc/sysctl.conf on the command line and if not, change it by running
chown root:wheel /etc/sysctl.conf
and
chmod 0644 /etc/sysctl.conf from the command line
In order for MoSMB share access to work with krb5 authentication Kerberos ticket should be generated and the ticket should be valid.
Generating a Kerberos Ticket (Releases before March 2022)

To generate a ticket on the server without having to use the password, use command kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab <SPN>@<DOMAIN NAME> . -k Specifies to obtain the key for the ticket principal from a key table. So you are not prompted to enter the password for the ticket principal. -t will refer to the keytab file specified. For MoSMB it should be /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
Example:
Test Data : mosmbad.dom, cifs/my-mosmb-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM (keytab entry of cifs) Command   : sudo kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab cifs/my-mosmb-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM
Generating Kerberos Ticket (Releases after March 2022)

In new releases, user will NOT be needed to acquire a ticket manually. To generate a ticket on the server, MoSMB will use principal name from /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt , match against the one present in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab and use it to generate the kerberos ticket on MoSMB service start.
Verifying a Kerberos Ticket
To verify if the ticket is acquired for the domain_user, run the klist command.
Example:

Command : sudo klist -A Output  : Ticket cache: KCM:0:96571 Default principal: cifs/my-mosmb-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM Valid starting       Expires              Service principal 05/16/2022 11:33:50  05/16/2022 21:33:50  krbtgt/MOSMBAD.DOM@MOSMBAD.DOM renew until 05/17/2022 11:33:45
Notes :

1. By default, the Kerberos ticket is valid for 10 hours . 
   2. The Kerberos ticket defaults may be modified by the Group Policy Management → Kerberos Policy → Security Settings in the AD.
   3. MoSMB releases after March 22 - Tickets will be auto renewed on every MoSMB start/restart.
   4. On releases after March 22 - ldap ticket will also be taken at mosmb start/restart if ldap configurations are correct.

cmdpipe
cmdpipe is a helper script located in /etc/mosmb/scripts directory and it support following tasks -
Reload the MoSMB configuration file - /etc/mosmb/mosm.json after adding new share
Update MoSMB logging at runtime
Notifies SMB Command frequency count
Display the MoSMB server statistics
Prerequisites
Python and pip of same version
Command-line JSON processor : jq package
MoSMB service should be running
Usage

Command: cd /etc/mosmb/scripts/ Command: ./cmdpipe -h Usage: ./cmdpipe <command>
       command:
       config_reload_add : Reloads mosmb config to add new share
       add_runtime_logging : Notifies mosmb of the changes in /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf
       smb_frequency_count : Notifies smb command frequency count in  <log folder from mosmb.json>/mosmb_smb_frequency_cnt.log file
       set_maintenance_mode : Set the MoSMB server in Maintenance Mode. Note: Available from Release 069 get_mosmb_config : Get the configuration parameters of MoSMB server. Note: Available from Release 087 statson : Starts collecting statistics
       domainlist_reload : Reloads the domainlist.txt contents in mosmb Note: Available from Release 100

Use the following options for mosmb config reload
       -n : Specifies share name
       -t : Specifies share type
       -h : Display this help message and exit
       Share type can be one if these two : VFS or S3
       Following is an Example:
       ./cmdpipe config_reload_add -n share_name -t vfs
       OR
       ./cmdpipe config_reload_add -n share_name -t S3
           
       Use the following options for dumping stats into csv periodically
       -p : Specifies csv path. Default is /var/log/
       -i : Specifies time period in seconds. This is the repeat interval for the dump job. Default is 300
       -c : Display stats on 'console'
       -h : Shows statson help message and Exit
       -n : Number count for stats operation
       Following is an Example:
       ./cmdpipe statson -p /var/log/ -i 600
       ./cmdpipe statson -p /var/log/ -i 600 -c : Display the stats on console after every '-i' interval
       ./cmdpipe statson -p /var/log/ -i 600 -c -n 5: Display the stats on console after every '-i' interval for '-n' number of time
Examples
config_reload_add
Prerequisites:
MoSMB should be running.
New share should be added to MoSMB configurations /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json either manually or by mosmbcli set --json command for share option
Usage:

Command: /etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe config_reload_add -h Use the following options for mosmb config reload
       -n : Specifies share name
       -t : Specifies share type
       -h : Display this help message and exit
       Share type can be one if these two : VFS or S3
       Following is an Example:
       ./cmdpipe config_reload_add -n share_name -t vfs
       OR
       ./cmdpipe config_reload_add -n share_name -t S3
Example:

Command: ./cmdpipe config_reload_add -n "Local xfs" -t vfs Success
       Command executed successfully
add_runtime_logging
Prerequisites:
MoSMB should be running.
Logging level changes will take effect for mosmb logs as per changes in /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf
Usage:

Command: /etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe add_runtime_logging -h 1) First make the required log level changes in  /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf
       2) Now run the command /etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe add_runtime_logging
Example:

Command: ./cmdpipe add_runtime_logging Logging Reload Command initialized
       Success
       Command executed successfully
smb_frequency_count
Prerequisites:
MoSMB should be running. Note: File mosmb_smb_frequency_cnt.log will be created at the MoSMB logs directory mentioned in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json.
Usage:
Command: ./cmdpipe smb_frequency_count -h Simply run the command /etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe smb_frequency_count
Example:

Command: ./cmdpipe smb_frequency_count Getting frequency count of smb commands
       Success
       Command executed successfully
set_maintenance_mode
Prerequisites:
MoSMB should be running to enable or disable maintenance mode. Note: New option available only from MoSMB release 069 onwards.
Usage:

Command: /etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe set_maintenance_mode -h Usage options for mosmb set maintenance mode -o : Specifies option to enable or disable the maintenance mode
       -h : Display this help message and exit
Example:

Command: ./cmdpipe set_maintenance_mode -o enable Success
       Command executed successfully
       Also mosmb.log will show the notification message as follows - [MO_CRITICAL]:[SMBCORE]:[TID 42496]: MoSMB server has entered into Maintenance Mode. No new connections/sessions will be accepted now.
get_mosmb_config
Prerequisites:
MoSMB should be running to get the current static and dynamic configurations. Note: New option available only from MoSMB release 087 onwards.
Usage:

Command: ./cmdpipe get_mosmb_config -p <path> -f humanreadable OR ./cmdpipe get_mosmb_config -p <path> -f csv Usage options for mosmb get configuration parameters -p : Specify path where the configuration parameters will be dumped. Default is path pointed out by logsPath parameter in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json
       -f : Specify format in which the configuration parameters will be dumped. Default is 'humanreadable'
       -h : Display this help message and exit
Example:

Command: ./cmdpipe get_mosmb_config -f csv Sending get server config params command to the MoSMB server.
     Command executed successfully
     Dump of the configuration parameters is at /var/log/ -- csv file named mosmb_config_params_dump.csv is created.
statson
Prerequisites:

MoSMB should be running. Note: Following files will be created when -p option is given depending on the build type 1. preprocessor_stats.csv 2. server_stats.csv 3. worker_thread_stats.csv 4. smbdirect_stats.csv 5. processed_stats.csv
Usage:

Command: ./cmdpipe statson -h Usage: cmdpipe statson [OPTION]...
       MOSMB server statistics
       Optional Arguments to statson
               -p, --path       Specifies csv path. Default is /var/log/
               -i, --interval   Specifies time period in seconds. This is the repeat interval for the dump job. Default is 300
               -c, --console    Display stats on 'console'
               -n, --number     Number count for stats operation
               -h, --help       Shows this help message and Exit
       
       Following Abbreviations used while showing output on console using '-c' option
       
       RIO(Read IOPS), WIO(Write IOPS), TIOPs(Total IOPS), RT(Read Throughput), WT(Write Throughput),
       TT(MB/s)(Total Throughput MB/s), RL(Read Latency), WL(Write Latency), SRL(Storage Read Latency),
       SWL(Storage Write Latency), FO(Total File/Pipe Opened), ES(Total Established Sessions),
       DC(Total Disconnects), PV(Password Violations), PE(Total Permission Error)
Example: Following is an Example:

Support Added for .prom file format from version 093 onwards MoSMB version 093 onwards cmdpipe supports .prom file format to collect metric in the Prometheus text exposition format. (key value pair) More details on how to use Prometheus to monitor mosmb metrics is available HERE

Use the following options for dumping stats into csv/prom periodically
  -p : Specifies stats path. Default path for 'csv' format is /var/log/. Note: While using 'prom' stats format, it is mandatory to use same path for both i.e. 'cmdpipe' and 'node_exporter' ('--collector.textfile.directory' option) of prometheus. -f : Specifies format in which the stats will be dumped (prom / csv) . Default is 'csv' format
  -i : Specifies time period in seconds. This is the repeat interval for the dump job. Default is 300
  -c : Displays stats on 'console'
  -h : Shows statson help message and Exit
  -n : Number count for stats operation
   
Following is an Example: ./cmdpipe statson -p /var/lib/node_exporter -f prom -i 600 -c -n 5 i.e. Display the stats on console after every '-i' interval for '-n' number of time, save stats in processed_stats.prom file in folder /var/lib/node_exporter

Sample processed_stats.prom file MoSMB_BytesSent 520115452
   MoSMB_BytesReceived 577892654
   MoSMB_Throughput_MBps 45.53
   MoSMB_NoOfReadRequests 123
   MoSMB_NoOfWriteRequests 126
   MoSMB_IOPS 10
   MoSMB_ReadLatency_msec 76.01
   MoSMB_WriteLatency_msec 382.00
   MoSMB_StorageReadLatency_msec 71.04
   MoSMB_StorageWriteLatency_msec 378.59
   MoSMB_TotalFileOrPipeOpens 5
   MoSMB_TotalActiveSessions 1
   MoSMB_Disconnects 0
   MoSMB_TotalPasswordViolations 0
   MoSMB_TotalPermissionErrors 0
domainlist_reload
Prerequisites:

MoSMB should be running to reload the domain's list from domain_list.txt And it will also check if the domain has a whitelist present, if yes, it will use the IPs mentioned in the whitelist of the specific domain to reconnect to AD Note: New option available only from MoSMB release 100 onwards.
Usage:
Command: ./cmdpipe domainlist_reload
Error Codes
sssd configuration
sssd helps during the User ID mapping for domain users.
If in case sssd is not installed before, install sssd-common and configure sssd on all the MoSMB server machine in cluster.
Create a sssd configuration file for MoSMB /etc/sssd/conf.d/mosmb_sssd.conf and it should contain parameters as shown below.

[sssd] domains = mosmbad.dom config_file_version = 2
   services = nss, pam [domain/mosmbad.dom] ad_domain = mosmbad.dom
   krb5_realm = MOSMBAD.DOM krb5_keytab = /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab realmd_tags = manages-system joined-with-adcli
   cache_credentials = True
   id_provider = ad
   krb5_store_password_if_offline = True
   default_shell = /bin/bash ldap_id_mapping = True use_fully_qualified_names = True
   fallback_homedir = /home/%u@%d
   access_provider = ad
sssd configuration files must be at /etc/sssd/ with permissions of file set to 600 (-rw-------) and for both /etc/sssd/sssd.conf and /etc/sssd/conf.d/mosmb_sssd.conf
Start sssd service with service sssd start .
nsswitch.conf Configuration
Verify that nsswitch.conf file is configured to use SSSD as a provider
Open /etc/nsswitch.conf file and verify that it points to sss for the name service switch

passwd:     files sss
   shadow:     files sss
   group:      files sss netgroup:   files sss
Note :
Restart the sssd service after making any changes in the sssd configurations.
Note :
MoSMB service restart is mandatory in case any changes are made to the configuration file.

To install and configure MoSMB package in your environment, perform following steps with root user or with sudo privileges.
Getting started with MoSMB
Mentions the steps to follow for installing and configuration of different MoSMB server modes. The package installs configuration files at /etc/mosmb directory.

MoSMB Installation: To install the MoSMB on any linux distribution like centos, ubuntu, debian, you must meet certain requirements. System Requirements Firewall settings Installer pre-dependencies Install MoSMB package MoSMB Ansible Installer Guide
System Requirements
Firewall settings
Installer pre-dependencies
Install MoSMB package
MoSMB Ansible Installer Guide

MoSMB Server Mode: Depending on the package provided/installed server mode is selected. MoSMB-SA : Standalone Server, works with all authentication modes MoSMB-CL : Single-Domain Cluster, works with krb5 and NTLM-AD authentication mode - Zookeeper Server Installation and Configuration is must MoSMB-CL : Multi-Domain Cluster, works with krb5 and NTLM-AD authentication mode - Zookeeper Server Installation and Configuration is must MoSMB-SA-S3 : S3 based Standalone Server, works with all authentication modes - Zookeeper Server Installation and Configuration is must
MoSMB-SA : Standalone Server, works with all authentication modes
MoSMB-CL : Single-Domain Cluster, works with krb5 and NTLM-AD authentication mode - Zookeeper Server Installation and Configuration is must
MoSMB-CL : Multi-Domain Cluster, works with krb5 and NTLM-AD authentication mode - Zookeeper Server Installation and Configuration is must
MoSMB-SA-S3 : S3 based Standalone Server, works with all authentication modes - Zookeeper Server Installation and Configuration is must

MoSMB server Authentication Modes: MoSMB offers serval authentication modes which require different configuration settings. Ntlm-AD: NTLMSSP(Ntlm-AD) ntlmLocal : ntlml local authentication mode is honored krb5: strict kerberos authentication mode is honored - Network Configurations: pre-requisites1 for krb5 authentication Kerberos Configurations: pre-requisites2 for krb5 authentication
Ntlm-AD: NTLMSSP(Ntlm-AD)
ntlmLocal : ntlml local authentication mode is honored
krb5: strict kerberos authentication mode is honored - Network Configurations: pre-requisites1 for krb5 authentication Kerberos Configurations: pre-requisites2 for krb5 authentication
Network Configurations: pre-requisites1 for krb5 authentication
Kerberos Configurations: pre-requisites2 for krb5 authentication

MoSMB server Authorization Modes: MoSMB offers serval authorization modes dependent on the authentication mode selected. none: No ACL's, works with all types of authentication modes. posix: Posix ACL's works with ntlmLocal or krb5 authentication modes. ntacl: ntacl/windows ACL's works only with krb5 authentication mode.
none: No ACL's, works with all types of authentication modes.
posix: Posix ACL's works with ntlmLocal or krb5 authentication modes.
ntacl: ntacl/windows ACL's works only with krb5 authentication mode.

NTP Configuration on MoSMB server is a prerequisite for the Kerberos Configurations.

Important :
All the components - AD, MoSMB Server, and SMB client machines should always be time synced for the AD-Kerberos authentication to work.
Install Network Time Protocol - Package names ntp / chronyd depending on the machine OS
Update firewall settings as per your Evaluation and/or Production environment settings to allow ntp through firewall.
Start or restart this Network Time Protocol (ntp) / (chronyd) service on the MoSMB server.
Example:

Command : yum install ntp or apt-get install ntp or dnf install chrony Command : firewall-cmd --permanent --add-service=ntp Command : firewall-cmd --reload Command : systemctl stop ntpd.service or service ntp stop or systemctl stop chronyd Command : systemctl start ntpd.service or service ntp start or systemctl start chronyd
Warning : The Kerberos authentication fails in case:
The Network Time Protocol (NTP) is unavailable.
The time between the MoSMB server, AD and SMB clients is unsynced.
Note : To resolve the Fully Qualified Domain Name(FQDN) of a Network Time Protocol(NTP) server, ensure that your Domain Name Server(DNS) settings are well configured.
All MoSMB-CL builds supports single domain clustering. To form a single domain MoSMB cluster simply follow the configurations mentioned in steps below one by one.
1. Carry out Network Time Protocol (NTP) Configuration settings on all MoSMB server nodes. 2. Request the Domain Administration team for AD and DNS Configurations.
Configure DNS entries for the MoSMB Cluster
Carry out name resolution settings on all MoSMB servers.
3. ntlmLocal and related services configurations on all MoSMB server machines in the cluster.
Ntlm (ntlmLocal) Authentication Mode Configurations
For each node in a cluster, you need to create MoSMB users with the same Username and password
All Users should belong to same primary group on all nodes for ACL's to get applied appropriately.
All groups and users should should have same uid and gid on all nodes
4. MoSMB server is ZooKeeper client; Perform MoSMB -ZooKeeper settings
MoSMB-ZooKeeper Server installation
MoSMB-ZooKeeper Cluster Configuration
Start MoSMB-ZooKeeper
5. Carry out required authorization (ACL) settings
Using MoSMB with Posix ACL OR
Using MoSMB without any authorization
6. Setup the MoSMB configuration file (/etc/mosmb/mosmb.json) for cluster use.
In globalSection set enableHighAvailability to yes
In persistentStoreSection keep pstorePath location on the common storage path on all MoSMB nodes in the cluster.
In section nodeInfoSection , assign a different nodeId to each node in MoSMB cluster.
In section authSection , set authMode as ntlmLocal
In shareSection keep sharePath location on the common storage path on all MoSMB nodes in the cluster.
In shareSection set parameters isContinouslyAvailable and isCluster to yes
Example:
7. Set Priority List for AD IPs (in an AD Farm) if required If you have a AD farm, and want mosmb to use only few of them for any reason:
Add Priority List for AD IPs in /etc/mosmb
8. If the server machines have openssl version 3 and above configuration changes are required.
Open /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf
Existing/Default Configuration :

[provider_sect]
   default = default_sect
   [default_sect]
   # activate = 1
Edits/Required Configuration :

[provider_sect]
   default = default_sect legacy = legacy_sect [openssl_init]
   providers = provider_sect
 
   [default_sect] activate = 1 [legacy_sect] activate = 1
8. Start MoSMB service on all nodes in the MoSMB cluster and check service mosmb status and check the mosmb.log for details. You can check details of MoSMB server Maintenance here
Notes :

1. Make sure ZooKeeper standalone server / ZooKeeper cluster is running.
   2. Make sure pstorePath and sharePath are not the same directory but are on the common storage path. 
   3. Make sure all the MoSMB configuration on all cluster nodes is similar. 
   4. To know other mosmb.json parameters in details refer this .

When there are restricted Kerberos entries on the server machine seen as output of command klist -k /etc/krb5.keytab Check if they are also present on the AD side if present we need to remove them from AD. Removing these entries from AD side only is sufficient.
Login to AD machine with user who has admin rights and open Active directory administrative Center
Open computers section
In computers open properties of the MoSMB server machine
In Properties of server machine  → Extensions → Attribute Editor
In Attribute editor select the attribute "servicePrincipalName"
Remove restricted entries from the servicePrincipalName against the MoSMB server.
Example -

restrictedkrbhost/mymosmbserver.mosmbad.dom
   restrictedkrbhost/MYMOSMBSERVER
Restricted Entries in AD representation
Restricted Entries in AD representation

Restricted Entries in AD representation
Note :
After making changes on AD machine destroy the previously taken Kerberos tickets if any from server machine with kdestroy -A and generate new ticket.

Server maintenance includes allowing user to perform clean start, stop operations along with dynamically adding a share and compute performance parameters depending on the MoSMB Server Machine Configurations.
Note :

* The MoSMB Server should be started in only one way at a time, that is either as binary or as a service.
   * The MoSMB Server will start when the product key or trial license is valid, persistent storage and share paths mentioned are available on the server. Refer to the MoSMB License Manager .

Service
User can perform a Start, Stop or Restart operations on the MoSMB service.
For any change/s in the MoSMB server configuration files /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json and/or /etc/mosmb/s3.json in any section/s it is mandatory to restart the MoSMB server.
Notes: : 1. systemctl support is available after release builds 073. 2. One can use Either service commands or systemctl commands throughout the cycle.
MoSMB Start
Command : EITHER service mosmb start OR systemctl start mosmb Output  : Starting MoSMB service...

When MoSMB is being used with ZooKeeper server, the sequence of starting these services is important as MoSMB is ZooKeeper's client and ZooKeeper takes the locks on certain files which is released after 200 secs timeout if the MoSMB / ZooKeeper server is exited abruptly or MoSMB / ZooKeeper server machine is rebooted.

1. Start ZooKeeper - Command : sh  /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh start Output  : JMX enabled by default Using config: ../apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/conf/zoo.cfg' Mode: Standalone Mode: Leader / Follower / Standalone this output will be different on individual servers depending on the MoSMB-ZooKeeper configurations 2. ZooKeeper Cleanup -
       ZooKeeper Cleanup is must when MoSMB or Zookeeper goes down (services or machines) while any smb operations are in progress.
       To Cleanup ZooKeeper nodes refer - MoSMB-ZooKeeper server cleanup 3. Start MoSMB - Command : EITHER service mosmb start OR systemctl start mosmb Output  : Starting MoSMB service...

When MoSMB is being used with ZooKeeper server, the sequence of starting these services is important as MoSMB is ZooKeeper client and ZooKeeper takes the locks on certain files which is released after 200 secs timeout if the MoSMB / ZooKeeper server is exited abruptly or MoSMB / ZooKeeper server machine is rebooted.

1. Start ZooKeeper on all nodes - Command : sh  /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh start Output  : JMX enabled by default Using config: ../apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/conf/zoo.cfg' Mode: Leader Mode: Leader / Follower this output will be different on individual servers depending on the MoSMB-ZooKeeper configurations 2. ZooKeeper Cleanup on all nodes -
       ZooKeeper Cleanup is must when MoSMB or Zookeeper goes down (services or machines) while any smb operations are in progress.
       To Cleanup ZooKeeper nodes refer - MoSMB-ZooKeeper server cleanup 3. Start MoSMB on all nodes - Command : EITHER service mosmb start OR systemctl start mosmb Output  : Starting MoSMB service...
Note :
* If MoSMB server and ZooKeeper server are configured on 2 separate machines and only MoSMB service/server goes down then ZooKeeper cleanup is not a requirement.
MoSMB Stop
Command : EITHER service mosmb stop OR systemctl stop mosmb Output  : Stopping MoSMB service...

When MoSMB is being used with ZooKeeper server, the sequence of stopping these services / shutting down the machines is important as MoSMB is ZooKeeper client and ZooKeeper takes the locks on certain files which is released after 200 secs timeout if the MoSMB / ZooKeeper server is exited abruptly or MoSMB / ZooKeeper server machine is rebooted.

1. Stop MoSMB service Command : EITHER service mosmb stop OR systemctl stop mosmb Output  : Stopping MoSMB service... 2. Stop ZooKeeper service/s Command : apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh stop Output  : .... Stopping zookeeper ... STOPPED

When MoSMB is being used with ZooKeeper server, the sequence of stopping these services / shutting down the machines is important as MoSMB is ZooKeeper client and ZooKeeper takes the locks on certain files which is released after 200 secs timeout if the MoSMB / ZooKeeper server is exited abruptly or MoSMB / ZooKeeper server machine is rebooted.

1. Stop MoSMB service on all nodes - Command : EITHER service mosmb stop OR systemctl stop mosmb Output  : Stopping MoSMB service... 2. Stop ZooKeeper service on all nodes - Command : apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh stop Output  : .... Stopping zookeeper ... STOPPED
MoSMB Restart
MoSMB service restart is mandatory in case any changes are made to the configuration file.
Command : EITHER service mosmb restart OR systemctl restart mosmb Output  : Stopping MoSMB service... Starting MoSMB service...

In case MoSMB goes in an unstable state due to node/process/network failures causing MoSMB and/or zookeeper node/s to go down or unreachable within recovery time periods, then follow the steps as mentioned below for clean restart the MoSMB along with MoSMB-ZooKeeper.

1. Stop MoSMB - Command : EITHER service mosmb stop OR systemctl stop mosmb 2. Run /etc/mosmb/scripts/zk-cleanup.sh script to clear the zookeeper database and logs on zookeeper node. ( If zookeeper is not on the MoSMB node, in that case manually delete all the files in <zookeeper-datadir>/version-2 and <zookeeper-install-dir>/logs ) /etc/mosmb/scripts/zk-cleanup.sh <zookeper-install-dir> 3. Stop zookeeper <zookeeper-install-dir>/bin/zkServer.sh stop 4. Start zookeeper <zookeeper-install-dir>/bin/zkServer.sh start 5. Start MoSMB service mosmb start

In case MoSMB cluster goes in an unstable state due to node/process/network failures causing multiple MoSMB and/or zookeeper nodes to go down or unreachable within recovery time periods, then follow the steps as mentioned below for clean restart the whole MoSMB cluster along with MoSMB-ZooKeeper cluster.

1. Stop MoSMB cluster (mosmb service on all nodes) Command : EITHER service mosmb stop OR systemctl stop mosmb 2. Delete all mo_openfiletable files from pstore path
   3. Stop zookeeper cluster (zookeeper on all nodes) <zookeeper-install-dir>/bin/zkServer.sh stop 4. Run the zookeeper cleanup script by MoSMB to clear the zookeeper database and logs on all zookeeper nodes in the cluster. <zk-cleanup script> <zookeper-install-dir path> /etc/mosmb/scripts/mosmb_zk-cleanup.sh <zookeper-install-dir path> (For Release versions MoSMB : 3.1.1.005 onwards) /etc/mosmb/scripts/zk-cleanup.sh <zookeper-install-dir path> (For Release versions MoSMB : 3.1.1.003 and before) ( If zookeeper is not on the MoSMB node, in that case manually delete all the files in <zookeeper-datadir>/version-2 and <zookeeper-install-dir>/logs OR in a workaround copy the clean up script to mosmb-zookeeper node and run it from there.)   
   
   5. Start zookeeper cluster (zookeeper on all nodes) <zookeeper-install-dir>/bin/zkServer.sh start 6. Start MoSMB cluster (mosmb service on all nodes) service mosmb start

MoSMB Logs

Once you start the MoSMB service, check the mosmb.log located at default path /var/log/ to see if MoSMB has started successfully without any errors. For any errors, check the Troubleshooting Guide or FAQs for assistance. Logs directory can be configured as per the requirement by editing parameter logsPath in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json . In logs directory MoSMB will create files mosmb.log, mosmb_audit.log, mosmb_vinode.log Please send your queries to support@ryussi.com
For mosmb logs, the log level values for all modules can be set in the conf file /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf
MoSMB Logs in release versions 3.1.1.039 and above
MO_CRITICAL = 0
MO_ERROR = 1
MO_WARN = 2
MO_INFO = 3
MO_DEBUG = 4
Default mosmb_logging.conf looks like

FSA_L           = 2
SMBCORE_L       = 2
CONNECTION_L    = 2
MULTICHANNEL_L  = 2
IOS_L           = 2
RDMA_L          = 2
WITNESS_L       = 2
OPLOCKS_L       = 2
MORPC_L         = 2
CONFIG_L        = 3
STATISTICS_L    = 2
LEASE_L         = 2
NDR_L           = 2
RPC_L           = 2
HASH_L          = 2
PST_L           = 2
LICENSE_L       = 2
AUDIT_L         = 2
AUTH_L          = 2
ENCRYPTION_L    = 2
VINODE_L        = 2
DEFAULT_L       = 3
COMMON_L        = 2
CLUSTER_L       = 2
PROFILER_L      = 2
The MO_CRITICAL log messages are printed with loglevel set to 0
(They will be printed for all other log level > 0 as well)
When the log level value set is to 1, error level logs are enabled (MO_ERROR Level) (They will be printed for all other log level > 1 as well)
The MO_WARN log messages are printed with log level set to 2 in the conf file.
(They will be printed for all other log level > 2 as well)

For modules CONFIG_L and DEFAULT_L, default value is set to 3, which is MO_INFO. The INFO logs are printed to give more information. You can choose to disable it and print only error level logs by setting the value to < 3
For module PROFILER_L, default is set to 0. When the value is set to 4, the detailed information is printed.
Any changes to log levels can be reloaded at runtime, (no need to restart mosmb) reload Log Level Changes
In case debug level is required, following guidelines can be used to set respective log modules to debug level

Login/Access issues- enable AUDIT_L , AUTH_L, SMBCORE_L
File I/O issues - enable IOS_L, FSA_L, SMBCORE_L
Connection related issues - enable CONNECTION_L , SMBCORE_L
Overall general profiling for timings - enable PROFILER_L
RPC failures- enable - RPC_L, SMBCORE_L
mosmb-zookeeper related issues- VINODE_L, SMBCORE_L

Use these logs for Security Monitoring.
One can set the audit module’s log level in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf file to 3 to get detailed audit logs.

AUDIT_L = 3
This level logs File operations like CREATE RENAME DELETE Or PERMISSION CHANGE With FileName and the User who is attempting the operation
Note: Logs for MO_CRIT, MO_ERROR, MO_WARN will also be included
If MO_WARN level logs are required, one can set

AUDIT_L = 2
This level logs LOGIN and LOGOFF attempts, success or failure, With Time and IP of machine the attempt was made from
The mosmb_audit.log audit log file will then be generated at the log location (default  /var/log). Further details are same as older releases.

These are the logs generated by the api used to communicate to the (zookeeper/inode) database.
In case of any vinode related errors (either inode Mechanism or zookeeper), user can refer to these logs to get more details. The vinode loglevel starts from 1. For value 0 you may get error and mosmb will not start.

VINODE_L = 1
  Or 2/3/4 etc
The mosmb_vinode.log log file is populated at the log location, with MO_ERROR,MO_WARN,MO_INFO (Rest could be the Zookeeper logs.)

Sample output of critical log in mosmb.log:
[2023-02-28 11:16:54.357]:[MO_CRITICAL]:[SMBCORE]:[Thread 4876]: Stopping mosmb as mosmb-zookeeper has got disconnected. Please check mosmb-zookeeper service availability. pid : 4849
When the log level value set is to 0, only error level logs are enabled (MO_ERROR Level) (They will be printed for all other log level > 0 as well)
The MO_WARN log messages are printed with log level set to 1 in the conf file.
(They will be printed for all other log level > 1 as well)

VINODE_L = -1
  Or 0/1/2 etc
The mosmb_vinode.log log file is populated at the log location, with MO_CRITICAL,MO_ERROR,MO_WARN,MO_INFO (Rest could be ZOO logs.)

Use these logs for Security Monitoring.
One can set the audit module’s log level in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf file to 2 to get detailed audit logs.
AUDIT_L = 2
If MO_WARN level logs are required, one can set
AUDIT_L = 1
The mosmb_audit.log audit log file will then be generated at the log location (default  /var/log). Audit logs are populated for following categories:

CREATE
RENAME
DELETE
PERMISSION_CHANGE
LOGIN
LOGOFF
These logs would be helpful to see the failed login attempts, successful login attempts which smb clients connected, which users tried connecting etc.
Sample o/p for AUDIT module level 2

[2023-02-28 12:18:27.125]:[MO_INFO]:[AUDIT]:[Thread 7589]: [CREATE]:/home/SA_share/share/New folder is opened with Desired_access [0x0017019b] by myuser@domain.com.
[2023-02-28 12:18:31.405]:[MO_WARN]:[AUDIT]:[Thread 7594]: [RENAME]:renamed "/home/SA_share/share/New folder" to /home/SA_share/share/vai" by "myuser@domain.com"
[2023-02-28 12:18:57.827]:[MO_WARN]:[AUDIT]:[Thread 7593]:[DELETE]:/home/SA_share/share/vai is deleted by myuser@TESTDOMAIN.DOM
[2023-02-28 12:13:41.018]:[MO_WARN]:[AUDIT]:[Thread 7591]:[LOGOFF]:myuser@TESTDOMAIN.DOM user has logged-off from 10.68.0.24.
[2023-02-28 12:14:35.977]:[MO_WARN]:[AUDIT]:[Thread 7592]:[LOGIN]:myuser@TESTDOMAIN.DOM user has logged in from 10.68.0.24 using KRB5
[2023-02-28 12:14:35.999]:[MO_WARN]:[AUDIT]:[Thread 9667]: [PERMISSION_CHANGE]: /home/share/vtvm1's permissions has been changed by myuser@TESTDOMAIN
MoSMB Logs rotation
When MoSMB is installed, the scripts for the log rotation of the mosmb logs are added in the /etc/cron.hourly directory (mologrotate) and the other files are in /etc/logrotate.d directory.
/etc/logrotate.d/mosmbauditlr
/etc/logrotate.d/mosmblr
/etc/logrotate.d/zookeeperlr
Logs are rotated when the log file size reaches 500 MB(default). The log size threshold reached is checked on an hourly basis.
mosmb.log and other log files are rotated nd refer to files mosmbauditlr (mosmb_audit.log), mosmblr (mosmb.log) and zookeeperlr (mosmb_vinode.log) present under /etc/logrotate.d/ respectively
Adding a share dynamically

Script: cmdpipe Function:
To add a new share to MoSMB server and reload the configuration without stopping the MoSMB server. Only single share can be applied at a time on reload command. To add multiple shares, add all the share names to mosmb.json under shareSection. Run the script to add each share one at a time. Reload MoSMB config will take some time. The newly added share's shareName should be unique. sharePath must be valid and must exists on the server. Before running the command, please make sure the mosmb.json is correct.
Prerequisite to run cmdpipe:
python2 should be installed.
tabulate module should be installed.
To install tabulate :
pip2 install tabulate

Example: To add a share without restarting the mosmb server 1. Add required VFS share details in mosmb.json under shareSection as a new share e.g. ‘share3' as follows. ’share1’ is an existing share. 2. Add required S3 share details in mosmb.json under shareSection as a new share e.g. ‘s3_share' as follows. ’share1’ is an existing share. 3. The command mentioned below will add share3 and reload MoSMB server configuration [root@sivm31 ~]# /etc/mosmb/cmdpipe config_reload_add -h Error occurred in GetOpt Use the following commands for mosmb config reload -n : Specifies share name -t : Specifies share type Share type can be one of these two : VFS or S3
Following is an Example: ./cmdpipe config_reload_add -n <share_name> -t vfs OR ./cmdpipe config_reload_add -n <share_name> -t S3

[root@sivm31 ~]# /etc/mosmb/cmdpipe config_reload_add -n share3 -t vfs 
Success
Command executed successfully

[root@sivm31 ~]# /etc/mosmb/cmdpipe config_reload_add -n s3_share -t S3 
Success
Command executed successfully
MoSMB statistics
MoSMB statistics can be collected anytime with the command pipe utility
/etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe statson
Optional Arguments to statson

-p, --path Specifies csv path. Default is /var/log/
-i, --interval Specifies time period in seconds. Default is 300 s
-c, --console Display statistics on 'console'
-n, --number Number of counts for statistics collection operation
-h, --help Shows this help message and Exit
Examples:

/etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe statson -p /var/log/ -i 60 & => Statistic collection will keep running in background untill mosmb is restarted or cmdpipe instance is stopped /etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe statson -p /var/log/ -i 120 -c => Display the stats on console after every '-i' interval /etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe statson -p /var/log/ -i 600 -c -n 5 => Display the stats on console after every '-i' interval for '-n' number of time
Following Abbreviations used while showing output on console using '-c' option

RIO(Read IOPS), WIO(Write IOPS), TIOPs(Total IOPS), RT(Read Throughput), WT(Write Throughput),
TT(MB/s)(Total Throughput MB/s), RL(Read Latency), WL(Write Latency), SRL(Storage Read Latency),
SWL(Storage Write Latency), FO(Total File/Pipe Opened), ES(Total Established Sessions),
DC(Total Disconnects), PV(Password Violations), PE(Total Permission Error)
When statistics are collected in csv files, following files get populated in the default path or the path mentioned in command with –p

1. preprocessor_stats.csv
2. server_stats.csv
3. worker_thread_stats.csv
4. smbdirect_stats.csv
5. processed_stats.csv
User can use the processed_stats.csv file to check the collected and processed statistics
processed_stats.csv can be used to integrate with monitoring tools to visualizing and monitoring the metrics
Performance Tuning
Set the mosmb log level to 0 (zero) in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf file.

Set the Required Message Signing to Off to improve the performance. This will not disable the signing completely: When the client sends a signed request the MoSMB server will send the signed response. For more information refer to Microsoft Network Server: Digitally Sign Communications

You can switch on multichannel by setting Enable MultiChannel to yes in the MoSMB configuration. Multiple network cards can be added at the server end. In case there are multiple network paths between the client and the server, the SMB3 protocol uses the multi-channel feature.
MoSMB is pre-tuned to perform well for most of the workloads. The MoSMB performance scales linearly with smb connections.
MoSMB service restart is mandatory in case any changes are made to the configuration file.

One can use the performance tuning script provided by MoSMB at location /etc/mosmb/scripts to apply suggested parameters w.r.t server machines CPU and RAM. To run for smbd parameters tuning ---- sh /etc/mosmb/scripts/perf_tuning.sh smbdirect To run for tcp parameters tuning ---- sh /etc/mosmb/scripts/perf_tuning.sh tcp Detailed usage is provided in script ---- sh /etc/mosmb/scripts/perf_tuning.sh -h
TCP Performance
In the MoSMB configuration, set all the transaction sizes to 8M. (Max Transaction Size, Max Read Size, Max Write Size)
Memory allocation is shared in all the TCP connections and Memory utilization is dependent on the following formula: preProcessorThreads * ringBufferCount * Transaction size

Ways to scale-up and scale-out MoSMB performance: i. In case the client connections are more in number, increase the server threads or pre-processing threads. ii. The number of threads in the worker thread-pool can be increased so that more number of SMB packets can be served at a time. The number of threads can be increased to 64/128/256 for a high-end system, depending on the CPU cores of the system. iii. The number of pre-allocated buffers can be increased as per the RAM memory. iv. Add an additional network card to make the multi-channel feature functional and enable the multi-channel settings in the MoSMB configuration. v. To scale up the MoSMB on one node, add more NICs, CPU or memory as desired. If the backend storage is a bottleneck, add high performance storage like NVMe disks. vi. To scale out, add another MoSMB node to the cluster and expose the same share.

Note : MoSMB provides a performance tuning script as per the server configuration so as MoSMB will use only upto  60% of the available memory on the server machine. This script is located at /etc/mosmb/scripts/perf_tuning.sh Just run the script to get tuning parameters like preProcessorThreads, threadPoolSize and ringBufferCount set as per the server configuration.
RDMA (SMBDirect) Performance
Note : Refer to the Troubleshooting Guide for the RDMA (SMBDirect) Communication on a MoSMB Server to understand how to make the RDMA (SMBDirect) basic setup work.

The SMBDirect protocol uses the RDMA Read/Write requests to transfer file data within the client and the server. Every RDMA Read/Write operation consists of a SMB2 Read request with 1 or more buffer descriptors . Each buffer descriptor contains information for an I/O buffer with the desired transfer size. Each buffer descriptor thus requires to be allocated and pinned in memory till the transfer is completed through the RDMA channel. So for every buffer descriptor a buffer of desired size is allocated and pinned, this is a control operation and consumes a significant amount of time. Therefore we have introduced the following parameters to ensure that we reduce control operations during data operations:

SMB Direct ring size: i. Default value: 512 ii. Per connection based parameter. iii. Accepts value in the multiples of 64. iv. The server should be restarted to reflect the effect when a  parameter is changed. The SMB Direct ring size is a parameter that allocates and reserves the specified number of buffers, each of the SMBDirect buffer size size. On every RDMA Read/Write request the buffer is consumed from this ring and replenished after use. This allows faster operations as we can avoid memory pinning operations (control) in the data path resulting in faster speeds. This can to be tuned w.r.t the I/O pattern for typical application. Setting it to very higher number can guarantee faster transfers at the cost of higher memory footprint. Whereas setting it to very lower number can lead to slower speeds due to the lack of available buffers for transfers. We recommend it to be set 1024 for media applications, and 256 for non-media operations (basic file server, disk io apps)

SMB Direct buffer size: i. Default value: 1 ii. Per connection-based parameter. iii. Accepts value from 1 to 8 inclusive. This value is in Mega Bytes (for example 1 represents 1MB). iv. The server should be restarted to reflect the effect of a changed parameter. This parameter represents the size of transfer for every buffer descriptor from SMB Direct ring . As per the SMBD Specification this parameter should be less than or equal to the maximum fragmented receive size (1MB). However, this parameter could be less than or equal to the maximum transaction size (8MB) as well. The SMB client has 1MB value  that is the maximum fragmented receive size and hence it the recommended value.

TCP parameters i. For RDMA connections only, allocate minimal resources to TCP parameters since the TCP buffers will consume some amount of RAM ii. For example, preProcessorThreads = 4, ringBufferCount = 256 (or 128 or 64)
Mac Client Performance
With the help of following settings on the MAC client one can improve performance. Mac needs reboot after applying any of the changes.
Turn off packet signing by setting following in the /etc/nsmb.conf file. signing_required=no https://support.apple.com/en-gb/HT205926
Speed up browsing on network shares defaults write com.apple.desktopservices DSDontWriteNetworkStores -bool TRUE https://support.apple.com/en-in/HT208209
for more troubleshooting with MAC, refer to the troubleshooting section for MAC

Cache Cleanup

a. Truncates the file in local cache which is older than the “threshold” time mentioned in the s3.json. b. Scans the entire local cache in interval of “periodicity” time mentioned in the s3.json and marks the file
enableCacheCleanup

Specify if cache cleanup needs to be enabled or not. 
If set to "yes", cache cleanup will be executed.
If set to "no" , cache cleanup will not be executed.
threshold

If last accessed/modified time of the file is beyond the threshold value, then the files will be cleaned up from the cache. User can specify duration in minutes<m>/hours<H>/days<D>/months<M>. Default it set to 30 days. Configuring this value in days is recommended.
periodicity

Specify the duration of how frequently clean-up thread should run. User can specify duration in minutes<m>/hours<H>/days<D>/months<M>. Default it set to 1 day. Configuring this value in days is recommended.
enableFifoCleanup

Specify if FIFO cache cleanup needs to be enabled or not. FIFO cache cleanup will start cleanup of the LRU least recently used files in the cache.
The check will happen periodically at an interval provided in 'periodicity'
If set to "yes", cache cleanup with FIFO method will be executed.
If set to "no" , cache cleanup with FIFO method will not be executed.
lowerLimit
Specify The lower limit of the disk space in percentage. The cleanup of files activity will continue until the lower limit of 'cache storage' is reached.
upperLimit
Specify The upper limit of the disk space in percentage.  The cleanup of files activity will begin when the upper limit of 'cache storage' is used and when the threshold value is satisfied.

bucketName
Specify the name of the s3 bucket to be exported as share. This is a mandatory parameter.
sharePath

Share path to be exported. The “sharePath’’ is same as as “sharePath” parameter in mosmb.conf or mosmb.json file. The bucket in s3.json will be mapped to share in mosmb.json which has same sharePath as mentioned for bucket. sharePath must be unique for every bucket. This  is a mandatory parameter.

URL which is the entry point for the object store web service. This is optional for AWS, but mandatory for other object stores.
region
Specify the region of s3 bucket.
accessKeyId
Specify the Access ID of the s3 account.
secretKey
Specify the Secret Key of the s3 account.
multipartSize

Specify single part size in case of multipart upload (In bytes). Part size should be minimum 5 MB (5242880 Bytes). Part size can be maximum 5 GB (5368709120 Bytes). Maximum number of parts per upload is 10,000. If kept blank, default is 125 MB.
httpScheme
Mention HTTP scheme either https/http. By default it is set to https.
verifySsl
Specify if “yes” if ssl certificate needs to be checked. By default it is set to “yes”.
asyncThreads

Specify the number of async threads to be used for object store operations.
By default it is set to 16.
throttleBandwidthStrategy
Specify “yes” if bandwidth throttling is needed. By default it is set to “no”.

Specify “Write bandwidth” to be allocated for s3. Mention in KBps, MBps or in GBps. 
By default all the available bandwidth is used for s3 operations.

Specify “Read bandwidth” to be allocated for s3. Mention in KBps, MBps or in GBps. 
By default all the available bandwidth is used for s3 operations.

Installing Kerberos

The MoSMB server authenticates the AD Domain users using Kerberos authentication method. This requires a Kerberos ticket granted to the server by configured AD machine. This ticket generation can be done using the kinit commands.
On CentOS
Search and install the Kerberos package on CentOS:
Example:
Command 1 : yum search kerberos Output 1  : .... krb5-workstation.x86_64 : Kerberos 5 programs for use on workstations Command 2 : yum install krb5-workstation.x86_64
On Ubuntu
Search and install the Kerberos package on Ubuntu:
Example:
Command 1 : apt-cache search krb5 Output 1  : .... krb5-user - basic programs to authenticate using MIT Kerberos Command 2 : apt-get install krb5-user
krb5.conf
Make sure these entries are present in the /etc/krb5.conf file.
Include directory - /etc/krb5.conf.d
Modify libdefaults section - rdns, default_ccache_name entries are present refer - cache type limitations
Example:
includedir /etc/krb5.conf.d/ [libdefaults] .......... rdns = false default_ccache_name = FILE:/opt/mosmb/kerberos_tkt ..........
Create and open a new file /etc/krb5.conf.d/mosmb_krb5.conf
Add realms section - kdc and admin_server entries.
Add domain_realm section - domain name entry is present.
Example:
[realms] MOSMBAD.DOM = { kdc = mo-ad.mosmbad.dom master_kdc = mo-ad.mosmbad.dom } [domain_realm] mosmbad.dom = MOSMBAD.DOM .mosmbad.dom = MOSMBAD.DOM
Limitations for cache type
with kerberos 1.13 and above  MoSMB support cache types FILE, KCM, DIR. keyring is not supported.

# cat /etc/krb5.conf | grep default_ccache_name
       default_ccache_name = FILE:/opt/mosmb/mosmb_kerberos_ticket OR default_ccache_name = DIR:/opt/mosmb/kerberos_ticket_dir #default_ccache_name = KEYRING:persistent:%{uid}      [not supported] # cat /etc/krb5.conf.d/kcm_default_ccache | grep default_ccache_name
       default_ccache_name = KCM:
Notes :
MoSMB will support only cache type FILE for kerberos versions below 1.13
The FILE and DIR path can be of your choice. Make sure path is anything other than /tmp for persistence
mosmb.keytab
Standalone Server MoSMB Keytab generation
The MoSMB keytab location is /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab

Assumption is domain join is carried out successfully and there are no restricted entries for kerberos on configured AD. If restricted entries are present on AD side please go through Removing restricted Kerberos entries
Run the ktutil to Read and add existing host keys to /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab from krb5.keytab and then add required entries.
Run the ktutil commands as follows:

ktutil ktutil: rkt /etc/krb5.keytab
   ktutil: addent -password -p <domain user>@<DOMAIN NAME> -k 1 -e rc4-hmac Password for <domain user>@<domain name>: 
   
   ktutil: addent -password -p <domain user>@<DOMAIN NAME> -k 1 -e aes256-cts Password for <domain user>@<domain name>: 
   
   ktutil: wkt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab ktutil: q
Example:

ktutil ktutil: rkt /etc/krb5.keytab
   ktutil: addent -password -p mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM -k 1 -e rc4-hmac Password for mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM:

   ktutil: addent -password -p mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM -k 1 -e aes256-cts Password for mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM: 
   
   ktutil: wkt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab ktutil: q
Notes :
The domain user used in above commands and the user used to generate the ticket (as explained in next few steps using kinit) should be the same.
The domain name in above commands must be in uppercase.
If and when the user password or user spn is changed Kerberos keytab related settings are needed to carry out again
MoSMB Cluster Keytab generation
Refer to keytab generation steps in section Cluster Keytab Generation
Removing restricted Kerberos entries

When there are restricted Kerberos entries on the server machine seen as output of command klist -k /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab Check if they are also present on the AD side if present we need to remove them from AD,
Login to AD machine with user who has admin rights and open Active directory administrative Center
Open computers section
In computers open properties of the MoSMB server machine
In Properties of server machine  → Extensions → Attribute Editor
In Attribute editor select the attribute "servicePrincipalName"
Remove restricted entries from the servicePrincipalName against the MoSMB server.
Example -

restrictedkrbhost/moserver1.mosmbad.dom
   restrictedkrbhost/MOSERVER1
Note :
After making changes on AD machine destroy the previously taken Kerberos tickets if any from server machine with kdestroy -A and generate new ticket.
Generating a Kerberos Ticket (Releases before March 2022)
To generate a ticket on the server use the kinit -V <AD_Domain_username>@<DOMAIN NAME> command. The command asks password for the specified user.
Example:
Test Data : mosmbad.dom, mouser (domain user) Command   : sudo kinit -V mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM Output    : Password for mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM:

OR To generate a ticket on the server without having to use the password, use command kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab <AD_Domain_username>@<DOMAIN NAME> . -k Specifies to obtain the key for the ticket principal from a key table. So you are not prompted to enter the password for the ticket principal, and -t will refer to the keytab file specified
Example:
Test Data : mosmbad.dom, mouser (domain user) Command   : sudo kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM
Note :
The domain name in above commands must be in UPPERCASE to avoid KDC mismatch issues while getting credentials.
Also check the ticket time with klist -a, (time of MoSMB server and AD should be always synced or Kerberos authentication may fail)
Generating Kerberos Ticket (Releases after March 2022)

In new releases, user will NOT be needed to acquire a ticket manually. /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt will now use SPN entries instead of domain names. Entries will be in the format of user@DOMAIN / cifs/user.domain@DOMAIN for standalone server, similar to the SPNs and will be case sensitive . To generate a ticket on the server, MoSMB will use principal name from domain_list.txt, match against the one present in mosmb.keytab and use it to generate the kerberos ticket.
Example for STANDALONE Server

# cat /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM # klist -k /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab name: FILE:/etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
   KVNO Principal---- --------------------------------------------
    .
   2 mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM
   2 mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM
Example for CLUSTER
# cat /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt cifs/my-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM

# klist -k /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab name: FILE:/etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
    KVNO Principal---- -------------------------------------------- 
   3 cifs/my-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM 
   3 cifs/my-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM 
   3 cifs/my-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM 
   3 cifs/my-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM 
   3 cifs/my-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM
Verifying a Kerberos Ticket
To verify if the ticket is acquired for the domain_user, run the klist command.
Example:

Command : sudo klist -A Output  : Ticket cache: FILE:/opt/mosmb/kerberos_tkt Default principal: mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM Valid starting       Expires              Service principal 01/16/2020 11:33:50  01/16/2020 21:33:50  krbtgt/MOSMBAD.DOM@MOSMBAD.DOM renew until 01/17/2020 11:33:45
Note :
By default, the Kerberos ticket is valid for 10 hours .
The Kerberos ticket defaults may be modified by the Group Policy Management → Kerberos Policy → Security Settings in the AD.
Muti-Domain kerberos configuration
Carry out following changes in /etc/krb5.conf and /etc/krb5.conf.d/mosmb_krb5.conf

Set default cache path to a directory location other than /tmp directory. DIR / KCM cache type is used in case of the multiple Kerberos tickets. default_ccache_name = DIR:<directory path> OR default_ccache_name = KCM: in /etc/krb5.conf
Example :
[libdefaults] #default_ccache_name = KEYRING:persistent:%{uid} default_ccache_name = DIR:/opt/mosmb/Kerberos_tickets default_realm = MOSMBAD1.DOM
Mention default realm as the domain to which server machine is joined in file /etc/krb5.conf.d/mosmb_krb5.conf
In the realms section mention all the realms in file /etc/krb5.conf.d/mosmb_krb5.conf
Example :
[realms] MOSMBAD1.DOM = { kdc = mo-ad1.mosmbad1.dom admin_server = mo-ad1.mosmbad1.dom } MOSMBAD2.DOM = { kdc = mo-ad2.mosmbad2.dom admin_server = mo-ad2.mosmbad2.dom }

Note: In case of common global name space across sites. 1. Add only local keytab on the server side. 2. For sssd configuration to work MoSMB servers must be part of all domains. 3. Generation of only local Kerberos ticket would be sufficient. (instead of DIR cache path in /etc/krb5.conf mention FILE path and file name)
LDAP Client Configurations
To enable the  ACL view on the client side you are required to perform the following operations, in case of Kerberos authentication:
A. Add a BASE entry in the LDAP configuration file /etc/openldap/ldap.conf (centOS) or /etc/ldap/ldap.conf (ubuntu/debian) . The format should be as follows:
BASE   dc=example,dc=com
Example:
BASE    dc=mosmbad,dc=dom
B. Add the entry for the LDAP URI OR HOST to the /etc/openldap/ldap.conf (centOS) or /etc/ldap/ldap.conf (ubuntu/debian) file. The format should be as follows:

URI    ldap://<FQDN_OF_ACTIVE_DIRECTORY_SERVER>
   OR
   HOST    <FQDN_OF_ACTIVE_DIRECTORY_SERVER>
Example :
URI    ldap://mosmb-winad.mosmbad.dom OR HOST    mosmb-winad.mosmbad.dom
Note :
When MoSMB server is joined to Multiple domains, make sure all domain entries are present in the LDAP related configuration, similar to above examples.

sssd helps during the User ID mapping for domain users.

If in case sssd is not installed before, install sssd-common and configure sssd on the server machine.
Create a sssd configuration file /etc/sssd/conf.d/mosmb_sssd.conf and it should contain parameters as shown in image below.
sssd configuration file location must be at /etc/sssd/sssd.conf with permissions of file set to 600 (-rw-------) and same with /etc/sssd/conf.d/mosmb_sssd.conf
Start sssd service with service sssd start .
Note :
Restart the sssd service after making the sssd configurations as in the above image.
When MoSMB server is joined to Multiple domains, make sure all domain entries are present in the sssd related configuration, similar to above example.
Make entries of all domains MoSMB server is joined to as explained below.
domain_list.txt Configuration for sssd

For sssd related settings simply create a file named domain_list.txt at the /etc/mosmb/ location and mention the domain to which the MoSMB server is joined. Ensure that you make an entry for each domain on a new line.

For sssd related settings simply create a file named /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt will now use SPN entries instead of just domain names. Entries should be in the format of user@DOMAIN, similar to the SPNs and will be case sensitive.
Example: #cat /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt mosuer@MOSMBAD.DOM
In new releases, /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt will now use SPN entries instead of domain names. Entries should be in the format of user@DOMAIN, similar to the SPNs and will be case sensitive.
Example: #cat /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt cifs/my-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM
nsswitch.conf Configuration

group:      files sss

Configuration Data Verification and Collection

The mosmb_config_check.sh script is used to collect or verify data for easier debugging on any issues faced due to MoSMB configuration. mosmb_config_check.sh is located in /etc/mosmb/scripts directory and it support following tasks -
Check the memory usage and open handles by MoSMB
Check the required MoSMB configuration
Collection of configuration files and MoSMB logs
Note : Run this script with sudo privileges
Prerequisites
Command-line JSON processor : jq package
JSON Lint for PHP : php-jsonlint package
MoSMB service should be running
Usage

Command: sh /etc/mosmb/mosmb_config_check.sh -h Options :
           -s creates a summary of memory usage and lists handles opened by process
           -v verifies if the configuration is correct.
           -c collects configuration and log files in a compressed format.
           (run command 'sudo yum install jq' if using -c for the first time)
   
       Usage : ./mosmb_config_check.sh [-s] [-v] [-c]
           -s :
              Syntax:
              ./mosmb_config_check.sh -s
           -v :
              Syntax:
              ./mosmb_config_check.sh -v <domain name> <ip of the domain>
           -c :
              Syntax:
                For Basic Collect:
                ./mosmb_config_check.sh -c
                For collect with ldap servername, domain username and components:
                ./mosmb_config_check.sh -c <ldap_server_name> <domain_user_name> <domain_components>

Please note that the performance tuning recommendations provided in this guide are general guidelines. Optimal configuration for your environment may differ depending on your workload, hardware, and other factors. To achieve the best results, it's recommended that you conduct performance tests on your specific configuration and make adjustments as necessary.
To tune for best performance at a node level refer Performance tuning for Standalone MoSMB Server
MoSMB Cluster Performance Tuning
MoSMB cluster performance tuning is mainly dependent on the following :
Pstore path → A dedicated shared volume is recommended for pstore path. This must be configured for low latency.
MoSMB ZooKeeper's dataDir (data directory) path → A dedicated device is recommended for the MoSMB ZooKeeper dataDir path on each node in the Zookeeper cluster. This could be a low latency device.
In /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json of each node in MoSMB cluster for ZooKeeper's configurations one should mention the hostPorts in a way so that then ZooKeeper node closest to MoSMB should be mentioned first.

MoSMB ZooKeeper config parameters → MoSMB provides a mosmb_zoo.cfg file at location /etc/mosmb/ on MoSMB installation parameters within this file are for reference and must not be modified. Only recommended parameters must be configured mentioned in documentation.

MoSMB: The Advance SMB3 Server
We present to you the MoSMB SMB with Mojo!
You can refer this Wiki for complete information regarding MoSMB.
Features and Benefits
MoSMB offers you the following features and benefits:
MoSMB is built as an SMB2/SMB3 user mode server stack designed to run crucial enterprise workloads such as Enterprise File Server, Microsoft Hyper-V and SQL Server.
MoSMB is built to be feature-rich, fully compliant, light-weight, low footprint and proprietary license bearing.
MoSMB supports distributed file systems like Lustre FS, Ceph FS, MapR FS and Gluster FS.

MoSMB can be deployed in the Active-Passive or Active-Active Scale-Out Cluster mode over these file-system clients to provide High Availability (HA) and Transparent Failover of connections enabling users to support their Service-Level Agreements (SLAs).
Purpose
This Wiki provides information on the product - MoSMB, its features, usage and advanced configuration to support various use cases .
So in essence it explains in detail:
The storage and file system supported by the Ryussi® Technologies’ MoSMB Server.

Add NTLMSSP support
Machine Password
Prerequisite: Machine should be joined to domain. adcli package must be installed on the server machine. For more detailed information see: Join the MoSMB server to the Active Directory Domain
To join the domain with adcli:
adcli join --service-name=host -D <AD_domain_name> -U <AD_domain_administrator_user> --host-fqdn=<server_hostname>.<AD_domain_name>
Note: Ignore this step if machine is already joined to AD

To generate a new machine account password; run mosmb_passwd command. This will generate machine password for all domains to which the node is joined to, and are mentioned in the /etc/mosmb.domain_list.txt
mosmb_passwd -m
To generate a new machine account password for a specific domain, in case of multidomain setup, run following command
mosmb_passwd -m <domain_name>

Note: -On New installation of MoSMB, one needs to run mosmb_passwd -m, any subsequent upgrades will not require the command to be run.   
-Also when this command is run, if mosmb is already running you need not restart mosmb service for changes to take effect
-In case one removes the host from domain and rejoins it to domain, this needs to be run again too, so that the machine password is renewed via mosmb
-If the server machines have openssl version 3 and above, configuration changes are required in /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf
mosmb_sssd.conf / sssd.conf whichever is used, make changes
Since MoSMB is updating machine password, add the following to /etc/sssd/confd/mosmb_sssd.conf to disable sssd from updating machine password:
ad_maximum_machine_account_password_age = 0
The location of the keytab to use when validating credentials obtained from KDCs no longer needs to be /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
The parameter if present in mosmb_sssd.conf / sssd.conf; should be removed .
Remove krb5_keytab = /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
Service Restarts
restart sssd service after above changes to sssd.conf
restart mosmb service after Upgrade
The NTLMSSP authentication

If krb5 authentication fails, MoSMB will try and authenticate using NTLMSSP authentication. Also Instead of FQDN if share is accessed with machine IP, NTLMSSP authentication will be attempted.
This behavior is largely client driven.
Cron task to auto update machine password

A cron script will be added to /etc/cron.monthly during installation to call mosmb_passwd -m once a month to regenerate machine password.
Any errors seen during the cron update will add messages into dmesg with the message starting with error: mosmb_passwd.
User Mapping There are three schemes that can be adopted for user mapping to access the share.
