Scheme 1: User mapping for POSIX acl and Kerberos Auth mode with ID mapping done by SSSD
SSSD provides the following two integration options if you are using POSIX ACL, and Auth Mode is krb5
Let SSSD generate and assign uid gid for AD user

SSSD can use the SID of an AD user to algorithmically generate POSIX IDs in a process called ID mapping. ID mapping creates a map between SIDs in AD and IDs on Linux. Because the IDs for an AD user are generated in a consistent way from the same SID, the user has the same UID and GID when logging in to any Linux system.

The ID-mapping feature allows SSSD to act as a client of Active Directory without requiring administrators to extend user attributes to support POSIX attributes for user and group identifiers. Make sure the sssd.conf has
ldap_id_mapping = True
NOTE : When ID-mapping is enabled, the uidNumber and gidNumber attributes are ignored. And sssd synthesizes the uid gid for the user.

For domain user mouser , we can assign ownership to share for access # id mouser@mosmbad.dom
uid=1795001104(mouser@mosmbad.dom) gid=1795000513(domain users@mosmbad.dom) #set owndereship to the share path for the AD user, mouser@mosmbad.dom
chown mouser@mosmbad.dom:"Domain users@mosmbad.dom" /opt/share #Login to windows client using domain user mouser and access the share
Scheme 2: Use POSIX attributes defined in AD for mapping of UID/GID attributes of linux user.
User mapping for POSIX acl and Kerberos Auth mode done using POSIX attributes defined in AD

Administrator will map UIDs and GIDs of the linux user to the AD user. AD can create and store POSIX attributes, such as uidNumber, gidNumber, unixHomeDirectory, or loginShell. When using this scheme, to keep the AD-defined values, you must disable ID mapping in SSSD.
default_shell = /bin/bash
ldap_id_mapping = False
e.g.

cat /etc/sssd/sssd.conf [sssd]
domains = mosmbad.dom
config_file_version = 2
services = nss, pam

[domain/mosmbad.dom]
ad_domain = mosmbad.dom
krb5_realm = MOSMBAD.DOM
realmd_tags = manages-system joined-with-samba
cache_credentials = True
id_provider = ad
krb5_store_password_if_offline = True default_shell = /bin/bash ldap_id_mapping = False use_fully_qualified_names = True
fallback_homedir = /home/%u@%d
access_provider = ad

Go to active directory users and computers -> view : enable advance features -> user properties -> attribute editor. In the attribute editor need to set the following parameters as per mapping requirement matching to local linux user's properties.
loginShell,
uidNumber,
gidNumber,
unixHomeDirectory

Example: 
For AD user test_user1 we are assigning properties of local linux user linuxuser1 id linuxuser1 uid=1008(linuxuser1) gid=1008(linuxuser1) groups=1008(linuxuser1),27(sudo)
Before uidNumber and gidNumber mapping changes, this is what we see on linux system

id test_user1@mosmbad.dom 
   uid=191401645(test_user1@mosmbad.dom) gid=191400513(domain users@mosmbad.dom) groups=191400513(domain users@mosmbad.dom)
From AD you can check attributes of the user as follows on AD machine from powershell

PS C:\Windows\system32> Get-ADUser "test_user1" -Properties * | select Name, SID, ServicePrincipalNames, UserPrincipalName, SamAccountName, PrimaryGroup, loginShell, uidNumber, gidNumber, unixHomeDirectory Name                  : test user1
SID                      : S-1-5-21-3024565688-5515897-983784829-1645
ServicePrincipalNames : {}
UserPrincipalName       : test_user1@mosmbad.dom
SamAccountName        : test_user1
PrimaryGroup                : CN=Domain Users,CN=Users,DC=ryussicloud,DC=dom
loginShell                       : /bin/bash
uidNumber                     : 1008
gidNumber                     : 1008
unixHomeDirectory        : /home/linuxuser1/

# service sssd stop 
# rm -rf /var/lib/sss/db/*
# service sssd start
After uidNumber and gidNumber mapping changes and restarting sssd, this is what we see on linux system

# id test_user1@mosmbad.dom 
uid=1008(linuxuser1) gid=1008(linuxuser1) groups=1008(linuxuser1),27(sudo)
MoSMB share permissions can be set to local linux users as follows e.g. exported share: share1 = /mnt/share1

# ls -lah /mnt/share1/
total 12K
drwxr-xr-x 3 linuxuser1 testgroup 4.0K Jul  9 09:25 .
drwxr-xr-x 9 root       root       4.0K Jul  9 05:41 ..
drwxr-xr-x 2 linuxuser1 testgroup 4.0K Jul  9 06:04 new

Here, user linuxuser1 is owner of share and group level permissions are assigned to all users mapped with group- testgroup
So when a domain user test_user1 accesses the share, he is mapped to the user linuxuser1
Note : With these changes to sssd, properties on windows smb client side will not show appropriate user details in share/file properties.
Scheme 3: User mapping for POSIX ACL and NTLM Local Auth mode

If you are using POSIX ACL, and Auth Mode is NTLMLocal : In this case the linux user can be mapped to windows user, by having the same username. Add the User as SMB user using mosmb_passwd. The smb password for the user will be picked up for granting access. So if smb password matches the windows password of the user, then the user will get access to the share seamlessly.

e.g. If user mouser exists in Windows machine (either Local user or AD user), and same mouser is added to linux. useradd mouser
passwd mouser
mosmb_passwd -a mouser
(set SMB password for the user same as windows user password) Change owndership of share path to mouser chown mouser:mouser <path to share> Login to windows machine with mouser , and access the share. This will grant mouser access to the share seamlessly.
Setup and configure MoSMB server (Standalone mode) in the Kerberos mode for any server Operating System, following steps need to be performed:
Note : Kindly refer to Network Configuration to perform the steps underneath.

Use your Active Directory (AD) Administrator user account to integrate the MoSMB server with Active Directory. Note : The Active Directory user should be created in the very same domain in which the MoSMB Server Computer Account will be created.
Join the MoSMB server to the Active Directory domain.
Remove restricted kerberos keys, if any, from the Active Directory Manager from the domain joined machine.
Configure the MoSMB server as a DNS Client. Add a MoSMB server DNS Client entry to the DNS server if not auto-registered.
Configure NTP so that the machine with MoSMB is in time sync with the Windows Active Directory server. Note : The maximum time difference allowed is 5 minutes as per Kerberos documentation.
Configure the Kerberos settings by obtaining the Kerberos ticket from the Ticket Granting server (AD-KDC Server).
Ensure that you have mentioned the correct storage path in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json in Share Configuration .
Start / Restart the MoSMB server for the desired changes to take effect.

Note : In case the user mapping of the Windows AD domain user to the Linux user is required in certain IT infrastructures with a mix of Windows and Linux machines, the “sssd” service and its sssd.conf file can be configured appropriately. MoSMB can take advantage of this user mapping configuration to provide user access control.
MoSMB supports Microsoft Active Directory (AD) based Kerberos authentication. The MoSMB server is added to the AD Domain to authenticate and authorize the SMB Client's access to MoSMB shares.
Joining MoSMB Server to Active Directory
You must have a working models of the AD in your Evaluation and/or Production environment before you start using the appliance with kerberos authentication.
You require Active Directory Domain Administrator user to make related modifications.
Use adcli to join the MoSMB server to multiple AD Domains.
ADCLI join
Install the realm and dependent packages on the server machine. Command : yum install adcli OR apt-get install adcli

To join the domain with adcli: Command : adcli join --service-name=host -D <AD_domain_name> -U <AD_domain_administrator_user> --host-fqdn= <server_hostname>.<AD_domain_name> Example : adcli join --service-name=host -D MOSMBAD.DOM -U user1@MOSMBAD.DOM --host-fqdn=mymosmbserver.mosmbad.dom Password for user1@MOSMBAD.DOM: Note: The domain join creates a machine entry in the AD. You must update the DNS entries for forward lookup zone and reverse lookup zone manually.

To leave a domain with adcli: Command : adcli delete-computer --domain= <AD_domain_name> -U <AD_domain_administrator_user> Note: This deletes the entry from AD. You must update the DNS entries for forward lookup zone and reverse lookup zone manually.

Generating Kerberos Ticket
Generating Kerberos Ticket (Releases after March 2022)

In new releases, user will NOT be needed to acquire a ticket manually. To generate a ticket on the server, MoSMB will use principal name from /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt , match against the one present in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab and use it to generate the kerberos ticket on MoSMB service start.
Generating a Kerberos Ticket (Releases before March 2022)
To generate a ticket on the server run command kinit -V <AD_Domain_username>@<DOMAIN NAME> . The command asks password for the specified user.
Example:
Test Data : mosmbad.dom, mouser (domain user) Command   : sudo kinit -V mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM Output    : Password for mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM:

OR To generate a ticket on the server without having to use the password, use command kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab <AD_Domain_username>@<DOMAIN NAME> . -k Specifies to obtain the key for the ticket principal from a key table. So you are not prompted to enter the password for the ticket principal. -t will refer to the keytab file specified. For MoSMB it should be /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
Example:
Test Data : mosmbad.dom, mouser (domain user entry present mosmb.keytab) Command   : sudo kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM
Notes :

1. The domain name in above commands must be in UPPERCASE to avoid KDC mismatch issues while getting credentials.
   2. Check the ticket time with klist -a, (time of MoSMB server and AD should be always synced or Kerberos authentication may fail)
Verifying a Kerberos Ticket
To verify if the ticket is acquired for the domain_user, run the klist command.
Example:

Command : sudo klist -A Output  : Ticket cache: FILE:/opt/mosmb/kerberos_tkt Default principal: mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM Valid starting       Expires              Service principal 01/16/2020 11:33:50  01/16/2020 21:33:50  krbtgt/MOSMBAD.DOM@MOSMBAD.DOM renew until 01/17/2020 11:33:45
Notes :

1. By default, the Kerberos ticket is valid for 10 hours . 
   2. The Kerberos ticket defaults may be modified by the Group Policy Management → Kerberos Policy → Security Settings in the AD.
   3. MoSMB releases after March 22 - Tickets will be auto renewed on every MoSMB start/restart.
   4. On releases after March 22 - ldap ticket will also be taken at MoSMB start/restart if ldap configurations are correct.

MoSMB Integration with Ezmeral Data Fabric Managed Control System

The MoSMB services and metrics can also be integrated into the HPE Ezmeral Data Fabric to enable simplified management and data insights for the services. All the MoSMB services can be stopped, started or restarted at the same time or individually using the HPE Ezmeral Data Fabric Control System. 
The metrics for the MoSMB services could also be viewed using Grafana, which can be launched from the HPE Ezmeral Data Fabric Control System.

Integration for management of the MoSMB services from MCS via warden. Detailed steps that were performed on all HPE Ezmeral Data Fabric nodes to integrate the MoSMB services into HPE Ezmeral Data Fabric are listed below.
I. Add the following entry to the end of /etc/sudoers:

# cat /etc/sudoers  
mapr    ALL=(ALL)       NOPASSWD: ALL
II. Create roles in /opt/mapr/roles for mosmb and mosmb-zookeeper:

# cd /opt/mapr/roles/
# touch mosmb
# touch mosmb-zookeeper
# ls 
apiserver  fileserver  grafana        hbase      mastgateway  s3server 
cldb       fluentd     hadoop-client  hbaserest  mosmb        zookeeper 
collectd   gateway     hadoop-util    kibana     mosmb-zookeeper

III. Create scripts for the mosmb and mosmb-zookeeper services in /opt/mapr/initscripts/mosmb.service and /opt/mapr/initscripts/mosmb-zookeeper.service ( NOTE : plz refer to the link provided for sample scripts at the bottom of this page)
IV.	Make the scripts executable

# chmod +x /opt/mapr/initscripts/mosmb*
# ls -la /opt/mapr/initscripts/mosmb*
-rwxr-xr-x  1 root root   526 Aug 18 10:06 mosmb.service 
-rwxr-xr-x  1 root root   828 Aug 18 10:10 mosmb-zookeeper.service

V. Create Warden scripts for the MoSMB services in /opt/mapr/conf/conf.d/warden.mosmb.conf and /opt/mapr/conf/conf.d/warden.mosmb-zookeeper.conf ( NOTE : plz refer to the link provided for sample conf files at the bottom of this page)
VI. Change the owner and group for these mosmb warden conf files

# chown mapr /opt/mapr/conf/conf.d/warden.mosmb.conf 
# chgrp mapr /opt/mapr/conf/conf.d/warden.mosmb.conf 
# chown mapr /opt/mapr/conf/conf.d/warden.mosmb-zookeeper.conf 
# chgrp mapr /opt/mapr/conf/conf.d/warden.mosmb-zookeeper.conf
#ls -la /opt/mapr/conf/conf.d/warden.mosmb*
-rw-r--r-- 1 mapr mapr  614 Aug 18 10:19 warden.mosmb.conf 
-rw-r--r-- 1 mapr mapr  563 Aug 18 10:18 warden.mosmb-zookeeper.conf
VII. change the following parameter in /opt/mapr/conf/warden.conf on all nodes (NOTE: not needed for MoSMB Release 088 onwards)
services.retries = 5
VIII. The above change will require a Reconfigure of Warden for all hosts
Starting the mosmb services from MCS
With the MoSMB configured and all MoSMB servers forming a single domain cluster, all the services are to be started in the following order:
HPE Ezmeral Data Fabric Fused-based POSIX client
Apache ZooKeeper server cluster for MoSMB on all zookeeper nodes
MoSMB servers on all nodes

Following is a summary of how to start each service: The HPE Ezmeral Data Fabric Fuse-based POSIX client service was started first to mount the HPE Ezmeral Data Fabric to the directory that was previously created for that purpose. That directory/path is defined in opt/mapr/conf/fuse.conf
The shares pointing to EDF volumes created for sharing over SMB, need to be configured in mosmb.json (/etc/mosmb/mosmb.json)
I. To start the HPE Ezmeral Data Fabric Fuse-based POSIX client on all MoSMB servers, this command was executed on each server:
# service mapr-posix-client-platinum start
II. To verify the service successfully started and remained running, execute this command:
# service mapr-posix-client-platinum status

III. Next, the Apache ZooKeeper for MoSMB service ( mosmb-zookeeper ) needs to be started on all MoSMB servers from MCS (Managed Control System) dashboard. The MoSMB servers are clients of the Apache ZooKeeper servers so before the MoSMB service was started, the mosmb-zookeeper service must be started from MCS (Managed Control System) .
IV. To verify that the service was successfully started and remained running, this command was executed:
# /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh status
V. If the Apache ZooKeeper service fails to start, view the log files in the path where ZooKeeper was installed. E.g.
/opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/logs/.
Make sure to verify the parameters/zookeeper cluster configuration in the
/opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/conf/zoo.cfg  file.
VI. Finally, with all required services running, the mosmb service needs to be started from MCS (Managed Control System) .
VII. Check the mosmb service status from the mosmb cluster nodes
# service mosmb status
VIII. you can also use maprcli as alternative to MCS to start/stop mosmb and mosmb-zookeeper services(any user account with stop-start privileges can run this) on CLI:

e.g.
Strat MoSMB-ZK :
$ maprcli node services -name mosmb-zookeeper -action start –nodes <comma separated nodes with FQDN>
Start mosmb :
$ maprcli node services -name mosmb -action start –nodes <comma separated nodes with FQDN>

IX. If the MoSMB service fails to start, view the log file in /var/log/mosmb.log (This is the default log file path, which can be changed in the mosmb.json configuration file from /etc/mosmb folder). In case there is issue with the json formatting and mosmb gives error while starting, the logs for the errors will be available in /var/log/mosmb.log (that is the default log path)
X. Check MoSMB process When mosmb service is started you can check the service status and pid using

# service mosmb status
# pidof mosmb

cmdpipe
cmdpipe is a helper script located in /etc/mosmb/scripts directory and it support following tasks -
Reload the MoSMB configuration file - /etc/mosmb/mosm.json after adding new share
Update MoSMB logging at runtime
Notifies SMB Command frequency count
Display the MoSMB server statistics
Prerequisites
Python and pip of same version
Command-line JSON processor : jq package
MoSMB service should be running
Usage

Command: cd /etc/mosmb/scripts/ Command: ./cmdpipe -h Usage: ./cmdpipe <command>
       command:
       config_reload_add : Reloads mosmb config to add new share
       add_runtime_logging : Notifies mosmb of the changes in /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf
       smb_frequency_count : Notifies smb command frequency count in  <log folder from mosmb.json>/mosmb_smb_frequency_cnt.log file
       set_maintenance_mode : Set the MoSMB server in Maintenance Mode. Note: Available from Release 069 get_mosmb_config : Get the configuration parameters of MoSMB server. Note: Available from Release 087 statson : Starts collecting statistics
       domainlist_reload : Reloads the domainlist.txt contents in mosmb Note: Available from Release 100

Use the following options for mosmb config reload
       -n : Specifies share name
       -t : Specifies share type
       -h : Display this help message and exit
       Share type can be one if these two : VFS or S3
       Following is an Example:
       ./cmdpipe config_reload_add -n share_name -t vfs
       OR
       ./cmdpipe config_reload_add -n share_name -t S3
           
       Use the following options for dumping stats into csv periodically
       -p : Specifies csv path. Default is /var/log/
       -i : Specifies time period in seconds. This is the repeat interval for the dump job. Default is 300
       -c : Display stats on 'console'
       -h : Shows statson help message and Exit
       -n : Number count for stats operation
       Following is an Example:
       ./cmdpipe statson -p /var/log/ -i 600
       ./cmdpipe statson -p /var/log/ -i 600 -c : Display the stats on console after every '-i' interval
       ./cmdpipe statson -p /var/log/ -i 600 -c -n 5: Display the stats on console after every '-i' interval for '-n' number of time
Examples
config_reload_add
Prerequisites:
MoSMB should be running.
New share should be added to MoSMB configurations /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json either manually or by mosmbcli set --json command for share option
Usage:

Command: /etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe config_reload_add -h Use the following options for mosmb config reload
       -n : Specifies share name
       -t : Specifies share type
       -h : Display this help message and exit
       Share type can be one if these two : VFS or S3
       Following is an Example:
       ./cmdpipe config_reload_add -n share_name -t vfs
       OR
       ./cmdpipe config_reload_add -n share_name -t S3
Example:

Command: ./cmdpipe config_reload_add -n "Local xfs" -t vfs Success
       Command executed successfully
add_runtime_logging
Prerequisites:
MoSMB should be running.
Logging level changes will take effect for mosmb logs as per changes in /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf
Usage:

Command: /etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe add_runtime_logging -h 1) First make the required log level changes in  /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf
       2) Now run the command /etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe add_runtime_logging
Example:

Command: ./cmdpipe add_runtime_logging Logging Reload Command initialized
       Success
       Command executed successfully
smb_frequency_count
Prerequisites:
MoSMB should be running. Note: File mosmb_smb_frequency_cnt.log will be created at the MoSMB logs directory mentioned in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json.
Usage:
Command: ./cmdpipe smb_frequency_count -h Simply run the command /etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe smb_frequency_count
Example:

Command: ./cmdpipe smb_frequency_count Getting frequency count of smb commands
       Success
       Command executed successfully
set_maintenance_mode
Prerequisites:
MoSMB should be running to enable or disable maintenance mode. Note: New option available only from MoSMB release 069 onwards.
Usage:

Command: /etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe set_maintenance_mode -h Usage options for mosmb set maintenance mode -o : Specifies option to enable or disable the maintenance mode
       -h : Display this help message and exit
Example:

Command: ./cmdpipe set_maintenance_mode -o enable Success
       Command executed successfully
       Also mosmb.log will show the notification message as follows - [MO_CRITICAL]:[SMBCORE]:[TID 42496]: MoSMB server has entered into Maintenance Mode. No new connections/sessions will be accepted now.
get_mosmb_config
Prerequisites:
MoSMB should be running to get the current static and dynamic configurations. Note: New option available only from MoSMB release 087 onwards.
Usage:

Command: ./cmdpipe get_mosmb_config -p <path> -f humanreadable OR ./cmdpipe get_mosmb_config -p <path> -f csv Usage options for mosmb get configuration parameters -p : Specify path where the configuration parameters will be dumped. Default is path pointed out by logsPath parameter in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json
       -f : Specify format in which the configuration parameters will be dumped. Default is 'humanreadable'
       -h : Display this help message and exit
Example:

Command: ./cmdpipe get_mosmb_config -f csv Sending get server config params command to the MoSMB server.
     Command executed successfully
     Dump of the configuration parameters is at /var/log/ -- csv file named mosmb_config_params_dump.csv is created.
statson
Prerequisites:

MoSMB should be running. Note: Following files will be created when -p option is given depending on the build type 1. preprocessor_stats.csv 2. server_stats.csv 3. worker_thread_stats.csv 4. smbdirect_stats.csv 5. processed_stats.csv
Usage:

Command: ./cmdpipe statson -h Usage: cmdpipe statson [OPTION]...
       MOSMB server statistics
       Optional Arguments to statson
               -p, --path       Specifies csv path. Default is /var/log/
               -i, --interval   Specifies time period in seconds. This is the repeat interval for the dump job. Default is 300
               -c, --console    Display stats on 'console'
               -n, --number     Number count for stats operation
               -h, --help       Shows this help message and Exit
       
       Following Abbreviations used while showing output on console using '-c' option
       
       RIO(Read IOPS), WIO(Write IOPS), TIOPs(Total IOPS), RT(Read Throughput), WT(Write Throughput),
       TT(MB/s)(Total Throughput MB/s), RL(Read Latency), WL(Write Latency), SRL(Storage Read Latency),
       SWL(Storage Write Latency), FO(Total File/Pipe Opened), ES(Total Established Sessions),
       DC(Total Disconnects), PV(Password Violations), PE(Total Permission Error)
Example: Following is an Example:

Support Added for .prom file format from version 093 onwards MoSMB version 093 onwards cmdpipe supports .prom file format to collect metric in the Prometheus text exposition format. (key value pair) More details on how to use Prometheus to monitor mosmb metrics is available HERE

Use the following options for dumping stats into csv/prom periodically
  -p : Specifies stats path. Default path for 'csv' format is /var/log/. Note: While using 'prom' stats format, it is mandatory to use same path for both i.e. 'cmdpipe' and 'node_exporter' ('--collector.textfile.directory' option) of prometheus. -f : Specifies format in which the stats will be dumped (prom / csv) . Default is 'csv' format
  -i : Specifies time period in seconds. This is the repeat interval for the dump job. Default is 300
  -c : Displays stats on 'console'
  -h : Shows statson help message and Exit
  -n : Number count for stats operation
   
Following is an Example: ./cmdpipe statson -p /var/lib/node_exporter -f prom -i 600 -c -n 5 i.e. Display the stats on console after every '-i' interval for '-n' number of time, save stats in processed_stats.prom file in folder /var/lib/node_exporter

Sample processed_stats.prom file MoSMB_BytesSent 520115452
   MoSMB_BytesReceived 577892654
   MoSMB_Throughput_MBps 45.53
   MoSMB_NoOfReadRequests 123
   MoSMB_NoOfWriteRequests 126
   MoSMB_IOPS 10
   MoSMB_ReadLatency_msec 76.01
   MoSMB_WriteLatency_msec 382.00
   MoSMB_StorageReadLatency_msec 71.04
   MoSMB_StorageWriteLatency_msec 378.59
   MoSMB_TotalFileOrPipeOpens 5
   MoSMB_TotalActiveSessions 1
   MoSMB_Disconnects 0
   MoSMB_TotalPasswordViolations 0
   MoSMB_TotalPermissionErrors 0
domainlist_reload
Prerequisites:

MoSMB should be running to reload the domain's list from domain_list.txt And it will also check if the domain has a whitelist present, if yes, it will use the IPs mentioned in the whitelist of the specific domain to reconnect to AD Note: New option available only from MoSMB release 100 onwards.
Usage:

MoSMB Service Not Starting

If mosmb service fails to start, check the mosmb.log from the log folder (Default is /var/log) for Error messages. 
In case there is json syntax error you can use the mosmb json syntax validation script
Configuration Errors
The mosmb.log should indicate if any configuration errors pertaining to the json parameters are encountered. Accordingly use following to correct thoes.
Verify if the MoSMB share path mentioned in the configuration actually exists on the server.
Verify if the persistent store path mentioned in the MoSMB configuration actually exists on the server.

Verify if a valid combination of Authentication Modes and Authorization Modes is in use: i. When the authentication mode is krb5 , the permitted authorization modes are posix and ntacl . ii. When the authentication is ntlmLocal , the permitted authorization mode is posix .
Verify if the plugin path mentioned in the MoSMB configuration is correct.
Verify that the performance parameters like number of threads, buffer size and pre-processor threads are not exceeding the available resources. Refer to Performance Tuning
Verify if the bind interface value mentioned is actually an active interface on the server.
Refer to the MoSMB configuration parameters for further details.
Verify the required ports are open
Refer to Enable ports in firewall for MoSMB
License error in logs
For any Licensing related errors refer to section MoSMB Licensing Issues below
MoSMB Licensing Issues

To start the MoSMB server you require a trial license or a product key. Note : If the MoSMB server is a GCP instance then the following licensing scheme is not applicable. For more detailed information on Licensing refer to MoSMB License Manager page
Online Product Activation
Verify if the MoSMB server machine has a working internet connection.
Verify if the MoSMB server's network adapters are up.
Verify if the firewall policies allow port 443 to connect to the MoSMB license servers.
Verify if https://wyday.com/limelm/ is reachable from the MoSMB server.
Verify if the time and date settings of the MoSMB server are correct or up-to-date.
Verify if a valid product key is provided in the MoSMB configuration file for Licenses with product key.

Verify if the product key has reached the maximum number of activations limit. The information on the maximum limit for the number of machines the product key can be activated is given while providing the product key.
Online Trial Activation
Verify if the MoSMB server has a working internet connection.
Verify if MoSMB server's network adapters are up.
Verify if the firewall policies allow port 443 to connect to the MoSMB license servers.
Verify if https://wyday.com/limelm/ is reachable from the MoSMB server.
Verify if the time and date settings for the MoSMB server machine are correct or up-to-date.
Offline Product Activation
Verify if the MoSMB server's network adapters are up.
Verify if the product key mentioned in the MoSMB configuration and the product key used while activating the license is the same.
Make sure you have followed the steps for activation as per the offline product activation using product key
Offline Trial Activation
Verify if the trialresponse.xml file that is provided to you is placed at the /etc/mosmb/license/ location.
Verify if the trialrequest.xml is generated on the same MoSMB server machine on which you need to activate the MoSMB license.
Verify if the MoSMB server's network adapters are up.
Failed to check activation within grace period

When the Error - Failed to check product activation within grace period is seen in mosmb.log It would indicate that the product key was activated online on the server machine BUT now the server machine is not able to reach to | limelm site To activate the product key again, deactivate the current product key activation by running /etc/mosmb/license/.mosmb_license_deactivate Then follow offline activation steps
MoSMB Share Not Accessible
Check the mosmb.log for any obvious error messages that would indicate what is the problem.
Check if mosmb service is running, check pid or process information using ps

# service mosmb status
# pidof mosmb
# ps -aux| grep mosmb
Verify if port 445 is open for the TCP communication.
Verify if the TCP port mentioned in the MoSMB configuration file is also configured on the client machine and is open for communication over TCP.
If error is related to krb5, Refer to the Troubleshooting for the AD Kerberos MoSMB share access when authentication mode used for share access is krb5
If error is related to gss-api refer to the troubleshooting section for gss-api errors
If the authentication mode is ntlmLocal make sure that the (local linux) MoSMB user is created on the MoSMB server machine and you are accessing the share via the same user.

check the mosmb_audit.log for login from the user for whom the access is failing, to check if Login attempt is failing or user has successfully logged in.  Refer here for more details on the mosmb_audit.log
If Login is successful, Verify if the user has the permission to access the share for the authentication mode used (krb5, ntlm-AD or ntlm_local) as mentioned in Permission Issues section below.

If a previously working node is removed and Re Joined to AD , for ntlmAD to work after such a change, you will need to re run mosmb_passwd -m and restart mosmb service too. Also old kerberos ticket on client side may prevent you from accessing the share. You need to purge the cache to remove old cluster/node kerberos ticket with klist purge OR user can sign out and signing to the client. OR The kerberos cache will expire in scheduled 10 hours and next access will renew the ticket automatically.
MoSMB Share Not Accessible on Ubuntu 22.04 OR error seen with mosmb_passwd binary
possible error seen with mosmb_passwd :
ValueError: unsupported hash type md4
Verify if openssl configuration required for Ubuntu 22.04 are done
If the server machines have openssl version 3 and above configuration changes are required. Check openssl version with
openssl version
Open /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf
Existing/Default Configuration :

[provider_sect]
   default = default_sect
   [default_sect]
   # activate = 1
Edits/Required Configuration :

[provider_sect]
   default = default_sect legacy = legacy_sect [openssl_init]
   providers = provider_sect
 
   [default_sect] activate = 1 [legacy_sect] activate = 1
MoSMB Share Not Accessible for only a particular user

If access fails for a user, make sure audit logs are enabled and verify login attempts from the user for mosmb nodes. Check the mosmb_audit.log to see if Login attempt is failing or user has successfully logged in.

If login is successful check permission issues. 
If login is failed check the authentication password/domain information
If no login attempt message is seen check mosmb.log for MO_ERROR or MO_WARN logs
Permission Issues
Verify that the correct user permissions are set on the MoSMB server side and the same user is used to access the share and create or edit the shared files or directories.
POSIX Authorization Settings
NTACL Authorization Settings
Viewing File / Directory Permissions
Verify if port 88 is open on both the MoSMB server and the AD machine. This is required by the LDAP server.
When the authentication mode is krb5 verify if the Kerberos ticket is present on the MoSMB server.
In case the authentication mode is krb5 verify if all the LDAP client configuration settings are carried out.
In case the authentication mode is krb5 verify if all the SSSD related settings are carried out.
Verify if the package necessary for generating UUID is installed on the MoSMB server. If not please install the package.
ID Mapper Not Working Correctly
Verify if the sssd service is running on the MoSMB server.
check with id command that the AD user's details are retrieved and it does print the user ID and group IDs. In case it is failing to do so, try restarting the sssd service.
Verify the nsswitch.conf file and make sure that .sss is mentioned in the configuration.

Changes to the AD group membership are slow to take effect. The issue could be because of the sssd cache. When you change the primary group of the user then the old entry in the sssd cache becomes invalid. For new changes to take effect restart the sssd service and run the following command: sss_cache -E OR sss_cache -u <user-name>

If you are considering EVERYONE present in NTFS (Everyone includes the owner, group and other members) as Others (in Linux this means all the members other than the owner and group members) mapping will fail.
Note : In the case of user and group mapping, MoSMB currently supports only the primary group mapping.
Issue with cluster keytab. Cluster fqdn access not working. (gss-api errors in mosmb.log)
Check time is in sync with the AD
Make sure the keytab creation is correctly done using ktpass on AD by the Admin (w.o. any errors or warning during the creation) using correct CRYPTO values used to generate keys in the keytab.

Use following PS cmdlet to check the spn is correctly mapped the special user setspn -Q cifs/<cifs-server-name> And use the follwoing PS cmdlet to check the user's SPN Get-ADUser <username> -property ServicePrincipalName
The DNS forward lookup works for the cluster fqdn and reverse lookup work correctly for cluster node ips
Check firewall for correct ports opened for kerberos
Check that the special user created for keytab creation in the ktpass 'mapuser', has following properties set:

Go to Active Directory users and computers 
User properties -> user account -> password never expires
User properties -> user account -> Correct encryption support (in accordance with the CRYPTO values used in ktpass) is enabled, e.g. This account supports Kerberos AES 256 bit encryption This account supports Kerberos AES 128 bit encryption You can also check the encryptions used in the keytab with -e option of klist klist -kte /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
OR use following PS cmdlet to check the user's supported encryption types Get-ADUser <username> -property KerberosEncryptionType
Check KVno from keytab against the special user.

Note the msDS-KeyVersionNumber from the o/p of following PS cmdlet and compare with kvno in the keytab from the output of klist ran on the mosmb.keytab Get-ADUser <username> -property msDS-KeyVersionNumber klist -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
In mosmb.json, check that globalSection is set to have enableHighAvailability to yes
Check the o/p of klist -A to check a valid krb5 tkt is fetched during mosmb startup ( mosmb will use the mosmb.keytab and domain_list.txt to do a kinit )
Check sssd.conf, krb5.conf, ldap.conf are set in accordance with the mosmb documentation
Check permitted encryption types from the krb5 crypto-policies
cat /etc/krb5.conf.d/crypto-policies

If setup was working fine previously, and now we are getting "Preauthentication failed" error in mosmb.log, verify that the password for special user used for cluster keytab creation is not expired or changed. If so, need to recreate the mosmb.keytab using ktpass
Troubleshooting Guide on the Active Directory Kerberos MoSMB share access
Refer to the AD Kerberos Troubleshooting Guide .
Troubleshooting Guide on RDMA (SMBDirect) communication on the MoSMB server
Refer → Basic RDMA(SMBDirect) Setup Guide .
ZooKeeper Troubleshooting
1. Error 1:
Unable to start AdminServer, exiting abnormally
Solution : Check for admin.serverPort configuration in zoo.cfg :
#admin server port configuration admin.serverPort=8082
Make sure port 8082 is free for use, if not free, use some other port
netstat -ntlp | grep 8082
2. Error 2:
java.net.BindException: Address already in use (Bind failed)
Solution : Check if ports configured are available, if not free then use some other port combination
netstat -ntlp | grep 4888 & netstat -ntlp | grep 5888 on all ZooKeeper Server machines.

Example :	
server.1 = <ip_server1>:4888:5888
server.2 = <ip_server2>:4888:5888
server.3 = <ip_server3>:4888:5888
Here 4888 used for follower nodes to connect to the leader node and 5888 used for leader election.
3. MoSMB service not starting due to older zookeeper versions refer :- Uninstalling previous zookeeper version
4. Make sure after successful installation all required zookeeper libraries are present on the zookeeper server.
Microsoft Management Console (MMC)
Manage File Shares with Shared Folders MMC Snap-in. Close sessions and close open file handles can be managed via MMC
Open mmc on a windows → Navigate to the Start Menu and select Run, type 'mmc'.
On the MMC console from File menu select Add or Remove snap-ins
Select and add Shared Folders snap-in
In Shared Folder properties → Enter the node fqdn or ip and connect.
Check Shares to list the smb-shares on the nodes.
Check Sessions to list the smb-sessions connected to the node.
Check Open Files to list all the open files on the node.
MAC slow browsing performance
NOTE: sonoma 14.6.1 onwards nsmb.conf does not help, refer to below points for older MAC OS versions
To improve MAC smb client's browsing performance, following settings can be tried 1. edit /etc/nsmb.conf

[default]
dir_cache_off=yes
aapl_off=true
signing_required=no
file_ids_off=yes
dir_ids_off=yes
defaults write com.apple.desktopservices DSDontWriteNetworkStores -bool TRUE
On terminal run defaults write com.apple.desktopservices DSDontWriteNetworkStores -bool TRUE
check the setting with defaults read com.apple.desktopservices DSDontWriteNetworkStores
Restart your MAC once these changes are done
2. Disable icon previews and size calculation

connect to share
Browse to share
Press CMD + j (or rt click-> show view options )
  Disable “Calculate all sizes”
  Disable “Show icon preview”
Select “Use as Defaults”
3. some more view options
Going further u can disable showing 'date modified' and 'size' in the view options
4. Turn off indexing spotlight indexing can be turned off with
sudo mdutil -a -i off /Volumes/<share-name>
To see whether Spotlight indexing is enabled for a volume:

mdutil -s -v /Volumes/share-name
 example: mdutil -s /Volumes/share1 /System/Volumes/Data/Volumes/share1:
    Indexing and searching disabled.
5. 311 smb protocol in mosmb.json on releases 072 and later Try changing the max smb protocol to 311 on the mosmb server in mosmb.json file and restart mosmb service
maxSmbProtocol = 311
6. If access is still slow; try some more network settings
try following n/w settings on MAC , run from terminal

sysctl -w kern.ipc.maxsockbuf=8388608
sysctl -w net.inet.tcp.sendspace=2097152
sysctl -w net.inet.tcp.recvspace=2097152
sysctl -w net.inet.tcp.delayed_ack=2
sysctl -w net.inet.tcp.win_scale_factor=8
sysctl -w net.inet.tcp.autorcvbufmax=33554432
sysctl -w net.inet.tcp.autosndbufmax=33554432
No restart required, if restarted the settings will be lost. Some more settings

sysctl -w net.smb.fs.kern_deadtimer=0
sysctl -w net.smb.fs.kern_hard_deadtimer=0
sysctl -w net.smb.fs.kern_soft_deadtimer=0

In addition, if your Mac needs above network settings to be retained on reboot.
Create a file named /etc/sysctl.conf in your local /etc folder
This needs again to be done as root!

Go to the command line, become root and fire up your preferred editor (vi) by typing vi /etc/sysctl.conf
Put the following into the newly created file,

vi /etc/sysctl.conf 
kern.ipc.maxsockbuf=8388608
net.inet.tcp.sendspace=2097152
net.inet.tcp.recvspace=2097152
net.inet.tcp.delayed_ack=2
net.inet.tcp.win_scale_factor=8
net.inet.tcp.autorcvbufmax=33554432
net.inet.tcp.autosndbufmax=33554433
net.smb.fs.kern_deadtimer=0
net.smb.fs.kern_hard_deadtimer=0
net.smb.fs.kern_soft_deadtimer=0

and save the file. The file rights must be set to:
owner root:wheel and -rw-r--r--
please make sure by typing ls -al /etc/sysctl.conf on the command line and if not, change it by running
chown root:wheel /etc/sysctl.conf
and
chmod 0644 /etc/sysctl.conf from the command line
Note : MoSMB service restart is mandatory in case any changes are made to the configuration file.

WARNING: If value of maxOpenPerVinode is increased then it will need increase in the zookeeper memory. Hence one must change parameter jute.maxbuffer in zookeeper configuration file ~/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/conf/zoo.cfg Also note that any change here will need a full mosmb and zookeeper cluster stop with zookeeper clean up and then mosmb and zookeeper cluster start (ref: MoSMB cluster restart with cleanup for CL builds Note : For inode mechanism, this parameter is ignored.
"yes" becomes windows like behavior i.e. insensitive, and "no" becomes linux like i.e. sensitive.

- Specify the file system information to be used for this share. - If "fsInfoAutoDetect" is "no" then values of the "sectorsPerUnit" and "bytesPerSector" will be taken from the configuration file i.e. "mosmb.json" - If "fsInfoAutoDetect" is "yes" then values of the "sectorsPerUnit" and "bytesPerSector" will be taken by using the systme call. So in that case, the values of "sectorsPerUnit" and "bytesPerSector" mentioned in the configuration file i.e. "mosmb.json" will get ignored. - Example of the "fsInfo" is as given below:

- redirectToOwner : To enable TCR feature, the value of the 'redirectToOwner' parameter of the particular share should be set to 'yes'.  If it is set to 'no' then the TCR feature will be disabled. - redirectionMechanism : Currently only one mechanism (i.e. 'share_ownership') is supported. - ipList : IP addresses list for TCR mechanism ('share_ownership') should be specified. User can mention single IP address or comma separated IP addresses list. e.g. "10.11.0.81,10.11.0.82,10.11.0.83".
Note : If the value of 'redirectToOwner' is set to 'yes' then 'ipList' must be populated.

All MoSMB-SA builds are set to use the "Standalone Mode" of operation by default. To use MoSMB server in Standalone Mode with Kerberos authentication simply follow the configurations mentioned in steps below one by one.

1. Configure Network Time Protocol (NTP) Configuration settings on all MoSMB server nodes. 2. Install Kerberos packages on MoSMB server 3. Request the Domain Administration team for AD and DNS Configurations.
Create two-way AD trust between all the required domains if required. (Note: This is not a must).

Add conditional forwarders in the DNS manager w.r.t other domains for all domains if required. This is just so that all name resolutions work correctly depending on your IT infrastructure rules and needs.
Configure name resolution settings on MoSMB server.
Join the MoSMB server to the Active Directory Domain
Remove restricted Kerberos entries from Active Directory Domain
Configure DNS entries for the MoSMB Server
4. Kerberos and related services configurations on MoSMB server machine.
Configure MoSMB krb5 settings
Configure MoSMB Kerberos keytab
Configure LDAP client settings
Configure SSSD settings
Configure the domain_list.txt settings
For release versions:3.1.1_000 refer - MoSMB Kerberos Ticket Generation
For release versions:3.1.1_001 and above refer - MoSMB Kerberos Ticket Generation.
5. MoSMB server now supports NTLMSSP authentication from Release versions:3.1.1_004 and above. For MoSMB server node, carry out these changes to get the NTLMSSP authentication support working.
6. Configure required authorization (ACL) settings
Using MoSMB with Posix ACL OR
Using MoSMB with Advance Posix ACL OR
Using MoSMB with NTACL
7. Setup the MoSMB configuration file (/etc/mosmb/mosmb.json) for standalone use.
In globalSection check enableHighAvailability is set to no
In section nodeInfoSection , give any integer between 1 to 1023 to nodeId
In section vinodeSection check mechanism is set as inode
In shareSection check parameter isCluster is set to no
Example:
8. If the server machines are centos(or similar) 8 and above supported encryption options needs to be enabled from server side as well in order for Kerberos authentication to work with RC4.

On every CentOS 8.3 and above MoSMB server, where authentication against AD Domain Controllers takes place, use the update-crypto-policies command to enable the AD-SUPPORT cryptographic subpolicy in addition to the DEFAULT cryptographic policy.

Command : update-crypto-policies --set DEFAULT:AD-SUPPORT Output : Setting system policy to DEFAULT:AD-SUPPORT Command : init 6 Notes : 1. System-wide crypto policies are applied on application start-up so it is recommended to restart the system for the change of policies to fully take place.
           3. The AD-SUPPORT cryptographic subpolicy is only available on CentOS 8.3 and newer.

To enable support for RC4 in CentOS 8.2 , create and enable a custom cryptographic module policy with cipher = RC4-128+. For more details, see Customizing system-wide cryptographic policies with policy modifiers. Reference links :
1. Using the system wide cryptographic policies 2. how to customize crypto policies rhel 8.2
To enable support for RC4 in CentOS 8.0 and CentOS 8.1 , add +rc4 to the permitted_enctypes option in the /etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/krb5.config file

[libdefaults]
           permitted_enctypes = aes256-cts-hmac-sha1-96 aes256-cts-hmac-sha384-192 camellia256-cts-cmac aes128-cts-hmac-sha1-96 aes128-cts-hmac-sha256-128 camellia128-cts-cmac +rc4
9. If the server machines have openssl version 3 and above , following configuration changes are required.
Open /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf
Existing/Default Configuration :

[provider_sect]
   default = default_sect
   [default_sect]
   # activate = 1
Edits/Required Configuration :

[provider_sect]
   default = default_sect legacy = legacy_sect [openssl_init]
   providers = provider_sect
   
   [default_sect] activate = 1 [legacy_sect] activate = 1
10. Start MoSMB service and check status service mosmb status and check the mosmb.log for details. You can check details of MoSMB server Maintenance here
Notes :

1. Make sure sssd service is running on node.  
   2. Node id can be any integer in the 1 to 1023 range.
   3. To know other mosmb.json parameters in details refer this .

mosmbcli
mosmbcli is command line parser. mosmbcli binary is made available in /usr/bin after MoSMB installation. mosmbcli can be used for various purposes like,
To generate or update the MoSMB configuration in json format.
To manage mosmb services on local/standalone setups.
To manage mosmb services on cluster setups.
Prerequisites
Python and pip of same version.
Command-line JSON processor : jq package
zk-shell

Setup passwordless SSH : ssh-keygen -t rsa ssh-copy-id <hostname_of_other_mosmb_server> Allow user to run any commands anywhere without password - edit /etc/sudoers - <username> ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL For more reference see : passwordless-ssh
ssh-keygen -t rsa
ssh-copy-id <hostname_of_other_mosmb_server>
Allow user to run any commands anywhere without password - edit /etc/sudoers - <username> ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL
For more reference see : passwordless-ssh

Clustershell aka clush configuration is required for mosmbcli to work. Installing clustershell After installation open the file /etc/clustershell/groups.d/local.cfg and make changes to add the required mosmb_server_nodes. Example: all: host1 If in mosmb cluster mosmb node's hostnames are mohost1, mohost2, mohost3, then one can add a group as group1: mohost[1,2,3] Note: groups in /etc/clustershell/groups.d/local.cfg, should contain nodes having only hostnames. As username@ip will not work
Installing clustershell

After installation open the file /etc/clustershell/groups.d/local.cfg and make changes to add the required mosmb_server_nodes. Example: all: host1 If in mosmb cluster mosmb node's hostnames are mohost1, mohost2, mohost3, then one can add a group as group1: mohost[1,2,3] Note: groups in /etc/clustershell/groups.d/local.cfg, should contain nodes having only hostnames. As username@ip will not work
Usage
Initail help :

Command: mosmbcli -h
       usage: mosmbcli [-h] 
                       {zk-start,zk-restart,zk-stop,zk-status,mosmb-start,mosmb-restart,mosmb-stop,mosmb-status,get-req,deploy-resp,znode,get,set,del,validate,apply}
                       ...
       
       positional arguments:
         {zk-start,zk-restart,zk-stop,zk-status,mosmb-start,mosmb-restart,mosmb-stop,mosmb-status,get-req,deploy-resp,znode,get,set,del,validate,apply}
       
       optional arguments:
         -h, --help            show this help message and exit
To get help for each command use "-h" or "--help" at each level of command :
Example :

1) mosmbcli -h
   2) mosmbcli set -h
   3) mosmbcli set --json global -h
   4) mosmbcli set --json vinode -h
   5) mosmbcli get -h
Command line Options
There are various options as mentioned in the help. We will go through them all one by one.
MoSMB-ZooKeeper
For mosmb-zookeeper we have start, restart, stop and check status options supported with local or remote nodes.
Create a /etc/mosmb/mosmb-zookeeper-clustermap.txt and add entries in following format:

NodeId Machine-IP username Password 1 88.88.88.88 root Pa55w0rd
   2 88.88.88.89 root Pa55w0rd

MoSMB
For MoSMB we have start, restart, stop and check status options supported with local or remote nodes; similar to mosmb-zookeeper.
Create a /etc/mosmb/mosmb-clustermap.txt and add entries in following format:

NodeId Machine-IP username Password 1 88.88.88.88 root Pa55w0rd
   2 88.88.88.89 root Pa55w0rd

       optional arguments:
         -h, --help        show this help message and exit
         -l, --local       start Mosmb on local node
         -n, --nodeip      start Mosmb on mentioned ip node
         -c, --clustermap  start Mosmb on every node from cluster

         -c, --clustermap  Restart Mosmb on every node from cluster

       optional arguments:

MoSMB License

For MoSMB Offline License activation we have get-req which generates the mosmb offline license requests on the mosmb cluster nodes and deploy-resp which deploys the shared response file for mosmb offline license activation on the mosmb cluster nodes.
One needs MoSMB Product Key in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file;

Request and response will be created against mentioned product key in /etc/msomb/mosmb.json Once request's for all mentioned nodes are created then share the tar file with mosmb support and you will receive the corresponding response. Use deploy-resp command to deploy the responses on corresponding machines at required location.

Command: mosmbcli get-req -h usage: mosmbcli get-req [-h] -g GROUP
       
       optional arguments:
         -h, --help            show this help message and exit
         -g GROUP, --group GROUP
                               Specify a group defined in /etc/clustershell/groups.d/local.cfg to generate request.xml in Command: mosmbcli deploy-resp -h usage: mosmbcli deploy-resp [-h] -p PATH
       
       optional arguments:
         -h, --help            show this help message and exit
         -p PATH, --path PATH  deploy response.xml file on every node in cluster from the given path

Command: sudo mosmbcli get-req -g mosmb
   
       movm1:Status:Request.xml-generated
       movm2:Status:Request.xml-generated
       movm3:Status:Request.xml-generated
       
       Command Finished Executing
       
       For more information check /tmp/mosmb/clush_status
       Please share generated tar file /tmp/mosmb/mosmb-cluster-licenses.tar.gz with MoSMB support
Command: sudo mosmbcli deploy-resp -p /tmp/mosmb/mosmb-cluster-licenses-responses Command Executed Successfully

MoSMB json Manipulation

One can use the get, set, validate and apply functions to the manipulate the MoSMB configurations in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json Standard/Default path for the json file is /etc/mosmb/ . If user of "mosmbcli" wants to redirect the output files to particular location, then the environment variable MOSMB_CONF_PATH has to be set with path of that location. Eg. export MOSMB_CONF_PATH=/home/user Note that mosmb.json generated at exported path won't get applied on MoSMB service start/restart
Command-line JSON processor : jq package

Command: mosmbcli get -h usage: mosmbcli get [-h] [--json] [--jsont] [--nodeip NODEIP | --clustermap]
                           {all,global,smb_direct,product_key,persistent_store,auth,node_info,vinode,shares}
                           ...
       
       positional arguments:
         {all,global,smb_direct,product_key,persistent_store,auth,node_info,vinode,shares}
           all                 Displays all the parameters of the configuration
           global              displays global parameters of configuration
           smb_direct          displays SMBDirect parameters of configuration
           product_key         displays Product key parameters of configuration
           persistent_store    displays the persistent store parameters of the
                               configuration
           auth                displays the authentication parameters of the
                               configuration
           node_info           displays node info parameters of the configuration
           vinode              display the vinode parameters of the configuration
           shares              displaysall/single share present in the configuration
       
       optional arguments:
         -h, --help            show this help message and exit
         --json                Displays the data of mosmb.json in JSON format
         --jsont               Displays the data of template json file in JSON format
         --nodeip NODEIP       displays data from this node only
         --clustermap          display data from all the cluster nodes

Command: mosmbcli set -h usage: mosmbcli set [-h] [--json] [--jsont] [--nodeip NODEIP | --clustermap]
                           {global,smb_direct,product_key,persistent_store,auth,node_info,vinode,shares}
                           ...
       
       positional arguments:
         {global,smb_direct,product_key,persistent_store,auth,node_info,vinode,shares}
           global              updates the global parameters of the current server
                               configuration
           smb_direct          smb_direct
           product_key         product_key
           persistent_store    Updates the persistent store parameters of the current
                               server configuration.
           auth                updates the authentication parameters of the current
                               server configuration
           node_info           Node Information Settings
           vinode              updates vinode parameters of the current server
                               configuration
           shares              updates a share from the current server configuration
       
       optional arguments:
         -h, --help            show this help message and exit
         --json                Creates the data in JSON format
         --jsont               Creates the data in JSON format from template file
         --nodeip NODEIP       displays data from this node only
         --clustermap          display data from all the cluster nodes Command: mosmbcli set --json persistent_store -h usage: mosmbcli set persistent_store [-h] [-pstore_path PSTORE_PATH]
                                            [-max_opens MAX_OPENS]
                                            [-max_file_path_len MAX_FILE_PATH_LEN]
       
       optional arguments:
         -h, --help            show this help message and exit
         -pstore_path PSTORE_PATH
                               Stand alone server deployment - Specify local path
                               (E.g. /opt/mosmb). Clustered server deployment -
                               Specify common shared path (E.g. /shared/data)
         -max_opens MAX_OPENS  Maximum number of "Files opened" at given point in
                               time
         -max_file_path_len MAX_FILE_PATH_LEN
                               Set this value between 1 to 32

Command: mosmbcli del -h usage: mosmbcli del [-h] [--json] {share} ...
       
       positional arguments:
         {share}
           share     deletes a share from the current server configuration

         --json      Deletes the data in JSON format Command: mosmbcli del --json share -h usage: mosmbcli del share [-h] -share_name SHARE_NAME
       
       optional arguments:
         -h, --help            show this help message and exit
         -share_name SHARE_NAME
                               Name of the share to be deleted (Required=True)

Prerequisites
The ZooKeeper installation has the following dependencies:
Ubuntu / Debian
1. openjdk-8-jdk-headless 2. wget
CentOS / RHEL
1. java-11-openjdk or higher 2. wget
Installing ZooKeeper

We recommend to have separate MoSMB and MoSMB-ZooKeeper for better performance. If MoSMB-Zookeeper is to be used in the cluster mode ZooKeeper recommends to have odd number of servers present in the cluster for better service provision; so install MoSMB-ZooKeeper on atleast 3 nodes or 5,7,9... so on.
Installing ZooKeeper (Releases before March 2022)
1. Get ZooKeeper installer apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin
wget https://archive.apache.org/dist/zookeeper/zookeeper-3.6.3/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin.tar.gz
2. Extract downloaded packages
tar -xvzf apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin.tar.gz
Installing ZooKeeper on CentOS / RHEL using rpm (Releases after March 2022)

For Cluster builds, a ZooKeeper rpm will also be provided along with MoSMB rpm. MoSMB-ZooKeeper rpm is present at : /opt/mosmb/package/mosmb_install Command : rpm -ivh /opt/mosmb/package/mosmb_install/mosmb-zookeeper-3.6.3-1.x86_64.rpm Output : Verifying...                          ################################# [100%] Preparing...                          ################################# [100%] Updating / installing... 1:mosmb-zookeeper-3.6.3-1          ################################# [100%] zookeeper is installed at : /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/
Uninstall Previous ZooKeeper Version
1. Stop older version of ZooKeeper service (Older than zookeeper 3.6.3)
<ZooKeepr installed directory- apache bin directory>./bin/zkServer.sh stop
2. ZooKeeper’s previous version's source directory
For zookeeper 3.4.5 - zookeeper3.4.5/src/c
For zookeeper 3.6.1 - apache-zookeeper-3.6.1/zookeeper-client/zookeeper-client-c/
3. Run commands -
make clean make uninstall
4. Make sure all previous library files are not on the ZooKeeper server machine -
find /usr/ -name libzookeeper*

Server maintenance includes allowing user to perform clean start, stop operations along with dynamically adding a share and compute performance parameters depending on the MoSMB Server Machine Configurations.
Note :

* The MoSMB Server should be started in only one way at a time, that is either as binary or as a service.
   * The MoSMB Server will start when the product key or trial license is valid, persistent storage and share paths mentioned are available on the server. Refer to the MoSMB License Manager .

Service
User can perform a Start, Stop or Restart operations on the MoSMB service.
For any change/s in the MoSMB server configuration files /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json and/or /etc/mosmb/s3.json in any section/s it is mandatory to restart the MoSMB server.
Notes: : 1. systemctl support is available after release builds 073. 2. One can use Either service commands or systemctl commands throughout the cycle.
MoSMB Start
Command : EITHER service mosmb start OR systemctl start mosmb Output  : Starting MoSMB service...

When MoSMB is being used with ZooKeeper server, the sequence of starting these services is important as MoSMB is ZooKeeper's client and ZooKeeper takes the locks on certain files which is released after 200 secs timeout if the MoSMB / ZooKeeper server is exited abruptly or MoSMB / ZooKeeper server machine is rebooted.

1. Start ZooKeeper - Command : sh  /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh start Output  : JMX enabled by default Using config: ../apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/conf/zoo.cfg' Mode: Standalone Mode: Leader / Follower / Standalone this output will be different on individual servers depending on the MoSMB-ZooKeeper configurations 2. ZooKeeper Cleanup -
       ZooKeeper Cleanup is must when MoSMB or Zookeeper goes down (services or machines) while any smb operations are in progress.
       To Cleanup ZooKeeper nodes refer - MoSMB-ZooKeeper server cleanup 3. Start MoSMB - Command : EITHER service mosmb start OR systemctl start mosmb Output  : Starting MoSMB service...

When MoSMB is being used with ZooKeeper server, the sequence of starting these services is important as MoSMB is ZooKeeper client and ZooKeeper takes the locks on certain files which is released after 200 secs timeout if the MoSMB / ZooKeeper server is exited abruptly or MoSMB / ZooKeeper server machine is rebooted.

1. Start ZooKeeper on all nodes - Command : sh  /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh start Output  : JMX enabled by default Using config: ../apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/conf/zoo.cfg' Mode: Leader Mode: Leader / Follower this output will be different on individual servers depending on the MoSMB-ZooKeeper configurations 2. ZooKeeper Cleanup on all nodes -
       ZooKeeper Cleanup is must when MoSMB or Zookeeper goes down (services or machines) while any smb operations are in progress.
       To Cleanup ZooKeeper nodes refer - MoSMB-ZooKeeper server cleanup 3. Start MoSMB on all nodes - Command : EITHER service mosmb start OR systemctl start mosmb Output  : Starting MoSMB service...
Note :
* If MoSMB server and ZooKeeper server are configured on 2 separate machines and only MoSMB service/server goes down then ZooKeeper cleanup is not a requirement.
MoSMB Stop
Command : EITHER service mosmb stop OR systemctl stop mosmb Output  : Stopping MoSMB service...

When MoSMB is being used with ZooKeeper server, the sequence of stopping these services / shutting down the machines is important as MoSMB is ZooKeeper client and ZooKeeper takes the locks on certain files which is released after 200 secs timeout if the MoSMB / ZooKeeper server is exited abruptly or MoSMB / ZooKeeper server machine is rebooted.

1. Stop MoSMB service Command : EITHER service mosmb stop OR systemctl stop mosmb Output  : Stopping MoSMB service... 2. Stop ZooKeeper service/s Command : apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh stop Output  : .... Stopping zookeeper ... STOPPED

When MoSMB is being used with ZooKeeper server, the sequence of stopping these services / shutting down the machines is important as MoSMB is ZooKeeper client and ZooKeeper takes the locks on certain files which is released after 200 secs timeout if the MoSMB / ZooKeeper server is exited abruptly or MoSMB / ZooKeeper server machine is rebooted.

1. Stop MoSMB service on all nodes - Command : EITHER service mosmb stop OR systemctl stop mosmb Output  : Stopping MoSMB service... 2. Stop ZooKeeper service on all nodes - Command : apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh stop Output  : .... Stopping zookeeper ... STOPPED
MoSMB Restart
MoSMB service restart is mandatory in case any changes are made to the configuration file.
Command : EITHER service mosmb restart OR systemctl restart mosmb Output  : Stopping MoSMB service... Starting MoSMB service...

In case MoSMB goes in an unstable state due to node/process/network failures causing MoSMB and/or zookeeper node/s to go down or unreachable within recovery time periods, then follow the steps as mentioned below for clean restart the MoSMB along with MoSMB-ZooKeeper.

1. Stop MoSMB - Command : EITHER service mosmb stop OR systemctl stop mosmb 2. Run /etc/mosmb/scripts/zk-cleanup.sh script to clear the zookeeper database and logs on zookeeper node. ( If zookeeper is not on the MoSMB node, in that case manually delete all the files in <zookeeper-datadir>/version-2 and <zookeeper-install-dir>/logs ) /etc/mosmb/scripts/zk-cleanup.sh <zookeper-install-dir> 3. Stop zookeeper <zookeeper-install-dir>/bin/zkServer.sh stop 4. Start zookeeper <zookeeper-install-dir>/bin/zkServer.sh start 5. Start MoSMB service mosmb start

In case MoSMB cluster goes in an unstable state due to node/process/network failures causing multiple MoSMB and/or zookeeper nodes to go down or unreachable within recovery time periods, then follow the steps as mentioned below for clean restart the whole MoSMB cluster along with MoSMB-ZooKeeper cluster.

1. Stop MoSMB cluster (mosmb service on all nodes) Command : EITHER service mosmb stop OR systemctl stop mosmb 2. Delete all mo_openfiletable files from pstore path
   3. Stop zookeeper cluster (zookeeper on all nodes) <zookeeper-install-dir>/bin/zkServer.sh stop 4. Run the zookeeper cleanup script by MoSMB to clear the zookeeper database and logs on all zookeeper nodes in the cluster. <zk-cleanup script> <zookeper-install-dir path> /etc/mosmb/scripts/mosmb_zk-cleanup.sh <zookeper-install-dir path> (For Release versions MoSMB : 3.1.1.005 onwards) /etc/mosmb/scripts/zk-cleanup.sh <zookeper-install-dir path> (For Release versions MoSMB : 3.1.1.003 and before) ( If zookeeper is not on the MoSMB node, in that case manually delete all the files in <zookeeper-datadir>/version-2 and <zookeeper-install-dir>/logs OR in a workaround copy the clean up script to mosmb-zookeeper node and run it from there.)   
   
   5. Start zookeeper cluster (zookeeper on all nodes) <zookeeper-install-dir>/bin/zkServer.sh start 6. Start MoSMB cluster (mosmb service on all nodes) service mosmb start
MoSMB Status
Command : EITHER service mosmb status OR systemctl status mosmb Output  : MoSMB service is running OR MoSMB service is NOT running
MoSMB Process ID

Command : ps aux | grep mosmb OR pidof mosmb Output  : root      7521  4.7  4.5 1646804 272104 pts/1  Sl   14:31   0:00 mosmb root      7534  0.0  0.0 112708   976 pts/1    S+   14:31   0:00 grep --color=auto mosmb
MoSMB Logs

Once you start the MoSMB service, check the mosmb.log located at default path /var/log/ to see if MoSMB has started successfully without any errors. For any errors, check the Troubleshooting Guide or FAQs for assistance. Logs directory can be configured as per the requirement by editing parameter logsPath in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json . In logs directory MoSMB will create files mosmb.log, mosmb_audit.log, mosmb_vinode.log Please send your queries to support@ryussi.com
For mosmb logs, the log level values for all modules can be set in the conf file /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf
MoSMB Logs in release versions 3.1.1.039 and above
MO_CRITICAL = 0
MO_ERROR = 1
MO_WARN = 2
MO_INFO = 3
MO_DEBUG = 4
Default mosmb_logging.conf looks like

FSA_L           = 2
SMBCORE_L       = 2
CONNECTION_L    = 2
MULTICHANNEL_L  = 2
IOS_L           = 2
RDMA_L          = 2
WITNESS_L       = 2
OPLOCKS_L       = 2
MORPC_L         = 2
CONFIG_L        = 3
STATISTICS_L    = 2
LEASE_L         = 2
NDR_L           = 2
RPC_L           = 2
HASH_L          = 2
PST_L           = 2
LICENSE_L       = 2
AUDIT_L         = 2
AUTH_L          = 2
ENCRYPTION_L    = 2
VINODE_L        = 2
DEFAULT_L       = 3
COMMON_L        = 2
CLUSTER_L       = 2
PROFILER_L      = 2
The MO_CRITICAL log messages are printed with loglevel set to 0
(They will be printed for all other log level > 0 as well)
When the log level value set is to 1, error level logs are enabled (MO_ERROR Level) (They will be printed for all other log level > 1 as well)
The MO_WARN log messages are printed with log level set to 2 in the conf file.
(They will be printed for all other log level > 2 as well)

For modules CONFIG_L and DEFAULT_L, default value is set to 3, which is MO_INFO. The INFO logs are printed to give more information. You can choose to disable it and print only error level logs by setting the value to < 3
For module PROFILER_L, default is set to 0. When the value is set to 4, the detailed information is printed.
Any changes to log levels can be reloaded at runtime, (no need to restart mosmb) reload Log Level Changes
In case debug level is required, following guidelines can be used to set respective log modules to debug level

Login/Access issues- enable AUDIT_L , AUTH_L, SMBCORE_L
File I/O issues - enable IOS_L, FSA_L, SMBCORE_L
Connection related issues - enable CONNECTION_L , SMBCORE_L
Overall general profiling for timings - enable PROFILER_L
RPC failures- enable - RPC_L, SMBCORE_L
mosmb-zookeeper related issues- VINODE_L, SMBCORE_L

Use these logs for Security Monitoring.
One can set the audit module’s log level in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf file to 3 to get detailed audit logs.

AUDIT_L = 3
This level logs File operations like CREATE RENAME DELETE Or PERMISSION CHANGE With FileName and the User who is attempting the operation
Note: Logs for MO_CRIT, MO_ERROR, MO_WARN will also be included
If MO_WARN level logs are required, one can set

AUDIT_L = 2
This level logs LOGIN and LOGOFF attempts, success or failure, With Time and IP of machine the attempt was made from
The mosmb_audit.log audit log file will then be generated at the log location (default  /var/log). Further details are same as older releases.

These are the logs generated by the api used to communicate to the (zookeeper/inode) database.
In case of any vinode related errors (either inode Mechanism or zookeeper), user can refer to these logs to get more details. The vinode loglevel starts from 1. For value 0 you may get error and mosmb will not start.

VINODE_L = 1
  Or 2/3/4 etc
The mosmb_vinode.log log file is populated at the log location, with MO_ERROR,MO_WARN,MO_INFO (Rest could be the Zookeeper logs.)
