MoSMB Logs rotation
When MoSMB is installed, the scripts for the log rotation of the mosmb logs are added in the /etc/cron.hourly directory (mologrotate) and the other files are in /etc/logrotate.d directory.
/etc/logrotate.d/mosmbauditlr
/etc/logrotate.d/mosmblr
/etc/logrotate.d/zookeeperlr
Logs are rotated when the log file size reaches 500 MB(default). The log size threshold reached is checked on an hourly basis.
mosmb.log and other log files are rotated nd refer to files mosmbauditlr (mosmb_audit.log), mosmblr (mosmb.log) and zookeeperlr (mosmb_vinode.log) present under /etc/logrotate.d/ respectively
Adding a share dynamically

Script: cmdpipe Function:
To add a new share to MoSMB server and reload the configuration without stopping the MoSMB server. Only single share can be applied at a time on reload command. To add multiple shares, add all the share names to mosmb.json under shareSection. Run the script to add each share one at a time. Reload MoSMB config will take some time. The newly added share's shareName should be unique. sharePath must be valid and must exists on the server. Before running the command, please make sure the mosmb.json is correct.
Prerequisite to run cmdpipe:
python2 should be installed.
tabulate module should be installed.
To install tabulate :
pip2 install tabulate

Example: To add a share without restarting the mosmb server 1. Add required VFS share details in mosmb.json under shareSection as a new share e.g. ‘share3' as follows. ’share1’ is an existing share. 2. Add required S3 share details in mosmb.json under shareSection as a new share e.g. ‘s3_share' as follows. ’share1’ is an existing share. 3. The command mentioned below will add share3 and reload MoSMB server configuration [root@sivm31 ~]# /etc/mosmb/cmdpipe config_reload_add -h Error occurred in GetOpt Use the following commands for mosmb config reload -n : Specifies share name -t : Specifies share type Share type can be one of these two : VFS or S3
Following is an Example: ./cmdpipe config_reload_add -n <share_name> -t vfs OR ./cmdpipe config_reload_add -n <share_name> -t S3

[root@sivm31 ~]# /etc/mosmb/cmdpipe config_reload_add -n share3 -t vfs 
Success
Command executed successfully

[root@sivm31 ~]# /etc/mosmb/cmdpipe config_reload_add -n s3_share -t S3 
Success
Command executed successfully
MoSMB statistics
MoSMB statistics can be collected anytime with the command pipe utility
/etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe statson
Optional Arguments to statson

-p, --path Specifies csv path. Default is /var/log/
-i, --interval Specifies time period in seconds. Default is 300 s
-c, --console Display statistics on 'console'
-n, --number Number of counts for statistics collection operation
-h, --help Shows this help message and Exit
Examples:

/etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe statson -p /var/log/ -i 60 & => Statistic collection will keep running in background untill mosmb is restarted or cmdpipe instance is stopped /etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe statson -p /var/log/ -i 120 -c => Display the stats on console after every '-i' interval /etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe statson -p /var/log/ -i 600 -c -n 5 => Display the stats on console after every '-i' interval for '-n' number of time
Following Abbreviations used while showing output on console using '-c' option

RIO(Read IOPS), WIO(Write IOPS), TIOPs(Total IOPS), RT(Read Throughput), WT(Write Throughput),
TT(MB/s)(Total Throughput MB/s), RL(Read Latency), WL(Write Latency), SRL(Storage Read Latency),
SWL(Storage Write Latency), FO(Total File/Pipe Opened), ES(Total Established Sessions),
DC(Total Disconnects), PV(Password Violations), PE(Total Permission Error)
When statistics are collected in csv files, following files get populated in the default path or the path mentioned in command with –p

1. preprocessor_stats.csv
2. server_stats.csv
3. worker_thread_stats.csv
4. smbdirect_stats.csv
5. processed_stats.csv
User can use the processed_stats.csv file to check the collected and processed statistics
processed_stats.csv can be used to integrate with monitoring tools to visualizing and monitoring the metrics
Performance Tuning
Set the mosmb log level to 0 (zero) in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf file.

Set the Required Message Signing to Off to improve the performance. This will not disable the signing completely: When the client sends a signed request the MoSMB server will send the signed response. For more information refer to Microsoft Network Server: Digitally Sign Communications

You can switch on multichannel by setting Enable MultiChannel to yes in the MoSMB configuration. Multiple network cards can be added at the server end. In case there are multiple network paths between the client and the server, the SMB3 protocol uses the multi-channel feature.
MoSMB is pre-tuned to perform well for most of the workloads. The MoSMB performance scales linearly with smb connections.
MoSMB service restart is mandatory in case any changes are made to the configuration file.

One can use the performance tuning script provided by MoSMB at location /etc/mosmb/scripts to apply suggested parameters w.r.t server machines CPU and RAM. To run for smbd parameters tuning ---- sh /etc/mosmb/scripts/perf_tuning.sh smbdirect To run for tcp parameters tuning ---- sh /etc/mosmb/scripts/perf_tuning.sh tcp Detailed usage is provided in script ---- sh /etc/mosmb/scripts/perf_tuning.sh -h
TCP Performance
In the MoSMB configuration, set all the transaction sizes to 8M. (Max Transaction Size, Max Read Size, Max Write Size)
Memory allocation is shared in all the TCP connections and Memory utilization is dependent on the following formula: preProcessorThreads * ringBufferCount * Transaction size

Ways to scale-up and scale-out MoSMB performance: i. In case the client connections are more in number, increase the server threads or pre-processing threads. ii. The number of threads in the worker thread-pool can be increased so that more number of SMB packets can be served at a time. The number of threads can be increased to 64/128/256 for a high-end system, depending on the CPU cores of the system. iii. The number of pre-allocated buffers can be increased as per the RAM memory. iv. Add an additional network card to make the multi-channel feature functional and enable the multi-channel settings in the MoSMB configuration. v. To scale up the MoSMB on one node, add more NICs, CPU or memory as desired. If the backend storage is a bottleneck, add high performance storage like NVMe disks. vi. To scale out, add another MoSMB node to the cluster and expose the same share.

Note : MoSMB provides a performance tuning script as per the server configuration so as MoSMB will use only upto  60% of the available memory on the server machine. This script is located at /etc/mosmb/scripts/perf_tuning.sh Just run the script to get tuning parameters like preProcessorThreads, threadPoolSize and ringBufferCount set as per the server configuration.
RDMA (SMBDirect) Performance
Note : Refer to the Troubleshooting Guide for the RDMA (SMBDirect) Communication on a MoSMB Server to understand how to make the RDMA (SMBDirect) basic setup work.

The SMBDirect protocol uses the RDMA Read/Write requests to transfer file data within the client and the server. Every RDMA Read/Write operation consists of a SMB2 Read request with 1 or more buffer descriptors . Each buffer descriptor contains information for an I/O buffer with the desired transfer size. Each buffer descriptor thus requires to be allocated and pinned in memory till the transfer is completed through the RDMA channel. So for every buffer descriptor a buffer of desired size is allocated and pinned, this is a control operation and consumes a significant amount of time. Therefore we have introduced the following parameters to ensure that we reduce control operations during data operations:

SMB Direct ring size: i. Default value: 512 ii. Per connection based parameter. iii. Accepts value in the multiples of 64. iv. The server should be restarted to reflect the effect when a  parameter is changed. The SMB Direct ring size is a parameter that allocates and reserves the specified number of buffers, each of the SMBDirect buffer size size. On every RDMA Read/Write request the buffer is consumed from this ring and replenished after use. This allows faster operations as we can avoid memory pinning operations (control) in the data path resulting in faster speeds. This can to be tuned w.r.t the I/O pattern for typical application. Setting it to very higher number can guarantee faster transfers at the cost of higher memory footprint. Whereas setting it to very lower number can lead to slower speeds due to the lack of available buffers for transfers. We recommend it to be set 1024 for media applications, and 256 for non-media operations (basic file server, disk io apps)

SMB Direct buffer size: i. Default value: 1 ii. Per connection-based parameter. iii. Accepts value from 1 to 8 inclusive. This value is in Mega Bytes (for example 1 represents 1MB). iv. The server should be restarted to reflect the effect of a changed parameter. This parameter represents the size of transfer for every buffer descriptor from SMB Direct ring . As per the SMBD Specification this parameter should be less than or equal to the maximum fragmented receive size (1MB). However, this parameter could be less than or equal to the maximum transaction size (8MB) as well. The SMB client has 1MB value  that is the maximum fragmented receive size and hence it the recommended value.

TCP parameters i. For RDMA connections only, allocate minimal resources to TCP parameters since the TCP buffers will consume some amount of RAM ii. For example, preProcessorThreads = 4, ringBufferCount = 256 (or 128 or 64)
Mac Client Performance
With the help of following settings on the MAC client one can improve performance. Mac needs reboot after applying any of the changes.
Turn off packet signing by setting following in the /etc/nsmb.conf file. signing_required=no https://support.apple.com/en-gb/HT205926
Speed up browsing on network shares defaults write com.apple.desktopservices DSDontWriteNetworkStores -bool TRUE https://support.apple.com/en-in/HT208209
for more troubleshooting with MAC, refer to the troubleshooting section for MAC

Integration with Prometheus to monitor MoSMB statistics

Prometheus is an open-source systems monitoring toolkit that helps to monitor an IT infrastructure, including: Servers, Networks etc. It can be configured with the MoSMB to get MoSMB statistics and MoSMB node's metrics like disk usage, I/O operations etc. Prometheus has two major components : Prometheus server & Prometheus targets (where node exporter is installed). NOTE: This integration is available in MoSMB from version 093 onwards
Prometheus server installation, configuration & monitoring of metrics on that server
Install the "prometheus" (prometheus server) on the node.

On Ubuntu, use below command sudo apt install prometheus OR 
  For redhat/CentOS Download the prometheus package from the link below and "untar" it.
  Ref: https://prometheus.io/download/
Updation of configuration file and using it
If package is installed through package manager then "/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml" is a prometheus server configuration file.
OR
If a package tar file is downloaded and installed then "prometheus.yml" is a prometheus server configuration file in the local directory where the package is untarred.
In the configuration file, there is a "static_configs" label which is under the "scrape_configs" label. Need to add the targets of which the prometheus needs to scrape the metrics.

E.g. 
scrape_configs:
  static_configs:
   - targets: ['10.11.1.167:9090']
Whenever the configuration file is updated then re-start the prometheus server.
To start the prometheus server use

sudo systemctl start/stop prometheus OR
  /usr/bin/prometheus --config.file=<path to PROMETHEUS_CONFIG_FILE>
  E.g. /usr/bin/prometheus --config.file=/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml
Install the "prometheus-node-exporter" on the node where the prometheus server is installed.
E.g. On Ubuntu, use below command sudo apt install prometheus-node-exporter
Start the prometheus node exporter along with the prometheus server
To start the Prometheus Node Exporter use sudo systemctl start prometheus-node-exporter
'Node Exporter' installation and using it to send the metrics from Prometheus Target Machine
Configure the Prometheus Node Exporter on Target Machine (which has MoSMB service running)

# Download the "node_exporter" for the Target Machine from https://github.com/prometheus/node_exporter/releases # Untar the downloaded package of "node_exporter".
# Get into the directory and start the node_exporter as follows
  ./node_exporter --web.listen-address <TARGET_MACHINE>:<PORT_NUMBER> --collector.textfile.directory=<DIR_WHERE_METRICS_DUMPED>
  e.g. /node_exporter --web.listen-address 10.11.1.167:9090 --collector.textfile.directory=/var/lib/node_exporter Note : "--collector.textfile.directory" option is where the <FILE_NAME>.prom needs to be created.
  In case of the “MoSMB stats", the file "processed_stats.prom" gets created by “cmdpipe” but the path of the “DIR_WHERE_METRICS_DUMPED” (mentioned as option to node_exporter) should be same as that of the path given to the “cmdpipe” during statistics generation.
Example of cmdpipe command for prom filetype
The metric data is dumped in "key:value pair" format in the " processed_stats.prom " and the "key:value" pair gets updated in every iteration after the -i interval mentioned in cmdpipe command.
How to see the data through a browser on the Prometheus server side
Go to http://<IP_ADDRESS_OF_PROMETHEUS_SERVER>:9090

We can also check the metrics exposed on the /metric path
Go to http://<IP_ADDRESS_OF_PROMETHEUS_SERVER>:9090/metrics
Integration with Grafana

# Go to http://<IP_ADDRESS_OF_GRAFANA>:3000 E.g. http://10.11.0.102:3000 # Go to Connections => menu and select Connections => Add new connection or Data Sources . 
# Search for and select Prometheus as the data source type
# Select “ add new datasource ”. Give a name to this data source, add prometheus server url " http://<PROMETHEUS_SERVER>:9090 "
# Keep rest as default and click "Save and Test" to confirm Grafana can communicate with Prometheus.
Creating a New Dashboard in Grafana
Add Dashboard: On the left-hand menu, select Dashboard. Click “New” and add  New Dashboard
Add Visualization: Click Add Visualization to your dashboard.
Select the data source that we have added earlier
A panel will appear on the screen, ensure your newly added Prometheus data source is  selected.
Query Tab: Within the new panel, go to the Query tab.
In the Query field, Select the Metric and type an expression to retrieve the desired metric from Prometheus, for example MoSMB_StorageReadLatency_msec .
Under the Options select verbose as legend.
Clicking Run queries will give you the graph for this metric
Choose Visualization: Select your desired visualization type, such as a Time series graph on the right-hand side of the panel. Name the panel. Save the Dashboard.
Save: Click the Save icon in the top-right corner to save your dashboard and the new graph panel

Navigate to Dashboard and select your dashboard and click on “explore” Or click on "Explore" on the left-hand side and select the desired data source name from the drop down list and run queries as per requirement.
Your new panel will now display the metrics data from Prometheus in a visual graph within your Grafana Dashboard

MoSMB provides users with a product key to activate the MoSMB product.
Users can activate the product in two ways:
Online: For MoSMB servers with an active network connection.
Offline: For MoSMB servers that do not have any active network connection.
To start the MoSMB server you require a trial license or a product key. Note : If the MoSMB server is a GCP instance then the following licensing scheme is not applicable.
Checks for Online Activation
Verify if the MoSMB server machine has a working and active internet connection.
Verify if all network adapters of MoSMB server's are up.
Verify if the firewall policies allow port 443 to connect to the MoSMB license servers.
Verify if the time and date settings of the MoSMB server are correct or up-to-date.
Verify if https://wyday.com/limelm/ is reachable from the MoSMB server.
MoSMB checks license validity in certain time period for online activation MoSMB server must always be able to communicate with https://wyday.com/limelm
Checks of Offline Activation
Verify if the trialrequest.xml or request.xml is generated on the same MoSMB server machine on which you need to activate the MoSMB license.
Verify if the trialresponse.xml or response.xml file provided to you is placed at the /etc/mosmb/license/ location.
Verify if the product key mentioned in the MoSMB configuration and the product key used while activating the license is the same.

There are three types of license keys:
Trial License Key
Trial License Extension Key
Product key
Online Activation of the Product
Trial License Key
In the case of a trial license the Product key parameter in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file should be empty - "productKey": ""
Restart the MoSMB service for the changes made in MoSMB configuration file to take effect.
Note : The Trial license will be activated only once per server. After expiry contact the MoSMB support
support@ryussi.com for the Trial Extension Key or to purchase the Product Key .
Trial Extension Key
Contact the MoSMB support support@ryussi.com for a trial extension key.
In the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file in the Product key section enter the trial extension key.
Restart the MoSMB service for the changes made in MoSMB configuration file to take effect.
Note : Make sure you remove all the previous trialRequest.xml and trialResponse.xml files from the /etc/mosmb/license folder
Product Key
Contact the MoSMB support support@ryussi.com to purchase the product key.
In the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file in the Product key section enter the product key provided.
For the changes made in the MoSMB configuration file to take effect Restart the MoSMB service.
Note : Make sure you remove all the previous trialRequest.xml and trialResponse.xml files if at all they exist from previous offline activations, from the /etc/mosmb/license folder
Offline Activation of the Product
Trial/Trial Extension License Key
In the case of a trial license the Product key parameter in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file should be empty - "productKey": ""
Run the /etc/mosmb/license/mosmb_offline_trial binary as follows: /etc/mosmb/license/mosmb_offline_trial
This binary will generate a trialRequest.xml file at the /etc/mosmb/license/ location.
Send this file to the MoSMB support support@ryussi.com and we will share a response trialResponse.xml file.
Copy this trialResponse.xml file at the /etc/mosmb/license/ location.
Restart the MoSMB service for the changes to take effect.
Note : The Trial license will be activated only once per machine. After expiry contact the MoSMB support support@ryussi.com for the Trial Extension Key or to purchase the Product Key.
Product Key
Contact the MoSMB support support@ryussi.com to purchase the product key.

Run the /etc/mosmb/license/mosmb_offline_license binary as follows: /etc/mosmb/license/mosmb_offline_license <Product Key> The Product Key should be the one mentioned in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file.
This binary will generate a request.xml file at the /etc/mosmb/license/ location.
Send this file to MoSMB support support@ryussi.com and we will share a response.xml response file.
Copy the response.xml file to the /etc/mosmb/license/ location.
Restart the MoSMB service for the changes to take effect.

Note :Make sure you remove all the previous trialRequest.xml and trialResponse.xml files from the /etc/mosmb/license folder Alternatively , for a cluster setup, which is set to use clush (clustershell), mosmbcli get-req and deploy-resp options can be used to get the request.xml files from all nodes and put in a tar file which can be then emailed to the MoSMB support support@ryussi.com
Applying changes made to a product key - offline
If old request.xml exists in the license folder (/etc/mosmb/license/) then we use that to generate new responses

If old request.xml does not exist in the license folder, and license is still valid, to generate request.xml, we need to deactivate first (request.xml is not generated if product is activated) /etc/mosmb/license/.mosmb_license_deactivate

Run the /etc/mosmb/license/mosmb_offline_license binary as follows: etc/mosmb/license/mosmb_offline_license <Product Key> The Product Key should be the one mentioned in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file.
This binary will generate a request.xml file at the /etc/mosmb/license/ location.
Send the request.xml file to MoSMB support support@ryussi.com and we will share a response.xml response file.
Copy the response.xml file to the /etc/mosmb/license/ location.
if product is already deactivated, Restart the MoSMB service for the license changes to take effect.
if old request was used, then deactivate now.. and then restart service
Applying changes made to a product key - online
If license is still valid, we need to deactivate first /etc/mosmb/license/.mosmb_license_deactivate
Then restart mosmb service for the new activation to take effect

How to Update MoSMB to new version

How to Create HyperV VM
HyperV VM creation is currently only supported in the krb5 authentication mode and the posix acl authorization mode configuration. Verify if all of the following settings are done:
The MoSMB share must have the complete permissions set for all. (777 -- drwxrwxrwx)
The MoSMB share Ownership Settings must be carried out.
The File Creation Mask and Directory Creation Mask are set to 0777 in the MoSMB Share Configuration .
Sign in to the HyperV machine with the Domain Administrator user credentials.
The HyperV machine's primary group must be the same as the MoSMB share owner and it must be the Domain Admin.

Note : The Domain Admin requirement depends on the HyperV configuration settings. If a non-admin user has permissions to create the HyperV VM then this user can create the VM on the MoSMB share - if the share settings are configured for that non-admin user.
How to do Performance Tuning for MoSMB

To scale up the MoSMB on one node, add more NICs, CPU or memory as desired. If the backend storage is a bottleneck, add high performance storage like NVMe disks. To scale out, add another MoSMB node to the cluster and expose the same share.
For detailed information please refer Performance Tuning pages

Note: For TCP For a given NIC, set minimum values if you want MoSMB to use low RAM and CPU cores For a given NIC, set maximum values if you want MoSMB to use higher RAM and CPU cores For a given NIC - Keep the TCP buffers:thread ratio approximately as given in the above column for e.g. if you increase the no of threads by 1 for 10Gbps bw then increase the ringbufferCount by 8 (ringbufferCount × preprocessor threads 8 × 8=64 TCP buffers) Set TransactionSize=8 for large files transactions Set TransactionSize=1 for small file io transactions
Note: For RDMA + TCP threadPoolSize is common to both RDMA and TCP transport.
How to use MoSMB Audit Logs for Security Monitoring
Use these logs for Security Monitoring. One can set the audit module’s log level in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf file to 2 to get detailed audit logs .
Set the audit log level in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf file to 3. AUDIT_L = 3 Restart the MoSMB service OR update logging at runtime without restarting mosmb

The mosmb_audit.log audit log file will then be generated at the log location mentioned in the mosmb.json file, default is /var/log . Audit logs are populated for following categories: CREATE RENAME DELETE PERMISSION_CHANGE LOGIN LOGOFF

When MoSMB is installed, the scripts for the log rotation of the mosmb audit logs are added in the /etc/cron.hourly directory (mologrotate) and the /etc/logrotate.d directory. Logs are rotated when the log file size reaches 500 MB. The log size threshold reached is checked on an hourly basis.
To disable the audit logs set the audit log level to 0. (ERROR level) AUDIT_L = 0 Restart the MoSMB service OR update logging at runtime without restarting mosmb

Note that : Along with mosmb_audit.log other log files are rotated in similar way; with files mosmbauditlr (mosmb_audit.log), mosmblr (mosmb.log) and zookeeperlr (mosmb_vinode.log) present under /etc/logrotate.d/
Switching between ACL's

It is recommended not switch between the ACL strategies over existing data.
Refer - Switching ACL's page for more details.
NTACLs
1.  The following error occurred when I tried to access a new NTACL share :
Solution : Please initialize the share permissions by following the steps mentioned here .
2.  The following error occurred when assigning NTACL permissions to a mosmb share with existing files/folders when “Applies to”:”This folder” and we “Enable Inheritance”:

Solution : Reset the permissions for the share :
Please use the following command to reset the xattr values for NTACL:
setfattr -x user.mosmb_acl <share_path> Example: setfattr -x user.mosmb_acl /opt/share/
Access the server and set user permissions and owner again with the following settings:
Set the “Applies to” option in Properties->Security->Advanced->Add tab to “This folder, subfolders and files” as mentioned in Inheritance
3. Unable to assign share permissions for a windows local groups.
Solution : MoSMB currently supports Everyone and domain groups only .
Json Syntax

As part of strict json syntax handling while running the MoSMB server, the user should ensure that - 1. Opening or closing curly braces for the entire json file should not be missing. 2. All keys and values should be separated by : 3. Comments in mosmb.json are not allowed  currently.
You can use mosmbcli parser to validate mosmb.json
mosmbcli validate --json
RC4 encryption deprecated in CentOS 8.*
In CentOS 8.* , RC4 encryption is disabled in the DEFAULT crypto policy level.
Following changes are needed for kerberos to work on CentOS 8*
There are two solutions (To maintain stronger security, we suggest you use first option)
1. Changes in User's Properties in the AD

In AD, go to Users and Computers -> Users ->Select the special user-> Open
properties ->select account tab, and choose the following account options
This account supports Kerberos AES 128 bit encryption
This account supports Kerberos AES 256 bit encryption
OR
2. On mosmb server make changes to DEFAULT crypto policy, to include RC4
i) On every CentOS 8.3 and above mosmb server, where authentication against AD Domain Controllers takes place,
Use the update-crypto-policies command to enable the AD-SUPPORT cryptographic subpolicy in addition to the DEFAULT cryptographic policy.

[root@host ~]# update-crypto-policies --set DEFAULT:AD-SUPPORT
       Setting system policy to DEFAULT:AD-SUPPORT
       Note: System-wide crypto policies are applied on application start-up.
       It is recommended to restart the system for the change of policies
       to fully take place.
Restart the host.
IMPORTANT
The AD-SUPPORT cryptographic subpolicy is only available on CentOS 8.3 and newer.

ii) To enable support for RC4 in CentOS 8.2 , create and enable a custom cryptographic module policy with cipher = RC4-128+. For more details, see Customizing system-wide cryptographic policies with policy modifiers.

https://access.redhat.com/documentation/en-us/red_hat_enterprise_linux/8/html/security_hardening/using-the-system-wide-cryptographic-policies_security-hardening#customizing-system-wide-cryptographic-policies-with-policy-modifiers_using-the-system-wide-cryptographic-policies
https://www.redhat.com/en/blog/how-customize-crypto-policies-rhel-82?extIdCarryOver=true&sc_cid=701f2000001OH7JAAW
iii) To enable support for RC4 in CentOS 8.0 and CentOS 8.1 , add +rc4 to the permitted_enctypes option in the /etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/krb5.config file

[libdefaults]
       permitted_enctypes = aes256-cts-hmac-sha1-96 aes256-cts-hmac-sha384-192 camellia256-cts-cmac aes128-cts-hmac-sha1-96 aes128-cts-hmac-sha256-128 camellia128-cts-cmac +rc4
What if I want to change the node_id for some reason

In a situation, where one wants to change the node_id of a node, from a cluster, it is recommended that full cluster is stopped (MoSMB and Zookeeper both) and a clean up is carried out, before you change the node_id of a node or many nodes in the mosmb.json.

1. stop mosmb service on all nodes
2. stop mosmb-zookeeper on all zookeeper nodes
3. run the clean up command
/etc/mosmb/scripts/mosmb_zk-cleanup.sh <zookeeper install directory path>
e.g. /etc/mosmb/scripts/mosmb_zk-cleanup.sh /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin 4. once the clean up is finished, open /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json of the node where you want to change the node_id, and update it. (one node or many nodes of cluster as per your requirement)
5. Save the json files and restart the cluster, first all zk servers and then all mosmb services in the cluster nodes

What if I want to change the node_id for some reason

In a situation, where one wants to change the node_id of a node, from a cluster, it is recommended that full cluster is stopped (MoSMB and Zookeeper both) and a clean up is carried out, before you change the node_id of a node or many nodes in the mosmb.json.

1. stop mosmb service on all nodes
2. stop mosmb-zookeeper on all zookeeper nodes
3. run the clean up command
/etc/mosmb/scripts/mosmb_zk-cleanup.sh <zookeeper install directory path>
e.g. /etc/mosmb/scripts/mosmb_zk-cleanup.sh /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin 4. once the clean up is finished, open /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json of the node where you want to change the node_id, and update it. (one node or many nodes of cluster as per your requirement)
5. Save the json files and restart the cluster, first all zk servers and then all mosmb services in the cluster nodes
In order for MoSMB share access to work with krb5 authentication Kerberos ticket should be generated and the ticket should be valid.
Generating a Kerberos Ticket (Releases before March 2022)

To generate a ticket on the server without having to use the password, use command kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab <SPN>@<DOMAIN NAME> . -k Specifies to obtain the key for the ticket principal from a key table. So you are not prompted to enter the password for the ticket principal. -t will refer to the keytab file specified. For MoSMB it should be /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
Example:
Test Data : mosmbad.dom, cifs/my-mosmb-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM (keytab entry of cifs) Command   : sudo kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab cifs/my-mosmb-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM
Generating Kerberos Ticket (Releases after March 2022)

In new releases, user will NOT be needed to acquire a ticket manually. To generate a ticket on the server, MoSMB will use principal name from /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt , match against the one present in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab and use it to generate the kerberos ticket on MoSMB service start.
Verifying a Kerberos Ticket
To verify if the ticket is acquired for the domain_user, run the klist command.
Example:

Command : sudo klist -A Output  : Ticket cache: KCM:0:96571 Default principal: cifs/my-mosmb-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM Valid starting       Expires              Service principal 05/16/2022 11:33:50  05/16/2022 21:33:50  krbtgt/MOSMBAD.DOM@MOSMBAD.DOM renew until 05/17/2022 11:33:45
Notes :

1. By default, the Kerberos ticket is valid for 10 hours . 
   2. The Kerberos ticket defaults may be modified by the Group Policy Management → Kerberos Policy → Security Settings in the AD.
   3. MoSMB releases after March 22 - Tickets will be auto renewed on every MoSMB start/restart.
   4. On releases after March 22 - ldap ticket will also be taken at mosmb start/restart if ldap configurations are correct.
MoSMB supports Microsoft Active Directory (AD) based Kerberos authentication. The MoSMB server is added to the AD Domain to authenticate and authorize the SMB Client's access to MoSMB shares.
Joining MoSMB Server to Active Directory
You must have a working model of the AD in your Evaluation and/or Production environment before you start using the appliance with kerberos authentication.
You require Active Directory Domain Administrator user to make related modifications.
You can use any one of the following utility to join the MoSMB server to AD.
ADCLI join
Install the realm and dependent packages on the server machine. Command : yum install adcli OR apt-get install adcli

To join the domain with adcli: Command : adcli join --service-name=host -D <AD_domain_name> -U <AD_domain_administrator_user> --host-fqdn= <server_hostname>.<AD_domain_name> Example : adcli join --service-name=host -D MOSMBAD.DOM -U user1@MOSMBAD.DOM --host-fqdn=mymosmbserver.mosmbad.dom Password for user1@MOSMBAD.DOM: Note: The domain join creates a machine entry in the AD. You must update the DNS entries for forward lookup zone and reverse lookup zone manually.

To leave a domain with adcli: Command : adcli delete-computer --domain= <AD_domain_name> -U <AD_domain_administrator_user> Note: This deletes the entry from AD. You must update the DNS entries for forward lookup zone and reverse lookup zone manually.
Realm Join
Install the realm and dependent packages on the server machine. Command : yum install realmd OR apt-get install realm
Search for the domain you wish to join. Command : realm discover <domain name> Note: <domain name> here is the search value from the /etc/resolv.conf file, that is ad.example.com.

To join the domain with realm. Command : realm join --user= <AD_domain_administrator_user> <AD_domain_name> Example : realm join --user=user1 mosmbad.dom Password for user1: Note: The domain join creates a machine entry in the AD. You must update the DNS entries for forward lookup zone and reverse lookup zone manually.
To leave the domain with realm. Command : realm leave <AD_domain_name>
To list the current domain. Command : realm list
Note :
The above mentioned methods of AD joining have been provided solely to guide you. You can perform your network configuration in a method that you are comfortable in.
POSIX ACL is Portable Operating System Interface - Access Control Lists.
Note : POSIX ACL for MoSMB means only the Server user's ID mapping to the MoSMB share and its included files and directories.
MoSMB Configuration
For the POSIX ACL view on a MoSMB share, respective share's configurations are required as well. MoSMB configuration file location: /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json
"aclStrategy":{ "acl": "posix", "checkPosixPermissionBit": "yes" },
In case of Kerberos (krb5) Authentication Mode you must:
Execute a chown command.
In this chown command you must set permissions for the share path to required domain user and domain group .
chown -R “<domain-user>@<domain-name>”: “<group-name>@<domain-name>” <share path>
Example:
chown  -R "mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM":"domain users@MOSMBAD.DOM" /opt/share/
chown -R “<domain-name>\<domain-user>”: “<domain-name>\<group-name>” <share path>
Example:
chown  -R "MOSMBAD\mouser":"MOSMBAD\domain users" /opt/share/

The ACL view will not display proper names in the Security tabs if: 1. The ldap client configurations are not properly carried out. 2. The File or Directory owner/group is not a domain user. In such cases the names will be displayed as:
"LOCAL ACCOUNT" or "S-1-5-113" which is mapped to the owner permissions.
"SYSTEM" which is mapped to group permissions.
"Everyone" which is mapped to other permissions.
If properly configured, the domain user name will be displayed as shown underneath:

MIT kerberos package must be installed on the MoSMB server machine. The krb5-workstation/krb5-user package contains the basic Kerberos programs (kinit, klist, kdestroy, kpasswd). If your network uses Kerberos, this package should be installed on every workstation.
Search and install the Kerberos package on CentOS:
Example:
Command 1 : yum search kerberos Output 1  : .... krb5-workstation.x86_64 : Kerberos 5 programs for use on workstations Command 2 : yum install krb5-workstation.x86_64
Search and install the Kerberos package on Ubuntu:
Example:
Command 1 : apt-cache search krb5 Output 1  : .... krb5-user - basic programs to authenticate using MIT Kerberos Command 2 : apt-get install krb5-user
Search and install the Kerberos package on CentOS:
Example:
Command 1 : zypper search krb5-client Output 1  : .... krb5-client | Client programs of the MIT Kerberos5 implementation | package Command 2 : zypper install krb5-client
You need to verify the server side configuration in order to make sure the connectivity and reachability of MoSMB server to Domain Name Server (DNS).
/etc/resolv.conf
This configuration file is considered a trusted source of DNS information
Verify the /etc/resolv.conf . It should ideally contain the domain nameserver related information. And should be able to resolve all domain's AD and clients
Example:

Command : cat /etc/resolv.conf Output  : # Generated by NetworkManager options rotate options timeout:1 search mosmbad.dom mosmbad2.dom nameserver 10.190.19.101 nameserver 10.200.20.201 nameserver xxx.xx.xxx.x nameserver x.x.x.x

MoSMB Service Not Starting

If mosmb service fails to start, check the mosmb.log from the log folder (Default is /var/log) for Error messages. 
In case there is json syntax error you can use the mosmb json syntax validation script
Configuration Errors
The mosmb.log should indicate if any configuration errors pertaining to the json parameters are encountered. Accordingly use following to correct thoes.
Verify if the MoSMB share path mentioned in the configuration actually exists on the server.
Verify if the persistent store path mentioned in the MoSMB configuration actually exists on the server.

Verify if a valid combination of Authentication Modes and Authorization Modes is in use: i. When the authentication mode is krb5 , the permitted authorization modes are posix and ntacl . ii. When the authentication is ntlmLocal , the permitted authorization mode is posix .
Verify if the plugin path mentioned in the MoSMB configuration is correct.
Verify that the performance parameters like number of threads, buffer size and pre-processor threads are not exceeding the available resources. Refer to Performance Tuning
Verify if the bind interface value mentioned is actually an active interface on the server.
Refer to the MoSMB configuration parameters for further details.
Verify the required ports are open
Refer to Enable ports in firewall for MoSMB
License error in logs
For any Licensing related errors refer to section MoSMB Licensing Issues below
MoSMB Licensing Issues

To start the MoSMB server you require a trial license or a product key. Note : If the MoSMB server is a GCP instance then the following licensing scheme is not applicable. For more detailed information on Licensing refer to MoSMB License Manager page
Online Product Activation
Verify if the MoSMB server machine has a working internet connection.
Verify if the MoSMB server's network adapters are up.
Verify if the firewall policies allow port 443 to connect to the MoSMB license servers.
Verify if https://wyday.com/limelm/ is reachable from the MoSMB server.
Verify if the time and date settings of the MoSMB server are correct or up-to-date.
Verify if a valid product key is provided in the MoSMB configuration file for Licenses with product key.

Verify if the product key has reached the maximum number of activations limit. The information on the maximum limit for the number of machines the product key can be activated is given while providing the product key.
Online Trial Activation
Verify if the MoSMB server has a working internet connection.
Verify if MoSMB server's network adapters are up.
Verify if the firewall policies allow port 443 to connect to the MoSMB license servers.
Verify if https://wyday.com/limelm/ is reachable from the MoSMB server.
Verify if the time and date settings for the MoSMB server machine are correct or up-to-date.
Offline Product Activation
Verify if the MoSMB server's network adapters are up.
Verify if the product key mentioned in the MoSMB configuration and the product key used while activating the license is the same.
Make sure you have followed the steps for activation as per the offline product activation using product key
Offline Trial Activation
Verify if the trialresponse.xml file that is provided to you is placed at the /etc/mosmb/license/ location.
Verify if the trialrequest.xml is generated on the same MoSMB server machine on which you need to activate the MoSMB license.
Verify if the MoSMB server's network adapters are up.
Failed to check activation within grace period

When the Error - Failed to check product activation within grace period is seen in mosmb.log It would indicate that the product key was activated online on the server machine BUT now the server machine is not able to reach to | limelm site To activate the product key again, deactivate the current product key activation by running /etc/mosmb/license/.mosmb_license_deactivate Then follow offline activation steps
MoSMB Share Not Accessible
Check the mosmb.log for any obvious error messages that would indicate what is the problem.
Check if mosmb service is running, check pid or process information using ps

# service mosmb status
# pidof mosmb
# ps -aux| grep mosmb
Verify if port 445 is open for the TCP communication.
Verify if the TCP port mentioned in the MoSMB configuration file is also configured on the client machine and is open for communication over TCP.
If error is related to krb5, Refer to the Troubleshooting for the AD Kerberos MoSMB share access when authentication mode used for share access is krb5
If error is related to gss-api refer to the troubleshooting section for gss-api errors
If the authentication mode is ntlmLocal make sure that the (local linux) MoSMB user is created on the MoSMB server machine and you are accessing the share via the same user.

check the mosmb_audit.log for login from the user for whom the access is failing, to check if Login attempt is failing or user has successfully logged in.  Refer here for more details on the mosmb_audit.log
If Login is successful, Verify if the user has the permission to access the share for the authentication mode used (krb5, ntlm-AD or ntlm_local) as mentioned in Permission Issues section below.

If a previously working node is removed and Re Joined to AD , for ntlmAD to work after such a change, you will need to re run mosmb_passwd -m and restart mosmb service too. Also old kerberos ticket on client side may prevent you from accessing the share. You need to purge the cache to remove old cluster/node kerberos ticket with klist purge OR user can sign out and signing to the client. OR The kerberos cache will expire in scheduled 10 hours and next access will renew the ticket automatically.
MoSMB Share Not Accessible on Ubuntu 22.04 OR error seen with mosmb_passwd binary
possible error seen with mosmb_passwd :
ValueError: unsupported hash type md4
Verify if openssl configuration required for Ubuntu 22.04 are done
If the server machines have openssl version 3 and above configuration changes are required. Check openssl version with
openssl version
Open /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf
Existing/Default Configuration :

[provider_sect]
   default = default_sect
   [default_sect]
   # activate = 1
Edits/Required Configuration :

[provider_sect]
   default = default_sect legacy = legacy_sect [openssl_init]
   providers = provider_sect
 
   [default_sect] activate = 1 [legacy_sect] activate = 1
MoSMB Share Not Accessible for only a particular user

If access fails for a user, make sure audit logs are enabled and verify login attempts from the user for mosmb nodes. Check the mosmb_audit.log to see if Login attempt is failing or user has successfully logged in.

If login is successful check permission issues. 
If login is failed check the authentication password/domain information
If no login attempt message is seen check mosmb.log for MO_ERROR or MO_WARN logs
Permission Issues
Verify that the correct user permissions are set on the MoSMB server side and the same user is used to access the share and create or edit the shared files or directories.
POSIX Authorization Settings
NTACL Authorization Settings
Viewing File / Directory Permissions
Verify if port 88 is open on both the MoSMB server and the AD machine. This is required by the LDAP server.
When the authentication mode is krb5 verify if the Kerberos ticket is present on the MoSMB server.
In case the authentication mode is krb5 verify if all the LDAP client configuration settings are carried out.
In case the authentication mode is krb5 verify if all the SSSD related settings are carried out.
Verify if the package necessary for generating UUID is installed on the MoSMB server. If not please install the package.
ID Mapper Not Working Correctly
Verify if the sssd service is running on the MoSMB server.
check with id command that the AD user's details are retrieved and it does print the user ID and group IDs. In case it is failing to do so, try restarting the sssd service.
Verify the nsswitch.conf file and make sure that .sss is mentioned in the configuration.

Changes to the AD group membership are slow to take effect. The issue could be because of the sssd cache. When you change the primary group of the user then the old entry in the sssd cache becomes invalid. For new changes to take effect restart the sssd service and run the following command: sss_cache -E OR sss_cache -u <user-name>

If you are considering EVERYONE present in NTFS (Everyone includes the owner, group and other members) as Others (in Linux this means all the members other than the owner and group members) mapping will fail.
Note : In the case of user and group mapping, MoSMB currently supports only the primary group mapping.
Issue with cluster keytab. Cluster fqdn access not working. (gss-api errors in mosmb.log)
Check time is in sync with the AD
Make sure the keytab creation is correctly done using ktpass on AD by the Admin (w.o. any errors or warning during the creation) using correct CRYPTO values used to generate keys in the keytab.

Use following PS cmdlet to check the spn is correctly mapped the special user setspn -Q cifs/<cifs-server-name> And use the follwoing PS cmdlet to check the user's SPN Get-ADUser <username> -property ServicePrincipalName
The DNS forward lookup works for the cluster fqdn and reverse lookup work correctly for cluster node ips
Check firewall for correct ports opened for kerberos
Check that the special user created for keytab creation in the ktpass 'mapuser', has following properties set:

Go to Active Directory users and computers 
User properties -> user account -> password never expires
User properties -> user account -> Correct encryption support (in accordance with the CRYPTO values used in ktpass) is enabled, e.g. This account supports Kerberos AES 256 bit encryption This account supports Kerberos AES 128 bit encryption You can also check the encryptions used in the keytab with -e option of klist klist -kte /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
OR use following PS cmdlet to check the user's supported encryption types Get-ADUser <username> -property KerberosEncryptionType
Check KVno from keytab against the special user.

Note the msDS-KeyVersionNumber from the o/p of following PS cmdlet and compare with kvno in the keytab from the output of klist ran on the mosmb.keytab Get-ADUser <username> -property msDS-KeyVersionNumber klist -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
In mosmb.json, check that globalSection is set to have enableHighAvailability to yes
Check the o/p of klist -A to check a valid krb5 tkt is fetched during mosmb startup ( mosmb will use the mosmb.keytab and domain_list.txt to do a kinit )
Check sssd.conf, krb5.conf, ldap.conf are set in accordance with the mosmb documentation
Check permitted encryption types from the krb5 crypto-policies
cat /etc/krb5.conf.d/crypto-policies

If setup was working fine previously, and now we are getting "Preauthentication failed" error in mosmb.log, verify that the password for special user used for cluster keytab creation is not expired or changed. If so, need to recreate the mosmb.keytab using ktpass

ZooKeeper Troubleshooting
1. Error 1:
Unable to start AdminServer, exiting abnormally
Solution : Check for admin.serverPort configuration in zoo.cfg :
#admin server port configuration admin.serverPort=8082
Make sure port 8082 is free for use, if not free, use some other port
netstat -ntlp | grep 8082
2. Error 2:
java.net.BindException: Address already in use (Bind failed)
Solution : Check if ports configured are available, if not free then use some other port combination
netstat -ntlp | grep 4888 & netstat -ntlp | grep 5888 on all ZooKeeper Server machines.

Example :	
server.1 = <ip_server1>:4888:5888
server.2 = <ip_server2>:4888:5888
server.3 = <ip_server3>:4888:5888
Here 4888 used for follower nodes to connect to the leader node and 5888 used for leader election.
3. MoSMB service not starting due to older zookeeper versions refer :- Uninstalling previous zookeeper version
4. Make sure after successful installation all required zookeeper libraries are present on the zookeeper server.
Microsoft Management Console (MMC)
Manage File Shares with Shared Folders MMC Snap-in. Close sessions and close open file handles can be managed via MMC
Open mmc on a windows → Navigate to the Start Menu and select Run, type 'mmc'.
On the MMC console from File menu select Add or Remove snap-ins
Select and add Shared Folders snap-in
In Shared Folder properties → Enter the node fqdn or ip and connect.
Check Shares to list the smb-shares on the nodes.
Check Sessions to list the smb-sessions connected to the node.
Check Open Files to list all the open files on the node.
MAC slow browsing performance
NOTE: sonoma 14.6.1 onwards nsmb.conf does not help, refer to below points for older MAC OS versions
To improve MAC smb client's browsing performance, following settings can be tried 1. edit /etc/nsmb.conf

[default]
dir_cache_off=yes
aapl_off=true
signing_required=no
file_ids_off=yes
dir_ids_off=yes
defaults write com.apple.desktopservices DSDontWriteNetworkStores -bool TRUE
On terminal run defaults write com.apple.desktopservices DSDontWriteNetworkStores -bool TRUE
check the setting with defaults read com.apple.desktopservices DSDontWriteNetworkStores
Restart your MAC once these changes are done
2. Disable icon previews and size calculation

connect to share
Browse to share
Press CMD + j (or rt click-> show view options )
  Disable “Calculate all sizes”
  Disable “Show icon preview”
Select “Use as Defaults”
3. some more view options
Going further u can disable showing 'date modified' and 'size' in the view options
4. Turn off indexing spotlight indexing can be turned off with
sudo mdutil -a -i off /Volumes/<share-name>
To see whether Spotlight indexing is enabled for a volume:

mdutil -s -v /Volumes/share-name
 example: mdutil -s /Volumes/share1 /System/Volumes/Data/Volumes/share1:
    Indexing and searching disabled.
5. 311 smb protocol in mosmb.json on releases 072 and later Try changing the max smb protocol to 311 on the mosmb server in mosmb.json file and restart mosmb service
maxSmbProtocol = 311
6. If access is still slow; try some more network settings
try following n/w settings on MAC , run from terminal

sysctl -w kern.ipc.maxsockbuf=8388608
sysctl -w net.inet.tcp.sendspace=2097152
sysctl -w net.inet.tcp.recvspace=2097152
sysctl -w net.inet.tcp.delayed_ack=2
sysctl -w net.inet.tcp.win_scale_factor=8
sysctl -w net.inet.tcp.autorcvbufmax=33554432
sysctl -w net.inet.tcp.autosndbufmax=33554432
No restart required, if restarted the settings will be lost. Some more settings

sysctl -w net.smb.fs.kern_deadtimer=0
sysctl -w net.smb.fs.kern_hard_deadtimer=0
sysctl -w net.smb.fs.kern_soft_deadtimer=0

In addition, if your Mac needs above network settings to be retained on reboot.
Create a file named /etc/sysctl.conf in your local /etc folder
This needs again to be done as root!

Go to the command line, become root and fire up your preferred editor (vi) by typing vi /etc/sysctl.conf
Put the following into the newly created file,

vi /etc/sysctl.conf 
kern.ipc.maxsockbuf=8388608
net.inet.tcp.sendspace=2097152
net.inet.tcp.recvspace=2097152
net.inet.tcp.delayed_ack=2
net.inet.tcp.win_scale_factor=8
net.inet.tcp.autorcvbufmax=33554432
net.inet.tcp.autosndbufmax=33554433
net.smb.fs.kern_deadtimer=0
net.smb.fs.kern_hard_deadtimer=0
net.smb.fs.kern_soft_deadtimer=0

and save the file. The file rights must be set to:
owner root:wheel and -rw-r--r--
please make sure by typing ls -al /etc/sysctl.conf on the command line and if not, change it by running
chown root:wheel /etc/sysctl.conf
and
chmod 0644 /etc/sysctl.conf from the command line

MoSMB provides users with a product key to activate the MoSMB product.
Users can activate the product in two ways:
Online: For MoSMB servers with an active network connection.
Offline: For MoSMB servers that do not have any active network connection.
To start the MoSMB server you require a trial license or a product key. Note : If the MoSMB server is a GCP instance then the following licensing scheme is not applicable.
Checks for Online Activation
Verify if the MoSMB server machine has a working and active internet connection.
Verify if all network adapters of MoSMB server's are up.
Verify if the firewall policies allow port 443 to connect to the MoSMB license servers.
Verify if the time and date settings of the MoSMB server are correct or up-to-date.
Verify if https://wyday.com/limelm/ is reachable from the MoSMB server.
MoSMB checks license validity in certain time period for online activation MoSMB server must always be able to communicate with https://wyday.com/limelm
Checks of Offline Activation
Verify if the trialrequest.xml or request.xml is generated on the same MoSMB server machine on which you need to activate the MoSMB license.
Verify if the trialresponse.xml or response.xml file provided to you is placed at the /etc/mosmb/license/ location.
Verify if the product key mentioned in the MoSMB configuration and the product key used while activating the license is the same.

There are three types of license keys:
Trial License Key
Trial License Extension Key
Product key
Online Activation of the Product
Trial License Key
In the case of a trial license the Product key parameter in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file should be empty - "productKey": ""
Restart the MoSMB service for the changes made in MoSMB configuration file to take effect.
Note : The Trial license will be activated only once per server. After expiry contact the MoSMB support
support@ryussi.com for the Trial Extension Key or to purchase the Product Key .
Trial Extension Key
Contact the MoSMB support support@ryussi.com for a trial extension key.
In the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file in the Product key section enter the trial extension key.
Restart the MoSMB service for the changes made in MoSMB configuration file to take effect.
Note : Make sure you remove all the previous trialRequest.xml and trialResponse.xml files from the /etc/mosmb/license folder
Product Key
Contact the MoSMB support support@ryussi.com to purchase the product key.
In the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file in the Product key section enter the product key provided.
For the changes made in the MoSMB configuration file to take effect Restart the MoSMB service.
Note : Make sure you remove all the previous trialRequest.xml and trialResponse.xml files if at all they exist from previous offline activations, from the /etc/mosmb/license folder
Offline Activation of the Product
Trial/Trial Extension License Key
In the case of a trial license the Product key parameter in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file should be empty - "productKey": ""
Run the /etc/mosmb/license/mosmb_offline_trial binary as follows: /etc/mosmb/license/mosmb_offline_trial
This binary will generate a trialRequest.xml file at the /etc/mosmb/license/ location.
Send this file to the MoSMB support support@ryussi.com and we will share a response trialResponse.xml file.
Copy this trialResponse.xml file at the /etc/mosmb/license/ location.
Restart the MoSMB service for the changes to take effect.
Note : The Trial license will be activated only once per machine. After expiry contact the MoSMB support support@ryussi.com for the Trial Extension Key or to purchase the Product Key.
Product Key
Contact the MoSMB support support@ryussi.com to purchase the product key.

Run the /etc/mosmb/license/mosmb_offline_license binary as follows: /etc/mosmb/license/mosmb_offline_license <Product Key> The Product Key should be the one mentioned in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file.
This binary will generate a request.xml file at the /etc/mosmb/license/ location.
Send this file to MoSMB support support@ryussi.com and we will share a response.xml response file.
Copy the response.xml file to the /etc/mosmb/license/ location.
Restart the MoSMB service for the changes to take effect.

Note :Make sure you remove all the previous trialRequest.xml and trialResponse.xml files from the /etc/mosmb/license folder Alternatively , for a cluster setup, which is set to use clush (clustershell), mosmbcli get-req and deploy-resp options can be used to get the request.xml files from all nodes and put in a tar file which can be then emailed to the MoSMB support support@ryussi.com
Applying changes made to a product key - offline
If old request.xml exists in the license folder (/etc/mosmb/license/) then we use that to generate new responses

If old request.xml does not exist in the license folder, and license is still valid, to generate request.xml, we need to deactivate first (request.xml is not generated if product is activated) /etc/mosmb/license/.mosmb_license_deactivate

Run the /etc/mosmb/license/mosmb_offline_license binary as follows: etc/mosmb/license/mosmb_offline_license <Product Key> The Product Key should be the one mentioned in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file.
This binary will generate a request.xml file at the /etc/mosmb/license/ location.
Send the request.xml file to MoSMB support support@ryussi.com and we will share a response.xml response file.
Copy the response.xml file to the /etc/mosmb/license/ location.
if product is already deactivated, Restart the MoSMB service for the license changes to take effect.
if old request was used, then deactivate now.. and then restart service
Applying changes made to a product key - online
If license is still valid, we need to deactivate first /etc/mosmb/license/.mosmb_license_deactivate
Then restart mosmb service for the new activation to take effect
Error Codes
Domain list configurations are needed by MoSMB for completing sssd configuration settings.
Multi-Domain Standalone Server Mode (Releases after March 2022)

In new releases, /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt will now use SPN entries instead of domain names. Entries should be in the format of spn@DOMAIN , similar to the SPNs and will be case sensitive. Ensure that you make an each entry on a new line. Entries in /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt, must match against the one present in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab (the users we used during mosmb.keytab creation using ktutil to be put here)
Example:
Command : cat /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt Output  : mouser1@MOSMBAD.DOM mouser2@MOSMBAD2.DOM
Muilt-Domain Standalone Server Mode (Releases before March 2022)
Create a file named domain_list.txt at the /etc/mosmb/ location and mention all the domains to which the MoSMB server is joined. Ensure that you make an entry for domain on a new line.
Example:
Command : cat /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt Output  : mosmbad.dom mosmbad2.dom

Accessing S3 share
Once configurations are completed, start mosmb service
service mosmb start
Access shares from SMB clients using IP address/node fqdn of MoSMB server
Refer here for more details about S3 object store and its features
In order for MoSMB share access to work with krb5 authentication Kerberos ticket should be generated and the ticket should be valid.
Generating a Kerberos Ticket (Releases before March 2022)

To generate a ticket on the server run command kinit -V <AD_Domain_username>@<DOMAIN NAME> . The command asks password for the specified user. Make sure ticket is taken for all the domains to which MoSMB server is getting configured for.
Example:

Test Data : mosmbad.dom, mouser1 (domain user of mosmbad.dom), mosmbad2.dom, mouser2 (domain user of mosmbad2.dom) Command   : sudo kinit -V mouser1@MOSMBAD.DOM Output    : Password for mouser1@MOSMBAD.DOM: Command   : sudo kinit -V mouser2@MOSMBAD2.DOM Output    : Password for mouser2@MOSMBAD2.DOM:

OR To generate a ticket on the server without having to use the password, use command kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab <AD_Domain_username>@<DOMAIN NAME> . -k Specifies to obtain the key for the ticket principal from a key table. So you are not prompted to enter the password for the ticket principal. -t will refer to the keytab file specified. For MoSMB it should be /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
Example:

Test Data : mosmbad.dom, mouser1 (domain user of mosmbad.dom), mosmbad2.dom, mouser2 (domain user of mosmbad2.dom) [all domain users entries present mosmb.keytab] Command   : sudo kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab mouser1@MOSMBAD.DOM Command   : sudo kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab mouser2@MOSMBAD2.DOM
Notes :

1. The domain name in above commands must be in UPPERCASE to avoid KDC mismatch issues while getting credentials.
   2. Check the ticket time with klist -A, (time of MoSMB server and AD should be always synced or Kerberos authentication may fail)
Generating Kerberos Ticket (Releases after March 2022)

In new releases, user will NOT be needed to acquire a ticket manually. To generate a ticket on the server, MoSMB will use principal name from /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt , match against the one present in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab and use it to generate the kerberos ticket on MoSMB service start.
Verifying a Kerberos Ticket
To verify if the ticket is acquired for the domain_user, run the klist command.
Example:

Command : sudo klist -A Output  : Ticket cache: DIR:/opt/mosmb/kerberos_tkt/tktAD124 Default principal: mouser1@MOSMBAD.DOM Valid starting       Expires              Service principal 01/16/2020 11:33:50  01/16/2020 21:33:50  krbtgt/MOSMBAD.DOM@MOSMBAD.DOM renew until 01/17/2020 11:33:45 01/16/2020 11:33:50  01/16/2020 21:33:50   ldap/<AD1 name>.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM renew until 01/17/2020 11:33:45 Ticket cache: DIR:/opt/mosmb/kerberos_tkt/tkt12qun Default principal: mouser2@MOSMBAD2.DOM Valid starting       Expires              Service principal 01/16/2020 11:33:50  01/16/2020 21:33:50  krbtgt/MOSMBAD2.DOM@MOSMBAD2.DOM renew until 01/17/2020 11:33:45 01/16/2020 11:33:50  01/16/2020 21:33:50   ldap/<AD2 name>.mosmbad2.dom@MOSMBAD2.DOM renew until 01/17/2020 11:33:45
Notes :

1. By default, the Kerberos ticket is valid for 10 hours . 
   2. The Kerberos ticket defaults may be modified by the Group Policy Management → Kerberos Policy → Security Settings in the AD.
   3. MoSMB releases after March 22 - Tickets will be auto renewed on every MoSMB start/restart.
   4. On releases after March 22 - ldap ticket will also be taken at MoSMB start/restart if ldap configurations are correct.

To install the ZooKeeper Server, perform the following procedure:

Prerequisites
The ZooKeeper installation has the following dependencies:
Ubuntu / Debian
1. openjdk-8-jdk-headless
Redhat based Linux distributions
1. java-11-openjdk or higher
Zookeeper installation on Redhat based Linux distributions using rpm for New Releases after March 2022

For Cluster builds, a zookeeper rpm will also be provided along with MoSMB rpm. MoSMB-zookeeper rpm is present at : /opt/mosmb/package/mosmb_install To install you can use following command:
rpm -ivh /opt/mosmb/package/mosmb_install/mosmb-zookeeper-3.6.3-1.x86_64.rpm
Verifying...                          ################################# [100%]
Preparing...                          ################################# [100%]
Updating / installing...
   1:mosmb-zookeeper-3.6.3-1          ################################# [100%]
zookeeper is installed at : /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/
zookeeper will be installed at /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/ and user will need to make appropriate changes to zoo.cfg and then start zkServer as mentioned in above steps.
# /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh start
Get ZooKeeper for other builds and distributions
1. Get zookeeper installer apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin
wget https://archive.apache.org/dist/zookeeper/zookeeper-3.6.3/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin.tar.gz
2. Extract downloaded packages
tar -xvzf apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin.tar.gz
Configure the ZooKeeper Server
1. Go to other extracted directory apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin 2. Copy sample ZooKeeper configuration file from /etc/mosmb/.
cp /etc/mosmb/mosmb_zoo.cfg conf/zoo.cfg
3.  Create ZooKeeper server data directory can be any preferred location other than /tmp.
mkdir -p /var/lib/zookeeper-mosmb ..........
4.  ZooKeeper Standalone Server Configuration
5. ZooKeeper Cluster Server Configuration
For detailed information on the configuration parameters refer to the ZooKeeper Server Configuration Parameters page.
6. Create the myid file on the ZooKeeper server/s at the /var/lib/zookeeper-mosmb/ path.
Example on a Cluster ZooKeeper:

Test data                      : ZooKeeper Server IP list - 10.10.0.1, 10.10.0.2, 10.10.0.3 File on server 1 (10.10.0.1)   : cat /var/lib/zookeeper-mosmb/myid Output on server 1 (10.10.0.1) : 1 File on server 2 (10.10.0.2)   : cat /var/lib/zookeeper-mosmb/myid Output on server 2 (10.10.0.2) : 2 File on server 3 (10.10.0.3)   : cat /var/lib/zookeeper-mosmb/myid Output on server 3 (10.10.0.3) : 3
Setup zookeepr server side log rotation

This step is not mandatory. In case zookeeper restarts, the server side log located in the log folder of zookeeper installation path, (/opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/logs/) is overwritten and old logs are over-written/lost. To avoid loss of old logs, one can do the following
1. A solution is to modify zkEnv.sh and set ZOO_LOG4J_PROP

$ cat /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkEnv.sh | grep -i PROP
if [ "x${ZOO_LOG4J_PROP}" = "x" ]
 ZOO_LOG4J_PROP="INFO,CONSOLE"
2. here add ROLLINGFILE

$ cat /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkEnv.sh | grep -i PROP
if [ "x${ZOO_LOG4J_PROP}" = "x" ]
  ZOO_LOG4J_PROP="INFO,CONSOLE, ROLLINGFILE "
3. Now every restart of mosmb-zookeeper service will have two log files, one current (.out) and one backed up with old logs (.log).
Start the ZooKeeper Server
1. Go to the ZooKeeper directory apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin and run the following command:
./bin/zkServer.sh start
This will create a ZooKeeper log file at location ../apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/logs/ that will contain the ZooKeeper server related logs. 2. Check status.
Example for a Standalone ZooKeeper Server:
Command   : ./bin/zkServer.sh status Output    : JMX enabled by default Using config: ../apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/conf/zoo.cfg Mode: Standalone
Example for a Cluster ZooKeeper Server:

Test data : ZooKeeper Server IP list - 10.10.0.1, 10.10.0.2, 10.10.0.3 Command   : ./bin/zkServer.sh status Output    : JMX enabled by default Using config: ../apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/conf/zoo.cfg' Mode: Leader (This output will be different on all server one will be the leader and other servers will be Follower )

rpm -e can be used to uninstall the zookeeper rpm.
# rpm -e mosmb-zookeeper-3.6.3-1.x86_64
Uninstall Previous older ZooKeeper Versions
1. Stop older version of ZooKeeper service (Older than zookeeper 3.6.3)
./bin/zkServer.sh stop
2. Go to zookeeper’s previous version's source directory
For zookeeper 3.4.5 - zookeeper3.4.5/src/c
For zookeeper 3.6.1 - apache-zookeeper-3.6.1/zookeeper-client/zookeeper-client-c/
3. Run command -
make clean make uninstall
4. Make sure all previous library files are not on the zookeeper server machine -
find /usr/ -name libzookeeper*
5. Continue with required ZooKeeper version build and installation.
ZooKeeper - MoSMB Configuration Settings
Note:
ZooKeeper is a centralized service for maintaining configuration information, naming, providing distributed synchronization, and providing group services.
You must make ZooKeeper settings to use the MoSMB server. This depends on the MoSMB edition, it will be required for MoSMB-CL edition & MoSMB-SA-S3 edition .

Standalone ZooKeeper Server : MoSMB is set by default to work with the local Standalone ZooKeeper Server , as shown in the Standalone ZooKeeper Server Settings image underneath (setting in mosmb.json).
Remote ZooKeeper Server : For Remote ZooKeeper Server Settings in mosmb.json you can state the Hostport parameter as:
<server_hostname> : <zookeeper_client_port> OR <server_ip_address> : <zookeeper_client_port>
Clustered ZooKeeper Server : If required you can make the Clustered ZooKeeper Server settings. Refer to the Clustered ZooKeeper Server Settings image underneath.
The detailed ZooKeeper Server Configuration parameters are provided in the Zookeeper Server Configuration File Details .
mosmb.json representation -  Standalone Zookeeper Server Settings

mosmb.json representation -  Standalone Zookeeper Server Settings

Note : A MoSMB server restart is mandatory in case any changes are made to the configuration file.
All MoSMB-CL builds supports multi domain clustering. To form a multi domain MoSMB cluster simply follow the configurations mentioned in steps below one by one.

1. Carry out Network Time Protocol (NTP) Configuration settings on all MoSMB server nodes. 2. Install Kerberos packages on all MoSMB server nodes 3. Request the Domain Administration team for AD and DNS Configurations.
Create two-way AD trust between domains.
Add conditional forwarders in the DNS manager w.r.t other domains.
Create a Special user in the Active Directory (AD) (Step required to be carried out only once per MoSMB cluster)
Create MoSMB Cluster Keytab (Step required to be carried out only once per MoSMB cluster)
Configure DNS entries for the MoSMB Cluster
Carry out name resolution settings on all MoSMB servers nodes.
4. Kerberos and related services configurations on all MoSMB server nodes in the cluster.
Configure MoSMB krb5 settings
Configure LDAP client settings
Configure SSSD settings
Configure the domain_list.txt settings
For release versions:3.1.1_000 refer - MoSMB Kerberos Ticket Generation
For release versions:3.1.1_001 and above refer - MoSMB Kerberos Ticket Generation.

5. MoSMB server now supports NTLMSSP authentication from Release versions:3.1.1_004 and above. For each MoSMB server node in cluster, carry out these changes to get the NTLMSSP authentication support working.
6. MoSMB server is ZooKeeper client; Perform MoSMB -ZooKeeper settings
MoSMB-ZooKeeper Server installation
MoSMB-ZooKeeper Cluster Configuration
Start MoSMB-ZooKeeper
7. Carry out required authorization (ACL) settings
Using MoSMB with Posix ACL OR
Using MoSMB with NTACL
8. Setup the MoSMB configuration file (/etc/mosmb/mosmb.json) for cluster use.
In globalSection set enableHighAvailability to yes
In persistentStoreSection keep pstorePath location on the common storage path on all MoSMB nodes in the cluster.
In section nodeInfoSection , assign a different nodeId to each node in MoSMB cluster.

In section authSection , set authMode as ntlmLocal . (NOTE: This will ensure krb5 authentication is tried first, if that is not successful, the new releases support ntlmSSP authentication, if that fails then ntlmLocal authentication is tried.)
In shareSection keep sharePath location on the common storage path on all MoSMB nodes in the cluster.
In shareSection set parameters isContinouslyAvailable and isCluster to yes
Example:
9. If the server machines are centos(or similar) 8 and above supported encryption options needs to be enabled from server side as well in order for Kerberos authentication to work completely.

On every CentOS 8.3 and above MoSMB server node, where authentication against AD Domain Controllers takes place, use the update-crypto-policies command to enable the AD-SUPPORT cryptographic subpolicy in addition to the DEFAULT cryptographic policy.

Command : update-crypto-policies --set DEFAULT:AD-SUPPORT Output : Setting system policy to DEFAULT:AD-SUPPORT Command : init 6 Notes : 1. System-wide crypto policies are applied on application start-up so it is recommended to restart the system for the change of policies to fully take place.
           3. The AD-SUPPORT cryptographic subpolicy is only available on CentOS 8.3 and newer.

To enable support for RC4 in CentOS 8.2 , create and enable a custom cryptographic module policy with cipher = RC4-128+. For more details, see Customizing system-wide cryptographic policies with policy modifiers. Reference links :
1. Using the system wide cryptographic policies 2. how to customize crypto policies rhel 8.2
To enable support for RC4 in CentOS 8.0 and CentOS 8.1 , add +rc4 to the permitted_enctypes option in the /etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/krb5.config file

[libdefaults]
           permitted_enctypes = aes256-cts-hmac-sha1-96 aes256-cts-hmac-sha384-192 camellia256-cts-cmac aes128-cts-hmac-sha1-96 aes128-cts-hmac-sha256-128 camellia128-cts-cmac +rc4
10. If the server machines have openssl version 3 and above configuration changes are required.
Open /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf
Existing/Default Configuration :

[provider_sect]
   default = default_sect
   [default_sect]
   # activate = 1
Edits/Required Configuration :

[provider_sect]
   default = default_sect legacy = legacy_sect [openssl_init]
   providers = provider_sect
 
   [default_sect] activate = 1 [legacy_sect] activate = 1
11. Start MoSMB service on all nodes in the MoSMB cluster and check service mosmb status and check the mosmb.log for details. You can check details of MoSMB server Maintenance here
Notes :

1. Make sure ZooKeeper standalone server / ZooKeeper cluster is running.
   2. Make sure sssd service is running on all MoSMB cluster nodes.  
   3. Make sure pstorePath is not the same directory or any sub-directory within the smb share 
   4. Make sure all the MoSMB configuration on all cluster nodes is similar. 
   5. To know other mosmb.json parameters in details refer this .
