Installing Kerberos

The MoSMB server authenticates the AD Domain users using Kerberos authentication method. This requires a Kerberos ticket granted to the server by configured AD machine. This ticket generation can be done using the kinit commands.
On CentOS
Search and install the Kerberos package on CentOS:
Example:
Command 1 : yum search kerberos Output 1  : .... krb5-workstation.x86_64 : Kerberos 5 programs for use on workstations Command 2 : yum install krb5-workstation.x86_64
On Ubuntu
Search and install the Kerberos package on Ubuntu:
Example:
Command 1 : apt-cache search krb5 Output 1  : .... krb5-user - basic programs to authenticate using MIT Kerberos Command 2 : apt-get install krb5-user
krb5.conf
Make sure these entries are present in the /etc/krb5.conf file.
Include directory - /etc/krb5.conf.d
Modify libdefaults section - rdns, default_ccache_name entries are present refer - cache type limitations
Example:
includedir /etc/krb5.conf.d/ [libdefaults] .......... rdns = false default_ccache_name = FILE:/opt/mosmb/kerberos_tkt ..........
Create and open a new file /etc/krb5.conf.d/mosmb_krb5.conf
Add realms section - kdc and admin_server entries.
Add domain_realm section - domain name entry is present.
Example:
[realms] MOSMBAD.DOM = { kdc = mo-ad.mosmbad.dom master_kdc = mo-ad.mosmbad.dom } [domain_realm] mosmbad.dom = MOSMBAD.DOM .mosmbad.dom = MOSMBAD.DOM
Limitations for cache type
with kerberos 1.13 and above  MoSMB support cache types FILE, KCM, DIR. keyring is not supported.

# cat /etc/krb5.conf | grep default_ccache_name
       default_ccache_name = FILE:/opt/mosmb/mosmb_kerberos_ticket OR default_ccache_name = DIR:/opt/mosmb/kerberos_ticket_dir #default_ccache_name = KEYRING:persistent:%{uid}      [not supported] # cat /etc/krb5.conf.d/kcm_default_ccache | grep default_ccache_name
       default_ccache_name = KCM:
Notes :
MoSMB will support only cache type FILE for kerberos versions below 1.13
The FILE and DIR path can be of your choice. Make sure path is anything other than /tmp for persistence
mosmb.keytab
Standalone Server MoSMB Keytab generation
The MoSMB keytab location is /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab

Assumption is domain join is carried out successfully and there are no restricted entries for kerberos on configured AD. If restricted entries are present on AD side please go through Removing restricted Kerberos entries
Run the ktutil to Read and add existing host keys to /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab from krb5.keytab and then add required entries.
Run the ktutil commands as follows:

ktutil ktutil: rkt /etc/krb5.keytab
   ktutil: addent -password -p <domain user>@<DOMAIN NAME> -k 1 -e rc4-hmac Password for <domain user>@<domain name>: 
   
   ktutil: addent -password -p <domain user>@<DOMAIN NAME> -k 1 -e aes256-cts Password for <domain user>@<domain name>: 
   
   ktutil: wkt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab ktutil: q
Example:

ktutil ktutil: rkt /etc/krb5.keytab
   ktutil: addent -password -p mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM -k 1 -e rc4-hmac Password for mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM:

   ktutil: addent -password -p mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM -k 1 -e aes256-cts Password for mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM: 
   
   ktutil: wkt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab ktutil: q
Notes :
The domain user used in above commands and the user used to generate the ticket (as explained in next few steps using kinit) should be the same.
The domain name in above commands must be in uppercase.
If and when the user password or user spn is changed Kerberos keytab related settings are needed to carry out again
MoSMB Cluster Keytab generation
Refer to keytab generation steps in section Cluster Keytab Generation
Removing restricted Kerberos entries

When there are restricted Kerberos entries on the server machine seen as output of command klist -k /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab Check if they are also present on the AD side if present we need to remove them from AD,
Login to AD machine with user who has admin rights and open Active directory administrative Center
Open computers section
In computers open properties of the MoSMB server machine
In Properties of server machine  → Extensions → Attribute Editor
In Attribute editor select the attribute "servicePrincipalName"
Remove restricted entries from the servicePrincipalName against the MoSMB server.
Example -

restrictedkrbhost/moserver1.mosmbad.dom
   restrictedkrbhost/MOSERVER1
Note :
After making changes on AD machine destroy the previously taken Kerberos tickets if any from server machine with kdestroy -A and generate new ticket.
Generating a Kerberos Ticket (Releases before March 2022)
To generate a ticket on the server use the kinit -V <AD_Domain_username>@<DOMAIN NAME> command. The command asks password for the specified user.
Example:
Test Data : mosmbad.dom, mouser (domain user) Command   : sudo kinit -V mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM Output    : Password for mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM:

OR To generate a ticket on the server without having to use the password, use command kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab <AD_Domain_username>@<DOMAIN NAME> . -k Specifies to obtain the key for the ticket principal from a key table. So you are not prompted to enter the password for the ticket principal, and -t will refer to the keytab file specified
Example:
Test Data : mosmbad.dom, mouser (domain user) Command   : sudo kinit -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM
Note :
The domain name in above commands must be in UPPERCASE to avoid KDC mismatch issues while getting credentials.
Also check the ticket time with klist -a, (time of MoSMB server and AD should be always synced or Kerberos authentication may fail)
Generating Kerberos Ticket (Releases after March 2022)

In new releases, user will NOT be needed to acquire a ticket manually. /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt will now use SPN entries instead of domain names. Entries will be in the format of user@DOMAIN / cifs/user.domain@DOMAIN for standalone server, similar to the SPNs and will be case sensitive . To generate a ticket on the server, MoSMB will use principal name from domain_list.txt, match against the one present in mosmb.keytab and use it to generate the kerberos ticket.
Example for STANDALONE Server

# cat /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM # klist -k /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab name: FILE:/etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
   KVNO Principal---- --------------------------------------------
    .
   2 mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM
   2 mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM
Example for CLUSTER
# cat /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt cifs/my-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM

# klist -k /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab name: FILE:/etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
    KVNO Principal---- -------------------------------------------- 
   3 cifs/my-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM 
   3 cifs/my-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM 
   3 cifs/my-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM 
   3 cifs/my-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM 
   3 cifs/my-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM
Verifying a Kerberos Ticket
To verify if the ticket is acquired for the domain_user, run the klist command.
Example:

Command : sudo klist -A Output  : Ticket cache: FILE:/opt/mosmb/kerberos_tkt Default principal: mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM Valid starting       Expires              Service principal 01/16/2020 11:33:50  01/16/2020 21:33:50  krbtgt/MOSMBAD.DOM@MOSMBAD.DOM renew until 01/17/2020 11:33:45
Note :
By default, the Kerberos ticket is valid for 10 hours .
The Kerberos ticket defaults may be modified by the Group Policy Management → Kerberos Policy → Security Settings in the AD.
Muti-Domain kerberos configuration
Carry out following changes in /etc/krb5.conf and /etc/krb5.conf.d/mosmb_krb5.conf

Set default cache path to a directory location other than /tmp directory. DIR / KCM cache type is used in case of the multiple Kerberos tickets. default_ccache_name = DIR:<directory path> OR default_ccache_name = KCM: in /etc/krb5.conf
Example :
[libdefaults] #default_ccache_name = KEYRING:persistent:%{uid} default_ccache_name = DIR:/opt/mosmb/Kerberos_tickets default_realm = MOSMBAD1.DOM
Mention default realm as the domain to which server machine is joined in file /etc/krb5.conf.d/mosmb_krb5.conf
In the realms section mention all the realms in file /etc/krb5.conf.d/mosmb_krb5.conf
Example :
[realms] MOSMBAD1.DOM = { kdc = mo-ad1.mosmbad1.dom admin_server = mo-ad1.mosmbad1.dom } MOSMBAD2.DOM = { kdc = mo-ad2.mosmbad2.dom admin_server = mo-ad2.mosmbad2.dom }

Note: In case of common global name space across sites. 1. Add only local keytab on the server side. 2. For sssd configuration to work MoSMB servers must be part of all domains. 3. Generation of only local Kerberos ticket would be sufficient. (instead of DIR cache path in /etc/krb5.conf mention FILE path and file name)
LDAP Client Configurations
To enable the  ACL view on the client side you are required to perform the following operations, in case of Kerberos authentication:
A. Add a BASE entry in the LDAP configuration file /etc/openldap/ldap.conf (centOS) or /etc/ldap/ldap.conf (ubuntu/debian) . The format should be as follows:
BASE   dc=example,dc=com
Example:
BASE    dc=mosmbad,dc=dom
B. Add the entry for the LDAP URI OR HOST to the /etc/openldap/ldap.conf (centOS) or /etc/ldap/ldap.conf (ubuntu/debian) file. The format should be as follows:

URI    ldap://<FQDN_OF_ACTIVE_DIRECTORY_SERVER>
   OR
   HOST    <FQDN_OF_ACTIVE_DIRECTORY_SERVER>
Example :
URI    ldap://mosmb-winad.mosmbad.dom OR HOST    mosmb-winad.mosmbad.dom
Note :
When MoSMB server is joined to Multiple domains, make sure all domain entries are present in the LDAP related configuration, similar to above examples.
sssd Configuration

sssd helps during the User ID mapping for domain users.
After carrying out the domain join of the MoSMB server make sure that the sssd configurations are in place.
If in case sssd is not installed before, install sssd-common and configure sssd on the server machine.
Create a sssd configuration file /etc/sssd/conf.d/mosmb_sssd.conf and it should contain parameters as shown in image below.
sssd configuration file location must be at /etc/sssd/sssd.conf with permissions of file set to 600 (-rw-------) and same with /etc/sssd/conf.d/mosmb_sssd.conf
Start sssd service with service sssd start .
Note :
Restart the sssd service after making the sssd configurations as in the above image.
When MoSMB server is joined to Multiple domains, make sure all domain entries are present in the sssd related configuration, similar to above example.
Make entries of all domains MoSMB server is joined to as explained below.
domain_list.txt Configuration for sssd

For sssd related settings simply create a file named domain_list.txt at the /etc/mosmb/ location and mention the domain to which the MoSMB server is joined. Ensure that you make an entry for each domain on a new line.
Example:
#cat /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt mosmbad.dom

For sssd related settings simply create a file named /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt will now use SPN entries instead of just domain names. Entries should be in the format of user@DOMAIN, similar to the SPNs and will be case sensitive.
Example: #cat /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt mosuer@MOSMBAD.DOM
In new releases, /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt will now use SPN entries instead of domain names. Entries should be in the format of user@DOMAIN, similar to the SPNs and will be case sensitive.
Example: #cat /etc/mosmb/domain_list.txt cifs/my-cluster.mosmbad.dom@MOSMBAD.DOM
nsswitch.conf Configuration

Verify that nsswitch.conf file is configured to use SSSD as a provider
Open /etc/nsswitch.conf file and verify that it points to sss for the name service switch

passwd:     files sss
shadow:     files sss
group:      files sss
netgroup:   files sss

mosmb_passwd
mosmb_passwd binary is made available in /usr/bin after MoSMB installation. mosmb_passwd has two main purposes -
To create ntlmLocal user for MoSMB.
To update domain password for ntlmAD/ntlmSSP authentication.
Prerequisites
User must be present locally on linux before adding/editing it as MoSMB user.
To update domain password for ntlmAD/ntlmSSP authentication (-m) : Utility for Domain join : adcli Machine should be domain joined. All krb5 configuration carried out.
Utility for Domain join : adcli
Machine should be domain joined.
All krb5 configuration carried out.
Usage

Command: mosmb_passwd -h Output: Usage:
   To add a user: ./mosmb_passwd -a <username>
   To delete an user: ./mosmb_passwd -d <username>
   To update the password of an existing user: ./mosmb_passwd -u <username>
   To list the users: ./mosmb_passwd -l
   To import from samba backup: ./mosmb_passwd -i <path/to/file>
   To update all domain's passwords: ./mosmb_passwd -m 
   To update any one domain password: ./mosmb_passwd -m <domain_name>

NOTES: 1. mosmb_passwd -m will also work with multiple domains, from MoSMB version 083 onwards 2. mosmb_passwd -m <domain name> will work from MoSMB version 083 onwards, if machine password needs to be updated for one of the domains in multi domain setup 3. For openssl versions 3 and above refer configurations - mosmb_passwd binary configurations for openssl 3 & above 4. This command needs to be run again, if any existing node is rejoined to domain or goes through adcli update for any reason.
Examples

Command: mosmb_passwd -l' Output: The user accounts present on file are:
       test_user1
       motest
Command: mosmb_passwd -m Output: adcli is not installed

To install the ZooKeeper Server, perform the following procedure:

Prerequisites
The ZooKeeper installation has the following dependencies:
Ubuntu / Debian
1. openjdk-8-jdk-headless
Redhat based Linux distributions
1. java-11-openjdk or higher
Zookeeper installation on Redhat based Linux distributions using rpm for New Releases after March 2022

For Cluster builds, a zookeeper rpm will also be provided along with MoSMB rpm. MoSMB-zookeeper rpm is present at : /opt/mosmb/package/mosmb_install To install you can use following command:
rpm -ivh /opt/mosmb/package/mosmb_install/mosmb-zookeeper-3.6.3-1.x86_64.rpm
Verifying...                          ################################# [100%]
Preparing...                          ################################# [100%]
Updating / installing...
   1:mosmb-zookeeper-3.6.3-1          ################################# [100%]
zookeeper is installed at : /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/
zookeeper will be installed at /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/ and user will need to make appropriate changes to zoo.cfg and then start zkServer as mentioned in above steps.
# /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh start
Get ZooKeeper for other builds and distributions
1. Get zookeeper installer apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin
wget https://archive.apache.org/dist/zookeeper/zookeeper-3.6.3/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin.tar.gz
2. Extract downloaded packages
tar -xvzf apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin.tar.gz
Configure the ZooKeeper Server
1. Go to other extracted directory apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin 2. Copy sample ZooKeeper configuration file from /etc/mosmb/.
cp /etc/mosmb/mosmb_zoo.cfg conf/zoo.cfg
3.  Create ZooKeeper server data directory can be any preferred location other than /tmp.
mkdir -p /var/lib/zookeeper-mosmb ..........
4.  ZooKeeper Standalone Server Configuration
5. ZooKeeper Cluster Server Configuration
For detailed information on the configuration parameters refer to the ZooKeeper Server Configuration Parameters page.
6. Create the myid file on the ZooKeeper server/s at the /var/lib/zookeeper-mosmb/ path.
Example on a Cluster ZooKeeper:

Test data                      : ZooKeeper Server IP list - 10.10.0.1, 10.10.0.2, 10.10.0.3 File on server 1 (10.10.0.1)   : cat /var/lib/zookeeper-mosmb/myid Output on server 1 (10.10.0.1) : 1 File on server 2 (10.10.0.2)   : cat /var/lib/zookeeper-mosmb/myid Output on server 2 (10.10.0.2) : 2 File on server 3 (10.10.0.3)   : cat /var/lib/zookeeper-mosmb/myid Output on server 3 (10.10.0.3) : 3
Setup zookeepr server side log rotation

This step is not mandatory. In case zookeeper restarts, the server side log located in the log folder of zookeeper installation path, (/opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/logs/) is overwritten and old logs are over-written/lost. To avoid loss of old logs, one can do the following
1. A solution is to modify zkEnv.sh and set ZOO_LOG4J_PROP

$ cat /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkEnv.sh | grep -i PROP
if [ "x${ZOO_LOG4J_PROP}" = "x" ]
 ZOO_LOG4J_PROP="INFO,CONSOLE"
2. here add ROLLINGFILE

$ cat /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkEnv.sh | grep -i PROP
if [ "x${ZOO_LOG4J_PROP}" = "x" ]
  ZOO_LOG4J_PROP="INFO,CONSOLE, ROLLINGFILE "
3. Now every restart of mosmb-zookeeper service will have two log files, one current (.out) and one backed up with old logs (.log).
Start the ZooKeeper Server
1. Go to the ZooKeeper directory apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin and run the following command:
./bin/zkServer.sh start
This will create a ZooKeeper log file at location ../apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/logs/ that will contain the ZooKeeper server related logs. 2. Check status.
Example for a Standalone ZooKeeper Server:
Command   : ./bin/zkServer.sh status Output    : JMX enabled by default Using config: ../apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/conf/zoo.cfg Mode: Standalone
Example for a Cluster ZooKeeper Server:

Test data : ZooKeeper Server IP list - 10.10.0.1, 10.10.0.2, 10.10.0.3 Command   : ./bin/zkServer.sh status Output    : JMX enabled by default Using config: ../apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/conf/zoo.cfg' Mode: Leader (This output will be different on all server one will be the leader and other servers will be Follower )

rpm -e can be used to uninstall the zookeeper rpm.
# rpm -e mosmb-zookeeper-3.6.3-1.x86_64
Uninstall Previous older ZooKeeper Versions
1. Stop older version of ZooKeeper service (Older than zookeeper 3.6.3)
./bin/zkServer.sh stop
2. Go to zookeeper’s previous version's source directory
For zookeeper 3.4.5 - zookeeper3.4.5/src/c
For zookeeper 3.6.1 - apache-zookeeper-3.6.1/zookeeper-client/zookeeper-client-c/
3. Run command -
make clean make uninstall
4. Make sure all previous library files are not on the zookeeper server machine -
find /usr/ -name libzookeeper*
5. Continue with required ZooKeeper version build and installation.
ZooKeeper - MoSMB Configuration Settings
Note:
ZooKeeper is a centralized service for maintaining configuration information, naming, providing distributed synchronization, and providing group services.
You must make ZooKeeper settings to use the MoSMB server. This depends on the MoSMB edition, it will be required for MoSMB-CL edition & MoSMB-SA-S3 edition .

Standalone ZooKeeper Server : MoSMB is set by default to work with the local Standalone ZooKeeper Server , as shown in the Standalone ZooKeeper Server Settings image underneath (setting in mosmb.json).
Remote ZooKeeper Server : For Remote ZooKeeper Server Settings in mosmb.json you can state the Hostport parameter as:
<server_hostname> : <zookeeper_client_port> OR <server_ip_address> : <zookeeper_client_port>
Clustered ZooKeeper Server : If required you can make the Clustered ZooKeeper Server settings. Refer to the Clustered ZooKeeper Server Settings image underneath.
The detailed ZooKeeper Server Configuration parameters are provided in the Zookeeper Server Configuration File Details .

Installation
On Linux:
1. Download and install necessary drivers Chelsio or Mellanox (Check version of RDMA cards and OS)

Mellanox : Refer → Linux NVIDIA MLNX_OFED Drivers , Windows MLNX_OFED drivers Chelsio :  Refer → chelsio site Examples: mlnx-en-4.5-1.0.1.0-rhel7.4-x86_64 Cards: iw_cxgb4 T520 cr ; CentOS 7 Driver version: 3.9.0.0
2. Disable ipv6:

sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.all.disable_ipv6=1 
   sysctl -w net.ipv6.conf.default.disable_ipv6=1
3. Disable iwpmd service for chelsio setup

systemctl stop iwpmd 
   systemctl disable iwpmd
4. Set ulimit to higher number, minimum 1M
ulimit -l unlimited
5. Make sure following packages are installed on MoSMB server machine

libibverbs.x86_64, 
   librdmacm.x86_64,
   librdmacm-utils.x86_64
6. Install MoSMB and configure RDMA (SMBDirect) port as per the driver

for RoCE based implementation ‘445’ Example : Mellanox 
   for iwarp based implementation ‘5445’ Example : Chelsio
On Windows:
1. Download and install necessary drives Chelsio or mallanox (Check version of RDMA cards and OS)
Mellanox : Refer → Mellanox Site Chelsio : Refer → Chelsio Site Examples: MLNX_VPI_WinOF-5_50_52000_All_win2016_x64.exe Cards : iw_cxgb4 T520 cr ; Windows 2k16 Driver : 6.15.3.0
2. From PowerShell run following commands –

i. Enable NetworkDirect, NetworkDirectAcrossIPSubnets
       Set-NetOffloadGlobalSetting -NetworkDirect 1
       Set-NetOffloadGlobalSetting -NetworkDirectAcrossIPSubnets Allowed
   ii. Get the hardware info
       Get-NetAdapterHardwareInfo
   iii. Enable RDMA
       Enable-NetAdapterRDMA
       Get-NetAdapterRDMA
   iv. Check SmbClientNetworkInterface, RDMA value should be 'True'
       Get-SmbClientNetworkInterface
   v. After share access check SmbMultichannelConnection, RDMA value should be 'True'
       Get-SmbMultichannelConnection
RDMA (SMBDirect) Connectivity Verification
Mallanox : (nd_rping is default mellanox utility)

From Server(linux) : nd_rping/rping -s [-v -V -d] [-S size] [-C count] [-a addr] [-p port]
   From Client(windows) : nd_rping/rping -c [-v -V -d] [-S size] [-C count] -a addr [-p port]
   Example:
       Server: rping -v -s -a <IP address> -C 10
       Client: nd_rping -v -c -a <same IP as above> -C 10
Chelsio : (Install chrping)

From Server(linux) : rping -s -d [-S size] [-a addr] [-p port]
   From Client(windows) : .\chrping.exe c <ip>[:<port>] [S<size>]
   Example:
       Server: rping -s -d -S <size> -a <addr> -p <port>
       Client: .\chrping.exe c <same IP addr:port as server> S <same size as server>
       Reverse ping --
       Server(windows): .\chrping.exe s <ip:port> S <size>
       Client(linux): rping -c -d -S <same size as server> -a <same IP addr as server> -p <same port as server>
Performance limitation
Install and run iperf to check the hardware/network limitation

1. Install iperf on both server and client
   2. Running iperf without bind address: 
       Server : iperf3 -s
       Client Single Instance :  iperf3 -c <Server IP Address> -p <Port Number>
       Client Parallel Instances : iperf3 -c <Server IP Address> --parallel <number of parallel instances> -i 1 -t 60 -V -p <Port Number>
   3. Running iperf with bind address:
       Server : iperf3 -s -B <Server IP Address>
       Client Single Instance :  iperf3 -c  <Server IP Address> -B <Client IP Address> -p <Port Number>
       Client Parallel Instances : iperf3 -c <Server IP Address> -B <Client IP Address> --parallel <number of parallel instances> -i 1 -t 60 -V -p <Port Number>
Reloading modules

We need to reload rdma modules in case RDMA (SMBDirect) is not functioning correctly.
Modules required to be reloaded -

1. ib_umad
   2. ib_uverbs
   3. iwarp (for iwarp based implementations)
   4. ib_core
   5. RoCE or iwarp based core module. 
       Example: 
           iw_cxgb4
           mlx_core
Remove modules with command -

rmmod <module name>
   Example :
       rmmod ib_umad
Add modules with command -

modprobe <module name>
   Example :
       modprobe ib_umad

shareName

You - the user can name a share as per your requirement. The default share names are share1 and share2 . The maximum length of a share name can be 80 characters. The share name can contain spaces, underscores( _ ) and alphanumeric strings.
comment

This is a comment added for the share. User can add a message if required or it can be kept blank. This parameter is for the user's reference only. Message added here is displayed as a tool tip on hovering the share when accessed from client side.
sharePath
Share path is the path of the share that the user wishes to export as an MoSMB share. State the complete path of the share to be exported.
continuousAvailability
The isContinouslyAvailable field is set to yes by default. The user requires a continuous availability of the exported share at the client end.
isCluster

The user should set the isCluster to no and enableHighAvailability globalSection parameter to no when  server is in Standalone Mode The user should set the isCluster to yes and nodeId starts from 1 . For more configuration settings in the cluster mode refer to the Single Domain Cluster Mode / Multi Domain Cluster Mode . For the clustering to work the user must set the enableHighAvailability globalSection parameter to yes in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.conf or /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file.
mosmb.json representation - MoSMB Cluster settings for share
mosmb.json representation - MoSMB Cluster settings for share

mosmb.conf representation - MoSMB Cluster settings for share
mosmb.conf representation - MoSMB Cluster settings for share
shareEncrypt

Encryption does not support on-disk data encryption it is encryption over the wire. Encryption will encrypt the network layer packet exchanges. Set the shareEncrypt value in the share level settings to yes if desired. For Share encryption to work the user must set the Global level parameter shareLevelEncryption to yes in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.conf or "/etc/mosmb/mosmb.json" file. When the rejectUnencryptedAccess is set to yes in the Global level parameter, un-encrypted requests from the client side are not served. The user can set this parameter to yes or no as per the requirement.

Note : When globalEncryptData from the global level parameters is set to yes , all the packets are encrypted for the server client communication. This is irrespective of the share level encryption settings.

If share is mounted on a case insensitive disk, Set this to 'no'
msDfsSupport

MoSMB support MS-Dfs links. MS-Dfs links support. If there are MS-Dfs links in the MoSMB share then keep this parameter's value to yes, otherwise no. When msDfsSupport is set to yes metadataStore should be set to xattr. MoSMB -S3 edition does not support ms-dfs currently, so it is recommended for MoSMB-S3 edition to set msDfsSupport to no. fsInfo
File system information to be used (fsInfoAutoDetect, sectorsPerUnit, bytesPerSector)
Specify the file system information to be used for this share.
If "fsInfoAutoDetect" is "no" then values of the "sectorsPerUnit" and "bytesPerSector" will be taken from the configuration file i.e. "mosmb.json"

If "fsInfoAutoDetect" is "yes" then values of the "sectorsPerUnit" and "bytesPerSector" will be taken by using the systme call. So in that case, the values of "sectorsPerUnit" and "bytesPerSector" mentioned in the configuration file i.e. "mosmb.json" will get ignored.
Access Control List Strategy (aclStrategy)

An Access Control List (ACL) helps the user with share access authorization. MoSMB provides three types of ACL strategies currently, none, posix, advancePosix and ntacl MoSMB -S3 edition does not support ACL's currently, so it is recommended for MoSMB-S3 edition to set ACL to none.
None
Setting ACL to none means there will be no authorization required for creating, modifying or deleting data from the share with the ACL strategy set to none .
mosmb.json representation - ACL Strategy "none"
mosmb.json representation - ACL Strategy "none"
mosmb.json representation - ACL Strategy "none"
mosmb.json representation - ACL Strategy "none"
mosmb.conf representation - ACL Strategy "none"
mosmb.conf representation - ACL Strategy "none"
POSIX
When "acl": "posix", is used MoSMB permits the underlying filesystem to take a decision for the shared file/directory's read and write permissions.
However, in case
"aclStrategy":{ "acl": "posix", "checkPosixPermissionBit": "yes" },
is used MoSMB takes the decision for the shared file/directory's read and write permissions.

mosmb.json representation - ACL Strategy "posix"
mosmb.json representation - ACL Strategy "posix"
mosmb.conf representation - ACL Strategy "posix"
mosmb.conf representation - ACL Strategy "posix"

In case of posix ACL, MoSMB maps the Active Directory user who has established a SMB session to an equivalent Linux user. The effective UserID (UID) of this user is used by the file system to check access. To facilitate this, MoSMB needs to populate nsswitch.conf to use a proper ID mapper. Example: SSSD In case of the posix ACL and Kerberos authentication the user is required to set the permissions of the share path to a domain user and domain group by running the chown command.
sudo chown -R ''<domain-user-name> @ <domain-name> '':''<domain-group-name> @ <domain-name>'' <share path>
Example:
Test Data : mosmbad.dom, mouser (domain user), /opt/share Command   : sudo chown -R ''mouser@mosmbad.dom'':''domain users@mosmbad.dom'' /opt/share

In case of posix ACL, MoSMB maps the Active Directory user who has established a SMB session to an equivalent Linux user. The effective UserID (UID) of this user is used by the file system to check access. To facilitate this, MoSMB needs to populate the nsswitch.conf file to use a proper ID mapper. Example : SSSD In case of the posix ACL and Kerberos authentication the user is required to set the permissions of the share path to a domain user and domain group by running the chown command.
sudo chown -R ''<domain-name>\<domain-user-name>'' : ''<domain-name>\ <domain-group-name> '' <share path>
Example:
Test Data : mosmbad.dom, mouser (domain user), /opt/share Command   : sudo chown -R ''MOSMBAD\mouser'':''MOSMBAD\domain users'' /opt/share
In case of the posix ACL and ntlm_local authentication the user is required:
To set the permissions of the share path to a Linux local user.
To add the user group as an MoSMB user using the mosmb_passwd binary.
Refer to the ntlmLocal authentication mode settings for details.
sudo chown -R <linux-user-name> : <linux-group-name> <share path>
Example:
Test Data : motest, mogroup, /opt/share Command   : sudo chown -R motest:mogroup /opt/share
advancePosix
In the case of advancePosix , authorization is done based on the advance permissions set from the server or client side. To set advance acl permissions use setfacl command.
Example:

Test Data  : motest, mogroup, /opt/share Command1   : sudo setfacl -d -m -u:"motest@mosmbad.dom":rwx /opt/share (default permissions or inherited permissions for user) Command2   : sudo setfacl -m -u:"motest@mosmbad.dom":rwx /opt/share (user permissions set on the share path) Command3   : sudo setfacl -d -m -g:"mogroup@mosmbad.dom":r-x /opt/share (default permissions or inherited permissions for group) Command4   : sudo setfacl -m -u:"mogroup@mosmbad.dom":r-x /opt/share (group permissions set on the share path)
ntacl

In the case of ntacl , authorization is done according to the Active Directory Users (similar to ntfs). ntacl rules are used to check access. In this case, MoSMB uses the extended attributes provided by the file system for storing the ACLs. ntacl is not supported on "authMode": "ntlmLocal" Warning: The MoSMB Server will not start successfully when the "authMode": "ntlmLocal" and "acl": "ntacl", . Note : The ntacl authorization mode is not supported on the ntlmLocal authentication mode.
mosmb.json representation - ACL Strategy "ntacl"
mosmb.json representation - ACL Strategy "ntacl"
mosmb.json representation - ACL Strategy "ntacl"
mosmb.json representation - ACL Strategy "ntacl"
mosmb.conf representation - ACL Strategy "ntacl"
mosmb.conf representation - ACL Strategy "ntacl"
User Based Enumeration Strategy

User Based Enumeration (UBE) provides the configuration capability to control share visibility. It makes the share visible only to those users and groups that are listed in the configuration file.
To enable and apply setting related to UBE per share, make necessary changes in ubeStrategy section of sharesSection Supported in mosmb.json version 1.2.0 onwards.
kerberos authentication
The users that have one of the mentioned groups as their primary group or  secondary group can see the share. Nomenclature :

ubeUsers  : <domain_username>@<FULL_DOMAINNAME> 
   ubeGroups : <domain_groupname>  or <group_name>@<FULL_DOMAINNAME>
Example

“ubeUsers”  : [“mo_user1@MOSMB_AD.DOM”],
   “ubeGroups” : [“domain users@MOSMB_AD.DOM”]
mosmb.json representation - ubeStrategy for "krb5"
mosmb.json representation - ubeStrategy for "krb5"
mosmb.json representation - ubeStrategy for "krb5"
mosmb.json representation - ubeStrategy for "krb5"
mosmb.json representation - ubeStrategy for "ntlmLocal"
mosmb.json representation - ubeStrategy for "ntlmLocal"
ntlmAd / NTLMSSP authentication
The users that have one of the mentioned groups as their primary group or secondary group can see the share. Nomenclature :

ubeUsers  : <domain_username>@<DOMAINNAME> 
   ubeGroups : <domain_groupname>  or <group_name>@<DOMAINNAME>
Example

“ubeUsers”  : [“mo_user1@MOSMB_AD”],
   “ubeGroups” : [“domain users@MOSMB_AD”]
ntlmLocal authentication
Only the users that have one of the mentioned groups as their primary group can see the share. Nomenclature for domain joined clients :

ubeUsers  : <mosmb_username> or <mosmb_username>@<DOMAINNAME> 
   ubeGroups : <group_name> or <group_name>@<DOMAINNAME>
Example

“ubeUsers”  : [“user1”,”user2@MOSMB_AD”],
   “ubeGroups” : [“group1”,”group2@MOSMB_AD”]
Nomenclature for non-domain clients:

ubeUsers  : <mosmb_username> or <mosmb_username>@<client_hostname>
   ubeGroups : <group_name> or <group_name>@<client_hostname>
Example

“ubeUsers”  : [“user1”,”user2@my_client1”],
   “ubeGroups” : [“group1”,”group2@my_client1]
File and Directory Level Permissions (fileCreationMask & directoryCreationMask)
Specific permissions can be applied to newly created files or directories from the client end. This is an "octal value"  that represents permissions.
The default value for file permissions is set to 0644 [-rw-r--r--].
The default value for the directories permissions is set to 0755 [drwxr-xr-x].
mosmb.json representation - Newly created File or Directory Permissions

mosmb.json representation - Newly created File or Directory Permissions

mosmb.conf representation - Newly created File or Directory Permissions
metadataStore
Specify the storage strategy for the metadata storage. The values can be xattr or none .
When the metadataStore is set to "none" it means that the MoSMB server is not going to store any metadata associated with the file in the extended attribute space.
When the metadataStore is set to "xattr" it means that the MoSMB server will store the metadata associated with files or directories in the extended attribute space.
In this case, file metadata includes the security descriptor and smb file-attributes.
Note
When the authorization mode is "ntacl" the metadata storage must be "xattr".
For MoSMB -S3 edition the metadata storage must be "xattr"

useDirectIo
Specifies whether to use O_DIRECT flag, in cases application uses FILE_NO_INTERMEDIATE_BUFFERING for operations. The deafult value is set to no .
pluginPath
The user is required to set the IOS plugin path. The default plugin path configuration is for the Virtual File System (VFS).

To setup and configure the MoSMB server to work in the ntlmLocal mode for any server Operating System, perform the following basic steps:
Contents
1 Verify nsswitch
2 ntlmLocal users as SMB users 2.1 1. Create ntlmLocal user 2.2 2. List existing ntlmLocal users 2.3 3. Modify existing ntlmLocal user 2.4 4. Delete existing ntlmLocal user
2.1 1. Create ntlmLocal user
2.2 2. List existing ntlmLocal users
2.3 3. Modify existing ntlmLocal user
2.4 4. Delete existing ntlmLocal user
Verify nsswitch

Verify if /etc/nsswitch.conf points to passwd file for the name service switch. (This determines where the passwd command (and other commands) obtains and stores password information. )
Example:

passwd:     files passwd 
   shadow:     files passwd 
   group:      files passwd
ntlmLocal users as SMB users

Run the binary mosmb_passwd at the /usr/bin/ location to map the local Linux users as MoSMB users.
To know all available options run
/usr/bin/mosmb_passwd -h
1. Create ntlmLocal user

Test username : motest Command       : /usr/bin/mosmb_passwd -a motest Enter new SMB password: Confirm password: Output        : After adding user /etc/mosmb/mosmb_u.dat will be generated with added username and encrypted password.
2. List existing ntlmLocal users
Command       : /usr/bin/mosmb_passwd -l Output        : The user accounts present on file are: motest
3. Modify existing ntlmLocal user
Test username : motest Command       : /usr/bin/mosmb_passwd -u motest Enter new SMB password: Confirm password: Output        : Updated entry for motest
4. Delete existing ntlmLocal user
Test username : motest Command       : /usr/bin/mosmb_passwd -d motest Output        : Delete entry for motest
Notes :
If you see issues with mosmb_passwd binary, refer to this link

1. The MoSMB service need not be restarted after adding system users as MoSMB users.
   2. Users that are being added must be present on the Linux system. 
   3. The MoSMB password provided can be different from the System user's password.
   4. MoSMB user and AD user should not have same name. Eg. Local user/MoSMB user - mo_user1 and AD user - mo_user1@domain
       4.1 If such case is present ntlmLocal access will work correctly for domain joined clients, as krb5 authentication has precedence over ntlmSsp & ntlmLocal
       4.2 In this case one can carryout the uid gid mapping to resolve the user permissions issue.
MoSMB provides users with a product key to activate the MoSMB product.
Users can activate the product in two ways:
Online: For MoSMB servers with an active network connection.
Offline: For MoSMB servers that do not have any active network connection.
To start the MoSMB server you require a trial license or a product key. Note : If the MoSMB server is a GCP instance then the following licensing scheme is not applicable.
Checks for Online Activation
Verify if the MoSMB server machine has a working and active internet connection.
Verify if all network adapters of MoSMB server's are up.
Verify if the firewall policies allow port 443 to connect to the MoSMB license servers.
Verify if the time and date settings of the MoSMB server are correct or up-to-date.
Verify if https://wyday.com/limelm/ is reachable from the MoSMB server.
MoSMB checks license validity in certain time period for online activation MoSMB server must always be able to communicate with https://wyday.com/limelm
Checks of Offline Activation
Verify if the trialrequest.xml or request.xml is generated on the same MoSMB server machine on which you need to activate the MoSMB license.
Verify if the trialresponse.xml or response.xml file provided to you is placed at the /etc/mosmb/license/ location.
Verify if the product key mentioned in the MoSMB configuration and the product key used while activating the license is the same.
Contents
1 Checks for Online Activation
2 Checks of Offline Activation
3 Online Activation of the Product 3.1 Trial License Key 3.2 Trial Extension Key 3.3 Product Key
3.1 Trial License Key
3.2 Trial Extension Key
3.3 Product Key
4 Offline Activation of the Product 4.1 Trial/Trial Extension License Key 4.2 Product Key
4.1 Trial/Trial Extension License Key
4.2 Product Key
5 Applying changes made to a product key - offline
6 Applying changes made to a product key - online
7 Error Codes
There are three types of license keys:
Trial License Key
Trial License Extension Key
Product key
Online Activation of the Product
Trial License Key
In the case of a trial license the Product key parameter in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file should be empty - "productKey": ""
Restart the MoSMB service for the changes made in MoSMB configuration file to take effect.
Note : The Trial license will be activated only once per server. After expiry contact the MoSMB support
support@ryussi.com for the Trial Extension Key or to purchase the Product Key .
Trial Extension Key
Contact the MoSMB support support@ryussi.com for a trial extension key.
In the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file in the Product key section enter the trial extension key.
Restart the MoSMB service for the changes made in MoSMB configuration file to take effect.
Note : Make sure you remove all the previous trialRequest.xml and trialResponse.xml files from the /etc/mosmb/license folder
Product Key
Contact the MoSMB support support@ryussi.com to purchase the product key.
In the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file in the Product key section enter the product key provided.
For the changes made in the MoSMB configuration file to take effect Restart the MoSMB service.
Note : Make sure you remove all the previous trialRequest.xml and trialResponse.xml files if at all they exist from previous offline activations, from the /etc/mosmb/license folder
Offline Activation of the Product
Trial/Trial Extension License Key
In the case of a trial license the Product key parameter in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file should be empty - "productKey": ""
Run the /etc/mosmb/license/mosmb_offline_trial binary as follows: /etc/mosmb/license/mosmb_offline_trial
This binary will generate a trialRequest.xml file at the /etc/mosmb/license/ location.
Send this file to the MoSMB support support@ryussi.com and we will share a response trialResponse.xml file.
Copy this trialResponse.xml file at the /etc/mosmb/license/ location.
Restart the MoSMB service for the changes to take effect.
Note : The Trial license will be activated only once per machine. After expiry contact the MoSMB support support@ryussi.com for the Trial Extension Key or to purchase the Product Key.
Product Key
Contact the MoSMB support support@ryussi.com to purchase the product key.

Run the /etc/mosmb/license/mosmb_offline_license binary as follows: /etc/mosmb/license/mosmb_offline_license <Product Key> The Product Key should be the one mentioned in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file.
This binary will generate a request.xml file at the /etc/mosmb/license/ location.
Send this file to MoSMB support support@ryussi.com and we will share a response.xml response file.
Copy the response.xml file to the /etc/mosmb/license/ location.
Restart the MoSMB service for the changes to take effect.

Note :Make sure you remove all the previous trialRequest.xml and trialResponse.xml files from the /etc/mosmb/license folder Alternatively , for a cluster setup, which is set to use clush (clustershell), mosmbcli get-req and deploy-resp options can be used to get the request.xml files from all nodes and put in a tar file which can be then emailed to the MoSMB support support@ryussi.com
Applying changes made to a product key - offline
If old request.xml exists in the license folder (/etc/mosmb/license/) then we use that to generate new responses

If old request.xml does not exist in the license folder, and license is still valid, to generate request.xml, we need to deactivate first (request.xml is not generated if product is activated) /etc/mosmb/license/.mosmb_license_deactivate

Run the /etc/mosmb/license/mosmb_offline_license binary as follows: etc/mosmb/license/mosmb_offline_license <Product Key> The Product Key should be the one mentioned in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file.
This binary will generate a request.xml file at the /etc/mosmb/license/ location.
Send the request.xml file to MoSMB support support@ryussi.com and we will share a response.xml response file.
Copy the response.xml file to the /etc/mosmb/license/ location.
if product is already deactivated, Restart the MoSMB service for the license changes to take effect.
if old request was used, then deactivate now.. and then restart service
Applying changes made to a product key - online
If license is still valid, we need to deactivate first /etc/mosmb/license/.mosmb_license_deactivate
Then restart mosmb service for the new activation to take effect
Error Codes
All MoSMB-CL builds supports single domain clustering. To form a single domain MoSMB cluster simply follow the configurations mentioned in steps below one by one.

1. Carry out Network Time Protocol (NTP) Configuration settings on all MoSMB server nodes. 2. Install Kerberos packages on all MoSMB server nodes 3. Request the Domain Administration team for AD and DNS Configurations.
Create a Special user in the Active Directory (AD) (Step required to be carried out only once per MoSMB cluster)
Create MoSMB Cluster Keytab (Step required to be carried out only once per MoSMB cluster)
Copy generated keytab file to all the MoSMB server nodes which are needed in a cluster as mosmb.keytab at location /etc/mosmb/
Configure DNS entries for the MoSMB Cluster
Carry out name resolution settings on all MoSMB servers nodes.
4. Kerberos and related services configurations on all MoSMB server nodes in the cluster.
Configure MoSMB krb5 settings
Configure LDAP client settings
Configure SSSD settings
Configure the domain_list.txt settings
For release versions:3.1.1_000 refer - MoSMB Kerberos Ticket Generation
For release versions:3.1.1_001 and above refer - MoSMB Kerberos Ticket Generation.

5. MoSMB server now supports NTLMSSP authentication from Release versions:3.1.1_004 and above. For each MoSMB server node in cluster, carry out these changes to get the NTLMSSP authentication support working.
6. MoSMB server is ZooKeeper client; Perform MoSMB -ZooKeeper settings
MoSMB-ZooKeeper Server installation
MoSMB-ZooKeeper Cluster Configuration
Start MoSMB-ZooKeeper
7. Carry out required authorization (ACL) settings
Using MoSMB with Posix ACL OR
Using MoSMB with Advance Posix ACL OR
Using MoSMB with NTACL
8. Setup the MoSMB configuration file (/etc/mosmb/mosmb.json) for cluster use.
In globalSection set enableHighAvailability to yes
In persistentStoreSection keep pstorePath location on the common storage path on all MoSMB nodes in the cluster.
In section nodeInfoSection , assign a different nodeId to each node in MoSMB cluster.

In section authSection , set authMode as ntlmLocal . (NOTE: This will ensure krb5 authentication is tried first, if that is not successful, the new releases support ntlmSSP authentication, if that fails then ntlmLocal authentication is tried.)
In shareSection keep sharePath location on the common storage path on all MoSMB nodes in the cluster.
In shareSection set parameters isContinouslyAvailable and isCluster to yes
Example:
9. If the server machines are centos(or similar) 8 and above supported encryption options needs to be enabled from server side as well in order for Kerberos authentication to work completely.

On every CentOS 8.3 and above MoSMB server node, where authentication against AD Domain Controllers takes place, use the update-crypto-policies command to enable the AD-SUPPORT cryptographic subpolicy in addition to the DEFAULT cryptographic policy.

Command : update-crypto-policies --set DEFAULT:AD-SUPPORT Output : Setting system policy to DEFAULT:AD-SUPPORT Command : init 6 Notes : 1. System-wide crypto policies are applied on application start-up so it is recommended to restart the system for the change of policies to fully take place.
           3. The AD-SUPPORT cryptographic subpolicy is only available on CentOS 8.3 and newer.

To enable support for RC4 in CentOS 8.2 , create and enable a custom cryptographic module policy with cipher = RC4-128+. For more details, see Customizing system-wide cryptographic policies with policy modifiers. Reference links :
1. Using the system wide cryptographic policies 2. how to customize crypto policies rhel 8.2
To enable support for RC4 in CentOS 8.0 and CentOS 8.1 , add +rc4 to the permitted_enctypes option in the /etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/krb5.config file

[libdefaults]
           permitted_enctypes = aes256-cts-hmac-sha1-96 aes256-cts-hmac-sha384-192 camellia256-cts-cmac aes128-cts-hmac-sha1-96 aes128-cts-hmac-sha256-128 camellia128-cts-cmac +rc4
10. If the server machines have openssl version 3 and above configuration changes are required.
Open /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf
Existing/Default Configuration :

[provider_sect]
   default = default_sect
   [default_sect]
   # activate = 1
Edits/Required Configuration :

[provider_sect]
   default = default_sect legacy = legacy_sect [default_sect] activate = 1 [legacy_sect] activate = 1
11. Start MoSMB service on all nodes in the MoSMB cluster and check service mosmb status and check the mosmb.log for details. You can check details of MoSMB server Maintenance here
Notes :

1. Make sure ZooKeeper standalone server / ZooKeeper cluster is running.
   2. Make sure sssd service is running on all MoSMB cluster nodes.  
   3. Make sure pstorePath is not the same directory or any sub-directory within the smb share 
   4. Make sure all the MoSMB configuration on all cluster nodes is similar. 
   5. To know other mosmb.json parameters in details refer this .

Server maintenance includes allowing user to perform clean start, stop operations along with dynamically adding a share and compute performance parameters depending on the MoSMB Server Machine Configurations.
Note :

* The MoSMB Server should be started in only one way at a time, that is either as binary or as a service.
   * The MoSMB Server will start when the product key or trial license is valid, persistent storage and share paths mentioned are available on the server. Refer to the MoSMB License Manager .

Service
User can perform a Start, Stop or Restart operations on the MoSMB service.
For any change/s in the MoSMB server configuration files /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json and/or /etc/mosmb/s3.json in any section/s it is mandatory to restart the MoSMB server.
Notes: : 1. systemctl support is available after release builds 073. 2. One can use Either service commands or systemctl commands throughout the cycle.
MoSMB Start
Command : EITHER service mosmb start OR systemctl start mosmb Output  : Starting MoSMB service...

When MoSMB is being used with ZooKeeper server, the sequence of starting these services is important as MoSMB is ZooKeeper's client and ZooKeeper takes the locks on certain files which is released after 200 secs timeout if the MoSMB / ZooKeeper server is exited abruptly or MoSMB / ZooKeeper server machine is rebooted.

1. Start ZooKeeper - Command : sh  /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh start Output  : JMX enabled by default Using config: ../apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/conf/zoo.cfg' Mode: Standalone Mode: Leader / Follower / Standalone this output will be different on individual servers depending on the MoSMB-ZooKeeper configurations 2. ZooKeeper Cleanup -
       ZooKeeper Cleanup is must when MoSMB or Zookeeper goes down (services or machines) while any smb operations are in progress.
       To Cleanup ZooKeeper nodes refer - MoSMB-ZooKeeper server cleanup 3. Start MoSMB - Command : EITHER service mosmb start OR systemctl start mosmb Output  : Starting MoSMB service...

When MoSMB is being used with ZooKeeper server, the sequence of starting these services is important as MoSMB is ZooKeeper client and ZooKeeper takes the locks on certain files which is released after 200 secs timeout if the MoSMB / ZooKeeper server is exited abruptly or MoSMB / ZooKeeper server machine is rebooted.

1. Start ZooKeeper on all nodes - Command : sh  /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh start Output  : JMX enabled by default Using config: ../apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/conf/zoo.cfg' Mode: Leader Mode: Leader / Follower this output will be different on individual servers depending on the MoSMB-ZooKeeper configurations 2. ZooKeeper Cleanup on all nodes -
       ZooKeeper Cleanup is must when MoSMB or Zookeeper goes down (services or machines) while any smb operations are in progress.
       To Cleanup ZooKeeper nodes refer - MoSMB-ZooKeeper server cleanup 3. Start MoSMB on all nodes - Command : EITHER service mosmb start OR systemctl start mosmb Output  : Starting MoSMB service...
Note :
* If MoSMB server and ZooKeeper server are configured on 2 separate machines and only MoSMB service/server goes down then ZooKeeper cleanup is not a requirement.
MoSMB Stop
Command : EITHER service mosmb stop OR systemctl stop mosmb Output  : Stopping MoSMB service...

When MoSMB is being used with ZooKeeper server, the sequence of stopping these services / shutting down the machines is important as MoSMB is ZooKeeper client and ZooKeeper takes the locks on certain files which is released after 200 secs timeout if the MoSMB / ZooKeeper server is exited abruptly or MoSMB / ZooKeeper server machine is rebooted.

1. Stop MoSMB service Command : EITHER service mosmb stop OR systemctl stop mosmb Output  : Stopping MoSMB service... 2. Stop ZooKeeper service/s Command : apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh stop Output  : .... Stopping zookeeper ... STOPPED

When MoSMB is being used with ZooKeeper server, the sequence of stopping these services / shutting down the machines is important as MoSMB is ZooKeeper client and ZooKeeper takes the locks on certain files which is released after 200 secs timeout if the MoSMB / ZooKeeper server is exited abruptly or MoSMB / ZooKeeper server machine is rebooted.

1. Stop MoSMB service on all nodes - Command : EITHER service mosmb stop OR systemctl stop mosmb Output  : Stopping MoSMB service... 2. Stop ZooKeeper service on all nodes - Command : apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh stop Output  : .... Stopping zookeeper ... STOPPED
MoSMB Restart
MoSMB service restart is mandatory in case any changes are made to the configuration file.
Command : EITHER service mosmb restart OR systemctl restart mosmb Output  : Stopping MoSMB service... Starting MoSMB service...

In case MoSMB goes in an unstable state due to node/process/network failures causing MoSMB and/or zookeeper node/s to go down or unreachable within recovery time periods, then follow the steps as mentioned below for clean restart the MoSMB along with MoSMB-ZooKeeper.

1. Stop MoSMB - Command : EITHER service mosmb stop OR systemctl stop mosmb 2. Run /etc/mosmb/scripts/zk-cleanup.sh script to clear the zookeeper database and logs on zookeeper node. ( If zookeeper is not on the MoSMB node, in that case manually delete all the files in <zookeeper-datadir>/version-2 and <zookeeper-install-dir>/logs ) /etc/mosmb/scripts/zk-cleanup.sh <zookeper-install-dir> 3. Stop zookeeper <zookeeper-install-dir>/bin/zkServer.sh stop 4. Start zookeeper <zookeeper-install-dir>/bin/zkServer.sh start 5. Start MoSMB service mosmb start

In case MoSMB cluster goes in an unstable state due to node/process/network failures causing multiple MoSMB and/or zookeeper nodes to go down or unreachable within recovery time periods, then follow the steps as mentioned below for clean restart the whole MoSMB cluster along with MoSMB-ZooKeeper cluster.

1. Stop MoSMB cluster (mosmb service on all nodes) Command : EITHER service mosmb stop OR systemctl stop mosmb 2. Delete all mo_openfiletable files from pstore path
   3. Stop zookeeper cluster (zookeeper on all nodes) <zookeeper-install-dir>/bin/zkServer.sh stop 4. Run the zookeeper cleanup script by MoSMB to clear the zookeeper database and logs on all zookeeper nodes in the cluster. <zk-cleanup script> <zookeper-install-dir path> /etc/mosmb/scripts/mosmb_zk-cleanup.sh <zookeper-install-dir path> (For Release versions MoSMB : 3.1.1.005 onwards) /etc/mosmb/scripts/zk-cleanup.sh <zookeper-install-dir path> (For Release versions MoSMB : 3.1.1.003 and before) ( If zookeeper is not on the MoSMB node, in that case manually delete all the files in <zookeeper-datadir>/version-2 and <zookeeper-install-dir>/logs OR in a workaround copy the clean up script to mosmb-zookeeper node and run it from there.)   
   
   5. Start zookeeper cluster (zookeeper on all nodes) <zookeeper-install-dir>/bin/zkServer.sh start 6. Start MoSMB cluster (mosmb service on all nodes) service mosmb start

MoSMB Logs

Once you start the MoSMB service, check the mosmb.log located at default path /var/log/ to see if MoSMB has started successfully without any errors. For any errors, check the Troubleshooting Guide or FAQs for assistance. Logs directory can be configured as per the requirement by editing parameter logsPath in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json . In logs directory MoSMB will create files mosmb.log, mosmb_audit.log, mosmb_vinode.log Please send your queries to support@ryussi.com
For mosmb logs, the log level values for all modules can be set in the conf file /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf
MoSMB Logs in release versions 3.1.1.039 and above
MO_CRITICAL = 0
MO_ERROR = 1
MO_WARN = 2
MO_INFO = 3
MO_DEBUG = 4
Default mosmb_logging.conf looks like

FSA_L           = 2
SMBCORE_L       = 2
CONNECTION_L    = 2
MULTICHANNEL_L  = 2
IOS_L           = 2
RDMA_L          = 2
WITNESS_L       = 2
OPLOCKS_L       = 2
MORPC_L         = 2
CONFIG_L        = 3
STATISTICS_L    = 2
LEASE_L         = 2
NDR_L           = 2
RPC_L           = 2
HASH_L          = 2
PST_L           = 2
LICENSE_L       = 2
AUDIT_L         = 2
AUTH_L          = 2
ENCRYPTION_L    = 2
VINODE_L        = 2
DEFAULT_L       = 3
COMMON_L        = 2
CLUSTER_L       = 2
PROFILER_L      = 2
The MO_CRITICAL log messages are printed with loglevel set to 0
(They will be printed for all other log level > 0 as well)
When the log level value set is to 1, error level logs are enabled (MO_ERROR Level) (They will be printed for all other log level > 1 as well)
The MO_WARN log messages are printed with log level set to 2 in the conf file.
(They will be printed for all other log level > 2 as well)

For modules CONFIG_L and DEFAULT_L, default value is set to 3, which is MO_INFO. The INFO logs are printed to give more information. You can choose to disable it and print only error level logs by setting the value to < 3
For module PROFILER_L, default is set to 0. When the value is set to 4, the detailed information is printed.
Any changes to log levels can be reloaded at runtime, (no need to restart mosmb) reload Log Level Changes
In case debug level is required, following guidelines can be used to set respective log modules to debug level

Login/Access issues- enable AUDIT_L , AUTH_L, SMBCORE_L
File I/O issues - enable IOS_L, FSA_L, SMBCORE_L
Connection related issues - enable CONNECTION_L , SMBCORE_L
Overall general profiling for timings - enable PROFILER_L
RPC failures- enable - RPC_L, SMBCORE_L
mosmb-zookeeper related issues- VINODE_L, SMBCORE_L

Use these logs for Security Monitoring.
One can set the audit module’s log level in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf file to 3 to get detailed audit logs.

AUDIT_L = 3
This level logs File operations like CREATE RENAME DELETE Or PERMISSION CHANGE With FileName and the User who is attempting the operation
Note: Logs for MO_CRIT, MO_ERROR, MO_WARN will also be included
If MO_WARN level logs are required, one can set

AUDIT_L = 2
This level logs LOGIN and LOGOFF attempts, success or failure, With Time and IP of machine the attempt was made from
The mosmb_audit.log audit log file will then be generated at the log location (default  /var/log). Further details are same as older releases.

These are the logs generated by the api used to communicate to the (zookeeper/inode) database.
In case of any vinode related errors (either inode Mechanism or zookeeper), user can refer to these logs to get more details. The vinode loglevel starts from 1. For value 0 you may get error and mosmb will not start.

VINODE_L = 1
  Or 2/3/4 etc
The mosmb_vinode.log log file is populated at the log location, with MO_ERROR,MO_WARN,MO_INFO (Rest could be the Zookeeper logs.)

MoSMB Logs rotation
When MoSMB is installed, the scripts for the log rotation of the mosmb logs are added in the /etc/cron.hourly directory (mologrotate) and the other files are in /etc/logrotate.d directory.
/etc/logrotate.d/mosmbauditlr
/etc/logrotate.d/mosmblr
/etc/logrotate.d/zookeeperlr
Logs are rotated when the log file size reaches 500 MB(default). The log size threshold reached is checked on an hourly basis.
mosmb.log and other log files are rotated nd refer to files mosmbauditlr (mosmb_audit.log), mosmblr (mosmb.log) and zookeeperlr (mosmb_vinode.log) present under /etc/logrotate.d/ respectively
Adding a share dynamically

Script: cmdpipe Function:
To add a new share to MoSMB server and reload the configuration without stopping the MoSMB server. Only single share can be applied at a time on reload command. To add multiple shares, add all the share names to mosmb.json under shareSection. Run the script to add each share one at a time. Reload MoSMB config will take some time. The newly added share's shareName should be unique. sharePath must be valid and must exists on the server. Before running the command, please make sure the mosmb.json is correct.
Prerequisite to run cmdpipe:
python2 should be installed.
tabulate module should be installed.
To install tabulate :
pip2 install tabulate

Example: To add a share without restarting the mosmb server 1. Add required VFS share details in mosmb.json under shareSection as a new share e.g. ‘share3' as follows. ’share1’ is an existing share. 2. Add required S3 share details in mosmb.json under shareSection as a new share e.g. ‘s3_share' as follows. ’share1’ is an existing share. 3. The command mentioned below will add share3 and reload MoSMB server configuration [root@sivm31 ~]# /etc/mosmb/cmdpipe config_reload_add -h Error occurred in GetOpt Use the following commands for mosmb config reload -n : Specifies share name -t : Specifies share type Share type can be one of these two : VFS or S3
Following is an Example: ./cmdpipe config_reload_add -n <share_name> -t vfs OR ./cmdpipe config_reload_add -n <share_name> -t S3

[root@sivm31 ~]# /etc/mosmb/cmdpipe config_reload_add -n share3 -t vfs 
Success
Command executed successfully

[root@sivm31 ~]# /etc/mosmb/cmdpipe config_reload_add -n s3_share -t S3 
Success
Command executed successfully
MoSMB statistics
MoSMB statistics can be collected anytime with the command pipe utility
/etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe statson
Optional Arguments to statson

-p, --path Specifies csv path. Default is /var/log/
-i, --interval Specifies time period in seconds. Default is 300 s
-c, --console Display statistics on 'console'
-n, --number Number of counts for statistics collection operation
-h, --help Shows this help message and Exit
Examples:

/etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe statson -p /var/log/ -i 60 & => Statistic collection will keep running in background untill mosmb is restarted or cmdpipe instance is stopped /etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe statson -p /var/log/ -i 120 -c => Display the stats on console after every '-i' interval /etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe statson -p /var/log/ -i 600 -c -n 5 => Display the stats on console after every '-i' interval for '-n' number of time
Following Abbreviations used while showing output on console using '-c' option

RIO(Read IOPS), WIO(Write IOPS), TIOPs(Total IOPS), RT(Read Throughput), WT(Write Throughput),
TT(MB/s)(Total Throughput MB/s), RL(Read Latency), WL(Write Latency), SRL(Storage Read Latency),
SWL(Storage Write Latency), FO(Total File/Pipe Opened), ES(Total Established Sessions),
DC(Total Disconnects), PV(Password Violations), PE(Total Permission Error)
When statistics are collected in csv files, following files get populated in the default path or the path mentioned in command with –p

1. preprocessor_stats.csv
2. server_stats.csv
3. worker_thread_stats.csv
4. smbdirect_stats.csv
5. processed_stats.csv
User can use the processed_stats.csv file to check the collected and processed statistics
processed_stats.csv can be used to integrate with monitoring tools to visualizing and monitoring the metrics
Performance Tuning
Set the mosmb log level to 0 (zero) in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf file.

Set the Required Message Signing to Off to improve the performance. This will not disable the signing completely: When the client sends a signed request the MoSMB server will send the signed response. For more information refer to Microsoft Network Server: Digitally Sign Communications

You can switch on multichannel by setting Enable MultiChannel to yes in the MoSMB configuration. Multiple network cards can be added at the server end. In case there are multiple network paths between the client and the server, the SMB3 protocol uses the multi-channel feature.
MoSMB is pre-tuned to perform well for most of the workloads. The MoSMB performance scales linearly with smb connections.
MoSMB service restart is mandatory in case any changes are made to the configuration file.

One can use the performance tuning script provided by MoSMB at location /etc/mosmb/scripts to apply suggested parameters w.r.t server machines CPU and RAM. To run for smbd parameters tuning ---- sh /etc/mosmb/scripts/perf_tuning.sh smbdirect To run for tcp parameters tuning ---- sh /etc/mosmb/scripts/perf_tuning.sh tcp Detailed usage is provided in script ---- sh /etc/mosmb/scripts/perf_tuning.sh -h
TCP Performance
In the MoSMB configuration, set all the transaction sizes to 8M. (Max Transaction Size, Max Read Size, Max Write Size)
Memory allocation is shared in all the TCP connections and Memory utilization is dependent on the following formula: preProcessorThreads * ringBufferCount * Transaction size

Ways to scale-up and scale-out MoSMB performance: i. In case the client connections are more in number, increase the server threads or pre-processing threads. ii. The number of threads in the worker thread-pool can be increased so that more number of SMB packets can be served at a time. The number of threads can be increased to 64/128/256 for a high-end system, depending on the CPU cores of the system. iii. The number of pre-allocated buffers can be increased as per the RAM memory. iv. Add an additional network card to make the multi-channel feature functional and enable the multi-channel settings in the MoSMB configuration. v. To scale up the MoSMB on one node, add more NICs, CPU or memory as desired. If the backend storage is a bottleneck, add high performance storage like NVMe disks. vi. To scale out, add another MoSMB node to the cluster and expose the same share.

Note : MoSMB provides a performance tuning script as per the server configuration so as MoSMB will use only upto  60% of the available memory on the server machine. This script is located at /etc/mosmb/scripts/perf_tuning.sh Just run the script to get tuning parameters like preProcessorThreads, threadPoolSize and ringBufferCount set as per the server configuration.
RDMA (SMBDirect) Performance
Note : Refer to the Troubleshooting Guide for the RDMA (SMBDirect) Communication on a MoSMB Server to understand how to make the RDMA (SMBDirect) basic setup work.

The SMBDirect protocol uses the RDMA Read/Write requests to transfer file data within the client and the server. Every RDMA Read/Write operation consists of a SMB2 Read request with 1 or more buffer descriptors . Each buffer descriptor contains information for an I/O buffer with the desired transfer size. Each buffer descriptor thus requires to be allocated and pinned in memory till the transfer is completed through the RDMA channel. So for every buffer descriptor a buffer of desired size is allocated and pinned, this is a control operation and consumes a significant amount of time. Therefore we have introduced the following parameters to ensure that we reduce control operations during data operations:

SMB Direct ring size: i. Default value: 512 ii. Per connection based parameter. iii. Accepts value in the multiples of 64. iv. The server should be restarted to reflect the effect when a  parameter is changed. The SMB Direct ring size is a parameter that allocates and reserves the specified number of buffers, each of the SMBDirect buffer size size. On every RDMA Read/Write request the buffer is consumed from this ring and replenished after use. This allows faster operations as we can avoid memory pinning operations (control) in the data path resulting in faster speeds. This can to be tuned w.r.t the I/O pattern for typical application. Setting it to very higher number can guarantee faster transfers at the cost of higher memory footprint. Whereas setting it to very lower number can lead to slower speeds due to the lack of available buffers for transfers. We recommend it to be set 1024 for media applications, and 256 for non-media operations (basic file server, disk io apps)

SMB Direct buffer size: i. Default value: 1 ii. Per connection-based parameter. iii. Accepts value from 1 to 8 inclusive. This value is in Mega Bytes (for example 1 represents 1MB). iv. The server should be restarted to reflect the effect of a changed parameter. This parameter represents the size of transfer for every buffer descriptor from SMB Direct ring . As per the SMBD Specification this parameter should be less than or equal to the maximum fragmented receive size (1MB). However, this parameter could be less than or equal to the maximum transaction size (8MB) as well. The SMB client has 1MB value  that is the maximum fragmented receive size and hence it the recommended value.

TCP parameters i. For RDMA connections only, allocate minimal resources to TCP parameters since the TCP buffers will consume some amount of RAM ii. For example, preProcessorThreads = 4, ringBufferCount = 256 (or 128 or 64)
Mac Client Performance
With the help of following settings on the MAC client one can improve performance. Mac needs reboot after applying any of the changes.
Turn off packet signing by setting following in the /etc/nsmb.conf file. signing_required=no https://support.apple.com/en-gb/HT205926
Speed up browsing on network shares defaults write com.apple.desktopservices DSDontWriteNetworkStores -bool TRUE https://support.apple.com/en-in/HT208209
for more troubleshooting with MAC, refer to the troubleshooting section for MAC

Please note that the performance tuning recommendations provided in this guide are general guidelines. Optimal configuration for your environment may differ depending on your workload, hardware, and other factors. To achieve the best results, it's recommended that you conduct performance tests on your specific configuration and make adjustments as necessary.

Performance tuning of server level parameters

The parameter settings in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json and /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf file will help set the performance of the MoSMB server. Note: Any changes to /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json mandates restarting the MoSMB server.
Global level parameters in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file
"maxProtocol": "smb302" → Set this to smb302. Setting max protocol lower than this can give low performance.

"requiredMessageSigning": "no" → Set required signing to “no” to give better performance. This will not disable the signing completely. When the client sends a signed request the MoSMB server will send the signed response. For more information refer to Microsoft Network Server: Digitally Sign Communications For high security reasons you may want to all SMB clients to sign each and every packet, then you can set this to “yes”. This security feature can have some cost on performance.

"globalEncryptData": "no" → Set this parameter to “no” to give better performance. For very high security reasons you may want to set all SMB clients to encrypt each and every packet, then you can set this to “yes”. This security feature can have cost on performance.

"shareLevelEncryption": "no" → Set this parameter to “no” to give better performance. For very high security reasons you may want to set all SMB clients accessing this share to encrypt each and every packet, then you can set this to “yes”. This security feature can have cost on performance.

"enableMultiChannel": "yes" → Set this parameter to “yes”. If there are multiple NICs in the server and an SMB client is accessible via both the NICs then the bandwidth will get aggregated and provide HA in case of any NIC failure. In case there are multiple network paths between the client and the server, the SMB3 protocol uses the multi-channel feature.

"maxTransactionSize": 8 → Set this parameter to 8 if the use case is to support large data i/o to get good performance. Set this parameter to 1 if the use case is to support small data i/o to get good performance.

"maxReadSize": 8 → Set this parameter to 8 if the use case is to support large data i/o to get good performance. Set this parameter to 1 if the use case is to support small data i/o to get good performance.

"maxWriteSize": 8 → Set this parameter to 8 if the use case is to support large data i/o to get good performance. Set this parameter to 1 if the use case is to support small data i/o to get good performance.

"verificationLevel": "low" → Set this parameter to “low” for good performance. For security reasons you can set this to “high”. In this case, MoSMB server will perform all checks to verify each and every incoming packet prior to processing.
"enableByteRangeLock": "no" → Set this parameter to “no”. For use cases like SQL database etc., set this to “yes”. This will have an impact on performance.
"bindInterface": "network interface name” → Specify ‘the network interface(s)’ over which the SMB client is going to connect. This is an important setting for performance.

"smbDirectRing": 512 → This is an RDMA parameter that allocates and reserves the specified number of buffers per RDMA connection. RDMA performance can be increased by increasing this to 1024, 2048, etc.
"pstorePath": "/opt/mosmb" → pstorePath must never be a directory or sub-directory within a share
"maxFilePathLen": 1 → Maximum File Path Length for any file which will get opened through server share.
"threadPoolSize": 64
"ringBufferCount": 2560
"preProcessorThreads": 4

Above parameters tune the resource usage of MoSMB server and hence the performance of MoSMB. For a server, based on the CPU cores and RAM we have provided a script to help suggest the baseline numbers. MoSMB pre-allocates buffers and the memory utilization is dependent on the following formula: preProcessorThreads × ringBufferCount × Transaction size Refer to Performance Tuning numbers table for full understanding.
MoSMB Logging /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf Configurations
Set the MoSMB log level to 0 or 1 in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf file for all modules.
For config module, it can be set to 2.
Performance Tuning Script

MoSMB provides a performance tuning script on MoSMB installation. Script is located at /etc/mosmb/scripts/perf_tuning.sh. One can run this script w.r.t the MoSMB features enabled either TCP or smbDirect. Just run the script to get tuning parameters like preProcessorThreads, threadPoolSize and ringBufferCount are set as per the server configuration.
Name :
perf_tuning.sh
Location :
/etc/mosmb/scripts
Description :

This script is used to auto tune the performance parameters of MoSMB configuration based on the cpu and ram available on the server machine for both TCP and smbDirect options. This script will provide suggestions for config tuning parameters for a given number of CPU cores and RAM if provided as parameters. By default, it takes the machine CPU cores and RAM and suggests config parameters.
Usage:

perf_tuning.sh [[tcp|smbdirect] [-y] [cpu=<n> ram=<n<k|m|g>>]|[-h|--help]]
   perf_tuning.sh cpu=2 ram=4194304k / 4096m / 4g => Outputs parameters for 2 cpus and 4GB RAM
   perf_tuning.sh tcp => Outputs parameters for tcp only configuration
   perf_tuning.sh smbdirect => Outputs parameters for smbDirect configuration
   cpu = Number of CPUs (Default: system CPUs)   
   ram = Mention RAM size in Kilo/Mega/Gigabytes (Default: System RAM)    
   -y|Y = Answer "yes" for all prompts.
   -h => shows this help message Note: If CPU and/or RAM parameters are not mentioned, then the script will consider the system CPU cores and system RAM as the default. RAM parameter must be used when the RAM available on the server will be used by other applications and there is a maximum limit on the RAM that must be used by MoSMB server. CPU parameter must be provided when the expected number of cores and CPU time usage by MoSMB server is to be restricted.
Examples:

# ./perf_tuning.sh tcp Calculating possible performance tuning parameters for TCP
       Required Message Signing: no
       Maximum transaction size: 8 MB
       Ring buffers: 1216
       Worker threads: 96
       Pre-processor threads: 16
       Apply these values to configuration[y/n]:y
       Applied values to configuration
       
   # ./perf_tuning.sh smbdirect Configuration value for "enableSmbDirect" is not set.
       This would be set to "yes" if configuration is applied
       Calculating possible performance tuning parameters for TCP and smbDirect
       Required Message Signing: no
       Maximum transaction size: 8 MB
       Ring buffers: 192
       Worker threads: 96
       Pre-processor threads: 16
       smbDirect Buffer size: 1 MB
       smbDirect ring buffers: 2048
       Apply these values to configuration[y/n]:y
       Applied values to configuration
Performance Tuning for filesystem at share-level
"shareEncrypt": "no" → Enabling encryption for every smb packet request and response will increase security but cost performance.
"caseSensitive": "no" → Setting this param to “no” will improve performance compared to setting this to “yes”
"msDfsSupport": "no" → Setting this param to “no” will improve performance compared to setting this to “yes”
"enableUserBasedEnumeration": "no" → Setting this param to “no” will improve performance compared to setting this to “yes”
"useDirectIo": "yes" → If the usecase has a lot of DirectIO then setting this to “yes” will improve the performance. If the usecase does not involve DirectIO then you may set this param to “no”
You need to verify the server side configuration in order to make sure the connectivity and reachability of MoSMB server to Domain Name Server (DNS).
/etc/resolv.conf
This configuration file is considered a trusted source of DNS information
Verify the /etc/resolv.conf . It should ideally contain the domain related information.
Example:

Command : cat /etc/resolv.conf Output  : # Generated by NetworkManager search mosmbad.dom mosmbad2.dom nameserver 10.190.19.101 nameserver 10.200.20.201 nameserver xxx.xx.xxx.x nameserver x.x.x.x Note : There can be other entries in the /etc/resolv.conf as well but make sure respective domain entry is mentioned and the primary domain name and corresponding IP first in the list.
/etc/hosts
This configuration file associates IP addresses with hostname/s of MoSMB server.
Verify the /etc/hosts .
Example:

Command : cat /etc/hosts Output  : 127.0.0.1    localhost localhost.localdomain localhost4 localhost4.localdomain4 10.190.19.230    my-mosmb-cluster.mosmbad.dom    mymosmbserver 10.190.19.231    my-mosmb-cluster.mosmbad.dom    mymosmbserver 10.190.19.232    my-mosmb-cluster.mosmbad.dom    mymosmbserver 10.190.19.230    my-mosmb-cluster.mosmbad2.dom    mymosmbserver 10.190.19.231    my-mosmb-cluster.mosmbad2.dom    mymosmbserver 10.190.19.232    my-mosmb-cluster.mosmbad2.dom    mymosmbserver
