Installing ZooKeeper

We recommend to have separate MoSMB and MoSMB-ZooKeeper for better performance. If MoSMB-Zookeeper is to be used in the cluster mode ZooKeeper recommends to have odd number of servers present in the cluster for better service provision; so install MoSMB-ZooKeeper on atleast 3 nodes or 5,7,9... so on.
Installing ZooKeeper (Releases before March 2022)
1. Get ZooKeeper installer apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin
wget https://archive.apache.org/dist/zookeeper/zookeeper-3.6.3/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin.tar.gz
2. Extract downloaded packages
tar -xvzf apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin.tar.gz
Installing ZooKeeper on CentOS / RHEL using rpm (Releases after March 2022)

For Cluster builds, a ZooKeeper rpm will also be provided along with MoSMB rpm. MoSMB-ZooKeeper rpm is present at : /opt/mosmb/package/mosmb_install Command : rpm -ivh /opt/mosmb/package/mosmb_install/mosmb-zookeeper-3.6.3-1.x86_64.rpm Output : Verifying...                          ################################# [100%] Preparing...                          ################################# [100%] Updating / installing... 1:mosmb-zookeeper-3.6.3-1          ################################# [100%] zookeeper is installed at : /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/
Uninstall Previous ZooKeeper Version
1. Stop older version of ZooKeeper service (Older than zookeeper 3.6.3)
<ZooKeepr installed directory- apache bin directory>./bin/zkServer.sh stop
2. ZooKeeper’s previous version's source directory
For zookeeper 3.4.5 - zookeeper3.4.5/src/c
For zookeeper 3.6.1 - apache-zookeeper-3.6.1/zookeeper-client/zookeeper-client-c/
3. Run commands -
make clean make uninstall
4. Make sure all previous library files are not on the ZooKeeper server machine -
find /usr/ -name libzookeeper*
To setup and configure the MoSMB server to work in the noauth mode for any Server Operating System, perform the following basic steps:
Ensure that you have mentioned the correct storage path in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json in Share Settings .
In the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file, under section authSection , set the "authMode": "noAuth"
Start / Restart the MoSMB server for the desired changes to take effect.
In the noauth authentication mode, the only applicable authentication strategy (ACL)  is none .

Get ZooKeeper for other builds and distributions
1. Get zookeeper installer apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin
wget https://archive.apache.org/dist/zookeeper/zookeeper-3.6.3/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin.tar.gz
2. Extract downloaded packages
tar -xvzf apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin.tar.gz
Configure the ZooKeeper Server
1. Go to other extracted directory apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin 2. Copy sample ZooKeeper configuration file from /etc/mosmb/.
cp /etc/mosmb/mosmb_zoo.cfg conf/zoo.cfg
3.  Create ZooKeeper server data directory can be any preferred location other than /tmp.
mkdir -p /var/lib/zookeeper-mosmb ..........
4.  ZooKeeper Standalone Server Configuration
5. ZooKeeper Cluster Server Configuration
For detailed information on the configuration parameters refer to the ZooKeeper Server Configuration Parameters page.
6. Create the myid file on the ZooKeeper server/s at the /var/lib/zookeeper-mosmb/ path.
Example on a Cluster ZooKeeper:

Test data                      : ZooKeeper Server IP list - 10.10.0.1, 10.10.0.2, 10.10.0.3 File on server 1 (10.10.0.1)   : cat /var/lib/zookeeper-mosmb/myid Output on server 1 (10.10.0.1) : 1 File on server 2 (10.10.0.2)   : cat /var/lib/zookeeper-mosmb/myid Output on server 2 (10.10.0.2) : 2 File on server 3 (10.10.0.3)   : cat /var/lib/zookeeper-mosmb/myid Output on server 3 (10.10.0.3) : 3
Setup zookeepr server side log rotation

This step is not mandatory. In case zookeeper restarts, the server side log located in the log folder of zookeeper installation path, (/opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/logs/) is overwritten and old logs are over-written/lost. To avoid loss of old logs, one can do the following
1. A solution is to modify zkEnv.sh and set ZOO_LOG4J_PROP

$ cat /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkEnv.sh | grep -i PROP
if [ "x${ZOO_LOG4J_PROP}" = "x" ]
 ZOO_LOG4J_PROP="INFO,CONSOLE"
2. here add ROLLINGFILE

$ cat /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkEnv.sh | grep -i PROP
if [ "x${ZOO_LOG4J_PROP}" = "x" ]
  ZOO_LOG4J_PROP="INFO,CONSOLE, ROLLINGFILE "
3. Now every restart of mosmb-zookeeper service will have two log files, one current (.out) and one backed up with old logs (.log).
Start the ZooKeeper Server
1. Go to the ZooKeeper directory apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin and run the following command:
./bin/zkServer.sh start
This will create a ZooKeeper log file at location ../apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/logs/ that will contain the ZooKeeper server related logs. 2. Check status.
Example for a Standalone ZooKeeper Server:
Command   : ./bin/zkServer.sh status Output    : JMX enabled by default Using config: ../apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/conf/zoo.cfg Mode: Standalone
Example for a Cluster ZooKeeper Server:

Test data : ZooKeeper Server IP list - 10.10.0.1, 10.10.0.2, 10.10.0.3 Command   : ./bin/zkServer.sh status Output    : JMX enabled by default Using config: ../apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/conf/zoo.cfg' Mode: Leader (This output will be different on all server one will be the leader and other servers will be Follower )

rpm -e can be used to uninstall the zookeeper rpm.
# rpm -e mosmb-zookeeper-3.6.3-1.x86_64
Uninstall Previous older ZooKeeper Versions
1. Stop older version of ZooKeeper service (Older than zookeeper 3.6.3)
./bin/zkServer.sh stop
2. Go to zookeeper’s previous version's source directory
For zookeeper 3.4.5 - zookeeper3.4.5/src/c
For zookeeper 3.6.1 - apache-zookeeper-3.6.1/zookeeper-client/zookeeper-client-c/
3. Run command -
make clean make uninstall
4. Make sure all previous library files are not on the zookeeper server machine -
find /usr/ -name libzookeeper*
5. Continue with required ZooKeeper version build and installation.

ZooKeeper is a centralized service for maintaining configuration information, naming, providing distributed synchronization, and providing group services.

<server_hostname> : <zookeeper_client_port> OR <server_ip_address> : <zookeeper_client_port>
Clustered ZooKeeper Server : If required you can make the Clustered ZooKeeper Server settings. Refer to the Clustered ZooKeeper Server Settings image underneath.
The detailed ZooKeeper Server Configuration parameters are provided in the Zookeeper Server Configuration File Details .

Prerequisites

All MoSMB-CL builds supports single domain clustering. To form a single domain MoSMB cluster simply follow the configurations mentioned in steps below one by one. 1. One EDF node should have the clustershell configured for executing commands in parallel on a cluster. 2. Carry out Network Time Protocol (NTP) Configuration settings on all MoSMB server nodes. 3. Install Kerberos packages on all MoSMB server nodes
AD-Kerberos configurations
1. Request the Domain Administration team for AD and DNS Configurations.
Create a Special user in the Active Directory (AD) (Step required to be carried out only once per MoSMB cluster)
Create MoSMB Cluster Keytab (Step required to be carried out only once per MoSMB cluster)
Copy generated keytab file to all the MoSMB server nodes which are needed in a cluster as mosmb.keytab at location /etc/mosmb/

Copy the mosmb.keytab in /etc/mosmb folder of one node 
   Copy the keytab to all mosmb nodes clush -B -ac /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
Configure DNS entries for the MoSMB Cluster
Carry out name resolution settings on all MoSMB servers nodes.
2. Kerberos and related services configurations on all MoSMB server nodes in the cluster.
Configure MoSMB krb5 settings
Configure LDAP client settings
Configure SSSD settings
Configure the domain_list.txt settings
MoSMB Kerberos Ticket Generation.
mosmb.json Configuration
1. On one node where clush is set, update the mosmb.json
1. Add the Product Key in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file 2. mosmb.json parameters for cluster setup

* The mosmb housekeeping volume created with correct replication factor and low latency to be used as pstore * You can also use bindInterface parameter to bind a particular network interface to mosmb for the SMB traffic
   * In globalSection set enableHighAvailability to yes * In vinodeSection check that the "mechanism" is set to "zookeeper"
   * In persistentStoreSection keep pstorePath location on the common storage path on all MoSMB nodes in the cluster.
   * In section nodeInfoSection, assign a different nodeId to each node in MoSMB cluster. ( NOTE: this is a parameter that will differ on each of the mosmb nodes. )
   * In section authSection, set authMode as ntlmLocal . ( NOTE: This will ensure krb5 authentication is tried first, if that is not successful, the new releases support ntlmSSP authentication, if that fails then ntlmLocal authentication is tried. )
   * In shareSection keep sharePath location on the common storage path on all MoSMB nodes in the cluster.
   * In shareSection set parameters isContinouslyAvailable and isCluster to yes * In shareSection set parameter fsInfoAutoDetect to yes * In shareSection set parameter checkPosixPermissionBit to no * In shareSection set parameter useDirectIo to yes * In shareSection set parameters fileCreationMask and directoryCreationMask as per requirement For detailed mosmb.json parameters for cluster setup refer: step 8 in Setup the MoSMB configuration file for cluster use

3. Add all required shares to the mosmb.json. For this you can use the mosmbcli utility. First use the MCS or CLI or REST API to create volumes on the EDF cluster. Use this Path to add new share in mosmb.json Example: mosmbcli set --json shares -share_name <share name> -path <share path on EDF cluster> -is_cluster yes -fs_info_auto_detect yes  -check_posix_permission_bit no -use_direct_io yes -file_creation_mask 0664 -directory_creation_mask 0775
2. Copy this mosmb.json to all other nodes
clush -B -ac /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json

3. Set the zookeeper server list in vinode section under hostPort parameter in rotation. (keep the local node IP first in the list for better performance if zookeeper coexists with mosmb or else distribute the load by rotating the IPs)
Example:

4. In section nodeInfoSection, assign a different nodeId to each node in MoSMB cluster
Example:

clush -w node1name mosmbcli set --json node_info -node_id 1
   clush -w node2name mosmbcli set --json node_info -node_id 2
   clush -w node3name mosmbcli set --json node_info -node_id 3
   clush -w node4name mosmbcli set --json node_info -node_id 4
   clush -w node5name mosmbcli set --json node_info -node_id 5
Enable NTLMSSP (NTLM-AD) authentication support
Refer: Enable NTLMSSP Authentication Support
Check ulimits
Check ulimits for the open file and stack size are set to unlimited or 65536 for posix client and MoSMB
* For machine: ulimit -a * For MoSMB service: ps aux | grep mosmb cat /proc/<pid>/limits
Check fuse.conf for posix client
fuse.flush.inline value is set to 0 fuse.disable.writeback value is set to 1 fuse.use.compressed.inode.format value is set to 1
NOTE: Any changes here would need a posix client service restart
Set Priority List for AD IPs (in a AD Farm) if required
If you have a AD farm, and want mosmb to use only few of them for any reason create a list of AD IPs: Add Priority List for AD IPs in /etc/mosmb

How to Update MoSMB to new version

How to Create HyperV VM
HyperV VM creation is currently only supported in the krb5 authentication mode and the posix acl authorization mode configuration. Verify if all of the following settings are done:
The MoSMB share must have the complete permissions set for all. (777 -- drwxrwxrwx)
The MoSMB share Ownership Settings must be carried out.
The File Creation Mask and Directory Creation Mask are set to 0777 in the MoSMB Share Configuration .
Sign in to the HyperV machine with the Domain Administrator user credentials.
The HyperV machine's primary group must be the same as the MoSMB share owner and it must be the Domain Admin.

Note : The Domain Admin requirement depends on the HyperV configuration settings. If a non-admin user has permissions to create the HyperV VM then this user can create the VM on the MoSMB share - if the share settings are configured for that non-admin user.
How to do Performance Tuning for MoSMB

To scale up the MoSMB on one node, add more NICs, CPU or memory as desired. If the backend storage is a bottleneck, add high performance storage like NVMe disks. To scale out, add another MoSMB node to the cluster and expose the same share.
For detailed information please refer Performance Tuning pages

Note: For TCP For a given NIC, set minimum values if you want MoSMB to use low RAM and CPU cores For a given NIC, set maximum values if you want MoSMB to use higher RAM and CPU cores For a given NIC - Keep the TCP buffers:thread ratio approximately as given in the above column for e.g. if you increase the no of threads by 1 for 10Gbps bw then increase the ringbufferCount by 8 (ringbufferCount × preprocessor threads 8 × 8=64 TCP buffers) Set TransactionSize=8 for large files transactions Set TransactionSize=1 for small file io transactions
Note: For RDMA + TCP threadPoolSize is common to both RDMA and TCP transport.
How to use MoSMB Audit Logs for Security Monitoring
Use these logs for Security Monitoring. One can set the audit module’s log level in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf file to 2 to get detailed audit logs .
Set the audit log level in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf file to 3. AUDIT_L = 3 Restart the MoSMB service OR update logging at runtime without restarting mosmb

The mosmb_audit.log audit log file will then be generated at the log location mentioned in the mosmb.json file, default is /var/log . Audit logs are populated for following categories: CREATE RENAME DELETE PERMISSION_CHANGE LOGIN LOGOFF

When MoSMB is installed, the scripts for the log rotation of the mosmb audit logs are added in the /etc/cron.hourly directory (mologrotate) and the /etc/logrotate.d directory. Logs are rotated when the log file size reaches 500 MB. The log size threshold reached is checked on an hourly basis.
To disable the audit logs set the audit log level to 0. (ERROR level) AUDIT_L = 0 Restart the MoSMB service OR update logging at runtime without restarting mosmb

Note that : Along with mosmb_audit.log other log files are rotated in similar way; with files mosmbauditlr (mosmb_audit.log), mosmblr (mosmb.log) and zookeeperlr (mosmb_vinode.log) present under /etc/logrotate.d/

NTACLs
1.  The following error occurred when I tried to access a new NTACL share :
Solution : Please initialize the share permissions by following the steps mentioned here .
2.  The following error occurred when assigning NTACL permissions to a mosmb share with existing files/folders when “Applies to”:”This folder” and we “Enable Inheritance”:

Solution : Reset the permissions for the share :
Please use the following command to reset the xattr values for NTACL:
setfattr -x user.mosmb_acl <share_path> Example: setfattr -x user.mosmb_acl /opt/share/
Access the server and set user permissions and owner again with the following settings:
Set the “Applies to” option in Properties->Security->Advanced->Add tab to “This folder, subfolders and files” as mentioned in Inheritance
3. Unable to assign share permissions for a windows local groups.
Solution : MoSMB currently supports Everyone and domain groups only .
Json Syntax

As part of strict json syntax handling while running the MoSMB server, the user should ensure that - 1. Opening or closing curly braces for the entire json file should not be missing. 2. All keys and values should be separated by : 3. Comments in mosmb.json are not allowed  currently.
You can use mosmbcli parser to validate mosmb.json
mosmbcli validate --json
RC4 encryption deprecated in CentOS 8.*
In CentOS 8.* , RC4 encryption is disabled in the DEFAULT crypto policy level.
Following changes are needed for kerberos to work on CentOS 8*
There are two solutions (To maintain stronger security, we suggest you use first option)
1. Changes in User's Properties in the AD

In AD, go to Users and Computers -> Users ->Select the special user-> Open
properties ->select account tab, and choose the following account options
This account supports Kerberos AES 128 bit encryption
This account supports Kerberos AES 256 bit encryption
OR
2. On mosmb server make changes to DEFAULT crypto policy, to include RC4
i) On every CentOS 8.3 and above mosmb server, where authentication against AD Domain Controllers takes place,
Use the update-crypto-policies command to enable the AD-SUPPORT cryptographic subpolicy in addition to the DEFAULT cryptographic policy.

[root@host ~]# update-crypto-policies --set DEFAULT:AD-SUPPORT
       Setting system policy to DEFAULT:AD-SUPPORT
       Note: System-wide crypto policies are applied on application start-up.
       It is recommended to restart the system for the change of policies
       to fully take place.
Restart the host.
IMPORTANT
The AD-SUPPORT cryptographic subpolicy is only available on CentOS 8.3 and newer.

ii) To enable support for RC4 in CentOS 8.2 , create and enable a custom cryptographic module policy with cipher = RC4-128+. For more details, see Customizing system-wide cryptographic policies with policy modifiers.

https://access.redhat.com/documentation/en-us/red_hat_enterprise_linux/8/html/security_hardening/using-the-system-wide-cryptographic-policies_security-hardening#customizing-system-wide-cryptographic-policies-with-policy-modifiers_using-the-system-wide-cryptographic-policies
https://www.redhat.com/en/blog/how-customize-crypto-policies-rhel-82?extIdCarryOver=true&sc_cid=701f2000001OH7JAAW
iii) To enable support for RC4 in CentOS 8.0 and CentOS 8.1 , add +rc4 to the permitted_enctypes option in the /etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/krb5.config file

[libdefaults]
       permitted_enctypes = aes256-cts-hmac-sha1-96 aes256-cts-hmac-sha384-192 camellia256-cts-cmac aes128-cts-hmac-sha1-96 aes128-cts-hmac-sha256-128 camellia128-cts-cmac +rc4
What if I want to change the node_id for some reason

In a situation, where one wants to change the node_id of a node, from a cluster, it is recommended that full cluster is stopped (MoSMB and Zookeeper both) and a clean up is carried out, before you change the node_id of a node or many nodes in the mosmb.json.

1. stop mosmb service on all nodes
2. stop mosmb-zookeeper on all zookeeper nodes
3. run the clean up command
/etc/mosmb/scripts/mosmb_zk-cleanup.sh <zookeeper install directory path>
e.g. /etc/mosmb/scripts/mosmb_zk-cleanup.sh /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin 4. once the clean up is finished, open /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json of the node where you want to change the node_id, and update it. (one node or many nodes of cluster as per your requirement)
5. Save the json files and restart the cluster, first all zk servers and then all mosmb services in the cluster nodes

A keytab is a file containing pairs of Kerberos principals and encrypted keys that are derived from the Kerberos password. One can use this file to log on to Kerberos without being prompted for a password.
1. Create mosmb.keytab

Note : skip this step for Release versions:3.1.1_004 and above. After Domain join process is carried out /etc/krb5.keytab is created.
   Run ktutil commands to generate mosmb.keytab at location /etc/mosmb Command : ktutil ktutil: rkt /etc/krb5.keytab ktutil: l ktutil: delent <slot no for restricted entries> ktutil: wkt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab ktutil: q
2. Add host keys with encryption to /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab

Run ktutil commands as follows: Command : ktutil ktutil: addent -password -p <domain user>@<DOMAIN NAME> -k 1 -e rc4-hmac Password for <domain user>@<domain name>: 
       ktutil: addent -password -p <domain user>@<DOMAIN NAME> -k 1 -e aes256-cts Password for <domain user>@<domain name>: 
       ktutil: wkt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab ktutil: q
Example:

ktutil ktutil: addent -password -p mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM -k 1 -e rc4-hmac Password for mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM:
       ktutil: addent -password -p mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM -k 1 -e aes256-cts Password for mouser@MOSMBAD.DOM:
       ktutil: wkt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab ktutil: q
Notes :

1. The domain user used in above commands and the user used to generate the ticket with kinit should be the same.
   2. The domain name in above commands must be in uppercase.
   3. If and when the user password or user spn is changed Kerberos keytab related settings are needed to carry out again

The krb5 configuration file contains Kerberos configuration information, including the locations of KDCs and admin servers for the Kerberos realms of interest, defaults for the current realm and for Kerberos applications, and mappings of hostnames onto Kerberos realms.
Warning: Please ensure correct name settings for KDC, AD, DNS host FQDNs from your network administrator.
/etc/krb5.conf
Make sure these entries are present in the /etc/krb5.conf file.
Include directory - /etc/krb5.conf.d
Modify libdefaults section - rdns, default_ccache_name, default_realm
Example:
includedir /etc/krb5.conf.d/ [libdefaults] .......... rdns = false default_ccache_name = FILE:/opt/mosmb/kerberos_tkt default_realm = MOSMBAD.DOM #Please note this is example. ..........
/etc/krb5.conf.d/mosmb_krb5.conf
Create and open a new file /etc/krb5.conf.d/mosmb_krb5.conf
Add realms section with kdc and admin_server entries.
Add domain_realm section with domain name entry.
Example:
[realms] MOSMBAD.DOM = { kdc = mo-ad.mosmbad.dom master_kdc = mo-ad.mosmbad.dom } [domain_realm] mosmbad.dom = MOSMBAD.DOM .mosmbad.dom = MOSMBAD.DOM
Limitations for cache type
Kerberos 1.13 and above, MoSMB support cache types FILE, KCM, DIR. keyring is not supported.

# cat /etc/krb5.conf | grep default_ccache_name
       default_ccache_name = FILE:/opt/mosmb/mosmb_kerberos_ticket OR default_ccache_name = DIR:/opt/mosmb/kerberos_ticket_dir #default_ccache_name = KEYRING:persistent:%{uid}      [not supported] # cat /etc/krb5.conf.d/kcm_default_ccache | grep default_ccache_name
       default_ccache_name = KCM:
Notes :

1. MoSMB will support only cache type FILE for kerberos versions below 1.13
   2. The FILE and DIR path can be of your choice. Make sure path is anything other than /tmp for persistence of cache across system reboots.
Requirement
You must have a working model of the AD in your Evaluation and/or Production environment before you start using MoSMB with Kerberos authentication.
You require administrative rights of the AD machine to make related modifications.
Creating Special User in Active Directory
In order to use MoSMB server we need a special user in the AD
Login to AD machine with Domain Administrator user.
Open Active Directory Users and Computers
Enter the name of user for example - mo-special-user
Enter the user logon name - UPN for example - mospecialuser@mosmbad.dom → Next
Enter the user's password →
In Account section → Account options
Select This account supports Kerberos AES 128 bit encryption
Select This account supports Kerberos AES 256 bit encryption
Notes :

Every single standalone MoSMB server and MoSMB Cluster will have its own special user.
   Special user should be used to generate MoSMB cluster keytab from AD domain.

cmdpipe
cmdpipe is a helper script located in /etc/mosmb/scripts directory and it support following tasks -
Reload the MoSMB configuration file - /etc/mosmb/mosm.json after adding new share
Update MoSMB logging at runtime
Notifies SMB Command frequency count
Display the MoSMB server statistics
Prerequisites
Python and pip of same version
Command-line JSON processor : jq package
MoSMB service should be running
Usage

Command: cd /etc/mosmb/scripts/ Command: ./cmdpipe -h Usage: ./cmdpipe <command>
       command:
       config_reload_add : Reloads mosmb config to add new share
       add_runtime_logging : Notifies mosmb of the changes in /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf
       smb_frequency_count : Notifies smb command frequency count in  <log folder from mosmb.json>/mosmb_smb_frequency_cnt.log file
       set_maintenance_mode : Set the MoSMB server in Maintenance Mode. Note: Available from Release 069 get_mosmb_config : Get the configuration parameters of MoSMB server. Note: Available from Release 087 statson : Starts collecting statistics
       domainlist_reload : Reloads the domainlist.txt contents in mosmb Note: Available from Release 100

Use the following options for mosmb config reload
       -n : Specifies share name
       -t : Specifies share type
       -h : Display this help message and exit
       Share type can be one if these two : VFS or S3
       Following is an Example:
       ./cmdpipe config_reload_add -n share_name -t vfs
       OR
       ./cmdpipe config_reload_add -n share_name -t S3
           
       Use the following options for dumping stats into csv periodically
       -p : Specifies csv path. Default is /var/log/
       -i : Specifies time period in seconds. This is the repeat interval for the dump job. Default is 300
       -c : Display stats on 'console'
       -h : Shows statson help message and Exit
       -n : Number count for stats operation
       Following is an Example:
       ./cmdpipe statson -p /var/log/ -i 600
       ./cmdpipe statson -p /var/log/ -i 600 -c : Display the stats on console after every '-i' interval
       ./cmdpipe statson -p /var/log/ -i 600 -c -n 5: Display the stats on console after every '-i' interval for '-n' number of time
Examples
config_reload_add
Prerequisites:
MoSMB should be running.
New share should be added to MoSMB configurations /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json either manually or by mosmbcli set --json command for share option
Usage:

Command: /etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe config_reload_add -h Use the following options for mosmb config reload
       -n : Specifies share name
       -t : Specifies share type
       -h : Display this help message and exit
       Share type can be one if these two : VFS or S3
       Following is an Example:
       ./cmdpipe config_reload_add -n share_name -t vfs
       OR
       ./cmdpipe config_reload_add -n share_name -t S3
Example:

Command: ./cmdpipe config_reload_add -n "Local xfs" -t vfs Success
       Command executed successfully
add_runtime_logging
Prerequisites:

Logging level changes will take effect for mosmb logs as per changes in /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf
Usage:

Command: /etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe add_runtime_logging -h 1) First make the required log level changes in  /etc/mosmb/mosmb_logging.conf
       2) Now run the command /etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe add_runtime_logging
Example:

Command: ./cmdpipe add_runtime_logging Logging Reload Command initialized
       Success

smb_frequency_count
Prerequisites:
MoSMB should be running. Note: File mosmb_smb_frequency_cnt.log will be created at the MoSMB logs directory mentioned in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json.

set_maintenance_mode
Prerequisites:
MoSMB should be running to enable or disable maintenance mode. Note: New option available only from MoSMB release 069 onwards.
Usage:

Command: /etc/mosmb/scripts/cmdpipe set_maintenance_mode -h Usage options for mosmb set maintenance mode -o : Specifies option to enable or disable the maintenance mode
       -h : Display this help message and exit
Example:

Command: ./cmdpipe set_maintenance_mode -o enable Success
       Command executed successfully
       Also mosmb.log will show the notification message as follows - [MO_CRITICAL]:[SMBCORE]:[TID 42496]: MoSMB server has entered into Maintenance Mode. No new connections/sessions will be accepted now.
get_mosmb_config
Prerequisites:
MoSMB should be running to get the current static and dynamic configurations. Note: New option available only from MoSMB release 087 onwards.
Usage:

Command: ./cmdpipe get_mosmb_config -p <path> -f humanreadable OR ./cmdpipe get_mosmb_config -p <path> -f csv Usage options for mosmb get configuration parameters -p : Specify path where the configuration parameters will be dumped. Default is path pointed out by logsPath parameter in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json
       -f : Specify format in which the configuration parameters will be dumped. Default is 'humanreadable'
       -h : Display this help message and exit
Example:

Command: ./cmdpipe get_mosmb_config -f csv Sending get server config params command to the MoSMB server.
     Command executed successfully
     Dump of the configuration parameters is at /var/log/ -- csv file named mosmb_config_params_dump.csv is created.
statson
Prerequisites:

MoSMB should be running. Note: Following files will be created when -p option is given depending on the build type 1. preprocessor_stats.csv 2. server_stats.csv 3. worker_thread_stats.csv 4. smbdirect_stats.csv 5. processed_stats.csv
Usage:

Command: ./cmdpipe statson -h Usage: cmdpipe statson [OPTION]...
       MOSMB server statistics
       Optional Arguments to statson
               -p, --path       Specifies csv path. Default is /var/log/
               -i, --interval   Specifies time period in seconds. This is the repeat interval for the dump job. Default is 300
               -c, --console    Display stats on 'console'
               -n, --number     Number count for stats operation
               -h, --help       Shows this help message and Exit
       
       Following Abbreviations used while showing output on console using '-c' option
       
       RIO(Read IOPS), WIO(Write IOPS), TIOPs(Total IOPS), RT(Read Throughput), WT(Write Throughput),
       TT(MB/s)(Total Throughput MB/s), RL(Read Latency), WL(Write Latency), SRL(Storage Read Latency),
       SWL(Storage Write Latency), FO(Total File/Pipe Opened), ES(Total Established Sessions),
       DC(Total Disconnects), PV(Password Violations), PE(Total Permission Error)
Example: Following is an Example:

Support Added for .prom file format from version 093 onwards MoSMB version 093 onwards cmdpipe supports .prom file format to collect metric in the Prometheus text exposition format. (key value pair) More details on how to use Prometheus to monitor mosmb metrics is available HERE

Use the following options for dumping stats into csv/prom periodically
  -p : Specifies stats path. Default path for 'csv' format is /var/log/. Note: While using 'prom' stats format, it is mandatory to use same path for both i.e. 'cmdpipe' and 'node_exporter' ('--collector.textfile.directory' option) of prometheus. -f : Specifies format in which the stats will be dumped (prom / csv) . Default is 'csv' format
  -i : Specifies time period in seconds. This is the repeat interval for the dump job. Default is 300
  -c : Displays stats on 'console'
  -h : Shows statson help message and Exit
  -n : Number count for stats operation
   
Following is an Example: ./cmdpipe statson -p /var/lib/node_exporter -f prom -i 600 -c -n 5 i.e. Display the stats on console after every '-i' interval for '-n' number of time, save stats in processed_stats.prom file in folder /var/lib/node_exporter

   MoSMB_TotalPasswordViolations 0

domainlist_reload
Prerequisites:

MoSMB should be running to reload the domain's list from domain_list.txt And it will also check if the domain has a whitelist present, if yes, it will use the IPs mentioned in the whitelist of the specific domain to reconnect to AD Note: New option available only from MoSMB release 100 onwards.

Contents
1 Setup Details
2 Creating msdfs link/s 2.1 1. Link pointing to single node. 2.2 2. Link pointing to multiple nodes.
2.1 1. Link pointing to single node.
2.2 2. Link pointing to multiple nodes.
3 Export created msdfs link/s over MoSMB
Setup Details

Node1: IP: 10.10.0.10
   Host Name: server1.mosmbad.dom
   mosmb shareName: share1 Node2: IP: 10.10.0.20
   Host Name: server2.mosmbad.dom 
   mosmb shareName: share1 Node3: IP: 10.10.0.30
   Host Name: server3.mmosmbad.dom
   mosmb shareNames: share1, share2 & localshare
Creating msdfs link/s
1. Link pointing to single node.
msdfs link can be created by using either server IP or FQDN
ln -s msdfs:\\<ip>\\<shareName> <linkName> OR ln -s msdfs:\\<fqdn>\\<shareName> <linkName>
In following example we are creating a msdfs link on Node 3 pointing to Node 1's exported share named as share1.
Examples:

Command1 : mkdir /opt/share1 Command2 : cd /opt/share1 Command3 : ln -s msdfs:\\10.10.0.10\\share1 linka Command4 : ls -lah linka Output   : lrwxrwxrwx   1 root root 30 Aug 20 12:58 linka -> 'msdfs:\10.10.0.10\share1' Command5 : ln -s msdfs:\\server1.mosmbad.dom\\share1 linkb Command6 : ls -lah linkb Output   : lrwxrwxrwx   1 root root 30 Aug 20 12:58 linkb -> 'msdfs:\server1.mosmbad.dom\share1'
2. Link pointing to multiple nodes.
msdfs link can be created by using either server IP or FQDN
ln -s msdfs:\\<ip>\\<shareName>,\\<ip>\\<shareName> <linkName> OR ln -s msdfs:\\<fqdn>\\<shareName>,\\<fqdn>\\<shareName> <linkName>

In following example we are creating a msdfs link on Node 3 pointing to Node 1 and Node2's exported shares named as share1. Consider that Node1 and Node2 are in cluster and pointing to same storage exported over share1.
Examples:

Command1 : mkdir /opt/share2 Command2 : cd /opt/share2 Command3 : ln -s msdfs:\\10.10.0.10\\share1,\\10.10.0.20\\share1 multilinka Command4 : ls -lah multilinka Output   : lrwxrwxrwx   1 root root 30 Aug 20 12:58 multilinka -> 'msdfs:\10.10.0.10\share1,\10.10.0.20\share1' Command5 : ln -s msdfs:\\server1.mosmbad.dom\\share1,\\server2.mosmbad.dom\\share1 multilinkb Command6 : ls -lah multilinkb Output   : lrwxrwxrwx   1 root root 30 Aug 20 12:58 multilinkb -> 'msdfs:\server1.mosmbad.dom\share1,\server2.mosmbad.dom\share1'
Export created msdfs link/s over MoSMB

To support msdfs links with mosmb, some configuration changes are needed in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json only on the node where links are created. Set the parameters under Section "sharesSection" as "msDfsSupport": "yes", and "metadataStore": "xattr" for share which has links.
Example:

Command : cat /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json | grep -e shareName -e sharePath -e msDfsSupport -e metadata Output on Node1 : "shareName": "share1",
     "sharePath": "/mnt/share1",
     "msDfsSupport": "no",
     "metadataStore": "none", Output on Node2 : "shareName": "share1",
     "sharePath": "/mnt/share1",
     "msDfsSupport": "no",
     "metadataStore": "none", Output on Node3 : "shareName": "share1",
     "sharePath": "/opt/share1/", "msDfsSupport": "yes", "metadataStore": "xattr", "shareName": "share2",
     "sharePath": "/opt/share2/", "msDfsSupport": "yes", "metadataStore": "xattr", "shareName": "localshare",
     "sharePath": "/home/share1",
     "msDfsSupport": "no",
     "metadataStore": "none",
Notes :

1. If the format used while creating links is incorrect, then DFS link access will fail.
   2. msdfs links created only on files are supported currently. Links created on directory are not fully supported.
   3. If msDfsSupport parameter is set to no in mosmb.json where links are crated, then the links even though they are present on server side, will not be enumerated on the client side.
   4. Make sure permissions on all nodes for shares are for same user and group in case of domain users permissions.
   5.1 Make sure permissions on all nodes for shares are for same user and group and the uid and gid are are same on all servers.
   5.2 Make sure that local users are added as mosmb users and have same password on all servers.
   6. If any of the permissions related changes are not carried out then link may not be accessible and throw the permissions related issues.

#!/bin/bash
case $1 in
status)
STATUS=`/usr/bin/sudo /etc/mosmb/mosmb_service.sh status`
if [[ "$STATUS" = *"NOT"* ]]
 then
    echo "MOSMB Stopped"
    exit 1
 else
    if [[ "$STATUS" = *"Maintenance"* ]]
      then
         echo "MOSMB is Running in Maintenance mode"
         exit 0
      else
        echo "MOSMB Running"
        exit 0
    fi
fi
;;

stop)
/usr/bin/sudo systemctl stop mosmb ;;

start)
/usr/bin/sudo systemctl start mosmb ;;
*)
echo "No Entry Found" ;;
esac
/opt/mapr/initscripts/mosmb.service  -  for mosmb version 069 and onwards till 074 (addition of maintenance mode feature)

#/usr/bin/sudo /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh $1
case $1 in
status)
STATUS=`/usr/bin/sudo /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh status`
if [[ "$STATUS" = *"not"* ]]
 then
    ZK_MOSMB_PID=/var/lib/zookeeper-mosmb/zookeeper_server.pid
    if [[ -f "$ZK_MOSMB_PID" ]]
      then
          echo "MoSMB-Zookeeper is in Failed state"
          exit 2
      else
          echo "MoSMB-Zookeeper is Stopped"
          exit 1
    fi
 else
    echo "MoSMB-Zookeeper is Running"
    exit 0
 fi
 ;;
 
stop)
 /usr/bin/sudo /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh stop
exit 0
;;

start)
/usr/bin/sudo ZK_SERVER_HEAP=20000 /opt/mosmb/zookeeper/apache-zookeeper-3.6.3-bin/bin/zkServer.sh start
exit 0
;;
*)
echo "No Entry Found" ;;
esac

MoSMB Configured in the Kerberos Authentication
Prerequisites
Ensure that the machine is either: Ubuntu, Debian, or CentOS.
In case the Client is Ubuntu ensure that the Kernel is v 4.4. In case the Client is Debian ensure that the Kernel is jessie.
Make sure that the machine has been added to the domain and has a valid Kerberos ticket. Refer How to generate a Kerberos ticket .
Ensure that the Linux user has sudo privileges.
Mount the MoSMB Share
Mount the share using the Fully Qualified Domain Name (FQDN) of the MoSMB server.
Set the security to kerberos and the version to 3.0.
Run the command:
mount -t cifs //<fqdn of server>/<share name> <mount path> -o domain=<domain name>,user=<user name>,password=<password>,sec=krb5,vers=3.02
Example:

Test Data : moserver.mosmbad.dom, mouser, password (domain user and password), /opt/share (MoSMB share), /mnt/mosmb_share (mount location) Command   : mount -t cifs //moserver.mosmbad.dom/share1 /mnt/mosmb_share -o domain=mosmbad.dom,user=mouser,password=password,sec=krb5,vers=3.02 Output    : MoSMB share should be mounted at location - /mnt/mosmb_share
MoSMB Configured in the NTLM Authentication
Prerequisites
Ensure that the Linux local users are added as MoSMB users by executing the /usr/bin/mosmb_passwd binary. Refer to How to configure the MoSMB server to work in a ntlmLocal mode .
Ensure that the machine is either: Ubuntu, Debian or CentOS.
In case the Client is Ubuntu ensure that the Kernel is v 4.4. In case the Client is Debian ensure that the Kernel is jessie.
Make sure that the Linux user has sudo privileges.
Ensure that the MoSMB server and client machines are pingable.
Mount the MoSMB Share
Mount the share using the MoSMB server's IP.
Set the security to ntlm and the version to 3.0.
Run the command:
mount -t cifs //<IP address of server>/<sharename> <mount path> -o user=<user name>,password=<password>,sec=ntlm,vers=3.02
Example:

Test Data : 10.10.0.10, motest, password (linux local user and password), /opt/share (MoSMB share), /mnt/mosmb_share (mount location) Command   : mount -t cifs //10.10.0.10/share1 /mnt/mosmb_share -o user=motest,password=password,sec=ntlm,vers=3.02 Output    : MoSMB share should be mounted at location - /mnt/mosmb_share
MoSMB Configured in the ntlmAD Authentication
Prerequisites
Ensure that the machine is either: Ubuntu, Debian or CentOS.
In case the Client is Ubuntu ensure that the Kernel is v 4.4. In case the Client is Debian ensure that the Kernel is jessie.
Make sure that the Linux user has sudo privilege.
Ensure that the MoSMB server and client machines are pingable.
Mount the MoSMB Share
Mount the share using the MoSMB server's IP.
Set the version to 3.0.2
Run the command:
mount -t cifs //<IP address of server>/<share name> <mount path> -o domain=<domain name>,user=<user name>,password=<password>,sec=ntlmv2,vers=3.02
Example:

Test Data : 10.10.0.10, /opt/share (MoSMB share), /mnt/mosmb_share (mount location) Command   : mount -t cifs //10.10.0.10/share1 /mnt/mosmb_share -o domain=mosmbad.dom,user=mouser,password=password,sec=ntlmv2,vers=3.02 Output    : MoSMB share should be mounted at location - /mnt/mosmb_share

mosmbcli
mosmbcli is command line parser. mosmbcli binary is made available in /usr/bin after MoSMB installation. mosmbcli can be used for various purposes like,
To generate or update the MoSMB configuration in json format.
To manage mosmb services on local/standalone setups.
To manage mosmb services on cluster setups.
Prerequisites
Python and pip of same version.
Command-line JSON processor : jq package
zk-shell

Setup passwordless SSH : ssh-keygen -t rsa ssh-copy-id <hostname_of_other_mosmb_server> Allow user to run any commands anywhere without password - edit /etc/sudoers - <username> ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL For more reference see : passwordless-ssh
ssh-keygen -t rsa
ssh-copy-id <hostname_of_other_mosmb_server>
Allow user to run any commands anywhere without password - edit /etc/sudoers - <username> ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL
For more reference see : passwordless-ssh

Clustershell aka clush configuration is required for mosmbcli to work. Installing clustershell After installation open the file /etc/clustershell/groups.d/local.cfg and make changes to add the required mosmb_server_nodes. Example: all: host1 If in mosmb cluster mosmb node's hostnames are mohost1, mohost2, mohost3, then one can add a group as group1: mohost[1,2,3] Note: groups in /etc/clustershell/groups.d/local.cfg, should contain nodes having only hostnames. As username@ip will not work
Installing clustershell

After installation open the file /etc/clustershell/groups.d/local.cfg and make changes to add the required mosmb_server_nodes. Example: all: host1 If in mosmb cluster mosmb node's hostnames are mohost1, mohost2, mohost3, then one can add a group as group1: mohost[1,2,3] Note: groups in /etc/clustershell/groups.d/local.cfg, should contain nodes having only hostnames. As username@ip will not work
Usage
Initail help :

Command: mosmbcli -h
       usage: mosmbcli [-h] 
                       {zk-start,zk-restart,zk-stop,zk-status,mosmb-start,mosmb-restart,mosmb-stop,mosmb-status,get-req,deploy-resp,znode,get,set,del,validate,apply}
                       ...
       
       positional arguments:
         {zk-start,zk-restart,zk-stop,zk-status,mosmb-start,mosmb-restart,mosmb-stop,mosmb-status,get-req,deploy-resp,znode,get,set,del,validate,apply}
       
       optional arguments:
         -h, --help            show this help message and exit
To get help for each command use "-h" or "--help" at each level of command :
Example :

1) mosmbcli -h
   2) mosmbcli set -h
   3) mosmbcli set --json global -h
   4) mosmbcli set --json vinode -h
   5) mosmbcli get -h
Command line Options
There are various options as mentioned in the help. We will go through them all one by one.
MoSMB-ZooKeeper
For mosmb-zookeeper we have start, restart, stop and check status options supported with local or remote nodes.
Create a /etc/mosmb/mosmb-zookeeper-clustermap.txt and add entries in following format:

NodeId Machine-IP username Password 1 88.88.88.88 root Pa55w0rd
   2 88.88.88.89 root Pa55w0rd

MoSMB License

For MoSMB Offline License activation we have get-req which generates the mosmb offline license requests on the mosmb cluster nodes and deploy-resp which deploys the shared response file for mosmb offline license activation on the mosmb cluster nodes.
One needs MoSMB Product Key in the /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json file;

Request and response will be created against mentioned product key in /etc/msomb/mosmb.json Once request's for all mentioned nodes are created then share the tar file with mosmb support and you will receive the corresponding response. Use deploy-resp command to deploy the responses on corresponding machines at required location.

Command: mosmbcli get-req -h usage: mosmbcli get-req [-h] -g GROUP
       
       optional arguments:
         -h, --help            show this help message and exit
         -g GROUP, --group GROUP
                               Specify a group defined in /etc/clustershell/groups.d/local.cfg to generate request.xml in Command: mosmbcli deploy-resp -h usage: mosmbcli deploy-resp [-h] -p PATH
       
       optional arguments:
         -h, --help            show this help message and exit
         -p PATH, --path PATH  deploy response.xml file on every node in cluster from the given path

Command: sudo mosmbcli get-req -g mosmb
   
       movm1:Status:Request.xml-generated
       movm2:Status:Request.xml-generated
       movm3:Status:Request.xml-generated
       
       Command Finished Executing
       
       For more information check /tmp/mosmb/clush_status
       Please share generated tar file /tmp/mosmb/mosmb-cluster-licenses.tar.gz with MoSMB support
Command: sudo mosmbcli deploy-resp -p /tmp/mosmb/mosmb-cluster-licenses-responses Command Executed Successfully
MoSMB-ZooKeeper Node Manipulation

WARNING: This functionality is strictly for the administrator's use only. One can list and delete the zookeeper nodes. With delete option deleting open nodes can result into inaccessibility of the same on the client machines.
zk-shell must be installed.

Command: mosmbcli znode -h
       usage: mosmbcli znode [-h] [-l] [-d DELETE]
       
       optional arguments:
         -h, --help            show this help message and exit
         -l, --list            List MoSMB-zookeeper nodes
         -d DELETE, --delete DELETE
                               Use this command to Delete MoSMB-zookeeper nodes E.g.
                               mosmbcli znode -d "/mosmb_core/<node>"

Command: mosmbcli znode -l .
       ├── mosmb_s3_root
       │   ├── queue_manager_data
       │   ├── queue

MoSMB json Manipulation

One can use the get, set, validate and apply functions to the manipulate the MoSMB configurations in /etc/mosmb/mosmb.json Standard/Default path for the json file is /etc/mosmb/ . If user of "mosmbcli" wants to redirect the output files to particular location, then the environment variable MOSMB_CONF_PATH has to be set with path of that location. Eg. export MOSMB_CONF_PATH=/home/user Note that mosmb.json generated at exported path won't get applied on MoSMB service start/restart
Command-line JSON processor : jq package

Network Time Protocol Configuration

Important :
All the components - AD, MoSMB Server, and SMB client machines should always be time synced for the AD-Kerberos authentication.
For this reason, to configure the MoSMB server:
Install Network Time Protocol (ntp) / (chronyd).
Start or restart this Network Time Protocol (ntp) / (chronyd)  service on the MoSMB server.
Example:
Command : yum install ntp or apt-get install ntp Command : systemctl stop ntpd.service or service ntp stop Command : systemctl start ntpd.service or service ntp start
Note : To resolve the Fully Qualified Domain Name(FQDN) of a Network Time Protocol(NTP) server, ensure that your Domain Name Server(DNS) settings are well configured.
Warning : The Kerberos authentication fails in case:
The Network Time Protocol (NTP) is unavailable.
The time between the MoSMB server, AD and SMB clients is unsynced.
Active Directory Configuration
MoSMB supports Microsoft Active Directory (AD) based Kerberos authentication. The MoSMB server is added to the AD Domain to authenticate and authorize the SMB Clients's access to MoSMB shares.
Note :

MoSMB relies on the internal transitive trusts created between different domains in the AD Forest to authenticate the authorized AD users from other Domains to access the MoSMB shares. A single MoSMB server can join multiple AD Domains of different Forests to allow users of different AD Forests to access the Shares when configured as part of a Public Cloud Infrastructure.
From the MoSMB server run one of the following utility to carry out a domain join process:
Domainjoin-cli (Recommended for Ubuntu or Debian)
This uses PowerBrokerIdentityServices (PBIS).
Install likewise-open Command : sudo apt-get install likewise-open

To join the domain: Command : domainjoin-cli join <AD_Domain_Name> <AD_Domain_Administrator> [<password>] Note :If the password is not supplied on command line, you will be prompted to specify it at the next prompt.
To leave a domain: Command : domainjoin-cli leave
To list the current domain: Command : domainjoin-cli query
Realm Join (Recommended for CentOS)
Before a domain join verify the /etc/resolv.conf . It should ideally contain the domain related information.
Example:
Command : cat /etc/resolv.conf Output  : # Generated by NetworkManager search ad.example.com nameserver xx.xx.x.xx nameserver xxx.xx.xxx.x nameserver x.x.x.x
Install the realm and dependent packages on the server machine. Command : yum install realmd OR apt-get install realm
Search for the domain you wish to join. Command : realm discover <domain name> Note: <domain name> here is the search value from the /etc/resolv.conf file, that is ad.example.com.
To join the domain with realm. Command : realm join --user= <username> <domain name> Note: The domain join creates a machine entry in the AD. You must update the DNS entry manually.
To leave the domain with realm. Command : realm leave <domain name>
To list the current domain. Command : realm list
adcli join (Alternative to Ubuntu/CentOS)
Before a domain join verify the /etc/resolv.conf file. It should ideally contain the domain related information. Refer to the example in the Realm Join steps.
Install the realm and dependent packages on the server machine. Command : yum install adcli OR apt-get install adcli

To join the domain with adcli: Command : adcli join --service-name=host -S <domain_name> -U <domain_administrator_user> --host-fqdn= <server_hostname>.<domain_name> Note: The domain join creates a machine entry in the AD. You must update the DNS entry manually.
To leave a domain with adcli: Command : adcli delete-computer --domain= <domain_name> -U <domain_administrator_user> Note: This deletes the entry from AD. You must update the DNS entries manually.
Domain Name Server Configuration

The Domain Name Server (DNS) configuration is an important activity during the AD-Kerberos authentication. The authentication between SMB3 Clients and the MoSMB server will not work as expected if this above mentioned  DNS configuration is not done.
You must have a working model of the DNS in your Evaluation and/or Production environment before you start using the appliance.
You require administrative rights of the DNS machine to make related modifications.
Note :
To edit the DNS server settings manually, please coordinate with your DNS Manager or Network Administrator .
Validate if the entry for the MoSMB server is added to the corresponding DNS server(s).
Standalone
Sign in to the DNS machine as an Administrator.
Open the DNS manager.
Under Forward Lookup Zones , select the desired domain name. For example .dom.
Right click in the Window. Select New Host (A or AAAA)...
Fill in the related information: Name is the hostname of the MoSMB server. IP address is the IP address of the MoSMB server.
Name is the hostname of the MoSMB server.
IP address is the IP address of the MoSMB server.
Add the Host.
Repeat this procedure to add an entry in the Reverse Lookup Zones , if not added already.
Cluster
Sign in to the DNS machine of the cluster as an Administrator.
Open the DNS manager.
Delete all the entries containing hostname to ip address mapping of the cluster nodes in both - the forward and reverse lookup zones.
Select the desired domain name in Forward Lookup Zones . For  example .dom.
Assign a common hostname to all the nodes in the cluster.
Right click in the Window and select New Host (A or AAAA)...
Fill in the related information: Name is the hostname of the MoSMB server IP address is the IP address of the MoSMB server.
Name is the hostname of the MoSMB server
IP address is the IP address of the MoSMB server.
Add the Host.
Example: If 10.10.0.X(node1), 10.10.0.Y(node2) are the two nodes in the cluster then add:
Note :
The SMB clients cannot use the FQDN name, in case: The DNS is unavailable, or If the DNS entry is not added for the MoSMB server.
The DNS is unavailable, or
If the DNS entry is not added for the MoSMB server.
The above mentioned methods of AD DNS joining have been provided solely to guide you. You can perform your network configuration in a method that you are comfortable in.
For more Configuration Settings in the Cluster Mode, refer to the Single Domain Cluster Mode Documentation / Multi-Domain Cluster Mode Documentation .

Cache Cleanup

a. Truncates the file in local cache which is older than the “threshold” time mentioned in the s3.json. b. Scans the entire local cache in interval of “periodicity” time mentioned in the s3.json and marks the file
enableCacheCleanup

Specify if cache cleanup needs to be enabled or not. 
If set to "yes", cache cleanup will be executed.
If set to "no" , cache cleanup will not be executed.
threshold

If last accessed/modified time of the file is beyond the threshold value, then the files will be cleaned up from the cache. User can specify duration in minutes<m>/hours<H>/days<D>/months<M>. Default it set to 30 days. Configuring this value in days is recommended.
periodicity

Specify the duration of how frequently clean-up thread should run. User can specify duration in minutes<m>/hours<H>/days<D>/months<M>. Default it set to 1 day. Configuring this value in days is recommended.
enableFifoCleanup

Specify if FIFO cache cleanup needs to be enabled or not. FIFO cache cleanup will start cleanup of the LRU least recently used files in the cache.
The check will happen periodically at an interval provided in 'periodicity'
If set to "yes", cache cleanup with FIFO method will be executed.
If set to "no" , cache cleanup with FIFO method will not be executed.
lowerLimit
Specify The lower limit of the disk space in percentage. The cleanup of files activity will continue until the lower limit of 'cache storage' is reached.
upperLimit
Specify The upper limit of the disk space in percentage.  The cleanup of files activity will begin when the upper limit of 'cache storage' is used and when the threshold value is satisfied.

bucketSection
bucketName
Specify the name of the s3 bucket to be exported as share. This is a mandatory parameter.
sharePath

Share path to be exported. The “sharePath’’ is same as as “sharePath” parameter in mosmb.conf or mosmb.json file. The bucket in s3.json will be mapped to share in mosmb.json which has same sharePath as mentioned for bucket. sharePath must be unique for every bucket. This  is a mandatory parameter.

endpoint
URL which is the entry point for the object store web service. This is optional for AWS, but mandatory for other object stores.
region
Specify the region of s3 bucket.
accessKeyId
Specify the Access ID of the s3 account.
secretKey
Specify the Secret Key of the s3 account.
multipartSize

Specify single part size in case of multipart upload (In bytes). Part size should be minimum 5 MB (5242880 Bytes). Part size can be maximum 5 GB (5368709120 Bytes). Maximum number of parts per upload is 10,000. If kept blank, default is 125 MB.
httpScheme
Mention HTTP scheme either https/http. By default it is set to https.
verifySsl
Specify if “yes” if ssl certificate needs to be checked. By default it is set to “yes”.
asyncThreads

Specify the number of async threads to be used for object store operations.
By default it is set to 16.
throttleBandwidthStrategy
Specify “yes” if bandwidth throttling is needed. By default it is set to “no”.

Specify “Write bandwidth” to be allocated for s3. Mention in KBps, MBps or in GBps. 
By default all the available bandwidth is used for s3 operations.

Specify “Read bandwidth” to be allocated for s3. Mention in KBps, MBps or in GBps. 
By default all the available bandwidth is used for s3 operations.

MoSMB Service Not Starting

If mosmb service fails to start, check the mosmb.log from the log folder (Default is /var/log) for Error messages. 
In case there is json syntax error you can use the mosmb json syntax validation script
Configuration Errors
The mosmb.log should indicate if any configuration errors pertaining to the json parameters are encountered. Accordingly use following to correct thoes.
Verify if the MoSMB share path mentioned in the configuration actually exists on the server.
Verify if the persistent store path mentioned in the MoSMB configuration actually exists on the server.

Verify if a valid combination of Authentication Modes and Authorization Modes is in use: i. When the authentication mode is krb5 , the permitted authorization modes are posix and ntacl . ii. When the authentication is ntlmLocal , the permitted authorization mode is posix .
Verify if the plugin path mentioned in the MoSMB configuration is correct.
Verify that the performance parameters like number of threads, buffer size and pre-processor threads are not exceeding the available resources. Refer to Performance Tuning
Verify if the bind interface value mentioned is actually an active interface on the server.
Refer to the MoSMB configuration parameters for further details.
Verify the required ports are open
Refer to Enable ports in firewall for MoSMB
License error in logs
For any Licensing related errors refer to section MoSMB Licensing Issues below
MoSMB Licensing Issues

To start the MoSMB server you require a trial license or a product key. Note : If the MoSMB server is a GCP instance then the following licensing scheme is not applicable. For more detailed information on Licensing refer to MoSMB License Manager page
Online Product Activation
Verify if the MoSMB server machine has a working internet connection.
Verify if the MoSMB server's network adapters are up.
Verify if the firewall policies allow port 443 to connect to the MoSMB license servers.
Verify if https://wyday.com/limelm/ is reachable from the MoSMB server.
Verify if the time and date settings of the MoSMB server are correct or up-to-date.
Verify if a valid product key is provided in the MoSMB configuration file for Licenses with product key.

Verify if the product key has reached the maximum number of activations limit. The information on the maximum limit for the number of machines the product key can be activated is given while providing the product key.
Online Trial Activation
Verify if the MoSMB server has a working internet connection.
Verify if MoSMB server's network adapters are up.
Verify if the firewall policies allow port 443 to connect to the MoSMB license servers.
Verify if https://wyday.com/limelm/ is reachable from the MoSMB server.
Verify if the time and date settings for the MoSMB server machine are correct or up-to-date.
Offline Product Activation
Verify if the MoSMB server's network adapters are up.
Verify if the product key mentioned in the MoSMB configuration and the product key used while activating the license is the same.
Make sure you have followed the steps for activation as per the offline product activation using product key
Offline Trial Activation
Verify if the trialresponse.xml file that is provided to you is placed at the /etc/mosmb/license/ location.
Verify if the trialrequest.xml is generated on the same MoSMB server machine on which you need to activate the MoSMB license.
Verify if the MoSMB server's network adapters are up.
Failed to check activation within grace period

When the Error - Failed to check product activation within grace period is seen in mosmb.log It would indicate that the product key was activated online on the server machine BUT now the server machine is not able to reach to | limelm site To activate the product key again, deactivate the current product key activation by running /etc/mosmb/license/.mosmb_license_deactivate Then follow offline activation steps
MoSMB Share Not Accessible
Check the mosmb.log for any obvious error messages that would indicate what is the problem.
Check if mosmb service is running, check pid or process information using ps

# service mosmb status
# pidof mosmb
# ps -aux| grep mosmb
Verify if port 445 is open for the TCP communication.
Verify if the TCP port mentioned in the MoSMB configuration file is also configured on the client machine and is open for communication over TCP.
If error is related to krb5, Refer to the Troubleshooting for the AD Kerberos MoSMB share access when authentication mode used for share access is krb5
If error is related to gss-api refer to the troubleshooting section for gss-api errors
If the authentication mode is ntlmLocal make sure that the (local linux) MoSMB user is created on the MoSMB server machine and you are accessing the share via the same user.

check the mosmb_audit.log for login from the user for whom the access is failing, to check if Login attempt is failing or user has successfully logged in.  Refer here for more details on the mosmb_audit.log
If Login is successful, Verify if the user has the permission to access the share for the authentication mode used (krb5, ntlm-AD or ntlm_local) as mentioned in Permission Issues section below.

If a previously working node is removed and Re Joined to AD , for ntlmAD to work after such a change, you will need to re run mosmb_passwd -m and restart mosmb service too. Also old kerberos ticket on client side may prevent you from accessing the share. You need to purge the cache to remove old cluster/node kerberos ticket with klist purge OR user can sign out and signing to the client. OR The kerberos cache will expire in scheduled 10 hours and next access will renew the ticket automatically.
MoSMB Share Not Accessible on Ubuntu 22.04 OR error seen with mosmb_passwd binary
possible error seen with mosmb_passwd :
ValueError: unsupported hash type md4
Verify if openssl configuration required for Ubuntu 22.04 are done
If the server machines have openssl version 3 and above configuration changes are required. Check openssl version with
openssl version
Open /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf
Existing/Default Configuration :

[provider_sect]
   default = default_sect
   [default_sect]
   # activate = 1
Edits/Required Configuration :

[provider_sect]
   default = default_sect legacy = legacy_sect [openssl_init]
   providers = provider_sect
 
   [default_sect] activate = 1 [legacy_sect] activate = 1
MoSMB Share Not Accessible for only a particular user

If access fails for a user, make sure audit logs are enabled and verify login attempts from the user for mosmb nodes. Check the mosmb_audit.log to see if Login attempt is failing or user has successfully logged in.

If login is successful check permission issues. 
If login is failed check the authentication password/domain information
If no login attempt message is seen check mosmb.log for MO_ERROR or MO_WARN logs
Permission Issues
Verify that the correct user permissions are set on the MoSMB server side and the same user is used to access the share and create or edit the shared files or directories.
POSIX Authorization Settings
NTACL Authorization Settings
Viewing File / Directory Permissions
Verify if port 88 is open on both the MoSMB server and the AD machine. This is required by the LDAP server.
When the authentication mode is krb5 verify if the Kerberos ticket is present on the MoSMB server.
In case the authentication mode is krb5 verify if all the LDAP client configuration settings are carried out.
In case the authentication mode is krb5 verify if all the SSSD related settings are carried out.
Verify if the package necessary for generating UUID is installed on the MoSMB server. If not please install the package.
ID Mapper Not Working Correctly
Verify if the sssd service is running on the MoSMB server.
check with id command that the AD user's details are retrieved and it does print the user ID and group IDs. In case it is failing to do so, try restarting the sssd service.
Verify the nsswitch.conf file and make sure that .sss is mentioned in the configuration.

Changes to the AD group membership are slow to take effect. The issue could be because of the sssd cache. When you change the primary group of the user then the old entry in the sssd cache becomes invalid. For new changes to take effect restart the sssd service and run the following command: sss_cache -E OR sss_cache -u <user-name>

If you are considering EVERYONE present in NTFS (Everyone includes the owner, group and other members) as Others (in Linux this means all the members other than the owner and group members) mapping will fail.
Note : In the case of user and group mapping, MoSMB currently supports only the primary group mapping.
Issue with cluster keytab. Cluster fqdn access not working. (gss-api errors in mosmb.log)
Check time is in sync with the AD
Make sure the keytab creation is correctly done using ktpass on AD by the Admin (w.o. any errors or warning during the creation) using correct CRYPTO values used to generate keys in the keytab.

Use following PS cmdlet to check the spn is correctly mapped the special user setspn -Q cifs/<cifs-server-name> And use the follwoing PS cmdlet to check the user's SPN Get-ADUser <username> -property ServicePrincipalName
The DNS forward lookup works for the cluster fqdn and reverse lookup work correctly for cluster node ips
Check firewall for correct ports opened for kerberos
Check that the special user created for keytab creation in the ktpass 'mapuser', has following properties set:

Go to Active Directory users and computers 
User properties -> user account -> password never expires
User properties -> user account -> Correct encryption support (in accordance with the CRYPTO values used in ktpass) is enabled, e.g. This account supports Kerberos AES 256 bit encryption This account supports Kerberos AES 128 bit encryption You can also check the encryptions used in the keytab with -e option of klist klist -kte /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
OR use following PS cmdlet to check the user's supported encryption types Get-ADUser <username> -property KerberosEncryptionType
Check KVno from keytab against the special user.

Note the msDS-KeyVersionNumber from the o/p of following PS cmdlet and compare with kvno in the keytab from the output of klist ran on the mosmb.keytab Get-ADUser <username> -property msDS-KeyVersionNumber klist -kt /etc/mosmb/mosmb.keytab
In mosmb.json, check that globalSection is set to have enableHighAvailability to yes
Check the o/p of klist -A to check a valid krb5 tkt is fetched during mosmb startup ( mosmb will use the mosmb.keytab and domain_list.txt to do a kinit )
Check sssd.conf, krb5.conf, ldap.conf are set in accordance with the mosmb documentation
Check permitted encryption types from the krb5 crypto-policies
cat /etc/krb5.conf.d/crypto-policies

If setup was working fine previously, and now we are getting "Preauthentication failed" error in mosmb.log, verify that the password for special user used for cluster keytab creation is not expired or changed. If so, need to recreate the mosmb.keytab using ktpass

ZooKeeper Troubleshooting
1. Error 1:
Unable to start AdminServer, exiting abnormally
Solution : Check for admin.serverPort configuration in zoo.cfg :
#admin server port configuration admin.serverPort=8082
Make sure port 8082 is free for use, if not free, use some other port
netstat -ntlp | grep 8082
2. Error 2:
java.net.BindException: Address already in use (Bind failed)
Solution : Check if ports configured are available, if not free then use some other port combination
netstat -ntlp | grep 4888 & netstat -ntlp | grep 5888 on all ZooKeeper Server machines.

Example :	
server.1 = <ip_server1>:4888:5888
server.2 = <ip_server2>:4888:5888
server.3 = <ip_server3>:4888:5888
Here 4888 used for follower nodes to connect to the leader node and 5888 used for leader election.
3. MoSMB service not starting due to older zookeeper versions refer :- Uninstalling previous zookeeper version
4. Make sure after successful installation all required zookeeper libraries are present on the zookeeper server.

Manage File Shares with Shared Folders MMC Snap-in. Close sessions and close open file handles can be managed via MMC
Open mmc on a windows → Navigate to the Start Menu and select Run, type 'mmc'.
On the MMC console from File menu select Add or Remove snap-ins
Select and add Shared Folders snap-in
In Shared Folder properties → Enter the node fqdn or ip and connect.
Check Shares to list the smb-shares on the nodes.
Check Sessions to list the smb-sessions connected to the node.
Check Open Files to list all the open files on the node.
MAC slow browsing performance
NOTE: sonoma 14.6.1 onwards nsmb.conf does not help, refer to below points for older MAC OS versions
To improve MAC smb client's browsing performance, following settings can be tried 1. edit /etc/nsmb.conf

[default]
dir_cache_off=yes
aapl_off=true
signing_required=no
file_ids_off=yes
dir_ids_off=yes
defaults write com.apple.desktopservices DSDontWriteNetworkStores -bool TRUE
On terminal run defaults write com.apple.desktopservices DSDontWriteNetworkStores -bool TRUE
check the setting with defaults read com.apple.desktopservices DSDontWriteNetworkStores
Restart your MAC once these changes are done
2. Disable icon previews and size calculation

connect to share
Browse to share
Press CMD + j (or rt click-> show view options )
  Disable “Calculate all sizes”
  Disable “Show icon preview”
Select “Use as Defaults”
3. some more view options
Going further u can disable showing 'date modified' and 'size' in the view options
4. Turn off indexing spotlight indexing can be turned off with
sudo mdutil -a -i off /Volumes/<share-name>
To see whether Spotlight indexing is enabled for a volume:

mdutil -s -v /Volumes/share-name
 example: mdutil -s /Volumes/share1 /System/Volumes/Data/Volumes/share1:
    Indexing and searching disabled.
5. 311 smb protocol in mosmb.json on releases 072 and later Try changing the max smb protocol to 311 on the mosmb server in mosmb.json file and restart mosmb service
maxSmbProtocol = 311
6. If access is still slow; try some more network settings
try following n/w settings on MAC , run from terminal

sysctl -w kern.ipc.maxsockbuf=8388608
sysctl -w net.inet.tcp.sendspace=2097152
sysctl -w net.inet.tcp.recvspace=2097152
sysctl -w net.inet.tcp.delayed_ack=2
sysctl -w net.inet.tcp.win_scale_factor=8
sysctl -w net.inet.tcp.autorcvbufmax=33554432
sysctl -w net.inet.tcp.autosndbufmax=33554432
No restart required, if restarted the settings will be lost. Some more settings

sysctl -w net.smb.fs.kern_deadtimer=0
sysctl -w net.smb.fs.kern_hard_deadtimer=0
sysctl -w net.smb.fs.kern_soft_deadtimer=0

In addition, if your Mac needs above network settings to be retained on reboot.
Create a file named /etc/sysctl.conf in your local /etc folder
This needs again to be done as root!

Go to the command line, become root and fire up your preferred editor (vi) by typing vi /etc/sysctl.conf
Put the following into the newly created file,

vi /etc/sysctl.conf 
kern.ipc.maxsockbuf=8388608
net.inet.tcp.sendspace=2097152
net.inet.tcp.recvspace=2097152
net.inet.tcp.delayed_ack=2
net.inet.tcp.win_scale_factor=8
net.inet.tcp.autorcvbufmax=33554432
net.inet.tcp.autosndbufmax=33554433
net.smb.fs.kern_deadtimer=0
net.smb.fs.kern_hard_deadtimer=0
net.smb.fs.kern_soft_deadtimer=0

and save the file. The file rights must be set to:
owner root:wheel and -rw-r--r--
please make sure by typing ls -al /etc/sysctl.conf on the command line and if not, change it by running
chown root:wheel /etc/sysctl.conf
and
chmod 0644 /etc/sysctl.conf from the command line
Note :
We do not recommend switching between the ACL strategies over existing data.
For a share, switching between the ACL strategies over existing data will result in change of owner and group mapping for smb user equivalent Linux user.
In NTACL scheme the file ownership is owner/group as root/root. Windows ACL are stored in metadata store for the file.

In Posix ACL scheme the smb session user is mapped as per current service mentioned in /etc/nsswitch.conf So in case /etc/nsswitch.conf has SSSD settings it will map to UID/GID based on settings in /etc/sssd.conf .

If a user changes these schemes for existing shares from ntlmLocal authentication to krb5 authentication , sssd configuration is needed for user id mapping.
This will shift the user mapping scheme from local users to the one mapped by SSSD. The local linux users will not be mapped to the domain users in such case for the shared files or folders.

So we suggest that network administrator should not switch between authentication schemes and ACL schemes for existing shares. So that permissions are retained.
In case the switching of scheme is must network administrator would have to do file owner/group changes to share manually to adapt to the new scheme of ACL and authentication.
